What Session 182 Did

Deliverable: Implement issue #73 Part 1 – generalize the bundled speciesGestation lookup from rhesus-only to all 14 common colony NHP species, moving the two breeding-age columns integer -> numeric to hold fractional minima. Strict TDD. (DONE – committed on branch issue-73-populate-breeding-age-table; UNPUBLISHED; master unchanged. Part 2 (user-configurable) deferred -> #73 stays OPEN.) Started / Completed: 2026-06-23 / 2026-06-23 Status: DONE. Owner supplied the biological values (a veterinary call); 14-species table built; getSpeciesMinBreedingAge now returns numeric; strict TDD, every transition AskUserQuestion-gated (PRE-RED scope: Part-1-only + species keys + gestation-supply + rhesus-210-keep, then PRE-RED->RED -> RED->GREEN -> GREEN->REFACTOR). 0 stakeholder corrections. Grounding fanned out via a read-only 5-agent workflow (wf_b386b272-c0e; 4 returned, the config-mechanism agent hit the structured-output retry cap -> backfilled the config facts directly from the other agents + loadSiteConfig/the appUI.R:223-228 Settings placeholder). Design synthesis + owner gates main-loop. Owner picked: Part 1 this session (Part 2 separate); the proposed species keys; “I will supply gestation values”; “keep rhesus at 210”; and approved every TDD transition. - Owner-supplied values + conversion rules (surfaced, not invented). 14 species with min female/male breeding-age ranges + gestation ranges. Min breeding age = LOW end of each range; gestation column = conservative-MAX = HIGH end. Fractional minima (rhesus/cyno F 2.5, squirrel M 3.5, marmoset 1.0, tamarin 1.5) forced the breeding-age columns integer -> NUMERIC (owner-chosen). Rhesus gestation kept at its existing conservative 210 (owner chose keep-210 over the supplied 164-166 typical, via AskUserQuestion). - Blast radius PROVEN firsthand, not assumed. Rhesus female moved 3 -> 2.5, but NO shipped pedigree carries a species column -> the GVA correction uses the species-absent default cutoff 2 on real data; rhesus/new-species cutoffs are exercised only by accessor unit tests + the synthetic fixture in test_correctUnknownParentMeanKinship.R (which uses the rhesus MALE cutoff 4, unchanged). So the whole change is contained to TWO test files; test_correctUnknownParentMeanKinship.R + test_reportGV.R were verified-unaffected (20/0 and 25/0), not assumed. - RED (gated): 49 failing assertions – test_getSpeciesMinBreedingAge.R rewritten (numeric column types, rhesus M=4/F=2.5, “table carries all 14 species”, new-species values incl. fractional, injected/default/empty -> numeric, JAPANESE MACAQUE swapped out of the unknown-fallback case) + test_getSpeciesGestation.R (new-species gestation assertions; JAPANESE MACAQUE repointed to its shipped 180L). Confirmed failing for the right reason, no collateral; the 2 unaffected files green. - GREEN (gated, minimum): data-raw/speciesGestation.R -> 14 rows (gestation integer; breeding ages numeric); re-ran it to regenerate data/speciesGestation.RData; getSpeciesMinBreedingAge.R coercions as.integer -> as.numeric (male/female/default) + integer(0L) -> numeric(0L). All 4 targeted files green; full clean regression 0/0. - REFACTOR (gated): doc/roxygen only (R/data.R, both accessors: integer -> numeric, multi-species; getSpeciesGestation example repointed off the now-known JAPANESE MACAQUE; data-raw header comment) + devtools::document() (man regen; NAMESPACE byte-identical) + lint. .lintr excludes tests/ but NOT data-raw/, so the data vectors use explicit doubles (4.0) + the accessor default 2L -> 2.0. 0 lints; 0 spelling delta.

Phase-3E (build-equivalent + runtime): SATISFIED. Build-equivalent devtools::check(vignettes = FALSE) = 0/0/0; full clean regression 2183 pass / 0 fail / 0 error (203 files, 167 skip); 0 lints (namespace-loaded, Learning 167); spell_check_package(".") = 0 unrecognized (no WORDLIST change); NAMESPACE unchanged. Runtime: this is a bundled-data + accessor change with NO app-wiring change (no startup/dispatch/config-resolution touched) -> a browser smoke is N/A; the accessor unit tests + the synthetic species-bearing fixture + the real-qcPed test_reportGV.R integration test are the runtime evidence and all pass. The new-species values have unit/fixture coverage only – no shipped dataset carries a species column, so they are not exercised on real ONPRC/SNPRC data (inherent to the data; same as Slice 2/3) -> stated, not silently skipped.

Session 181 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 182): Score 8/10. S181’s => SUGGESTED NEXT listed #73 as the SECOND natural option with an accurate one-liner (“species breeding-age generalization – logically follows Slice 2 seeding the table; generalize to more species + make user-configurable”) – correctly naming both parts. The publish-state facts all held FIRSTHAND: origin/master == local master (no drift – Gotcha 2 said “branch directly from master with NO FF-first step”, and I did exactly that, cutting issue-73-... straight off 3937a918); #9 CLOSED, #76 the open follow-up; the standing keeps (PED_GV_AUDIT untracked, .DS_Store). The one hash it named (172252bf) was superseded by S181’s own follow-up commit 3937a918, but the SUBSTANCE (in-sync, branch-directly) was exactly right. The -2: #73 got a one-line treatment (reasonably – S181 paved #76/Reading A as its option 1, the predicted next, not #73), so for #73 I did all the grounding myself (the two-part structure, the “biological values are a veterinary call” framing, the config mechanism, the consumer map) – those lived in the issue #73 body + the code (which I read), not the handoff. A one-line “#73 Part 1’s values are a veterinary call -> expect to ask the owner; Part 2 is a separate feature” would have pre-framed it. ROI solid: the in-sync state + branch-directly was exactly right and saved the FF dance.

Self-assessment (Session 182): 8/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER read in full; SESSION_NOTES ACTIVE TASK; GH issues; dashboard 98/100; ghost-check -> HEAD 3937a918 = S181, no undocumented commits), reported, STOPPED for the owner’s pick; claimed with a 1B stub BEFORE technical work; declared the TDD phase every response; branched off master before any COMMIT. Strengths: (1) surfaced EVERY genuine decision to the owner rather than picking – scope (Part 1 vs plan-first), species keys, gestation-supply, the range->value rules (min=low end / gestation=high end), and rhesus 210-vs-166 – five AskUserQuestions + the three TDD gates ([[observation-vs-decision]]); (2) grounded the codebase firsthand via a read-only fan-out (consumers, data-build, plan context, UI modules; the config agent failed structured-output -> backfilled directly), then PROVED the load-bearing blast-radius claim firsthand (no shipped pedigree has a species column -> rhesus 3->2.5 changes no real-data/GVA behavior -> change contained to two test files); (3) strict TDD, every transition gated, clean RED (49 assertions failing for the right reason, 2 unaffected files confirmed green not assumed), GREEN minimum (data + 4 coercions), REFACTOR doc+lint only; (4) handled the type-system change completely (integer->numeric cascade through the column, accessor, default, empty-return, and every type-strict expect_identical) and caught the .lintr data-raw-not-excluded nuance (explicit doubles 4.0); (5) fully verified – 0/0/0 build-equivalent, 2183/0/0 regression, 0 lint, 0 spelling delta, NAMESPACE unchanged, man regenerated; scope-disciplined (Part 1 only; Part 2 deferred; #73 stays open), plain language, ASCII gates. Weaknesses (honest): (a) new-species values have unit/fixture coverage only – no shipped dataset carries a species column, so they’re not exercised on real data (inherent to the data, carried from Slice 2/3; documented); (b) one grounding agent failed the structured-output retry cap – backfilled directly, but a lighter schema would have avoided the gap; (c) I made the multi-file edits on master before branching (uncommitted) – a minor deviation from “branch for code first”; corrected by creating the branch before any commit with master verified clean at 3937a918; (d) no app/browser runtime smoke – but this is a data+accessor change with no app-wiring change, so the accessor + synthetic fixture + real-qcPed integration test are the right runtime evidence. Moderate base difficulty (a type-system change, five surfaced decisions, a veterinary-values component, a firsthand blast-radius proof), zero corrections, fully verified -> 8/10.

Learnings: Learning 170 added to PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md – the five #73-Part-1 reflexes: (1) fractional values force an integer->numeric column-type cascade through the accessor + every type-strict expect_identical; (2) biological values are an owner/veterinary call – surface, don’t invent (incl. the range->value rule as its own decision); (3) don’t silently overwrite a deliberate existing value (rhesus 210) just because the owner re-supplied a typical figure; (4) PROVE blast radius with the data (no shipped pedigree has a species column -> the change is contained to two test files); (5) .lintr excludes tests/ but not data-raw/ -> explicit doubles in the data vectors. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]] (the TDD-gate contract + branch-for-code + implementation->CHANGELOG / publish->NEWS rhythm + the veterinary-values-are-the-owner’s framing), [[observation-vs-decision]] / [[ascii-only-in-question-options]] / [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]] (the five scope + three phase AskUserQuestions; plain-ASCII gates), [[backlog-vs-changelog-placement]] (completed work -> CHANGELOG; #73 stays OPEN for Part 2), [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]] (read the plan §8-D/§8-G + the prior Slice-2/3 learnings firsthand); Learnings 161 (build-equivalent), 167 (the data-object extension checklist + namespace-loaded lint + implicit_integer/explicit doubles – this session is its direct successor), 157a (NEWS folds into the publish PR).

=> SUGGESTED NEXT = owner’s pick. The deliverable is committed on issue-73-populate-breeding-age-table (feat + this docs commit); master unchanged at 3937a918 (== origin/master). Issue #73 stays OPEN until Part 2 ships. Natural options (plain ASCII labels): - (Publish #73 Part 1) the standard publish session: the branch was cut DIRECTLY off master and master==origin/master (NO local-ahead drift this cycle), so NO “push master first” FF step is needed (unlike the S178/S180 Gotchas). Push the branch + open a PR -> master; FOLD a NEWS entry into the SAME PR (Learning 157a) – user-facing: the species breeding-age table now covers the common colony NHP species with species-specific minimum breeding ages, and the values are numeric so fractional minima (e.g. 2.5 years) are exact; keep it plain ASCII, re-render NEWS.md (html_preview:false+md_extensions:"-smart", 155), spell_check_package(".") before/after (159). Watch the 5-platform CI (do NOT merge blind – fresh non-watch gh pr checks, 157); AskUserQuestion-gate the merge; verified-merged-before-delete (146). Use “Relates to #73” NOT “Closes #73” (Part 2 remains). - (#73 Part 2 – user-configurable override path) the remaining part of #73: thread breedingTable/gestationTable overrides through reportGV -> correctUnknownParentMeanKinship and modGeneticValue/modPotentialParents (the accessors already accept them; reportGV.R:103 passes NULL today), and build the Settings UI (the appUI.R:223-228 placeholder) and/or a config-file path via loadSiteConfig. A genuine feature – likely a design/plan session THEN a separate implement. Closes #73 when shipped. - (Other options) #76 (Reading A, deep genetics – ratify-then-implement, do NOT bundle); #37 (unused exports); #36 (chimpanzee age-pyramid); #2 (GVA iteration-count advice); #28 (large, own plan); older #13/#12/#11/#10/#5/#1; CRAN Phase 5 (owner-run, docs/planning/cran-2.0.0-phase5-runbook.md). Do NOT bundle the Part 1 publish with Part 2 implementation (FM #18/#25); do NOT start Part 2 without deciding the config mechanism (Settings UI vs config-file via loadSiteConfig) – it depends on that choice.

Key files (this session): CHANGED – the deliverable (on the branch): data-raw/speciesGestation.R (14-row table; gestation int, breeding ages numeric), data/speciesGestation.RData (regenerated via Rscript data-raw/speciesGestation.R), R/getSpeciesMinBreedingAge.R (as.integer->as.numeric x3 + integer(0L)->numeric(0L) + default 2L->2.0 + roxygen integer->numeric), R/getSpeciesGestation.R (example repointed off JAPANESE MACAQUE, doc only), R/data.R (speciesGestation @description: 14 species + numeric breeding-age columns + rhesus F 2.5), man/speciesGestation.Rd + man/getSpeciesMinBreedingAge.Rd + man/getSpeciesGestation.Rd (regenerated). Tests: tests/testthat/test_getSpeciesMinBreedingAge.R (rewritten – numeric + 14-species), tests/testthat/test_getSpeciesGestation.R (new-species gestation assertions). NO man/NAMESPACE behavioral change (data + internal coercion). CHANGED – close-out docs (this docs commit, same branch): CHANGELOG.md (S182 [Unreleased] entry), PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 170), SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). NO NEWS change (folded into the publish PR, 157a). The issue (input): #73 body (owner-supplied 14-species breeding-age + gestation tables); plan §8-D/§8-G (Slice 2 context). Grounding workflow: wf_b386b272-c0e (5 read-only agents; script under the session dir, NOT in the repo). Scratch (NOT in repo): scratchpad/issue73-breeding-ages.md (the parsed values + decisions). NOT committed (standing keeps): PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked); .DS_Store.

Gotchas: (1) Issue #73 is OPEN – Part 1 (data) committed UNPUBLISHED on branch issue-73-populate-breeding-age-table; Part 2 (user-configurable override path) remains. Publish uses “Relates to #73” not “Closes #73”. (2) master == origin/master == 3937a918; the branch was cut DIRECTLY off master (NO local-ahead drift) – so the publish needs NO “push master first” FF step (the S178/S180 Gotcha does NOT apply this cycle). (3) The new-species breeding-age + gestation values are NOT exercised on real shipped data – no species column in qcPed/breederPed/examplePedigree; only accessor unit tests + the synthetic fixture exercise them. A real-data exercise needs a species-bearing pedigree (carried from Slice 2/3). (4) The breeding-age columns are now NUMERIC (gestation stays integer); getSpeciesMinBreedingAge returns numeric. Any later code/test asserting integer breeding ages will break – a full-suite grep + regression found none beyond the two updated test files. (5) .lintr excludes tests/ but NOT data-raw/ – new numeric data vectors must be explicit doubles (4.0) or CI lint_package() fails on implicit_integer. (6) Carried standing keeps (unchanged): package ARCHIVED on CRAN 2025-07-29; CRAN Phase 5 owner-gated (docs/planning/cran-2.0.0-phase5-runbook.md); getLkDirectRelatives()/getDemographics() FAIL SOFT (warning + NULL) without a LabKey credential/config; exactly ONE codecov config (codecov.yml); NEWS render traps CLOSED at source (html_preview:false+md_extensions:"-smart", 155); git pull is rebase (pull.rebase=true) + chokes on .DS_Store -> use fetch+reset (135); post-merge fetch before reset --hard must be ancestor-gated (146); skip_on_cran()-gated testServer tests need NOT_CRAN=true; the build-equivalent is devtools::check(vignettes = FALSE) = 0/0/0 (Learning 161).

What Session 181 Did

Deliverable: Publish issue #9 Slice 3 (classify unknown-parent animals + classification-aware GVA rank, S180 feat 3be9f818) from issue-9-slice3-classify-rank-aware to master via PR, folding the user-facing NEWS entry into the SAME PR (Learning 157a). (DONE – merged to master via PR #75, merge commit 5ada4b71; #9 verified CLOSED; branch deleted; Reading A filed as follow-up #76.) Started / Completed: 2026-06-23 / 2026-06-23 Status: DONE. The Slice 3 classification + classification-aware displayed rank + its NEWS entry are LIVE on origin/master (5ada4b71). Admin/publish + docs -> TDD declared N/A (the Slice 3 code was written/tested under strict TDD in S180; the only content change this session is one NEWS bullet). 0 stakeholder corrections. SOLO (a serial, irreversible git sequence – a workflow adds risk, not coverage; held solo despite ultracode, the S176/S179 judgment). Owner picked “Publish Slice 3” at orientation, “Closes #9” at the issue-close gate, and “Merge (merge commit)” at the AskUserQuestion merge gate. - Pre-publish content (one PR, Learning 157a): read the FOUR changed R/ files firsthand (classifyParentage.R, reportGV.R, orderReport.R, modGeneticValue.R + rankSubjects.R) before writing NEWS, so the bullet is precise – it describes BOTH user-facing effects: (a) the new parentage column (known/one unknown parent/both unknown, U-id aware) in the report and BOTH CSV exports, and (b) the displayed-rank demotion of no-origin both-unknown founders (marked value=="Undetermined", ranked last) with genuine imports still ranked. Re-rendered NEWS.md (permanent html_preview:false+md_extensions:"-smart", Learning 155) -> 0 non-ASCII, no stray .html, a confined pure-insertion diff (NEWS.Rmd +11 / NEWS.md +12, nothing reflowed, no date churn). spell_check_package(".") = 0 unrecognized before AND after -> no WORDLIST change. Committed NEWS only as 3b1c7d9f; kept the S181 1B stub OUT of the PR (stash-carried, popped clean post-merge). - Push master first (S180 Gotcha 2 – WORKED as predicted): local master was 1 ahead of origin/master (S179 close-out docs 25d0d191, unpushed) and the branch was cut from it -> FF-verified (git merge-base --is-ancestor 800fb98c 25d0d191) then pushed master first (800fb98c->25d0d191) THEN PRed the branch; the PR diff came out Slice-3-only = 3 commits (S180 feat 3be9f818 + S180 close-out 29854331 + S181 NEWS 3b1c7d9f), no S176/S177/S179 bundling (verified the commit list + changed-file list firsthand). - Issue-close decision (owner AskUserQuestion): Reading B resolves #9’s DISPLAYED-ranking complaint, so the PR CAN close #9. Surfaced close-vs-keep-open with the reasoning; owner chose Closes #9. The deferred Reading A (genuine gu de-inflation) was filed SEPARATELY (#76) so #9 does not stay open as a vague catch-all. - PR + CI (did NOT merge blind): opened PR #75 -> master (body Closes #9). All 10 checks PASS – lint 3m47s; R CMD check x5 (macos 4m51s / ubuntu release 7m40s + oldrel-1 7m11s + devel 16m35s the long pole / windows 9m16s); pkgdown 6m14s; test-coverage 4m28s; codecov/patch + codecov/project PASS. Background watch exited 0, but per Learning 157 re-queried FRESH non-watch gh pr checks 75 (10 pass, exit 0) before proceeding. - Merge (irreversible – AskUserQuestion-gated): guarded fresh pre-merge re-check (state OPEN / MERGEABLE / CLEAN, headRefOid==local 3b1c7d9f, origin/master==25d0d191) -> gh pr merge 75 --merge -> 5ada4b71; verified landed firsthand (MERGED, mergedAt 2026-06-23T13:52:05Z, mergedBy rmsharp); #9 re-verified CLOSED after the merge (closedAt 2026-06-23T13:52:06Z, auto-closed by Closes #9). - Reconcile + cleanup (Learning 146): stash-carried the stub; checkout master; fetch; ancestor-gated reset (both old 25d0d191 AND merged tip 3b1c7d9f asserted ancestors of 5ada4b71) -> reset --hard origin/master; stash pop clean. Branch deleted verified-merged-before-delete: git branch -d (was 3b1c7d9f) + push origin --delete + fetch --prune -> NO ref remains (local 0 / remote-tracking 0 / gh api 404). - Filed Reading A follow-up #76 (“GVA: de-inflate the genome-uniqueness statistic for both-unknown founders – Reading A, deferred from #9 Slice 3”, enhancement): ratification-first acceptance criteria, references §8-A/§8-F + calcGU.R:10-34, links the shipped display-side fix.

Phase-3E (build-equivalent / runtime smoke): SATISFIED on the merge-result tree + firsthand on master. PR #75’s R CMD check x5 matrix (incl. ubuntu-devel) re-verified the deliverable on the merge result, all PASS. Confirmed firsthand on master AFTER the reset: R/classifyParentage.R present; R/reportGV.R/R/orderReport.R/R/modGeneticValue.R carry the Slice 3 logic; NEWS.md has the Slice 3 bullet; the 4 targeted test files pass locally on the merged master – and critically test_modGeneticValue.R re-run with NOT_CRAN=true is 148 pass / 0 fail / 0 skip / 0 error with the “demotes both-unknown founders to the bottom (#9 Slice 3)” testServer test ACTUALLY EXECUTING (it shows as a silent “S” skip under a default local run – caught and re-ran rather than accepting the skip). No browser DT-render smoke (consistent with the §8-A/Slice 2 precedent + baseline e2e noise) -> stated, not silently skipped.

Session 180 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 181): Score 10/10. S180’s => SUGGESTED NEXT listed “Publish Slice 3” as the FIRST option with the EXACT end-to-end procedure – push master FIRST (FF origin/master 800fb98c to 25d0d191, local 1-ahead, Gotcha 2), fold NEWS into the SAME PR (157a) with the user-facing text (“flags each animal’s parentage (a new column) and no longer top-ranks animals whose parents are both unknown”), render flags (155), spell-check before/after (159), watch 5-platform CI, do NOT merge blind via fresh non-watch (157), AskUserQuestion-gate the merge, verified-merged-before-delete (146). Every load-bearing fact held FIRSTHAND: origin/master 800fb98c, local master 1-ahead at 25d0d191, #9 OPEN, the untracked standing keep. Gotcha 2 was exactly right – I pushed master first and the PR diff came out Slice-3-only (3 commits). It even pre-framed the one genuine decision (“DECIDE at publish whether the PR uses Closes #9”) with the full reasoning (Reading B resolves the displayed complaint so #9 CAN close; Reading A deferred -> keep open or file a follow-up), AND pre-listed the Reading A follow-up as its own option – so the close decision + the #76 filing were both anticipated. The Key files block listed all four changed R/ files with line numbers, so deriving the precise 2-part NEWS bullet was fast. This is what a 10 looks like: the path was fully paved, every claim verified true, the only open decision pre-framed with its reasoning, nothing to rediscover. The sole refinement (not a gap): S180’s 1-line NEWS sketch conflated the parentage column with the demotion; I read the code to split it into the two distinct user-facing effects (the column’s values vs the value=="Undetermined" demotion) – a precision improvement on authored content, not a handoff defect. ROI maximal.

Self-assessment (Session 181): 8/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER read in full; SESSION_NOTES ACTIVE TASK; GH issues; dashboard 98/100; ghost-check -> HEAD 29854331 = S180, no undocumented commits), reported, STOPPED for the owner’s pick; claimed the session with a 1B stub BEFORE technical work (stashed to keep it out of the PR). Strengths: (1) full safe-publish discipline, executed clean first try – NEWS in the SAME PR (157a) with 0 non-ASCII + 0 spell-delta + a confined +11/+12 pure insertion; FF-verified then pushed master first per Gotcha 2 and VERIFIED the resulting PR diff was Slice-3-only (3 commits, no bundling); did NOT merge blind (fresh non-watch re-query even though the watch exited 0, Learning 157); guarded AskUserQuestion-gated merge with a fresh pre-merge gate matching headRefOid to local and origin/master to expected; ancestor-gated reset --hard (both old-master AND merged-tip asserted ancestors first); stash-carried the S181 stub through the destructive reset and popped it clean; verified-merged-before-delete cleanup with a gh api 404; (2) surfaced the genuine Closes-vs-Relates owner decision via AskUserQuestion with the reasoning rather than silently picking, then HONORED the close by filing Reading A as a self-contained follow-up (#76) – backlog stays honest (Learning 169); (3) wrote a PRECISE NEWS bullet by reading the four changed R/ files firsthand (the two distinct user-facing effects), not paraphrasing S180’s 1-liner; (4) verified the deliverable on master firsthand rather than trusting the green merge – the new file, the three edited files, the NEWS bullet, and the 4 targeted test files pass on the merged tree; (5) caught a silent Phase-3E skip – the testServer demotion test reports “S” under a default run; re-ran with NOT_CRAN=true so it actually executed (148/0/0/0) instead of accepting the skip as a pass; (6) TDD N/A declared every response (confirmed docs/admin-only), plain language, ASCII, scope-disciplined (one deliverable, no creep into Reading A implementation). Weaknesses (honest): (a) low base difficulty – a textbook publish on a fully-paved 10/10 predecessor handoff; the value is the discipline + the one honest decision, not depth or recovery -> ceiling ~8; (b) CI was textbook-green first try – no recovery/judgment test to push the score higher; (c) the close-out docs were initially left UNPUSHED per the documented S173-S180 rhythm (I flagged the recurring 1-ahead drift as an owner observation rather than changing the convention unilaterally); the owner then directed pushing them, so origin/master == local master at 172252bf and the drift is resolved this cycle – the next session can branch directly from master. Clean, fully-verified, scope-disciplined publish with a firsthand on-master confirmation, one genuine decision surfaced + honored, a real new learning, and zero corrections -> 8/10.

Learnings: Learning 169 added to PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md – the close-with-explicit-follow-up backlog-hygiene pattern (a slice that resolves an issue’s surface complaint but defers a deeper sub-scope -> AskUserQuestion the close-vs-keep-open, then Closes #N AND file the deferred scope as a fresh self-contained issue; re-verify the issue’s CLOSED state after merge; re-run a skip_on_cran testServer Phase-3E test with NOT_CRAN=true so it isn’t a silent “S”). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]] (the publish convention + the irreversible-merge gate + implementation->CHANGELOG / publish->NEWS rhythm), [[observation-vs-decision]] / [[ascii-only-in-question-options]] / [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]] (the Closes-#9 + merge AskUserQuestions, plain-ASCII NEWS), [[backlog-vs-changelog-placement]] (completed work -> CHANGELOG; #9 now CLOSED, #76 is the OPEN follow-up); Learnings 133/135/146/152/155/157/157a/159/161 + the solo-for-irreversible-git-sequence judgment.

=> SUGGESTED NEXT = owner’s pick. origin/master == local master at 172252bf (the PR #75 merge 5ada4b71 – Slice 3 LIVE – plus the S181 close-out docs, which the owner directed pushing to origin -> no drift this cycle); issue #9 is CLOSED (all three slices published); #76 (Reading A) is the new OPEN follow-up; no dangling branches; dashboard 98/100. Natural options (plain ASCII labels): - (#76 – Reading A, deep genetics) the now-tracked follow-up: genuinely de-inflate the genome-uniqueness statistic for both-unknown founders inside calcGU/calcA/gene-drop. This is NOT a “just implement” – it reverses the documented calcGU.R:10-34 stance and breaks the golden invariants, so it needs a §8-E-style RATIFICATION first (an agreed formula + replacement calcGU/calcA golden values), and only then a separate strict-TDD implement. Expect a ratification session, then an implement session (two sessions minimum; do NOT bundle). - (#73 – species breeding-age generalization) logically follows Slice 2 seeding the minMaleBreedingAge/minFemaleBreedingAge table; generalize to more species + make user-configurable. - (Other options) #37 (unused exports); #36 (chimpanzee age-pyramid); #2 (GVA iteration-count advice); #28 (large, own plan); older #13/#12/#11/#10/#5/#1; CRAN Phase 5 (owner-run, docs/planning/cran-2.0.0-phase5-runbook.md). Do NOT start #76 (Reading A) implementation without ratifying the gu formula + replacement golden values FIRST (FM #18/#19/#25); do NOT bundle the #76 ratification + implement.

Key files (this session): CHANGED – the publish content (NEWS commit 3b1c7d9f, now on master via PR #75): NEWS.Rmd (+11, dev-version Changes bullet), NEWS.md (+12, re-rendered). CHANGED – close-out docs (this S181 docs commit, direct to local master, UNPUSHED): CHANGELOG.md (S181 [Unreleased] publish entry), PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 169), SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). NO R//test/man//NAMESPACE/data/ change (the Slice 3 code was S180’s). Published deliverable (S180’s, now on origin/master 5ada4b71): R/classifyParentage.R, R/reportGV.R, R/orderReport.R, R/modGeneticValue.R, the 4 S180 test files. The plan (context): docs/planning/issue9-gva-unknown-parent-ranking-plan.md §8-A/§8-F. NEW issue: #76 (Reading A follow-up). NOT committed (standing keeps): PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked); .DS_Store.

Gotchas: (1) Issue #9 is CLOSED – all three slices published; the displayed GVA top no longer shows both-unknown founders. Reading A (genuine gu de-inflation) is tracked in the NEW open issue #76, NOT in #9 – it needs a §8-E-style ratification (formula + replacement calcGU/calcA golden values) BEFORE any implement. (2) origin/master and local master are IN SYNC at 172252bf – the owner directed pushing the S181 close-out docs to origin, ending the recurring S173-S180 “local 1-ahead” drift. So the next session can branch directly from master with NO FF-first step. (If a future session again leaves close-out docs local-only, the routine “push master first” Gotcha-2 returns – but it does not apply now.) (3) The Slice 3 deliverable is LIVE on origin/master 5ada4b71 – the parentage column + the classification-aware displayed rank; no further publish action for #9. (4) The import-vs-stub (origin) distinction is exercised ONLY by the synthetic orderReport fixture – no shipped dataset (qcPed/breederPed/examplePedigree) carries an origin column, so on real data ALL both-unknown founders are treated as ONPRC-born and demoted (carried from S180). (5) A skip_on_cran()-gated testServer test shows as a silent “S” skip under a default local test_file run – set NOT_CRAN=true to actually execute it (e.g. the test_modGeneticValue.R demotion Phase-3E test). (6) Carried standing keeps (unchanged from S180): package ARCHIVED on CRAN 2025-07-29; CRAN Phase 5 owner-gated (docs/planning/cran-2.0.0-phase5-runbook.md); getLkDirectRelatives()/getDemographics() FAIL SOFT (warning + NULL) without a LabKey credential/config; exactly ONE codecov config (codecov.yml); NEWS render traps CLOSED at the source (permanent html_preview:false+md_extensions:"-smart", Learning 155); git pull is rebase (pull.rebase=true) + chokes on .DS_Store -> use fetch+reset (135); post-merge fetch before reset --hard must be ancestor-gated (146); the build-equivalent is devtools::check(vignettes = FALSE) = 0/0/0 (Learning 161).

What Session 180 Did

Deliverable: Implement issue #9 Slice 3 (§8-F charter: classify unknown-parent animals + make the Shiny-displayed Genetic Value rank classification-aware so both-unknown founders no longer falsely top-rank). Strict TDD. (DONE – committed on branch issue-9-slice3-classify-rank-aware; UNPUBLISHED; master unchanged.) Started / Completed: 2026-06-22 / 2026-06-23 Status: DONE. New internal classifyParentage + a parentage report column + a U-id-aware, origin-robust orderReport + a classification-aware path-B rank -> strict TDD, every transition AskUserQuestion-gated (PRE-RED scope: Reading A-vs-B -> demote-all-vs-flag -> PRE-RED->RED -> RED->GREEN -> GREEN->REFACTOR). 0 stakeholder corrections. Grounding fanned out via a read-only 5-agent workflow + 3 targeted real-data probes; design synthesis + owner gates were main-loop (ultracode honored for the grounding; the decisions are inherently solo). Owner directed “Slice 3”, then chose Reading B (classify + rank-aware, gu untouched) and demote-all-keep-imports at the two pre-RED scope gates, and approved every TDD transition. - Grounding surfaced that §8-F is a CHARTER, not a spec. Unlike Slice 2’s authoritative §8-E formula, §8-F bundled four concerns and the load-bearing one (“address the gu inflation”) had NO algorithm. Split into Reading B (rank AROUND gu – classification-aware display, gu untouched, all calcGU/calcA invariants preserved, faithful to the ratified D7: implementable now) vs Reading A (recompute founder gu in calcGU/calcA/gene-drop – reverses the documented calcGU.R:10-34 stance, breaks golden invariants, needs a §8-E-style ratification first). Surfaced via AskUserQuestion; owner picked Reading B. - Second decision, surfaced by real data: the plan called the both-unknown founders “stubs,” but on qcPed ALL 124 have offspring (they are the colony’s real founding breeders; verified readRDS(gv5000.rds) + simulated both gate variants). The existing noParentage bucket requires totalOffspring==0 -> keeping the gate flags zero of them (no-op); dropping it demotes all 124 (44% of the colony). Genuine genetics-policy fork -> AskUserQuestion; owner picked demote-all, keep imports (drop the offspring gate; imports with a recorded origin stay ranked). - Dissolved a potential cross-cutting bug firsthand: isGeneratedUnknownId = startsWith(id,"U") over-matches, but a 20-line probe proved qcPed’s 43 “U…” parents are genuine unknown stubs (both parents NA, NO birth, gen 0; examplePedigree has 1372 “U” ids -> “U” IS the ONPRC unknown convention). So the predicate is right, Slice 2 is sound, counts hold (124 / 43 / 113). - RED (gated): 4 specs failing for the right reason – test_classifyParentage.R [new] (U-id-aware known/one/both, vectorized, qcPed 124/43/113); test_orderReport.R rewritten (D8: brittle 34/21 golden counts -> named-animal behavior assertions on a deterministic origin-bearing fixture – STUB1 no-origin both-unknown founder WITH offspring -> Undetermined/rank NA, IMPORT1 origin-present -> kept); test_reportGV.R (the report carries parentage; the 124 both-unknown -> “Undetermined”); test_modGeneticValue.R (testServer: both-unknown founders ranked LAST, parentage column present). Confirmed only these 4 fail (3 errors + behavior failures), no collateral. - GREEN (gated, minimum): new R/classifyParentage.R (out <- rep("known",n); out[anyU]<-...; out[bothU]<-... – not ifelse, which returns logical(0) on empty input); reportGV cbinds parentage into finalData; orderReport treats absent origin as all-NA, keys on rpt$parentage (fallback getFounders), DROPS the totalOffspring==0 gate, fixes the stale @param (import->origin+parentage); modGeneticValue.R:204-206 ranks value=="Undetermined" LAST (order(demote, indivMeanKin - gu) then reseq, guarded for an absent value column). document() -> no man/NAMESPACE change (all internal). All 4 targeted files green; full clean regression 0 failed / 0 error (209 files / 1192 tests). - REFACTOR (gated): lint only – fixed 4 line-length(80) lints in the comments/roxygen I added (namespace-loaded lint, Learning 167); 0 lints across all 4 changed files; tests still green.

Phase-3E (build-equivalent + runtime smoke): SATISFIED. Build-equivalent devtools::check(vignettes = FALSE) = 0/0/0; spell_check_package(".") = 0 unrecognized (no WORDLIST change). Runtime: the real modGeneticValueServer on shipped qcPed (testServer) -> the displayed top-20 is now 18 known + 2 one-unknown, ZERO both-unknown; all 45 living both-unknown founders demoted to ranks 45-89 (“Undetermined”); the parentage column is present; shinyApp(appUI(), appServer) builds. The DT table auto-renders the new column (no column spec). A browser-driven DT render smoke is not run in this environment (the test-e2e-/shinytest2 harness is baseline noise; the server reactive IS the logic and is exercised on real data) -> stated, not silently skipped.

Session 179 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 180): Score 9/10. S179’s => SUGGESTED NEXT listed “Slice 3 / S2 – the substantive remaining work that closes #9” as the FIRST option with the exact four concerns (classify + gu + origin + D7 + D8), the “Read plan §8-F” pointer, the “needs a fixture WITH origin + a runModularApp() Phase-3E smoke” note, and two load-bearing warnings I followed exactly: “do NOT bundle Slice 3 implement + publish” and “do NOT start Slice 3’s RED without re-reading plan §8-F (it expands the original Slice 3 scope).” Every publish-state fact held firsthand (origin/master 800fb98c, local master 1-ahead, #9 OPEN, the standing keeps). Gotcha 4 correctly flagged that Slice 3 is bigger than the plan’s original “classify.” The -1: neither S179 nor the §8-F it pointed to flagged that §8-F is a CHARTER with no gu algorithm, nor that the both-unknown founders all have offspring (making the noParentage gate a no-op) – both were genuine discoveries of THIS session’s grounding, surfaced as the two owner decisions. A one-line “§8-F is a charter not a spec – expect to surface the gu-algorithm + the classification treatment as owner decisions” would have pre-framed it. But those lived in the data + §8-F (which S179 correctly told me to read), and S179 was a publish session, so this is a minor pre-framing gap, not a handoff defect. ROI high – it paved the publish-state facts perfectly and pointed me at the right deliverable with the right guardrails.

Self-assessment (Session 180): 9/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER read in full; SESSION_NOTES ACTIVE TASK; GH issues; dashboard 98/100; ghost-check -> HEAD 25d0d191 = S179, no undocumented commits), reported, STOPPED for the owner’s pick; claimed with a 1B stub BEFORE technical work; declared the TDD phase every response; branched off master before code. Strengths: (1) grounded the whole design firsthand before RED – a read-only 5-agent workflow mapped both rank paths + the gu mechanism + the predicates + real qcPed facts, then 3 targeted probes verified the load-bearing claims, surfacing the charter-vs-spec gap, the gu Reading A/B fork, the all-founders-have-offspring no-op trap, and the U-prefix false alarm; (2) surfaced the TWO genuine owner decisions (Reading B; demote-all) via AskUserQuestion rather than silently picking either – both are genetics/scope calls that materially change the deliverable ([[observation-vs-decision]], FM #23); (3) dissolved a would-be cross-cutting bug with a firsthand data probe instead of assuming isGeneratedUnknownId was broken (it’s correct; Slice 2 is sound); (4) strict TDD, every transition gated, RED failing for the right reasons (4 specs, no collateral), GREEN minimum (single source of truth: classify once -> mark via value==“Undetermined” -> demote in path B), REFACTOR lint-only; (5) full verification incl. real-data Phase-3E through the actual module server (not just a synthetic fixture) + build-equivalent 0/0/0 + 0 lints + 0 spelling; (6) scope-disciplined (implement only; no publish; Reading A deferred; no bundling), plain language, ASCII. Weaknesses (honest): (a) Reading A (genuine gu de-inflation) is DEFERRED – Slice 3 ranks AROUND gu rather than correcting the statistic; §8-A’s “two causes” are addressed in the DISPLAY, not in the gu number; if the owner wants true de-inflation it needs its own ratify+implement (owner-approved, documented, but worth naming); (b) the import-vs-stub (origin) branch is exercised only by the synthetic orderReport fixture – no shipped dataset carries an origin column, so “keep imports” has unit coverage but no real-data exercise (inherent to the data); (c) no browser-driven DT render smoke – the testServer + shinyApp-builds is strong runtime evidence but the rendered table in a real browser is not demonstrated (consistent with the Slice 2 precedent + baseline e2e noise). High base difficulty (a charter-level slice, two unanticipated owner decisions, a dissolved false-bug, two rank paths, real-data Phase-3E), zero corrections, fully verified -> 9/10.

Learnings: Learning 168 added to PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md – the five Slice-3 reflexes: (1) a charter (“address X”, no formula) carries unratified decisions -> enumerate implementable-now vs needs-ratification and let the owner choose; (2) simulate the proposed fix on REAL data before RED (a charter assumption can make it a no-op or a 44%-of-colony change); (3) the U-prefix predicate over-matches but is correct on qcPed – verify the data, don’t assume a bug; (4) the classify-once -> mark-via-value==“Undetermined” -> demote-in-path-B architecture (one source of truth across both rank paths; classifyParentage built with rep+assignment not ifelse); (5) Phase-3E without a browser via testServer on the real shipped data. Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]] (the TDD-gate contract + branch-for-code + implementation->CHANGELOG / publish->NEWS rhythm + §8-F as authoritative-but-charter), [[observation-vs-decision]] / [[ascii-only-in-question-options]] / [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]] (the two scope + three phase-gate AskUserQuestions), [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]] (read §8 + verified its claims firsthand), [[backlog-vs-changelog-placement]] (completed work -> CHANGELOG; #9 stays OPEN in GitHub Issues until publish); Learnings 161 (build-equivalent), 164 (the two rank paths), 165 (the column-agnostic GVA chain -> parentage auto-propagates), 166 (gu-dominance), 167 (Slice 2 reflexes + namespace-loaded lint).

=> SUGGESTED NEXT = owner’s pick. The deliverable is committed on issue-9-slice3-classify-rank-aware (feat + this docs commit); master unchanged. Issue #9 remains OPEN until this slice publishes. Natural options (plain ASCII labels): - (Publish Slice 3) the standard publish session: push master FIRST (FF origin/master 800fb98c to the S179 close-out commit – local master is 1 ahead, Gotcha 2) THEN push the branch and open a PR -> master; watch the 5-platform CI to completion (do NOT merge blind – fresh non-watch re-query, Learning 157); AskUserQuestion-gate the merge; verified-merged-before-delete cleanup (Learning 146). FOLD a NEWS entry into the SAME PR (Learning 157a) – user-facing: the GVA now flags each animal’s parentage (a new column) and no longer top-ranks animals whose parents are both unknown; keep it plain ASCII, re-render NEWS.md (html_preview:false+md_extensions:"-smart", 155), spell_check_package(".") before/after (159). DECIDE at publish whether the PR uses Closes #9: Reading B resolves #9’s DISPLAYED complaint (both-unknown founders no longer top-rank), so #9 CAN close on this slice – BUT Reading A (genuine gu de-inflation in calcGU/calcA) was deferred; if the owner wants that too, keep #9 open (or file Reading A as a follow-up issue) and use “Relates to #9”. - (Reading A follow-up, optional) if the owner wants the gu statistic itself de-inflated (not just ranked around), that is a NEW deep-genetics decision needing a §8-E-style ratification (formula + replacement calcGU/calcA golden values) FIRST, then a separate implement – file it as an issue. - (Other options) #73 (species breeding-age generalization); #37 (unused exports); #36 (chimpanzee age-pyramid); #2 (GVA iteration-count advice); #28 (large, own plan); older #13/#12/#11/#10/#5/#1; CRAN Phase 5 (owner-run runbook). Do NOT bundle Slice 3 publish with a Reading-A gu change (FM #18/#25).

Key files (this session): CHANGED – the deliverable (on the branch): NEW R/classifyParentage.R; R/reportGV.R (cbind parentage ~:161); R/orderReport.R (origin-robust + parentage + dropped offspring gate + @param fix); R/modGeneticValue.R (:204-216 classification-aware rank); NEW tests/testthat/test_classifyParentage.R; tests/testthat/test_orderReport.R (rewritten, D8 behavior assertions); tests/testthat/test_reportGV.R (+parentage block, +2 expect_named); tests/testthat/test_modGeneticValue.R (+D7 testServer test). CHANGED – close-out docs (this docs commit, same branch): CHANGELOG.md (S180 [Unreleased] entry), PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 168), SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). NO NEWS change (folded into the publish PR, 157a). NO man//NAMESPACE/data/ change (all new functions are internal @noRd). The charter (input): docs/planning/issue9-gva-unknown-parent-ranking-plan.md §8-F. Grounding workflow: wf_d9e4d6d6-e12 (5 read-only agents – script under the session dir, NOT in the repo); scratchpad probes verify_classify.R/verify_uid.R/verify_uanimals.R/verify_demote.R/smoke.R + gv5000.rds/gv1000.rds (NOT in the repo). NOT committed (standing keeps): PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked); .DS_Store.

Gotchas: (1) Issue #9: Slice 3 implemented (UNPUBLISHED), addresses the DISPLAYED ranking (Reading B). Reading A (recompute gu in calcGU/calcA) was DEFERRED – if genuine gu de-inflation is wanted it is a separate ratify+implement (a §8-E-style gate). Decide at publish whether #9 closes on this slice (the ranking fix) or stays open for Reading A. (2) Local master is 1 commit AHEAD of origin/master (S179 close-out docs, unpushed) AND this branch was cut from local master -> when publishing, push master first (FF origin/master 800fb98c to the S179 commit) so the PR diff is Slice-3-only (routine Gotcha-2). This S180 close-out commit lands on the BRANCH, not master. (3) The import-vs-stub (origin) distinction is exercised ONLY by the synthetic orderReport fixture – no shipped dataset (qcPed/breederPed/examplePedigree) carries an origin column, so on real data ALL both-unknown founders are treated as ONPRC-born and demoted. (4) The parentage column now appears in the GVA report -> the displayed table + BOTH CSV exports (auto, Learning 165); the NEWS entry should mention it. (5) The displayed-rank demotion keys on value == "Undetermined" (orderReport’s noParentage marker); orderReport now fires noParentage even WITHOUT an origin column (treats absent origin as all-NA) and WITHOUT the totalOffspring==0 gate. (6) test_orderReport’s old 34/21 golden counts are GONE (replaced by named-animal behavior assertions on an origin-bearing fixture) – do not expect them. (7) Carried standing keeps (unchanged from S179): package ARCHIVED on CRAN 2025-07-29; CRAN Phase 5 owner-gated (docs/planning/cran-2.0.0-phase5-runbook.md); getLkDirectRelatives()/getDemographics() FAIL SOFT (warning + NULL) without a LabKey credential/config; exactly ONE codecov config (codecov.yml); NEWS render traps CLOSED at the source (permanent html_preview:false+md_extensions:"-smart", Learning 155); git pull is rebase (pull.rebase=true) + chokes on .DS_Store -> use fetch+reset (135); post-merge fetch before reset --hard must be ancestor-gated (146); skip_on_cran()-gated test files need NOT_CRAN=true; the build-equivalent is devtools::check(vignettes = FALSE) = 0/0/0 (Learning 161).

The Slice 2 mean-kinship correction + its NEWS entry are LIVE on origin/master (800fb98c). Admin/publish + docs -> TDD declared N/A every response (the Slice 2 code was written/tested under strict TDD in S178; the only content change this session is one NEWS bullet). 0 stakeholder corrections. SOLO (a serial, irreversible git sequence – a workflow adds risk, not coverage; held solo despite ultracode, the S176 judgment). Owner picked option 1 (publish) at orientation, then “Merge (merge commit)” at the AskUserQuestion merge gate. - Pre-publish content (one PR, Learning 157a): added a dev-version Changes bullet to NEWS.Rmd – the GVA now corrects the mean kinship of animals missing one parent (adds an estimate of the missing parent’s contribution from a contemporaneous breeding-age peer cohort of the same sex), so they no longer falsely rank low on mean kinship; both-known and both-unknown animals unchanged. Re-rendered NEWS.md (permanent html_preview:false+md_extensions:"-smart", Learning 155) -> 0 non-ASCII, no stray .html, a confined pure-insertion diff (NEWS.Rmd +9 / NEWS.md +10, nothing reflowed, no date churn). spell_check_package(".") = 0 unrecognized before AND after -> no WORDLIST change. Committed NEWS only as d683bbfc; kept the S179 1B stub OUT of the PR (stash-carried, popped clean post-merge). - Push master first (S178 Gotcha 3 – WORKED exactly as predicted): master was 2 ahead of origin/master (S176 488f2b09 + S177 2bee0f03, unpushed) and the branch was cut from that master -> pushed master first (FF origin/master 0d559d3b -> 2bee0f03) THEN PRed the branch; the PR diff came out = 3 commits (S178 feat af4b7f69 + S178 close-out 19ac25ed + S179 NEWS d683bbfc), no S176/S177 bundling. - PR + CI (did NOT merge blind): opened PR #74 -> master (body “Relates to #9”, NOT Closes #9). All 10 checks PASS – lint 5m5s; R CMD check x5 (macos 5m2s / ubuntu release 8m12s + oldrel-1 7m1s + devel 16m42s the long pole / windows 9m21s); pkgdown 5m36s; test-coverage 4m8s; codecov/patch + codecov/project PASS. Background watch exited 0, but per Learning 157 re-queried FRESH non-watch gh pr checks 74 (10 pass, exit 0) before proceeding. - Merge (irreversible – AskUserQuestion-gated): guarded fresh pre-merge re-check (state OPEN / MERGEABLE / CLEAN, headRefOid==local d683bbfc, origin/master==2bee0f03) -> gh pr merge 74 --merge -> 800fb98c; verified landed firsthand (MERGED, mergedAt 2026-06-23T04:37:15Z, mergedBy rmsharp); #9 re-verified OPEN after the merge. - Reconcile + cleanup (Learning 146): stash-carried the stub; checkout master; fetch; ancestor-gated reset (both 2bee0f03 AND merged tip d683bbfc asserted ancestors of 800fb98c) -> reset --hard origin/master; stash pop clean. Branch deleted verified-merged-before-delete: git branch -d (was d683bbfc) + push origin --delete + fetch --prune -> NO ref remains (local 0 / remote-tracking 0 / gh api 404).

Self-assessment (Session 179): 8/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER read in full; SESSION_NOTES ACTIVE TASK; GH issues; dashboard 98/100; ghost-check -> HEAD 19ac25ed = S178, no undocumented commits), reported, STOPPED for the owner’s pick; claimed the session with a 1B stub BEFORE technical work. Strengths: (1) full safe-publish discipline, executed clean first try – NEWS in the SAME PR (157a) with 0 non-ASCII + 0 spell-delta + a confined +10 insertion; pushed master first per Gotcha 3 and VERIFIED the resulting PR diff was S178-only (3 commits, no S176/S177 bundling); PR body “Relates to #9” not Closes #9 and re-verified #9 OPEN after the merge; did NOT merge blind (fresh non-watch re-query even though the watch exited 0, Learning 157); guarded AskUserQuestion-gated merge with a fresh pre-merge gate matching headRefOid to local and origin/master to expected; ancestor-gated reset --hard (both old-master AND merged-tip asserted ancestors first); stash-carried the S179 stub through the destructive reset and popped it clean; verified-merged-before-delete cleanup with a gh api 404; (2) verified the deliverable on master firsthand rather than trusting the green merge – both new R/ files, the reportGV.R:103 injection, NEWS.md:55, and a local test_reportGV.R pass on the merged tree; (3) confirmed S178’s load-bearing prediction – Gotcha 3’s master-first push kept the PR diff clean; (4) TDD N/A declared every response (confirmed docs/admin-only), plain language, ASCII, scope-disciplined (one deliverable, no creep into Slice 3). Weaknesses (honest): (a) low base difficulty – a textbook publish on a fully-paved convention with a flawless (10/10) predecessor handoff; the value is the discipline, not depth or recovery -> ceiling ~8; (b) no new numbered learning emerged – every step reused a documented Learning (133/135/146/152/155/157/157a/159/161); manufacturing one would be noise (the honest S172/S176 outcome); (c) CI was textbook-green first try – no recovery/judgment test to push the score higher; (d) I left the S179 close-out docs UNPUSHED on local master (the documented S173-S178 rhythm) rather than pushing – this re-creates a 1-commit-ahead drift the next publish must FF (Gotcha 2 below); a defensible convention call, not a defect, but flagged. Clean, fully-verified, scope-disciplined publish with a firsthand on-master confirmation and zero corrections -> 8/10.

Code change (2 new R/ files + reportGV.R injection + speciesGestation table extension + 3 test files) -> strict TDD, every transition gated via AskUserQuestion (pre-RED scope/approach [the §8-C fallback ladder] -> PRE-RED->RED -> RED->GREEN -> GREEN->REFACTOR). 0 stakeholder corrections. SOLO under ultracode (the design was already ratified + adversarially reviewed in S177; this is a contained, fully test-anchored implementation – a multi-agent sweep adds no coverage; the S175 contained-work judgment). Owner directed “implement Slice 2 of #9”, then chose “Tier 1 + tier 3, defer tier 2” at the pre-RED fallback gate (my recommendation), and approved each TDD phase transition. - PRE-RED (firsthand grounding before any test): read §8 (authoritative) + the full surface (reportGV.R, meanKinship.R, getSpeciesGestation.R, getPotentialParents.R, getIdsWithOneParent.R, autoIdFormat.R, data.R, data-raw/speciesGestation.R, test_getSpeciesGestation.R, test_reportGV.R, getGVPopulation.R, the [0,1] invariant + test_orderReport). Verified S177’s claims FIRSTHAND: qcPed has NO species column (-> cutoff 2, rhesus 4/3 never exercised), 43 one-unknown all missing SIRE / 124 both-unknown / 113 known, the U-id targeting trap (raw is.na finds 0 one-unknown; the U-id-aware predicate finds 43), trimPedigree (server path) PRESERVES birth/sex/exit (R3 mitigated), and – with reportGV(qcPed) defaulting population to ALL 280 – the strict male cohort is never empty (sizes 17-26, 0 empty), so the fallback never fires (confirms §8-A). Sample: focal K0ACWS mk0=0.004911 -> corrected 0.009698 (+sexMean/2). - Owner decision (pre-RED scope gate): §8-C ratified a 3-tier fallback ladder; tier 2 (“nearest-earlier same-era cohort”) is UNDER-SPECIFIED (no era mechanism exists in the package) and NEVER fires on qcPed. Surfaced this as an AskUserQuestion rather than silently dropping a ratified element or over-engineering it -> owner chose tier 1 (strict contemporaneous) + tier 3 (flag + add 0, never NA, never a colony-wide mean); tier 2 DEFERRED + documented as a known limitation. - RED (gated): 3 units, all failing for the right reason. (a) test_getSpeciesMinBreedingAge.R – rhesus M=4/F=3; unknown/NA/empty species or non-M/F sex -> 2; case/whitespace-insensitive; vectorized; injected table + custom default; integer; + the shipped table carries the two new columns (4/3). (b) test_correctUnknownParentMeanKinship.R – cohort selector on a hand-built fixture (species-absent cutoff 2 = {M_old,M_mid,M_exited_ok}; rhesus cutoff 4 drops M_mid = {M_old,M_exited_ok}); orchestrator: one-unknown -> pmin(MK+sexMean/2,1) (sire-missing male cohort + dam-missing female cohort + U-id-string sire), clamp to 1, known + both-unknown unchanged, empty-cohort -> flagged-not-NA, no-birth -> flagged, names/order preserved. (c) test_reportGV.R integration on qcPed – recomputes the expected correction INDEPENDENTLY in base R from the uncorrected baseline (filterKinMatrix+meanKinship), asserting all 43 one-unknown probands == pmin(baseline+sexMean/2,1) and raised, known + both-unknown unchanged, [0,1]. RED showed 43/43 at baseline (0.00491 vs expected 0.00970), matching the firsthand hand-computation. - GREEN (gated, minimum impl): (1) data-raw/speciesGestation.R + regen data/speciesGestation.RData (added minMaleBreedingAge=4L/minFemaleBreedingAge=3L); (2) R/data.R \describe{} documents the two columns; (3) NEW R/getSpeciesMinBreedingAge.R (exported, mirrors getSpeciesGestation); (4) NEW R/correctUnknownParentMeanKinship.R (internal getBreedingPeerCohort() + correctUnknownParentMeanKinship()); (5) R/reportGV.R:94->95 injection (after indivMeanKin[probands], before scale()); (6) devtools::document() (regen man/getSpeciesMinBreedingAge.Rd + man/speciesGestation.Rd + NAMESPACE export); NHP added to inst/WORDLIST. kinship() untouched. All 3 targeted files green (29+20+19), clean regression read 0 failed / 0 error (real files), build-equivalent 0/0/0. - REFACTOR (gated): lint only (no behavior change) – .lintr enforces line_length_linter(80) + implicit_integer_linter, and lint.yaml sets LINTR_ERROR_ON_LINT: true. Fixed 5 lints I introduced (2 long lines + sexMean*0.5/1.0 + 1 long \describe line); re-documented; lint clean under the namespace-loaded CI mode (the object_usage_linter cross-file warnings are confirmed false positives – a pre-existing one at fillGroupMembers.R:40 is green on master’s lint CI – that resolve once pkgload::load_all() populates the namespace); re-ran the 3 targeted files green + build-equivalent 0/0/0 a second time.

Phase-3E (build-equivalent / runtime smoke): SATISFIED at the integration + server-reactive level + build-equivalent; no browser smoke needed (§8-E). Build-equivalent devtools::check(vignettes = FALSE) = 0/0/0 (runs all examples + the full testthat suite + spelling). The runtime correction is exercised end-to-end by the test_reportGV.R integration test (the REAL reportGV on qcPed, all 43 one-unknown animals corrected) and by the modGeneticValueServer testServer tests (server -> reportGV path, the [0,1] invariant), all green in the suite. §8-E explicitly waives a runModularApp() browser smoke for Slice 2 because the visible app top-20 does NOT change (it is gu-dominated, §8-A) – stated, not silently skipped.

Session 177 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 178): Score 9/10. S177’s handoff was authoritative and directly implementable. The “Read plan §8 FIRST / §8-E is the AUTHORITATIVE spec, §3/§4/§6 are SUPERSEDED” pointer + the per-item banners meant I implemented the right design with zero risk of following stale specs. Every Gotcha held FIRSTHAND: qcPed has no species column (so the rhesus 4/3 is never exercised – a RED hand-computed with cutoff 4 would be WRONG; I used 2), all 43 one-unknown are missing-sire (so I added a missing-DAM fixture for the female branch), the U-id targeting trap (raw is.na -> 0), test_orderReport:24,42 NOT changed by Slice 2, and the +sexMean/2 self-consistency caveat (don’t assert 8e-18 against a kinship() rebuild). The “don’t bundle Slice 2 + Slice 3 / implement + publish” warning kept the scope clean. The -1: the ratified §8-C tier-2 fallback (“nearest-earlier same-era cohort”) is UNIMPLEMENTABLE AS WRITTEN – there is no era mechanism anywhere in the package, so “same era side” has no definition – and the handoff did not pre-flag that gap; I had to surface it as an owner decision. A one-line “tier-2 has no era mechanism yet – consider deferring at implementation” would have removed the friction. Also §8-E left the exact cohort contemporaneity-window form (present-at-conception via birth - gestation) and the probands-only cohort constraint to the implementer; reasonable, but a note would have helped. ROI maximal.

Self-assessment (Session 178): 9/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER read in full; SESSION_NOTES ACTIVE TASK; GH issues; dashboard 98/100; ghost-check -> HEAD 2bee0f03 = S177, no undocumented commits), reported, STOPPED for the owner’s task; claimed with a 1B stub BEFORE technical work; declared TDD phase every response; branched off master before committing code. Strengths: (1) grounded EVERYTHING firsthand before RED – the 43/124/113 split, the U-id trap (raw is.na=0), the absent species column, cohort sizes 17-26 (fallback never fires), trimPedigree column preservation – so the RED asserted real numbers and the design rested on code state, not the spec’s prose (FM #6/#11/#20); (2) surfaced the ONE genuine owner decision (the under-specified tier-2 fallback) via AskUserQuestion rather than silently dropping a ratified element OR shipping untested genetics ([[observation-vs-decision]], FM #23); (3) caught a subtle correctness point unprompted – the substitution must be computed from the UNCORRECTED snapshot (some cohort members are themselves one-unknown), else the result is iteration-order-dependent; pinned it with the independent base-R integration recompute; (4) strict TDD, every transition gated, RED failing for the right reason (43/43 at baseline matching my hand-computation), GREEN minimum, REFACTOR = lint only; (5) diagnosed the lint object_usage false-positive vs CI mode (namespace-loaded lint_package matches local::.), not a guess – proved by the pre-existing green fillGroupMembers.R:40; (6) full verification (3 files green, clean regression 0/0, build-equivalent 0/0/0 TWICE – after GREEN and after REFACTOR), plain language, ASCII, scope-disciplined (one slice; no Slice 3; no publish). Weaknesses (honest): (a) tier-2 fallback DEFERRED, not implemented – a deliberate owner-approved choice, but the ratified §8-C ladder is only partially realized (documented as a limitation); (b) the rhesus 4/3 breeding-age path is never exercised on real data (qcPed has no species column -> cutoff 2); it is covered only by a synthetic species-present fixture – inherent to the data, and #73 will generalize; (c) no browser/runtime smoke – Phase-3E rests on the integration + server-reactive tests + build-equivalent; acceptable since the visible top-20 does not change by design (§8-A), but visual app confirmation is not demonstrated; (d) UNPUBLISHED – the authoritative 5-platform CI gate is the publish session’s job. High base difficulty (the plan’s #1 here-be-dragons, the deepest genetics decision), a non-obvious correctness catch, fully verified, zero corrections -> 9/10.

Learnings: Learning 167 added to PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md – the five Slice-2 reflexes: (1) compute the substitution from the UNCORRECTED snapshot (order-independence; pinned by the integration test); (2) the peer cohort is necessarily a subset of the probands (a structural consequence of the scalar-level fix; reportGV(qcPed) defaults population to all 280 so all 43 one-unknown are probands and cohorts are non-empty); (3) inject at the reportGV.R:94 choke point to move both rank paths; (4) the data-object extension checklist (edit data-raw AND re-run it; R/data.R \describe; document(); adding columns keeps getSpeciesGestation+test green); (5) lint with the namespace LOADED to match CI’s local::. (the object_usage cross-file warnings are false positives; the real lints are line_length/implicit_integer). Carried as applied: [[consult-project-source-of-truth]] (the TDD-gate contract + branch-for-code + implementation->CHANGELOG / publish->NEWS rhythm), [[observation-vs-decision]] / [[ascii-only-in-question-options]] / [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]] (the fallback + phase-gate AskUserQuestions), [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]] (read §8 + verified its claims firsthand, Learning 163), [[backlog-vs-changelog-placement]] (completed work -> CHANGELOG; #9 stays OPEN in GitHub Issues); Learnings 161 (build-equivalent recipe), 164 (the choke point / two rank paths), 166 (the gu-dominance reframing + the absent-species-column trap + the +sexMean/2-is-a-modeling-choice caveat), 157a (NEWS in the publish PR).

=> SUGGESTED NEXT = owner’s pick. The deliverable is committed on issue-9-slice2-unknown-parent-mean-kinship (feat af4b7f69 + this docs commit); master unchanged at 2bee0f03 (still 2 ahead of origin/master 0d559d3b – S176+S177 close-out docs, unpushed). Issue #9 remains OPEN (Slice 3 remains; #9 does NOT close on Slice 2). Natural options (plain ASCII labels): - (Publish Slice 2 / S1) push issue-9-slice2-unknown-parent-mean-kinship, open a PR -> master, watch the 5-platform CI to completion (do NOT merge blind – fresh non-watch re-query, Learning 157), AskUserQuestion-gate the merge, then verified-merged-before-delete cleanup (Learning 146). FOLD a NEWS entry into the SAME PR (Learning 157a) – user-facing: the GVA now corrects the mean kinship of animals missing one parent (they no longer falsely top-rank on mean kinship); keep it plain ASCII, re-render NEWS.md (html_preview:false+md_extensions:"-smart", Learning 155), spell_check_package(".") before/after (verify the delta, Learning 159 – note NHP was added to WORDLIST this session). GOTCHA: master is 2 commits AHEAD of origin/master and the branch was cut from it, so push master first (FF origin/master to 2bee0f03) THEN PR the branch, or the PR diff bundles S176+S177 docs (harmless but noisy). Issue #9 stays OPEN after this (Slice 3 remains). - (Slice 3 / S2 – expanded: classify + gu axis + reconcile rank paths) the substantive remaining work that actually moves the visible top-20: classify unknown-parent animals (flag both-unknown founders; distinguish genuine imports via origin), address the gu inflation (D6), reconcile the two rank paths so the fix survives the Shiny rank(indivMeanKin - gu) override (D7), and update test_orderReport:24,42 golden counts -> behavior assertions (D8). Needs a fixture WITH an origin column (qcPed lacks it) + a runModularApp() Phase-3E smoke. #9 closes after Slice 3. Read plan §8-F. - (Other options) #73 (species breeding-age generalization – logically AFTER Slice 2 seeds the table); #37 (unused exports); #36 (chimpanzee age-pyramid); #2 (GVA iteration-count advice); #28 (large, own plan); older #13/#12/#11/#10/#5/#1; CRAN Phase 5 (owner-run). Do NOT bundle publish + Slice 3, or Slice 3 implement + publish (FM #18/#25).

Key files (this session): CHANGED – the deliverable (feat af4b7f69 on the branch): NEW R/getSpeciesMinBreedingAge.R (exported accessor), NEW R/correctUnknownParentMeanKinship.R (getBreedingPeerCohort + correctUnknownParentMeanKinship), R/reportGV.R (lines 92-103 injection), R/data.R (speciesGestation \describe), data-raw/speciesGestation.R + data/speciesGestation.RData (the two new columns), man/getSpeciesMinBreedingAge.Rd (new) + man/speciesGestation.Rd (regen), NAMESPACE (export), inst/WORDLIST (NHP), NEW tests/testthat/test_getSpeciesMinBreedingAge.R, NEW tests/testthat/test_correctUnknownParentMeanKinship.R, tests/testthat/test_reportGV.R (integration block appended). CHANGED – close-out docs (this docs commit, same branch): CHANGELOG.md (S178 [Unreleased] entry), PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 167), SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). NO NEWS change (folded into the publish PR, 157a). The authoritative spec (input): docs/planning/issue9-gva-unknown-parent-ranking-plan.md §8. NOT committed (standing keeps): PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked); .DS_Store.

Gotchas: (1) Issue #9 is OPEN – Slice 2 of remaining 2 done; Slice 3 (expanded: classify + gu + origin + D7) remains. #9 does NOT close on Slice 2 (the displayed top-20 is gu-dominated and unchanged by Slice 2 – §8-A; this is EXPECTED, not a failure). (2) The S178 work is UNPUBLISHED on issue-9-slice2-unknown-parent-mean-kinship – working tree is on that branch; the 5-platform CI gate is the publish session’s job. (3) master is 2 commits AHEAD of origin/master (S176+S177 close-out docs, unpushed) and the S178 branch was cut from it -> push master first before PRing the branch to keep the PR diff S178-only (the routine Gotcha pattern). (4) NEWS entry NOT yet written – fold it into the publish PR (157a); NHP was added to inst/WORDLIST this session. (5) On qcPed the seeded rhesus 4/3 breeding ages are NEVER exercised (no species column -> cutoff 2); the species-present path is covered only by a synthetic fixture in test_correctUnknownParentMeanKinship.R – a real-data exercise needs a species-bearing pedigree (and #73 generalizes the table). (6) Tier-2 fallback (“nearest-earlier same-era cohort”) is DEFERRED (owner decision S178) – the impl is tier-1 strict cohort -> tier-3 flag+add-0; it never fires on qcPed; “same era side” has no mechanism in the package yet. (7) The correction computes sexMean from the UNCORRECTED snapshot (original <- indivMeanKin in correctUnknownParentMeanKinship) – do NOT “optimize” to an in-place incremental update; some cohort members are themselves one-unknown and the result would become iteration-order-dependent. (8) Lint: verify with the namespace LOADED (pkgload::load_all(".") THEN lintr::lint_package()) to match CI’s local::.; bare source-only lint over-reports object_usage_linter for cross-file package functions (false positives). (9) Carried standing keeps (unchanged from S177): package ARCHIVED on CRAN 2025-07-29; CRAN Phase 5 owner-gated (docs/planning/cran-2.0.0-phase5-runbook.md); getLkDirectRelatives()/getDemographics() FAIL SOFT (warning + NULL) without a LabKey credential/config; exactly ONE codecov config (codecov.yml) – do NOT re-add a second; NEWS render traps CLOSED at the source (permanent html_preview:false+md_extensions:"-smart", Learning 155); git pull is rebase (pull.rebase=true) + chokes on .DS_Store -> use fetch+reset (135); post-merge fetch before reset --hard must be ancestor-gated (146); the shipped deidentified_jmac_ped.csv halts a full qcStudbook() run on a pre-existing “both sire and dam” conflict (test the layer you change, Learning 156); skip_on_cran()-gated test files need NOT_CRAN=true; the build-equivalent is devtools::check(vignettes = FALSE) = 0/0/0 (Learning 161).

What Session 177 Did

Deliverable: Ratify §7 of the issue #9 plan (docs/planning/issue9-gva-unknown-parent-ranking-plan.md) before implementing Slice 2 – resolve D1/D3/D4/D6/D7/D8 + slice order for owner sign-off, run a /grill-me on D2 (the substitution algebra), and record the ratified decisions into the plan. (DONE – plan §8 “Ratification Record” written + §7 boxes checked + per-item SUPERSEDED banners; follow-up issue #73 filed; NO production code changed.) Started / Completed: 2026-06-22 Status: DONE. Design/ratification deliverable -> TDD code-phases N/A every response (the plan doc states RED/GREEN/REFACTOR are inapplicable to a plan; nothing under R//tests//man//NAMESPACE/data/ changed). 0 stakeholder corrections (the owner OVERRODE two of my data-driven recommendations on genetics grounds during the grill – design refinement via the grill, exactly its purpose, not fixes of my errors). Did NOT implement Slice 2 (FM #18/#25 – a separate next session). Owner picked “Ratify §7 incl. D2 grill” at the scope AskUserQuestion. Used TWO workflows (a pre-compute to ground the grill + an adversarial self-review of the ratification record); the grill itself was the interactive core. - Pre-compute workflow (wf_7f819b92-a12, real qcPed): produced the load-bearing finding that reframed #9 – the DISPLAYED GVA rank is dominated by genome uniqueness (gu 0..50), NOT mean kinship (~0.003..0.017), by 3-4 orders of magnitude, so the mean-kinship fix corrects the number but moves NOTHING in the app top-20 (same 20 both-unknown founders, ranks 1-20; orderReport‘s gu>10 tier sits above the kinship tiers). #9’s false top-ranking has TWO causes; the 2020 remedy fixes only one. Also: verified MK_corrected = MK_current + sexMean/2 (numeric 8e-18 self-consistency), the [0,1] invariant, the U-id-normalization targeting trap, 43 one-unknown (all sire-missing) / 124 both-unknown (47 NA-birth) / 113 known. - The /grill-me (9 decisions, owner ratified each): Root target = hybrid A+B sequenced (Slice 2 = correctness fix, NOT advertised as fixing the visible top; D6 gu axis flips IN-SCOPE -> expanded Slice 3; #9 does not close on Slice 2). D2(a) sexMean = mean of the cohort’s individual mean-kinships. D2(c) self-term -> (1+sexMean)/2 (implied inbreeding = sexMean). D2(b) OWNER OVERRIDE: per-focal CONTEMPORANEOUS peer cohort, NOT a global mean – line-bred colonies’ inbreeding distribution drifts across eras (SNPRC baboon example), so a colony-wide mean imports the wrong era. D2(d) Slice 2 = ONE-unknown animals only (both-unknown deferred). Fallback = strict cohort -> nearest-earlier same-era cohort -> flag-uncorrected (never NA, never cross-era global); clamp [0,1]. 6c OWNER OVERRIDE: breeding age from a species/sex table, NOT scalar minParentAge=2 – extend speciesGestation + getSpeciesMinBreedingAge (rhesus male 4 / female 3, fallback 2); generalization -> new issue #73. Batch confirm: D1 scalar-level, D3 = the peer cohort, D4 direct mean, D7 deferred to Slice 3, D8 test_orderReport:24,42 NOT changed by Slice 2 (verified – both-unknown U-stubs on a frozen fixture), slice order S3(done)->Slice 2->Slice 3. - Adversarial self-review (wf_1408109e-bf8, 4 reviewers): GO-WITH-FIXES – caught a MAJOR implementability gap (the species column is ABSENT on qcPed/breederPed, so the helper must guard it AND the seeded rhesus 4/3 is never exercised on the fixture -> RED numbers use cutoff 2) + three stale §3/§6 statements that now contradicted §8. All fixes applied (the absent-column guard in §8-D/E, the missing-DAM fixture note, the 8e-18-is-self-consistency wording, per-item SUPERSEDED banners in §1/§3/§4/§6, the roxygen/man update step, the §7 new-issue line).

Phase-3E (build-equivalent / runtime smoke): N/A – stated, not silently skipped. No runtime behavior changed: the deliverable is the plan’s Ratification Record (§8) + close-out docs; no R//test/man//NAMESPACE/data/ change. Package build/runtime on master is unchanged from 488f2b09. The design’s correctness is verified at the algebra/real-numbers level (the pre-compute workflow + the adversarial review), not by an implementation – that is Slice 2’s RED.

Session 176 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 177): Score 9/10. S176’s => SUGGESTED NEXT named Slice 2 with the EXACT load-bearing guidance that shaped this whole session: “requires the §7 ratification FIRST … Best done as: ratify (its own focused session, D2 via /grill-me) -> THEN implement Slice 2 … Do NOT bundle ratify+implement (D2 is genuinely deep, the #1 here-be-dragons).” That single instruction (a) blocked me from the implement-Slice-2 trap when the owner picked “Slice 2,” (b) named the real deliverable (ratify §7), (c) flagged D2 as THE grill candidate and the deepest decision, (d) pointed to the plan. Every standing fact held firsthand: master 1 ahead of origin/master (the documented Gotcha 2), #9 OPEN / Slice 1 of 3 published, dashboard 98/100, the standing keeps (so I knew not to commit PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html). The -1: nothing pre-warned that ratifying would REVERSE several of the plan’s OWN recommendations (global->per-focal sexMean; gu out-of-scope->in-scope) or that Slice 2’s scope would balloon from “one line in reportGV” to a species/sex table + helper – but those are genuine discoveries of THIS session’s workflow+grill, and S176 DID flag D2 as “the #1 here-be-dragons,” which was the honest warning. ROI maximal.

Self-assessment (Session 177): 9/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER read in full; SESSION_NOTES ACTIVE TASK; GH issues; dashboard 98/100; ghost-check -> HEAD 488f2b09 = S176, no undocumented commits), reported, STOPPED for the owner’s pick; surfaced the ratify-vs-implement decision via AskUserQuestion rather than coding from the “Slice 2” prompt (FM #18/#19/#23); claimed with a 1B stub BEFORE technical work; TDD N/A declared every response. Strengths: (1) grounded the grill in REAL numbers via the pre-compute workflow, which surfaced the gu-dominance finding – the single highest-value output, since Slice 2 would otherwise ship a user-visible no-op; this directly fixed S174’s self-noted “D2 left as a recommendation, no worked numbers” gap; (2) ran a genuine /grill-me where the owner MATERIALLY reshaped the design (per-focal peer cohort; species/sex table) – the grill caught era-specificity genetics a data-only recommendation missed; (3) adversarially reviewed my OWN record before commit and caught a MAJOR implementability gap (absent species column) + 3 stale contradictions, then fixed all; (4) recording discipline – kept the predecessor’s plan intact, added an authoritative §8 + per-item SUPERSEDED banners so the implementer can’t follow stale specs; filed the owner-requested #73; (5) scope-disciplined (ratify only; did NOT implement; one deliverable), plain language, ASCII. Weaknesses (honest): (a) my FIRST §8 draft carried the major absent-species-column gap – caught by the review, but getPotentialParents already guards it, so a more careful first pass should have too; (b) the grill ran long (9 turns) – justified by genuine depth, but tighter early bundling of confirmations was possible; (c) no executable/runtime verification (inherent to a ratification) – correctness rests on the algebra + real numbers + the adversarial review, with the true test being Slice 2’s RED; (d) the rhesus 4/3 values are owner-supplied biology I can’t verify and aren’t even exercised on qcPed (fallback 2) – they ride on the owner’s authority + #73.

Learnings: Learning 166 added to PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md – the gu-dominance finding (the GVA displayed rank is governed by genome uniqueness, not mean kinship, so a kinship-only fix is a visible no-op; verify WHICH signal drives the displayed order before scoping a ranking fix) + the three D2 findings (per-focal era-specificity over a global mean; species/sex breeding-age table with the absent-species-column guard so qcPed uses fallback 2 not rhesus 4; the +sexMean/2 identity is exact by construction, not a kinship() rebuild) + the two-workflow process pattern (ground a /grill-me with a pre-compute workflow of real numbers; adversarially review your OWN ratification record before commit) + the ratification-recording pattern (authoritative §8 + per-item SUPERSEDED banners). Carried as applied: [[observation-vs-decision]] (surfaced ratify-vs-implement + presented the gu finding as input, not a unilateral plan change), [[consult-project-source-of-truth]] (the don’t-bundle / ratify-first contract + the /grill-me convention from the plan), [[ascii-only-in-question-options]] / [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]] (the AskUserQuestions + the grill), [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]] (read the plan + the prior audit’s #9 disposition); Learnings 161 (build-equivalent), 164 (the two-rank-paths structure), 165 (the column-agnostic GVA chain).

=> SUGGESTED NEXT = owner’s pick. origin/master clean at 0d559d3b; local master will be 1 commit ahead after this S177 close-out commit (unpushed docs – Gotcha 6). RED tests are spelled out in §8-E; the design is fully ratified and adversarially reviewed, so no further grill is needed. Read plan §8 FIRST. Do NOT bundle Slice 2 + Slice 3, or Slice 2 implement + publish (FM #18/#25).

Key files (this session): CHANGED – the deliverable: docs/planning/issue9-gva-unknown-parent-ranking-plan.md (added §8 Ratification Record [authoritative], checked the §7 boxes, added per-item SUPERSEDED banners to §1/§3/§4/§6).
