Key files (this session): CHANGED – code (branch walk-unification, this close-out commit): R/getLkDirectRelatives.R (now DELEGATES to getPedDirectRelatives – 4-line wrapper: fetch via getPedigreeSource("labkey"), NULL-guard, delegate; in-line strict-lineage walk + addIdRecords tail removed; roxygen @return/title updated), tests/testthat/test_getLkDirectRelatives.R (flipped the guard to the full component incl. sibling O2 + equivalence assertion; removed dead getSiteInfo/getDemographics stubs), man/getLkDirectRelatives.Rd (regenerated), NEWS.Rmd + NEWS.md (“Changes” entry). GIT: item 1 published – PR #59 MERGED (merge 6424509b); origin/master now at 6424509b. Item 2 – labkey-config-defaults DELETED (local + remote). New branch walk-unification off 6424509b (UNPUSHED). NO change to: NAMESPACE, data/, DESCRIPTION. Gotchas: (1) S149’s walk-unification is on branch walk-unification (UNPUSHED after this commit); origin/master is at 6424509b (carries S148’s getPedigreeSource). Local master is reconciled to 6424509b. Publishing S149 is the owner’s call; reconcile any stale branch via git fetch + (strict-ancestor confirmed) git reset --hard, NOT git pull (Learning 135). (2) labkey-pedsource-adapter is now MERGED (via PR #59) but still exists local + remote – a deletion candidate (owner’s call; history preserved in merge 6424509b). I deleted ONLY labkey-config-defaults this session (the explicitly-authorized one). (3) THE BEHAVIOR CHANGE IS LIVE: getLkDirectRelatives() now returns the full connected pedigree component (collaterals included), so the LabKey/EHR focal-animal pedigree (via getFocalAnimalPed()) GROWS vs the old strict lineage. The deterministic test in test_getLkDirectRelatives.R asserts {S1,D1,X1,O1,O2,GC1} incl. sibling O2 – it FAILS if anyone reverts to the strict-lineage walk. Verified via in-memory fixture + Phase-3E; NOT observed against a live LabKey server (none here) – the actual EHR result-set growth is inferred, not observed. (4) getLkDirectRelatives() now DELEGATES to getPedDirectRelatives() (fetch via getPedigreeSource("labkey"), NULL-guard, delegate). getParents/getOffspring/addIdRecords remain in the package (used by getPedDirectRelatives). (5) NEWS render traps (BOTH): re-rendering NEWS.Rmd (a) drops a NEWS.html byproduct that R CMD check flags as a top-level NOTE – delete it (Learning 139); (b) smart-converts -- to a non-ASCII en-dash in NEWS.md – avoid -- in the source (Learning 132). Permanent fix (html_preview: false + pandoc smart-off / .Rbuildignore) still a candidate, not applied. (6) git pull is rebase (pull.rebase=true) and chokes on the .DS_Store keep – use git fetch + git reset --hard origin/<branch> on a stale strict-ancestor branch (Learning 135). (7) Standing keeps: .DS_Store + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html – never commit. (8) Carried: feature-branch -> PR -> merge; package ARCHIVED on CRAN 2025-07-29; CRAN Phase 5 owner-gated; getLkDirectRelatives()/getDemographics() FAIL SOFT (warning + NULL) without a LabKey credential/config.

What Session 148 Did

Deliverable: Owner-directed 2-item pairing (the S146/S147 pattern – trivial admin + one substantive deliverable): (1) publish S147’s LabKey Rec #2 work (defaultSiteParams() centralization) from branch labkey-config-defaults to master [admin/verification]; (2) LabKey research Rec #4/#5 – formalize a data-source adapter on the getPedDirectRelatives fetch boundary + a deterministic mocked integration test [the substantive deliverable, strict-TDD]. (BOTH DONE.) Started / Completed: 2026-06-20 Status: DONE. Owner first said “Publish S147”; I claimed the session (1B stub) and started the publish, but the owner immediately flagged that a publish is admin, not a true deliverable – a correct call. I agreed, gave the honest analysis (the publish is the deferred tail of S147’s work), and re-scoped via AskUserQuestion to the 2-item pairing (publish rides along as the trivial admin item; the owner picked Rec #4 as the real work). Item 1 = VERIFICATION/admin (push -> PR -> CI -> confirm CLEAN -> merge; no production code -> no TDD gates). Item 2 = strict-TDD (RED -> GREEN -> REFACTOR, all three phase gates via AskUserQuestion, preceded by a pre-RED scope AskUserQuestion (fetch-boundary slice vs walk-unification vs plan-only) and a pre-RED approach AskUserQuestion (internal @noRd vs exported)). 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized SOLO (a serial publish + one small cohesive TDD refactor against one package; fanning out file-mutating agents would risk conflicts and add no confidence over firsthand verification – the S144/S145/S147 rationale). - REAL WORK #1 – item 1, published with full CI gate (Learning 133/135): git fetch; confirmed labkey-config-defaults was 2 ahead / 0 behind origin/master (exactly S147’s two commits cefc14a4 + 3da6723f), origin/master a strict ancestor (clean publish); the uncommitted 1B stub was NOT pushed (commits only). git push -u origin; opened PR #58 -> master; watched all checks via a background gh pr checks 58 --watch -> 10/10 PASS (lint 3m26s, all 5 R CMD check platforms incl. ubuntu-devel 16m17s, pkgdown, test-coverage, codecov patch+project). Confirmed mergeStateStatus: CLEAN BEFORE gh pr merge 58 --merge -> merge commit 1dd0c7e6; verified it landed (PR state: MERGED; both cefc14a4 and 3da6723f are ancestors of origin/master). The Rec #2 centralization is now on master. - REAL WORK #2 – item 2 grounding caught a walk-divergence landmine (-> Learning 141): read getPedDirectRelatives.R, getLkDirectRelatives.R, getDemographics.R, getParents.R, getOffspring.R, addIdRecords.R, test_getDemographics.R, test_getLkDirectRelatives.R, .lintr, and the research doc (§5 Option B, §6 risk 6, §7 Rec 4/5). The doc’s Rec #4 says “make getLkDirectRelatives() delegate its walk to getPedDirectRelatives()” – but reading both walks, they are NOT equivalent: getLkDirectRelatives() re-seeds parents/offspring from the previous generation only (a strict ancestor-up + descendant-down lineage), while getPedDirectRelatives() re-seeds from the full accumulated id set (the full connected-component closure, pulling in collaterals – siblings/cousins). So “delegate the walk” is a behavior change, not a refactor. Surfaced this via the pre-RED scope AskUserQuestion; owner chose “Adapter at fetch boundary” (true refactor, walk byte-identical) + “Internal @noRd adapter.” - REAL WORK #3 – strict TDD (RED -> GREEN -> REFACTOR): RED: new tests/testthat/test_getPedigreeSource.R (6 tests: dataframe passthrough; dataframe validation errors; labkey rename via mapPedColumns; labkey NULL-on-warning + NULL-on-error; bad-source match.arg) + a deterministic walk test in test_getLkDirectRelatives.R (focal O1 -> strict-lineage set {O1,S1,D1,GC1,X1}, EXCLUDING the collateral sibling O2 – the guard against swapping in the full-component walk). Ran -> failing. Caught + fixed a RED-discipline flaw: the expect_error tests initially PASSED falsely (a bare expect_error is satisfied by “could not find function”) – constrained them to the intended error regexps so absence-of-function no longer satisfies them -> genuinely RED. GREEN: new internal R/getPedigreeSource.R (@noRd, sourceType = c("labkey","dataframe")); rewired getLkDirectRelatives() to obtain pedSourceDf from the adapter, walk + addIdRecords tail byte-identical (the ignored unrelatedParents arg preserved). REFACTOR: removed the now-unused @import futile.logger/@importFrom stringi stri_c tags from getLkDirectRelatives (verified git diff NAMESPACE empty); NEWS.Rmd “Internal changes” entry + re-rendered NEWS.md; CHANGELOG/BACKLOG/PROJECT_LEARNINGS. - REAL WORK #4 – lint false-positive caught + resolved by rename, not nolint: undesirable_function_linter flagged the selector parameter named source (the bare symbol resolves to base source()). .lintr excludes tests/ and there is no existing source identifier in R/. Rather than scatter # nolint, I renamed the parameter source -> sourceType (lint-clean by construction; function name + behavior unchanged), updating the two R/ files + the (lint-excluded but call-bound) test calls.

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): SATISFIED. The deliverable changes getLkDirectRelatives()’s runtime fetch path, so I exercised the real, un-stubbed getPedigreeSource: "dataframe" passthrough returns the ped identical; ped=NULL -> error matching data.frame; missing id/sire/dam -> error matching column; "bogus" -> match.arg “should be one of”; and confirmed getLkDirectRelatives’s body now calls getPedigreeSource and no longer calls getDemographics directly. Build equivalent: devtools::check() Status OK (0/0/0) (full testthat suite + spelling + examples + vignette rebuild ran inside it). Full suite test_dir: 0 failed / 0 error / 1959 passed / 167 skipped; lint_package() 0; roxygenise made no NAMESPACE/man change. Caught + removed the NEWS.html render artifact before check (Learning 139); no smart-quote artifacts in the NEWS.md diff (Learning 132).

Session 147 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 148): Score 9/10. S147’s handoff (my own immediately-prior session) was a near-perfect executor’s map. Its SUGGESTED-NEXT listed both of my items as the first and third options – “(Publish S147) push labkey-config-defaults -> PR -> CI -> merge” (both publish paths pre-stated) and “(LabKey Rec #4 – the larger remaining work) formalize a data-source adapter on the getPedDirectRelatives seam + a mocked integration test. The last substantial LabKey item before CRAN re-submission.” Gotcha 1 (Rec #2 UNPUSHED on labkey-config-defaults; master at c12cfafd) held exactly firsthand (2 ahead / 0 behind). Every operational gotcha held: pull.rebase=true -> fetch (used it), the NEWS.html render artifact (deleted it again), the load-bearing no-config fallback / defaultSiteParams() single-source-of-truth (the adapter consumes siteInfo$lkPedColumns/mapPedColumns, didn’t touch it), feature-branch -> PR -> merge convention. ROI strongly positive – I executed straight off it. The -1 (to make it a 10): it pointed at Rec #4 as “formalize a data-source adapter … + a mocked integration test” but carried the research doc’s “delegate the walk” framing without flagging that getLkDirectRelatives’s walk and getPedDirectRelatives’s walk produce different result sets – the landmine I had to find firsthand in the grounding pass (Learning 141). Minor and discoverable (arguably the research doc’s gap, not the handoff’s), but naming it would have made it a 10.

Self-assessment (Session 148): 8/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER read in full; SESSION_NOTES ACTIVE TASK read in chunks; dashboard 98/100; ghost-check -> HEAD 3da6723f = S147, no undocumented commits), reported, STOPPED for the owner; claimed the session (1B stub BEFORE technical work). Headline strengths: (1) took the owner’s pushback seriously – when the owner flagged “Publish S147” is not a true deliverable, I agreed, gave the honest analysis, and re-scoped to a genuine substantive deliverable via AskUserQuestion rather than dressing up an admin task (observation-vs-decision, applied); (2) grounding caught the walk-divergence landmine (getLkDirectRelatives strict-lineage vs getPedDirectRelatives full-component) and I surfaced it via the pre-RED scope AskUserQuestion rather than blindly following the doc -> Learning 141; (3) published item 1 with the full CI gate (10/10 + CLEAN before merge, verified landed firsthand); (4) strict TDD held end-to-end – phase declared every response, scope + approach + all 3 gates, RED proven failing before GREEN, no impl in RED, REFACTOR behavior-neutral; (5) caught my own RED-discipline flaw (bare expect_error false-passes) and tightened the regexps so the tests genuinely fail RED; (6) no-behavior-change verified, not assumed – byte-identical walk + a characterization test that pins the strict lineage AND the deliberately-excluded collateral; (7) lint false-positive resolved by rename, not # nolint + cleaned the stale import tags with git diff NAMESPACE verification; (8) full verification – suite 0/0, lint 0, check 0/0/0, 3E, NEWS.html deleted; ASCII-only, plain language, 0 corrections, scope confined. Weaknesses (honest): (a) the RED tests had false-passes on the first run (caught + fixed, but a cleaner first pass would have constrained the expect_error regexps from the start); (b) the lint false-positive on source cost a rename iteration – anticipatable (a param named source is a known undesirable_function_linter trip); (c) moderate difficulty – a bounded TDD refactor + a publish; the value is the grounding discipline + the walk-divergence catch, not algorithmic depth; (d) the deliverable is the fetch-boundary SLICE of Rec #4 (walk-unification + a file/other-EHR provider deferred) – correct scoping (the full thing is a behavior change + ~1-2 days), but a slice. I reserve 9-10 for a higher-difficulty single deliverable with a clean first-pass (no RED false-pass fix, no lint-rename iteration).

Learnings: Learning 141 (two similarly-named functions can produce different result sets, so a “make A delegate its walk to B” recommendation is a BEHAVIOR change, not a refactor – read both algorithms + diff their results before unifying; the true-refactor slice is the FETCH boundary with the consuming algorithm byte-identical, locked with a characterization test) added to PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md. Carried as applied: 133 (don’t merge blind), 135 (fetch not pull), 137 (re-lint after load_all), 139 (delete NEWS.html), 140 (ground a recommendation firsthand).

=> SUGGESTED NEXT = owner’s pick. Both items DONE. Item 1 published (master = 1dd0c7e6). Item 2 (Rec #4 fetch-slice) is committed on labkey-pedsource-adapter (UNPUSHED). Natural options (plain ASCII labels): - (Publish S148) push labkey-pedsource-adapter -> PR -> CI (lint + R CMD check x5) -> merge (the S142 convention), OR fast-forward master + push. Owner’s call – I took no outward action on item 2’s publish. - (Delete the merged labkey-config-defaults branch) fully merged via PR #58; local + remote can be deleted (the S143/S146 hygiene). Owner’s call. - (LabKey Rec #4 – walk unification, a BEHAVIOR CHANGE) decide whether to unify getLkDirectRelatives’s strict ancestor/descendant walk with getPedDirectRelatives’s full-connected-component walk, and add a file/other-EHR provider on the new getPedigreeSource seam. Needs explicit acceptance that the live LabKey result set GROWS (adds collaterals). The deterministic test guards the current behavior, so this change is now safe to attempt deliberately. - (Permanent NEWS.html fix) tiny hygiene: html_preview: false in NEWS.Rmd or NEWS.html in .Rbuildignore/.gitignore (Learning 139). Candidate, not done this session. - (Answer Open Q §8.1 against the live server) the actual ONPRC/SNPRC LabKey server version (owner / live-server only). - (CRAN Phase 5, owner-run) win-builder x3 + R-hub v2 + submit_cran() – owner PAT + email; HARD STOP (docs/planning/cran-2.0.0-phase5-runbook.md). - A GitHub issue – #46, #45/#28/#9, #2, #37, #36, #29, or older #13/#12/#11/#10/#5/#1. Do NOT bundle options (FM #18/#25); do NOT start any without the owner picking.

Key files (this session): CHANGED – code (branch labkey-pedsource-adapter, this close-out commit): R/getPedigreeSource.R (NEW, internal @noRd data-source adapter, sourceType = c("labkey","dataframe")), R/getLkDirectRelatives.R (fetch now via getPedigreeSource("labkey"); walk + addIdRecords byte-identical; removed now-unused @import/@importFrom tags), tests/testthat/test_getPedigreeSource.R (NEW, 6 tests), tests/testthat/test_getLkDirectRelatives.R (+ deterministic strict-lineage walk test), NEWS.Rmd + NEWS.md (“Internal changes” entry). CHANGED – docs: CHANGELOG.md (S148 [Unreleased]), BACKLOG.md (Rec #4/#5 fetch-slice DONE; walk-unification + file-provider deferred), PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 141), SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). GIT: item 1 published – PR #58 MERGED (merge 1dd0c7e6); origin/master now at 1dd0c7e6. New branch labkey-pedsource-adapter off 3da6723f (UNPUSHED). NO change to: NAMESPACE, man/ (@noRd), data/, DESCRIPTION. Read firsthand: R/getPedDirectRelatives.R, R/getLkDirectRelatives.R, R/getDemographics.R, R/getParents.R, R/getOffspring.R, R/addIdRecords.R, R/defaultSiteParams.R, tests/testthat/test_getDemographics.R + test_getLkDirectRelatives.R, .lintr, docs/research/labkey-integration-options-2026-06-19.md (§5 Option B, §6 risk 6, §7 Rec 4/5). NOT committed (standing keeps): PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked); .DS_Store. REMOVED (render artifact, never committed): NEWS.html.

Gotchas: (1) S148’s Rec #4 work is on branch labkey-pedsource-adapter (UNPUSHED after this commit); origin/master is at 1dd0c7e6 (carries S147’s Rec #2 + S146’s floor). Local master may be stale at c12cfafd – reconcile via git fetch + (strict-ancestor confirmed) git reset --hard origin/master, NOT git pull (Learning 135). Publishing S148 is the owner’s call. (2) labkey-config-defaults is MERGED (via PR #58) but still exists local + remote – a deletion candidate (owner’s call; history preserved in merge 1dd0c7e6). (3) THE WALK DIVERGENCE IS LOAD-BEARING: getLkDirectRelatives walks a strict ancestor/descendant lineage; getPedDirectRelatives walks the full connected component (adds collaterals). They are NOT interchangeable – the research doc’s “delegate the walk” is a behavior change (deferred). The deterministic test in test_getLkDirectRelatives.R pins the strict lineage (asserts {O1,S1,D1,GC1,X1}, excludes sibling O2) – it will FAIL if anyone swaps in the full-component walk. (4) getPedigreeSource is INTERNAL (@noRd), selector param sourceType (not source – avoids undesirable_function_linter), values "labkey"|"dataframe"; the "dataframe" source is the offline/deterministic seam (and the foundation for a future file/other-EHR provider). (5) NEWS render artifact: re-rendering NEWS.Rmd creates NEWS.html (github_document html_preview) which R CMD check flags as a top-level NOTE – delete it before check/commit (Learning 139; permanent fix not yet applied). (6) .lintr excludes tests/ (so test files are not linted) and disables coalesce_linter; a parameter named source trips undesirable_function_linter. (7) git pull is rebase (pull.rebase=true) and chokes on the .DS_Store keep – use git fetch + git reset --hard origin/<branch> on a stale strict-ancestor branch (Learning 135). (8) Standing keeps: .DS_Store + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html – never commit. (9) Carried: feature-branch -> PR -> merge; package ARCHIVED on CRAN 2025-07-29; CRAN Phase 5 owner-gated; getDemographics() FAILS FAST without a credential (S144).

What Session 147 Did

Deliverable: Owner-directed 2-item pairing (“Publish S146; LabKey Rec #2”, the S142/S143/S146 pattern – trivial admin + one substantive deliverable): (1) publish S146’s Rlabkey (>= 3.2.0) floor to master [admin/verification]; (2) LabKey research Rec #2 – config-ize the ONPRC defaults (centralize the hardcoded no-config ONPRC defaults in getSiteInfo() + reconcile the example-config drift) [the substantive deliverable, strict-TDD]. (BOTH DONE.) Started / Completed: 2026-06-20 Status: DONE. Item 1 = VERIFICATION/admin (push -> PR -> CI -> merge; no production-code change -> no TDD gates). Item 2 = strict-TDD (RED -> GREEN -> REFACTOR; all three phase gates via AskUserQuestion, preceded by a pre-RED scope AskUserQuestion – 2 questions: the getSiteInfo-change scope + the example-drift reconciliation direction). 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized: SOLO for the serial git/PR publish + the small cohesive TDD refactor (fanning out file-mutating agents would risk conflicts and add no confidence over firsthand verification – the S144/S145 rationale). New session within the conversation; claimed it (1B stub BEFORE technical work, now this handoff). - REAL WORK #1 – item 1, published with full CI gate (Learning 133/135): git fetch; confirmed rlabkey-version-floor was 2 ahead / 0 behind origin/master (exactly S146’s two commits 799e98f3 + 4fd9bd40); the uncommitted 1B stub was NOT pushed (commits only). git push -u origin; opened PR #57 -> master with a scoped body; watched all checks via a background gh pr checks 57 --watch -> 11/11 PASS (lint 4m36s, all 5 R CMD check platforms incl. ubuntu-devel 17m7s, pkgdown, test-coverage, codecov patch+project). Confirmed mergeStateStatus: CLEAN BEFORE gh pr merge 57 --merge -> merge commit c12cfafd; verified it landed (PR state: MERGED; both 4fd9bd40 and 799e98f3 are ancestors of origin/master). Reconciled local master: stashed the stub, git switch master, confirmed strict-ancestor, git reset --hard origin/master (not git pull – Learning 135), created branch labkey-config-defaults off master, popped the stub. The floor Rlabkey (>= 3.2.0) confirmed on master (DESCRIPTION:52). - REAL WORK #2 – item 2 grounding caught two research-doc landmines (-> Learning 140): read getSiteInfo.R, the example config, test_getSiteInfo.R, test_loadSiteConfig.R, loadSiteConfig.R, getParamDef.R, the research doc (§4.3 / §7 Rec 2 / §6 risk 5), and grepped ALL getSiteInfo callers + every test asserting its shape. Found the doc’s literal Rec #2 sub-steps were unsafe for THIS repo: (a) “reduce the no-config fallback to a clear error” is a BREAKING change – the ONPRC fallback backs the Shiny app’s default launch (appUI/appServer/modORIPReporting) and is pinned by 5 test files + examples + the silent-expectConfigFile=FALSE contract; (b) “align the example flat dam/sire to the lookup form” would make the SNPRC example WRONG – the doc’s own §4.3 says flat dam/sire is CORRECT for SNPRC (direct columns). Surfaced both via the pre-RED scope AskUserQuestion; owner chose “Centralize, no behavior change” + “Document the center-specific form.” - REAL WORK #3 – strict TDD (RED -> GREEN -> REFACTOR): RED: tests/testthat/test_defaultSiteParams.R (2 tests / 17 assertions: canonical ONPRC values + names; getSiteInfo(expectConfigFile=FALSE) LabKey fields == defaultSiteParams(), deterministic via withr::local_envvar(HOME=tempdir)) -> both ERROR (“could not find function defaultSiteParams”), proven failing. GREEN: new internal R/defaultSiteParams.R (@noRd) = single source of truth for the 7 ONPRC fallback params; rewired getSiteInfo()’s no-config branch to source from it, return byte-identical. REFACTOR: center-specific lkPedColumns comments in the example config (SNPRC flat dam/sire = direct columns; ONPRC Id/parents/dam = curated lookup) + a characterization guard in test_loadSiteConfig.R pinning the SNPRC flat form; NEWS.Rmd Documentation entry + re-rendered NEWS.md; CHANGELOG/BACKLOG/PROJECT_LEARNINGS. - REAL WORK #4 – caught + removed a NEWS.html render artifact (-> Learning 139): the first devtools::check() returned 0/0/1 NOTE (“Non-standard file/directory at top level: ‘NEWS.html’”) – rmarkdown::render of a github_document writes an html_preview byproduct. rm -f NEWS.html (untracked, not in the change set) and re-ran devtools::check() -> 0/0/0 (claim never precedes evidence – Learning 137).

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): SATISFIED. The deliverable changes getSiteInfo()’s runtime path (now sources the fallback from defaultSiteParams()), so I exercised the real un-stubbed function: with HOME forced to a config-less temp dir, getSiteInfo(expectConfigFile=FALSE)’s 7 LabKey fields each MATCH the exact pre-change hardcoded literals, the 17-field structure is unchanged, and defaultSiteParams() == the literals -> “no behavior change” proven, not assumed. Build equivalent: devtools::check() Status OK (0/0/0) (full testthat suite + spelling + examples + vignette rebuild ran inside it); lint_package() 0 (after load_all – the one object_usage_linter flag on defaultSiteParams was the Learning-137 stale-install artifact); roxygenise made no NAMESPACE/man change (@noRd).

Session 146 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 147): Score 9/10. S146’s handoff (my own immediately-prior session) was a near-perfect executor’s map. Its SUGGESTED-NEXT listed exactly my two items as the first two options – “(Publish S146)” with both publish paths pre-stated, and “(LabKey Rec #2 – config-ize ONPRC defaults)” with the precise scope (“move baseUrl/folderPath/lkPedColumns out of getSiteInfo()’s no-config branch + reconcile the example-config drift (flat dam/sire vs Id/parents/dam). Self-contained TDD session.”). Gotcha 1 (floor UNPUSHED on rlabkey-version-floor; master at 2554c846) held exactly firsthand (2 ahead / 0 behind). Every operational gotcha held: pull.rebase=true -> fetch+reset (used it), the standing keeps (respected), feature-branch->PR->merge (followed). ROI strongly positive – I executed straight off it. The -1 (to make it a 10): it carried the research doc’s Rec #2 phrasing without flagging the two design landmines I had to find firsthand – that the no-config fallback is load-bearing (so “reduce to error” is breaking) and that flat dam/sire is correct for SNPRC (so “reconcile” != unify). Discoverable in the grounding pass (I found them in minutes), minor, and arguably the research doc’s gap rather than the handoff’s – but naming them would have made it a 10.

Self-assessment (Session 147): 8/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER read in full; SESSION_NOTES ACTIVE TASK read in chunks; dashboard 98/100; ghost-check -> HEAD 799e98f3 = S146, no undocumented commits), reported, STOPPED for the owner; claimed the session (1B stub BEFORE technical work). Headline strengths: (1) grounding caught two research-doc landmines (load-bearing fallback; SNPRC-flat-is-correct) and I surfaced them via a pre-RED scope AskUserQuestion rather than blindly following the doc -> Learning 140; (2) published with the full CI gate – 11/11 + CLEAN before merge (did not merge blind), verified it landed firsthand, reconciled master via provably-safe fetch+reset; (3) strict TDD held end-to-end – phase declared every response, all 3 gates + the scope gate, RED proven failing before GREEN, no impl in RED, REFACTOR behavior-neutral; (4) no-behavior-change verified, not assumed – runtime smoke against the exact pre-change literals + a fallback-equals-source agreement test; (5) caught + removed the NEWS.html artifact and RE-RAN check to confirm 0/0/0 (claim never precedes evidence); (6) full verification – suite 0/0, lint 0, check 0/0/0, no NAMESPACE/man drift; ASCII-only in prompts, plain language, scope confined, 0 corrections. Weaknesses (honest): (a) moderate difficulty – a small bounded TDD refactor + a publish; the value is the grounding discipline + verification, not algorithmic depth; (b) the NEWS.html artifact cost a check re-run – a cleaner first pass would have anticipated the github_document html_preview byproduct (now Learning 139), and I left the PERMANENT fix (html_preview: false / .Rbuildignore) as a candidate rather than applying it (avoided scope creep, but the trap can recur); (c) the “config-ize” landed as a centralization-into-an-internal-constant (the owner’s no-behavior-change pick), the lightest of the scoped options – correct, but not the heavier “ship a config file”; (d) left the merged rlabkey-version-floor branch undeleted (owner’s call) – recreates a small cleanup candidate. I reserve 9-10 for a higher-difficulty single deliverable with a clean first-pass verification (no artifact re-run).

Learnings: Learning 139 (re-rendering a github_document Rmd drops a <name>.html preview byproduct that R CMD check flags as a top-level NOTE – delete it before check/commit) and Learning 140 (a research/recommendation doc can contradict its OWN ground-truth sections + the live code – ground a recommendation firsthand before implementing; both of Rec #2’s literal sub-steps were unsafe here) added to PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md. Carried as applied: 133 (don’t merge blind), 135 (fetch+reset not pull), 137 (object_usage stale-install artifact + claim-never-precedes-evidence).

=> SUGGESTED NEXT = owner’s pick. Both items DONE. The Rec #2 work is committed on labkey-config-defaults (UNPUSHED); master carries the S146 floor but NOT yet the Rec #2 centralization. The last substantial LabKey item before CRAN re-submission. - (Permanent NEWS.html fix) tiny hygiene: html_preview: false in NEWS.Rmd or NEWS.html in .Rbuildignore/.gitignore (Learning 139). Candidate, not done this session. - (Answer Open Q §8.1 against the live server) the actual ONPRC/SNPRC LabKey server version (owner / live-server only) – would let the floor be pinned precisely vs the 26.6 module-target inference. - (CRAN Phase 5, owner-run) win-builder x3 + R-hub v2 + submit_cran() – owner PAT + email; HARD STOP (docs/planning/cran-2.0.0-phase5-runbook.md). - A GitHub issue – #46, #45/#28/#9, #2, #37, #36, #29, or older #13/#12/#11/#10/#5/#1. Key files (this session): CHANGED – code (branch labkey-config-defaults, this close-out commit): R/defaultSiteParams.R (NEW, internal @noRd single source of truth), R/getSiteInfo.R (no-config branch now sources from defaultSiteParams(), return byte-identical), tests/testthat/test_defaultSiteParams.R (NEW, 2 tests/17 assertions), tests/testthat/test_loadSiteConfig.R (+ SNPRC flat-form characterization guard), inst/extdata/example_nprcgenekeepr_config (center-specific lkPedColumns comments – comments only, data unchanged), NEWS.Rmd + NEWS.md (Documentation entry). CHANGED – docs: CHANGELOG.md (S147 [Unreleased]), BACKLOG.md (Rec #2 marked DONE; all 3 quick wins done), PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learnings 139, 140), SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). GIT: item 1 published – PR #57 MERGED (merge c12cfafd); local master reconciled to c12cfafd; new branch labkey-config-defaults off master. NO change to: NAMESPACE, man/ (@noRd -> no Rd, no export), data/, DESCRIPTION (the floor already landed via item 1). Read firsthand: R/getSiteInfo.R, R/loadSiteConfig.R, R/getParamDef.R, inst/extdata/example_nprcgenekeepr_config, tests/testthat/test_getSiteInfo.R + test_loadSiteConfig.R, docs/research/labkey-integration-options-2026-06-19.md (§4.3, §6 risk 5, §7 Rec 2). NOT committed (standing keeps): PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked); .DS_Store. REMOVED (render artifact, never committed): NEWS.html.

Gotchas: (1) S147’s Rec #2 work is on branch labkey-config-defaults (UNPUSHED after this commit); master (local + origin) is at c12cfafd – it carries the S146 Rlabkey (>= 3.2.0) floor but NOT the Rec #2 centralization. Publishing S147 is the owner’s call. (2) rlabkey-version-floor is MERGED (via PR #57) but still exists local + remote – a deletion candidate (owner’s call; history preserved in merge c12cfafd). (3) The no-config ONPRC fallback is LOAD-BEARING – it backs the Shiny app’s default launch + is pinned by 5 test files + examples + the silent-expectConfigFile=FALSE contract; any future change to it MUST keep getSiteInfo()’s return byte-identical or it is a breaking change. defaultSiteParams() (R/defaultSiteParams.R) is now the single source of truth for those 7 params. (4) lkPedColumns is CENTER-SPECIFIC: SNPRC flat dam/sire (direct columns); ONPRC Id/parents/dam (curated lookup). The example config (center=SNPRC) keeps flat; the no-config default (ONPRC) uses the lookup form – do NOT “align” them, they are correct as-is (now documented in the example + the guard test). (5) NEWS render artifact: re-rendering NEWS.Rmd creates NEWS.html (github_document html_preview) which R CMD check flags as a top-level NOTE – delete it before check/commit (Learning 139; permanent fix not yet applied). (6) git pull is rebase (pull.rebase=true) and chokes on the .DS_Store keep – on a stale strict-ancestor branch use git fetch + git reset --hard origin/<branch>, NOT git pull (Learning 135). (7) Standing keeps: .DS_Store + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html – never commit. (8) Carried: feature-branch -> PR -> merge convention; coalesce_linter disabled in .lintr; package ARCHIVED on CRAN 2025-07-29; CRAN Phase 5 owner-gated; getDemographics() FAILS FAST without a credential (S144).

What Session 146 Did

Deliverable: Owner-directed 2-item pairing (“1 then 2”): (1) delete the merged-dormant labkey-apikey-auth branch (local + remote hygiene); (2) LabKey research Rec #1 — pin an Rlabkey version floor in DESCRIPTION (the substantive deliverable). (BOTH DONE.) Started / Completed: 2026-06-20 Status: DONE. Item 1 = VERIFICATION/admin (a ref op, no production-code change). Item 2 = a CONFIG/packaging change (owner pick “Config change, R CMD check” — a DESCRIPTION version floor has no behavioral logic to unit-test, so no RED→GREEN→REFACTOR gates; the build equivalent IS the verification). One AskUserQuestion (2 questions: floor value + verify approach), posed AFTER grounding the floor + the version research — preceded by an owner “clarify” on that question, which the owner used to direct me to mine the EHR-module repos for the live LabKey version. 0 stakeholder corrections. Owner-authorized 2-item pairing (S142/S143 pattern): item 1 trivial admin, item 2 substantive. Right-sized: SOLO for the git admin + the one-line edit + verification; one focused workflow (4 per-repo extractors → adversarial verify → synthesis) to resolve the genuinely knowledge-gated “what LabKey version do the centers run.” New session within the conversation; claimed it (1B stub BEFORE technical work, now this handoff). - REAL WORK #1 — item 1, verified-merged-before-delete (S143 pattern): git fetch; confirmed labkey-apikey-auth (local + remote both at 6b0e892b) is a strict ancestor of origin/master (fully merged via PR #56). Deleted local (git branch -d — safe merged-only flag) + remote (git push origin --delete — outward-facing, explicitly owner-authorized “1”); verified no ref remains either side and master still carries the auth feature (merge-base --is-ancestor 6b0e892b master = YES). - REAL WORK #2 — item 2, grounded the floor FIRSTHAND (caught a doc off-by-one): the package calls exactly two Rlabkey fns — labkey.selectRows() (old) and S144’s labkey.setDefaults(apiKey=, baseUrl=). The S143 research doc said apiKey landed in 2.1.130, but the call also passes baseUrl=, and Rlabkey’s own installed NEWS shows baseUrl= support landed at 2.1.131 (apiKey 2.1.130, baseUrl 2.1.131) — so the true minimal client-correctness floor is 2.1.131, not 2.1.130. (→ Learning 138.) - REAL WORK #3 — resolved the “what server version” gate via the EHR-module repos (owner-requested): a workflow read the four vendor repos (ehrModules + onprcEHRModules + snprcEHRModules + nircEHRModules) via gh api. None pins a server version in-file (build-injected via ManageVersion/centralized Gradle), so the signal is the highest non-SNAPSHOT release branch under LabKey’s YY.M scheme: all four target 26.6 (June 2026; corroborated by newest *-26.000-26.001.sql dbscripts), maintained range ~19.x..26.6, all confirmed:true after adversarial per-repo verify. Honesty caveat carried: a maintained release branch = the version the module code is BUILT FOR, not proof the live ONPRC/SNPRC servers RUN 26.6 (a center can deploy older) — bounded by the maintained range, not pinned; Open Q §8.1 stays strictly unobserved. - REAL WORK #4 — owner decision, edit, verify: one AskUserQuestion (2 questions) → owner picked floor >= 3.2.0 (conservative policy bump: Rlabkey 3.2.0 assumes server ≥ 24.1, consistent with the 26.6 evidence) + “Config change, R CMD check.” Before writing the number, confirmed firsthand it is a real release (Changes in 3.2.0 in NEWS — “only supported for LabKey Server v24.1 or later”) and installed 3.4.6 ≥ 3.2.0. Edited DESCRIPTION:52 Rlabkey, → Rlabkey (>= 3.2.0),; committed 4fd9bd40 on new feature branch rlabkey-version-floor (off master).

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A — a DESCRIPTION metadata change, no runtime behavior change (a dependency floor changes install-time resolution, not the runtime code path, which is byte-identical). The build equivalent / verification is devtools::check() → Status OK (0/0/0) — the full testthat suite (58s), spelling, examples + --run-donttest, and the vignette rebuild all ran inside it and passed; installed Rlabkey 3.4.6 satisfies the new floor. Matches S144’s baseline. (Per the agreed config-change verification path; not silently skipped — FM #24.)

Session 145 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 146): Score 9/10. S145’s handoff (my own immediately-prior session) was a near-perfect executor’s map. Its SUGGESTED-NEXT listed exactly my two items as the first two options — “(Delete the now-merged labkey-apikey-auth branch)” with the key fact pre-stated (fully merged via PR #56; the same add-methodology/S143 hygiene) and “(LabKey Rec #1 — Rlabkey version floor) pin a floor on DESCRIPTION:52; needs the live ONPRC/SNPRC server version (research doc §8.1) or a conservative pick. Quick but knowledge-gated.” Both held exactly firsthand: the branch was a strict ancestor (safe delete), and the floor was knowledge-gated (resolved conservatively). Gotcha 1 pre-stated the branch was merged & dormant local+remote — precise. Every carried fact held (standing keeps, pull.rebase=true → fetch+reset, the fail-fast behavior, the live-server caveat, CRAN-archived). ROI strongly positive — I executed straight off it. The −1 (to make it a 10): it pointed at “research doc §8.1 / the live server version” for the gate but did NOT note that the vendor EHR-module repos themselves could be mined for the TARGETED version (which is what actually unblocked it) — minor, discoverable, and the owner prompted it; naming the module-repo signal would have made it a 10.

Self-assessment (Session 146): 8/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER read in full; SESSION_NOTES ACTIVE TASK read in chunks since the file is 1.5 MB; dashboard 98/100; ghost-check → HEAD 2554c846 = S145, no undocumented commits), reported, STOPPED for the owner; claimed the session (1B stub BEFORE technical work). Headline strengths: (1) verified-merged-before-delete for item 1 (strict-ancestor + post-delete master-retains check) — safe, history-preserving cleanup, not a blind delete; (2) grounded the floor firsthand and CAUGHT the 2.1.130 → 2.1.131 off-by-one by reading the dependency’s own NEWS against the EXACT call (all args) — exactly the error a version pin must not make; (3) resolved a genuinely knowledge-gated question (live server version) by mining the vendor module repos rather than declaring it unanswerable — workflow + adversarial per-repo verify; (4) honest about the residual gap — module-target ≠ deployed-version; Open Q §8.1 still unobserved; (5) confirmed 3.2.0 is real + satisfied by the install BEFORE writing it (claim never precedes evidence — Learning 137); (6) posed the two author’s-call decisions via AskUserQuestion (and honored the owner’s clarify → mine-the-repos request) before touching DESCRIPTION; (7) verified as the agreed config change — devtools::check() OK 0/0/0; (8) ASCII-only, plain language, 0 corrections, scope confined. Weaknesses (honest): (a) low-to-moderate difficulty — a one-line DESCRIPTION change + a bounded research lookup; the value is the off-by-one catch + verification discipline, not depth; (b) the live ONPRC/SNPRC server version is STILL strictly unobserved — the floor rests on the module-target inference + the fact that a >= floor doesn’t actually need the exact server version (its job is client-correctness + CRAN hygiene, which a floor CAN enforce; server-compat it cannot); (c) item 2 was a 2-item pairing (owner-authorized, so legitimate). I reserve 9–10 for a higher-difficulty single-deliverable session with no inherited unobservable dependency.

Learnings: Learning 138 added to PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md — pinning a dependency version floor: derive the client-correctness minimum from the dependency’s own changelog against the EXACT call (every arg); resolve a “what version does the site run” gate by mining the vendor module repos (highest non-SNAPSHOT release branch = target, distinct from deployed); a >= floor enforces only client-correctness + CRAN hygiene, not server-compat (CRAN discourages upper bounds); verify a DESCRIPTION floor as a config change via R CMD check. Carried as applied: Learning 137 (claim never precedes evidence; confirmed 3.2.0 real + satisfied before writing), Learning 135 (fetch+reset not pull, not needed this session but held).

⇒ SUGGESTED NEXT = owner’s pick. Both items DONE. The floor change is committed on rlabkey-version-floor (UNPUSHED); master is unchanged. Natural options (plain ASCII labels): - (Publish S146) the floor change is committed 4fd9bd40 (+ this docs commit) on local branch rlabkey-version-floor (off master, unpushed). Per the S142 convention (feature branch → PR → merge), push + open a PR so CI (lint + R CMD check x5 platforms) runs, then merge; OR fast-forward master + push. Owner’s call — I took no outward action on item 2’s publish. - (LabKey Rec #2 — config-ize ONPRC defaults) the LAST remaining quick-win: move baseUrl/folderPath/lkPedColumns out of getSiteInfo()’s no-config branch + reconcile the example-config drift (flat dam/sire vs Id/parents/dam). Self-contained TDD session. - (Larger LabKey work) formalize a data-source adapter on the getPedDirectRelatives seam + a mocked integration test (would consume the S144 setLabKeyDefaults() on the LabKey provider). - (Answer Open Q §8.1 against the live server) the actual ONPRC/SNPRC LabKey server version — would let the floor be pinned precisely (vs the 26.6 module-target inference); owner / live-server only. - (CRAN Phase 5, owner-run) win-builder x3 + R-hub v2 + submit_cran() — owner PAT + email; HARD STOP (docs/planning/cran-2.0.0-phase5-runbook.md). - A GitHub issue — #46, #45/#28/#9, #2, #37, #36, #29, or older #13/#12/#11/#10/#5/#1. Do NOT bundle options (FM #18/#25); do NOT start any without the owner picking.

Key files (this session): CHANGED — code (commit 4fd9bd40, branch rlabkey-version-floor): DESCRIPTION (Rlabkey, → Rlabkey (>= 3.2.0),, line 52). CHANGED — close-out docs commit (same branch): SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff), CHANGELOG.md (S146 [Unreleased]), BACKLOG.md (Rec #1 marked DONE), PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 138). GIT REFS: deleted labkey-apikey-auth local + origin/labkey-apikey-auth remote (item 1); new local branch rlabkey-version-floor (UNPUSHED). NO change to: R/, data/, man/, tests/, NAMESPACE, NEWS.md/NEWS.Rmd (packaging-metadata-only; the Rd/code are byte-unchanged). Read firsthand: DESCRIPTION, Rlabkey installed NEWS (apiKey 2.1.130 / baseUrl 2.1.131 / Changes in 3.2.0 real / 3.4.6 ≥ 3.2.0), docs/research/labkey-integration-options-2026-06-19.md (§3.4, §7 Rec 1, §8.1), the 4 EHR-module repos via gh api. NOT committed (standing keeps): PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked); .DS_Store. Transient (NOT repo): the version-detection workflow output (/tmp tasks dir); /tmp/s146_check.R + /tmp/s146_check.log.

Gotchas: (1) The floor change is on local branch rlabkey-version-floor (UNPUSHED) at the docs commit; master (local + origin) is still at 2554c846 (2.0.0) with NO floor. Publishing item 2 is the owner’s call (see Suggested Next) — I took no outward action on it. Do NOT assume master carries the floor yet. (2) labkey-apikey-auth is DELETED (local + remote) — done this session (item 1); do not push/checkout it (its history is preserved in PR #56 merge a39e73dc on master). (3) The floor is >= 3.2.0 — a conservative POLICY pick (server ≥ 24.1), NOT the minimal correctness floor (which is 2.1.131: apiKey 2.1.130 + baseUrl 2.1.131, both used by setLabKeyDefaults). A >= floor canNOT stop a too-NEW client outrunning an old server (deployment matter; CRAN discourages the upper bound that would) — its job is client-correctness + CRAN hygiene. (4) Module-target ≠ deployed version: all four centers’ EHR modules TARGET LabKey 26.6 (highest release branch), but that is the version the code is BUILT FOR, not proof the live ONPRC/SNPRC servers RUN 26.6 — research doc Open Q §8.1 stays strictly unobserved (bounded by the maintained range ~19.x..26.6). Pinning precisely needs the live server. (5) getDemographics() FAILS FAST without a credential (carried S144) — stop("No LabKey credential found."); the real LabKey path needs NPRCGENEKEEPR_LABKEY_APIKEY / a config apiKey token / .netrc. (6) git pull is configured to rebase (pull.rebase=true) and chokes on the .DS_Store keep; on a stale branch use git fetch + (after merge-base --is-ancestor confirms strict-ancestor) git reset --hard origin/<branch> — NOT git pull (Learning 135). (7) Standing keeps: .DS_Store + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html — never commit. (8) Carried: feature-branch → PR → merge convention; coalesce_linter disabled in .lintr; package ARCHIVED on CRAN 2025-07-29; CRAN Phase 5 owner-gated.

(DONE.) Started / Completed: 2026-06-19 (merge timestamp 2026-06-20T04:17:56Z UTC) Status: DONE. VERIFICATION/admin phase (declared; no production-code change -> no RED->GREEN->REFACTOR gates). 1 decision AskUserQuestion (publish path; the one owner decision, posed before any outward git action). 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized SOLO under ultracode – a serial, outward-facing git/PR sequence against one repo; nothing to parallelize and fanning out repo-mutating agents would be harmful (consistent with the S139/S140/S142 admin sessions). New session within the conversation; claimed it (1B stub BEFORE technical work, later discarded + rewritten as this handoff). - REAL WORK #1 – pre-flight + push (verified the exact payload first): git fetch; confirmed labkey-apikey-auth was 2 ahead / 0 behind origin/master and the two commits were exactly 6b0e892b (S144 feat) + 1a61dd4a (S143 docs), tree clean modulo the standing keep. git push -u origin labkey-apikey-auth -> new remote branch, upstream set. The uncommitted 1B stub was NOT committed into the feature branch (kept the PR = the CI-verified 2 commits only). - REAL WORK #2 – PR + did NOT merge blind (Learning 133): opened PR #56 (labkey-apikey-auth -> master) with a scoped body (substantive change = the API-key auth; S143 = docs; verification status; the live-server caveat). Watched all checks via a background gh pr checks 56 --watch -> 10/10 PASS: lint (4m6s), all 5 R CMD check platforms (macOS / Windows / Ubuntu devel 16m53s + oldrel + release), pkgdown, test-coverage, codecov/patch, codecov/project. Confirmed mergeStateStatus: CLEAN + mergeable: MERGEABLE BEFORE merging. - REAL WORK #3 – merge + verified it landed firsthand: gh pr merge 56 --merge -> merge commit a39e73dc; re-fetched and verified: PR state: MERGED, origin/master tip = a39e73dc, BOTH 6b0e892b and 1a61dd4a are ancestors of origin/master (merge-base --is-ancestor = YES x2), DESCRIPTION Version still 2.0.0. - REAL WORK #4 – reconciled local master (Learning 135, not git pull): discarded the disposable 1B stub (git restore SESSION_NOTES.md), git switch master (was 2 behind), confirmed local master was a strict ancestor of origin/master (merge-base --is-ancestor HEAD origin/master = YES -> ff-equivalent reset, no local commits to lose), git reset --hard origin/master -> local master at a39e73dc, tree clean, the 3 auth files (R/setLabKeyDefaults.R, R/getConfigApiKey.R, R/hasNetrc.R) present on master.

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A – a push/PR/merge/admin action, no local runtime/build change (no R//data//startup/config path edited; the merged code is byte-identical to what S144 already smoke-tested + R CMD check-passed). The proportionate “build verification” is PR #56’s own CI, run before the merge: all 5 R CMD check platforms + lint + test-coverage + codecov + pkgdown PASS – i.e. the auth feature is now verified GREEN across all 5 platforms (S144 had only the local single-platform R CMD check OK + mocks). The only path still unexercised is the live LabKey-server handshake (no server/credentials available here) – inherited from S144, stated, not silently skipped (FM #24). Pushing this close-out docs commit to master re-triggers CI (passes – docs only).

Session 144 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 145): Score 9/10. S144’s handoff (my own immediately-prior session) was a near-perfect executor’s map for a publish. Its FIRST suggested-next was “(Publish S144)” with the exact mechanism pre-stated – “push the branch + open a PR so CI … runs, then merge. OR fast-forward master” – so I came in already knowing the two paths and that the publish decision was the owner’s. Gotcha 1 pre-stated the precise git topology I had to act on (two unpushed commits: S143’s 1a61dd4a on local master + S144’s branch off that tip; origin/master still at 659aa864), and it held exactly firsthand (2 ahead / 0 behind). Every carried operational fact held: the standing keeps, pull.rebase=true -> fetch+reset not pull (Learning 135), the S142 feature-branch -> PR -> merge convention, the fail-fast behavior change, the live-server caveat. ROI strongly positive – I executed straight off it with zero rediscovery. The -1 (to make it a 10): it didn’t pre-flag that after the merge the local+remote labkey-apikey-auth becomes a merged-dormant cleanup candidate (the exact add-methodology -> S143-deletion pattern) – minor, discoverable, and now flagged below.

Self-assessment (Session 145): 8/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER read in full; SESSION_NOTES ACTIVE TASK read in chunks since the file is 1.5 MB; dashboard 98/100; ghost-check -> HEAD 6b0e892b = S144, no undocumented commits), reported, STOPPED for the owner; claimed the session (1B stub BEFORE technical work). Headline strengths: (1) resolved the one genuine owner decision (publish path) via AskUserQuestion before any outward action – did not assume PR-vs-fast-forward, did not assume merge-vs-hand-off; (2) did NOT merge blind – pushed, opened the PR, watched all 10 CI checks pass (esp. the auth feature green across all 5 R CMD check platforms, which S144 could not verify locally) AND confirmed mergeStateStatus: CLEAN before gh pr merge (Learning 133 applied); (3) verified every git step firsthand rather than trusting exit codes – pre-push commit list + ahead/behind, post-merge merge-base --is-ancestor x2 + version-intact check, strict-ancestor check before the reset; (4) reconciled local master with the provably-safe fetch+reset (confirmed strict-ancestor first) instead of git pull (which pull.rebase=true + the .DS_Store keep would have choked on – Learning 135); (5) kept the PR = the CI-verified 2 commits (did not leak the 1B stub into the feature branch); (6) right-sized SOLO, ASCII-only, plain language, 0 corrections, scope confined to the publish. Weaknesses (honest): (a) low-to-moderate difficulty – git admin + one decision + a PR; the value is verification discipline, not depth; (b) the live-server auth handshake is still unverified from here – inherited from S144, not this session’s to fix, but it bounds what “published + verified” means (CI proves build/test/lint green across platforms; it does NOT prove a real LabKey login works); (c) left the merged feature branch undeleted (local + remote) – norm-consistent (S142 kept its merged branch; S143 deleted it as a discrete owner-authorized act), but it recreates the same small cleanup candidate. I reserve 9-10 for a higher-difficulty, single-deliverable session with no inherited unverifiable-from-here dependency.

Learnings: No new project learning warranted. This session executed the S142-established publish pattern (feature-branch -> PR -> watch CI -> confirm CLEAN -> merge -> fetch+reset reconcile) cleanly with no new trap hit; the load-bearing prior learnings (133 = don’t merge blind; 135 = fetch+reset not pull on a stale branch) both applied as written. Forcing a new numbered learning here would be padding. (PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md unchanged.)

What Session 144 Did

Deliverable: LabKey research-doc Rec #3 (owner pick from the doc’s three quick-wins) — explicit optional API-key authentication for the Rlabkey data path, with .netrc fallback and a clear fail-fast error. New exported setLabKeyDefaults(); getDemographics() now auto-configures auth before querying. (DONE.) Started / Completed: 2026-06-19 Status: DONE. Strict-TDD (RED → GREEN → REFACTOR; all three phase gates via AskUserQuestion). 0 stakeholder corrections. Did NOT bundle the three quick-wins (FM #18/#25): posed a pre-RED scope AskUserQuestion (owner picked Rec #3 of {version floor, config defaults, API-key auth}), then a pre-RED approach AskUserQuestion for the author’s-call design (credential source = env var NPRCGENEKEEPR_LABKEY_APIKEY precedence then config apiKey token; getDemographics() auto-configures = fail-fast), then the three TDD phase gates. Right-sized SOLO under ultracode — a single small, cohesive TDD feature against one package; fanning out file-mutating agents would risk conflicts and add no confidence over firsthand verification. New session within the conversation; claimed it (1B stub BEFORE technical work). - REAL WORK #1 — grounded the design firsthand BEFORE RED (found two load-bearing constraints): read getSiteInfo.R, getDemographics.R, getConfigFileName.R, getTokenList.R, getParamDef.R, the example config, test_getSiteInfo.R, test_getDemographics.R, test_getFocalAnimalPed.R (the mockery idiom). Constraints: (a) getParamDef() stops on an absent token → an optional apiKey needs a soft reader; (b) test_getSiteInfo.R asserts getSiteInfo()’s exact field-name vector → do NOT add apiKey to getSiteInfo (would break the test + ripple through ~10 Shiny-module callers). So the helper reads the token itself; getSiteInfo untouched (lowest blast radius). - REAL WORK #2 — RED (7 + 3 failing tests, proven): tests/testthat/test_setLabKeyDefaults.R (7 tests / 25 assertions: env-var key, config-token key, env-over-config precedence, netrc via $NETRC, netrc via home dir, no-credential stop, empty-string-env-as-absent) using mockery (stubs ONLY labkey.setDefaults) + withr (env control), all routed through an injected siteInfo so every branch is deterministic off host state. Added getDemographics() wiring tests (auto-auth + error propagation) to test_getDemographics.R + made its skip-heavy integration test tolerate the new fail-fast. Ran both → all fail (function undefined / wiring absent). - REAL WORK #3 — GREEN (minimum impl): R/setLabKeyDefaults.R (exported): apiKey from Sys.getenv("NPRCGENEKEEPR_LABKEY_APIKEY") else getConfigApiKey(siteInfo$configFile); if present → Rlabkey::labkey.setDefaults(apiKey=, baseUrl=) (returns method="apiKey"); else if hasNetrc() → method="netrc"; else stop("No LabKey credential found." ...). Internal R/getConfigApiKey.R (soft token read) + R/hasNetrc.R ($NETRC then home .netrc/_netrc). Wired R/getDemographics.R to call setLabKeyDefaults(siteInfo) before labkey.selectRows(). roxygenise() → NAMESPACE export + man/setLabKeyDefaults.Rd. Full suite 0 failed / 0 error / 1926 passed / 167 skipped. - REAL WORK #4 — REFACTOR/finalize + verify: diagnosed 4 object_usage_linter warnings as a stale-install artifact (only the NEW cross-refs flagged; 0 after load_all; CI installs local::. before linting — verified lint.yaml) → no # nolint on correct code (→ Learning 137). Docs: setup guide docs/setup/labkey-authentication.md; documented the optional apiKey token as a COMMENT in inst/extdata/example_nprcgenekeepr_config (doc only — NOT the separate Rec #2 defaults-move); NEWS.Rmd “(development version)” entry + re-rendered NEWS.md (Learning 132 applied: error message → code span to dodge the pandoc smart-quote artifact; confirmed no smart quotes in the diff). spell_check_package() → 0 flags.

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): SATISFIED. The deliverable changes runtime behavior (getDemographics() now wires auth), so I exercised the real, un-stubbed functions in the loaded namespace: no-cred → No LabKey credential found. (real stop); apiKey env → real labkey.setDefaults call, method="apiKey"; $NETRC file present → method="netrc"; setLabKeyDefaults exported = TRUE; getDemographics body calls it = TRUE. Build equivalent: R CMD check Status: OK (0/0/0) — the tests (testthat + spelling) and the \donttest example ran inside the check. The only unverifiable-from-here path is the actual LabKey-server handshake (no server/credentials available) — branch logic is fully mock-tested + runtime-smoked; stated, not silently skipped (FM #24).

Session 143 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 144): Score 9/10. S143’s handoff (my own immediately-prior session) was an excellent executor’s map. Its FIRST SUGGESTED-NEXT was exactly “(Act on the LabKey research recommendations)” with all three quick-wins enumerated precisely — including “(3) explicit optional labkey.setDefaults(apiKey=) auth with .netrc fallback + clearer errors” — and it flagged “Each is a self-contained implementation session (strict-TDD applies),” so I came in already knowing not to bundle. The research doc it produced (§7) functioned as a precise spec; its gotchas held firsthand (recommendations not server-validated — I hit the same limit; the getPedDirectRelatives seam; mockery in test_getFocalAnimalPed.R — used exactly that; test_getDemographics.R skips on CRAN+network — I had to make it tolerate the fail-fast). ROI strongly positive. The −1: neither the handoff nor the doc pre-flagged the two design-shaping code constraints (getParamDef() stops on absent; test_getSiteInfo.R asserts the exact field set) — both discoverable firsthand (I found them in minutes), minor; naming them would have made it a 10.

Self-assessment (Session 144): 8/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER read in full; dashboard 98/100; ghost-check → HEAD 1a61dd4a = S143 close-out, clean modulo standing keep), reported, STOPPED for the owner; claimed the session (1B stub BEFORE technical work). Headline strengths: (1) did not bundle the three quick-wins — scope AskUserQuestion first, one deliverable; (2) settled author’s-call public-API decisions (env-var/token names, fail-fast wiring) via a separate pre-RED AskUserQuestion before declaring RED; (3) strict TDD held end-to-end — phase declared every response, all three gates, RED proven failing before GREEN, no impl during RED; (4) chose the low-blast-radius design from firsthand reads — left getSiteInfo untouched (exact-names test) and avoided getParamDef (stops on absent); (5) deterministic off-machine tests — all external deps injected via siteInfo + withr, only the true side-effect stubbed, so the owner’s real .netrc can’t perturb them; (6) diagnosed the lint warnings as a stale-install artifact instead of # nolint-ing correct code, and verified the CI installs local::.; (7) full verification — RED fail, GREEN pass, suite 0/0, lint 0 (loaded), spell 0, R CMD check OK, runtime smoke of the real functions; (8) complete job (FM #13): tests + impl + setup doc + example config + NEWS + CHANGELOG; ASCII-only, plain language, 0 corrections. Weaknesses (honest): (a) I wrote “R CMD check Status OK” into the CHANGELOG before running it — caught and corrected (ran it; first run failed on a /tmp libpath, re-ran with R_LIBS → genuine OK), but a claim must never precede its evidence; (b) the live-server auth handshake is unverifiable from here — bounds the confidence (mock-tested only); (c) the first R CMD check stumbled on the renv libpath — recovered, but a cleaner first pass would have used devtools::check() or set R_LIBS up front; (d) moderate difficulty — a well-bounded TDD feature, not deeply algorithmic. I reserve 9-10 for a first pass with no premature claim, no failed verification command, and no unverifiable-from-here dependency.

⇒ SUGGESTED NEXT = owner’s pick. Rec #3 DONE. Publish decision pending (see Gotcha 1). Natural options (plain ASCII labels): - (Publish S144) the work is committed on local branch labkey-apikey-auth (off master). Per the S142 convention (feature branch → PR → merge), push the branch + open a PR so CI (lint + R CMD check x5 platforms) runs, then merge. OR fast-forward master + push. Owner’s call — I took no outward action. - (LabKey Rec #1 — Rlabkey version floor) pin a floor on DESCRIPTION:52; needs the live ONPRC/SNPRC server version (doc §8.1) or a conservative pick. Quick but knowledge-gated. - (LabKey Rec #2 — config-ize ONPRC defaults) move baseUrl/folderPath/lkPedColumns out of getSiteInfo()’s no-config branch + reconcile the example-config drift. Self-contained TDD session. - (Larger LabKey work) formalize a data-source adapter on the getPedDirectRelatives seam + a mocked integration test (would consume the new setLabKeyDefaults() on the LabKey provider). - (CRAN Phase 5, owner-run) win-builder x3 + R-hub v2 + submit_cran() — owner PAT + email; HARD STOP (docs/planning/cran-2.0.0-phase5-runbook.md). - A GitHub issue — #46, #45/#28/#9, #2, #37, #36, #29, or older #13/#12/#11/#10/#5/#1. Do NOT bundle options (FM #18/#25); do NOT start any without the owner picking.

Key files (this session): CREATED: R/setLabKeyDefaults.R (exported), R/getConfigApiKey.R + R/hasNetrc.R (internal @noRd), man/setLabKeyDefaults.Rd (generated), tests/testthat/test_setLabKeyDefaults.R, docs/setup/labkey-authentication.md. CHANGED — code: R/getDemographics.R (calls setLabKeyDefaults(siteInfo) before the query), NAMESPACE (+export +importFrom, generated), tests/testthat/test_getDemographics.R (wiring tests + fail-fast-tolerant integration test), inst/extdata/example_nprcgenekeepr_config (commented optional apiKey token), NEWS.Rmd + NEWS.md (“(development version)” entry), .gitignore (whitelisted docs/setup/ so the setup guide is versioned — docs/* is otherwise ignored). CHANGED — close-out: CHANGELOG.md (S144 [Unreleased]), BACKLOG.md (Rec #3 marked DONE; remaining quick-wins + adapter left open), PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 137), SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). NO change to: getSiteInfo.R, DESCRIPTION, data/. Read firsthand: the files in REAL WORK #1. NOT committed (standing keeps): PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked); .DS_Store. Transient (NOT repo): /tmp/nprc_check/ build+check dir.

Gotchas: (1) Two commits are now UNPUSHED: S143’s close-out 1a61dd4a was already on local master unpushed at session start; S144’s work is on a NEW local branch labkey-apikey-auth (off that master tip), also unpushed. origin/master is still at 659aa864 (S142). Publishing is the owner’s call — push the feature branch + PR (the S142 convention; lets CI run), or fast-forward master. (2) getDemographics() now FAILS FAST without a credential — calls setLabKeyDefaults() which stop()s with “No LabKey credential found.” if there is no NPRCGENEKEEPR_LABKEY_APIKEY env var, no apiKey config token, and no netrc. This is intended; it replaces a later opaque Rlabkey error. Anything that drives getDemographics/getLkDirectRelatives for real now needs a credential (the test suite stubs them, so tests are unaffected). (3) The auth path is NOT validated against a live LabKey server (none available here) — the branch logic is fully mock-tested + runtime-smoked, but the actual server handshake is unexercised. (4) lintr::object_usage_linter falsely flags freshly-added functions as “no visible global function” until the package is re-installed/loaded — re-lint after pkgload::load_all(".") (→ Learning 137); CI is clean because lint.yaml installs local::.. (5) getSiteInfo() was deliberately NOT given an apiKey field (its exact field set is asserted by test_getSiteInfo.R); the credential reader is self-contained — keep it that way unless you also update that test + the Shiny callers. (6) Standing keeps: .DS_Store + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html — never commit. (7) Carried: working line master, feature branch → PR → merge; pull.rebase=true (use fetch+reset on a stale branch, Learning 135); coalesce_linter disabled; package ARCHIVED on CRAN 2025-07-29; CRAN Phase 5 owner-gated.

What Session 143 Did

Deliverable: Owner-directed 2-item pairing in one message — (1) delete the dormant, fully-merged add-methodology branch (local + remote cleanup); (2) LabKey integration research — a RESEARCH_DOCUMENTATION pass on the BACKLOG.md item → docs/research/labkey-integration-options-2026-06-19.md (223 lines, ~42 KB): current Rlabkey API surface + auth model, alternative integration approaches, EHR schema/query ground truth, version/maintenance risk, with a recommendation. (BOTH DONE.) Started / Completed: 2026-06-19 Status: DONE. VERIFICATION/RESEARCH phase (declared; no production-code change → no RED→GREEN→REFACTOR gates, no phase-gate AskUserQuestion). 0 stakeholder corrections. Noted the 2-item pairing as a deliberate, owner-authorized “1 and done” exception (item 1 is trivial admin; the research is the substantive deliverable). Right-sized fan-out under ultracode for item 2 — a 43-agent workflow (investigate → adversarial-verify → synthesize), but the load-bearing step was MY firsthand grounding + verification (read the 8 LabKey R files + the example config to build the ground-truth anchor BEFORE the fan-out, fed it to every agent as fact, and re-verified the recommendation-bearing codebase claims after the draft). New session within the conversation; claimed it (1B stub BEFORE technical work). - REAL WORK #1 — branch deletion (verified merged first): git fetch; confirmed add-methodology is a strict ancestor of origin/master (git merge-base --is-ancestor add-methodology origin/master = YES — fully merged via PR #55 / merge f44a5322; local + remote tips both 7016c376). Deleted local (git branch -d add-methodology — the safe merged-only flag) + remote (git push origin --delete add-methodology — outward-facing, explicitly owner-authorized). Verified no ref remains and master still carries the work (git merge-base --is-ancestor 507de407 master = YES, the S141 lint fix). - REAL WORK #2 — built the ground-truth anchor firsthand: read R/getDemographics.R, getSiteInfo.R, getLkDirectRelatives.R, getLkDirectAncestors.R, getTokenList.R, getConfigFileName.R, DESCRIPTION, and inst/extdata/example_nprcgenekeepr_config. Key facts pinned: the ENTIRE integration is one labkey.selectRows() of study.demographics; auth is the implicit .netrc/API-key default (the example config L32-34 explicitly requires .netrc + links the LabKey netrc docs); Rlabkey unversioned in Imports (DESCRIPTION:52); cross-center drift visible already (example config uses flat dam/sire + /snprcEHR vs the ONPRC fallback’s Id/parents/dam lookup). - REAL WORK #3 — the research workflow: 5 parallel investigators (Rlabkey/CRAN; base LabKey/ehrModules; ONPRC; SNPRC/NIRC; architecture/alternatives), each returning structured {claim, evidence, source, confidence, loadBearing}; a pipeline() adversarial-verify stage (refute each load-bearing claim against its primary source via WebFetch/gh api); a synthesis stage (drop/correct refuted, flag uncertain, build a claim-source appendix). 37 load-bearing claims → 33 confirmed, 3 refuted, 1 uncertain; the synthesis carried the CORRECTED claims with “Correction from review” notes. - REAL WORK #4 — finalized + firsthand-verified the draft: re-checked the codebase claims the recommendations rest on (getPedDirectRelatives.R:41-43 source-agnostic seam; test_getDemographics.R skips on CRAN + network → never deterministically tested; mockery in test_getFocalAnimalPed.R; getLkDirectRelatives does NOT yet delegate to the seam). Added a “Method/provenance” note, placed the doc in a NEW docs/research/ dir (no prior LabKey doc existed — checked first), removed the /tmp-style scratch draft.

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A — no runtime/behavior change (branch deletion is a ref op; the research deliverable is a doc; no R//data//startup/config path touched). The proportionate “build equivalent” for a RESEARCH_DOCUMENTATION deliverable is the claim-source verification: 33/37 load-bearing claims confirmed firsthand (adversarial stage), the rest corrected/flagged; all codebase claims re-verified firsthand against the working tree; live-server-only items explicitly marked “(unverified — requires confirmation)” in §8. No R CMD check re-run — out of scope (the R//tests//DESCRIPTION tree is byte-unchanged since S141’s Status: OK gate; this session shipped only docs). Stated explicitly, not silently skipped (FM #24).

Session 142 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 143): Score 9/10. S142’s handoff (my own immediately-prior session) was a near-perfect executor’s map. Its SUGGESTED-NEXT listed exactly the two options the owner picked — “(Delete the dormant add-methodology branch)” with the key fact pre-stated (it’s fully merged into master via PR #55, so local+remote deletion is safe and loses nothing) and “(LabKey integration research)” pointing at the BACKLOG.md item with the scope (Rlabkey API/auth, alternatives, schema/query, version risk). Every carried fact held firsthand: the merge state (add-methodology ancestor of origin/master), the standing keeps (PED_GV_AUDIT, .DS_Store), pull.rebase=true, the CRAN-archived status, coalesce_linter disabled. ROI clearly positive — I executed straight off it. The −1: it didn’t pre-state where a research doc should live (no docs/research/ existed — I created it) nor that the four LabKey EHR-module repos are the right primary sources (the owner supplied those mid-session) — minor, both discoverable/owner-driven; naming them would have made it a 10.

Self-assessment (Session 143): 9/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER read in full, notes top, dashboard 98/100, ghost-check → HEAD 659aa864 = S142 close-out, clean modulo standing keeps), reported, STOPPED for the owner; claimed the session (1B stub BEFORE technical work). Headline strengths: (1) verified the branch was fully merged before deleting (strict-ancestor check) and confirmed master retains the work after — safe, reversible-via-history cleanup, not a blind delete; (2) built the ground-truth anchor FIRSTHAND before fanning out and fed it to every agent as fact — so the doc’s baseline section is grounded, not hallucinated; (3) interposed an adversarial per-claim verification stage that caught 3 mis-sourced mechanisms + 1 over-bundled claim among 37 — and the synthesis carried the corrections, not the originals; (4) did not ship the fan-out on trust — re-verified firsthand the codebase claims the recommendations rest on; (5) honest about limits — live-server facts marked “(unverified — requires confirmation)” in an Open Questions section, not laundered into fact; (6) clean close-out — pruned BACKLOG to the actionable follow-up, recorded completion in CHANGELOG, Learning 136, removed the scratch draft; ASCII-only, plain language, 0 corrections. Weaknesses (honest): (a) the deliverable’s correctness ultimately rests on the workflow agents’ firsthand fetches for the external claims (CRAN/EHR repos) — I verified the verdicts + the codebase subset, but did not independently re-fetch every EHR-module file (the adversarial stage did, firsthand); (b) the research recommendations are not yet validated against the live ONPRC/SNPRC servers (can’t be, from here) — the doc is honest about this but it bounds the confidence of the version-floor / auth recommendations; (c) the four EHR claims I could verify locally were the codebase ones, not the LabKey Java — I trust the adversarial verdicts there. I reserve 10 for a deliverable with no unverifiable-from-here dependency.

⇒ SUGGESTED NEXT = owner’s pick. Both items DONE; add-methodology gone; the LabKey research doc is in docs/research/. Do NOT bundle options (FM #18/#25); do NOT start any without the owner picking.

Key files (this session): CREATED: docs/research/labkey-integration-options-2026-06-19.md (the research deliverable). CHANGED — close-out commit (on master): SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff), CHANGELOG.md (S143 [Unreleased] entry), BACKLOG.md (research item DONE → replaced with the actionable follow-up), PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 136). GIT REFS: deleted add-methodology local + origin/add-methodology remote. NO package source / R/ / data/ / man/ / tests/ / DESCRIPTION change. Read firsthand (anchor): R/getDemographics.R, getSiteInfo.R, getLkDirectRelatives.R, getLkDirectAncestors.R, getTokenList.R, getConfigFileName.R, getPedDirectRelatives.R (head + L41-59), DESCRIPTION, inst/extdata/example_nprcgenekeepr_config, test_getDemographics.R. NOT committed (standing keeps): PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked); .DS_Store. Gotchas: (1) The research doc’s version-floor + auth recommendations are NOT yet validated against the live ONPRC/SNPRC servers — see its §8 Open Questions; an implementer must confirm the actual server version before pinning Rlabkey. (2) add-methodology is DELETED (local + remote) — the working line is master; do not try to push/checkout it. (3) Id/parents/dam/sire resolve via base DefaultEHRCustomizer → study.demographicsParents (NOT study.parents, NOT ParentsDemographicsProvider) — a deep finding corrected by the adversarial pass; the doc has it right. (4) Cross-center divergence is real and silent — ONPRC curates genetic-preferred parents; SNPRC/NIRC collapse to 'Observed'; gender/lastDayAtCenter provenance differ — any LabKey-layer change must not assume the ONPRC shape holds everywhere. (5) Standing keeps: .DS_Store + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html — owner’s keep-call; never commit. (6) Carried: working line master, feature-branch → PR → merge; pull.rebase=true (use fetch+reset on a stale branch, Learning 135); coalesce_linter disabled; package ARCHIVED on CRAN 2025-07-29; CRAN Phase 5 owner-gated.

What Session 142 Did

Deliverable: Branch reconciliation (owner directive “work on 1, 2, and 3” from S141’s orientation report) — (1) push S141’s 2 local commits, (2) get lint GREEN on master, (3) settle the long-deferred branch-strategy decision. (DONE.) Plus a mid-session owner add: “add to backlog performing research about LabKey integration options” (done — BACKLOG.md). Owner picked branch plan “Merge now, switch to master” via AskUserQuestion. End state: master is the working line at 2.0.0 with the S141 lint fix and lint green; add-methodology is merged (dormant, NOT deleted). Started / Completed: 2026-06-19 Status: DONE. VERIFICATION/admin phase (declared; no production-code change — git operations + a PR). 1 phase-decision AskUserQuestion (branch plan; the only owner decision, posed before any merge). 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized SOLO under ultracode — a serial, outward-facing git sequence against one repo; nothing to parallelize and fanning out repo-mutating agents would be harmful (consistent with the S139/S140 admin sessions). New session within the conversation; claimed it (1B stub BEFORE technical work). - REAL WORK #1 — item 1 (push) + verified the branch state firsthand (Learning 129): git fetch + git rev-list --left-right --count before acting — add-methodology was 0/2 ahead of origin (S141’s pair) and 5/3 vs origin/master (master had the 46dfc766 PR #54 merge + old history; add-methodology had S140 close-out + the 2 S141 commits, building linearly on the PR #54 merge-base → clean merge). git push → fb91e739..7016c376, re-verified 0/0. - REAL WORK #2 — item 2 (lint green on master), did NOT merge blind (Learning 133): opened PR #55 (add-methodology→master) with an accurate body scoping the 3 commits (the lint fix is the only substantive one). Watched all checks via a background gh pr checks 55 --watch → 11/11 PASS, crucially lint PASS (4m28s) + mergeStateStatus: CLEAN (vs PR #54’s UNSTABLE) — the S141 fix verified green end-to-end (the lint workflow only runs on a PR / push-to-master, so a PR was the only way to see it pass, exactly as S140/S141 predicted). Then gh pr merge 55 --merge → merge commit f44a5322; verified firsthand it landed (state: MERGED; git merge-base --is-ancestor 507de407 origin/master = YES; origin/master still Version: 2.0.0). - REAL WORK #3 — item 3 (switch to master), recovered a stale-local-branch trap cleanly (→ Learning 135): discarded the disposable S142 stub, git switch master — but local master was 215 commits stale and git pull choked (pull.rebase=true reinterpreted it as a rebase, blocked by the standing .DS_Store change). Confirmed local master was a strict ancestor of origin/master (git merge-base --is-ancestor HEAD origin/master = YES → a hard reset is an ff-equivalent, no local commits to lose; only tracked modification was .DS_Store), then git reset --hard origin/master → at f44a5322, lint fix present, tree clean modulo the PED_GV_AUDIT standing keep. - REAL WORK #4 — LabKey backlog add (owner mid-session): added a research item under BACKLOG.md “Up Next” (the literal “backlog” → the BACKLOG.md file, lowest-ceremony; did NOT file an unrequested public GitHub issue — offered that as a follow-up).

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A — a merge/admin action, no local runtime/build change. The “build verification” for item 2 is PR #55’s own CI, run before merge: all 5 R CMD check platforms (macOS / Windows / Ubuntu devel+oldrel+release) + test-coverage + codecov + pkgdown + lint PASS. The tree content is unchanged since S141’s local R CMD check Status: OK gate; CI re-verified it across platforms. Stated explicitly, not silently skipped (FM #24).

Session 141 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 142): Score 9/10. S141’s handoff (my own immediately-prior session) was a near-perfect executor’s map: its SUGGESTED-NEXT listed all three items I worked on — “(See lint go green on a PR)”, “(Branch strategy)”, and implicitly the unpushed pair — and pre-stated every load-bearing fact: lint green locally but RED on master, the lint workflow triggers only on PR / push-to-master (so a PR is the only way to watch it pass), the 2 commits were local-only, the branch-strategy decision was open, the standing keeps. Every carried fact held firsthand (the 0/2 unpushed, the divergence, coalesce_linter disabled, CRAN gating). ROI clearly positive — I executed straight off it. The −1: it framed “(See lint go green on a PR)” as “Nothing for a Claude session unless you want a PR opened” — slightly underselling that opening + merging the PR is itself a clean, well-scoped session; and it didn’t flag the operational trap I hit (local master was 215 commits stale + pull.rebase=true), which a “if you switch to master, your local master is far behind — fetch+reset, don’t pull” note would have pre-armed (now Learning 135). Minor — both discoverable firsthand.

Self-assessment (Session 142): 8/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER read in full, notes top, dashboard 98/100, ghost-check → HEAD = S141 close-out, clean modulo standing keeps), reported, STOPPED for the owner; claimed the session (1B stub BEFORE technical work). Headline strengths: (1) resolved the one genuine owner decision (branch strategy) via AskUserQuestion before any merge — did not assume; the answer shaped item 2’s depth; (2) did NOT merge blind — opened the PR and confirmed all 11 checks green (esp. lint PASS = the deliverable’s goal verified end-to-end) before merging (Learning 133 applied); (3) verified every git step firsthand (rev-list before/after push, merge-base --is-ancestor + version after merge, strict-ancestor check before the reset) rather than trusting exit codes; (4) recovered the stale-local-master + pull.rebase trap with a provably-safe reset (confirmed ancestor first) instead of force-flailing; (5) honored the literal “backlog” request (BACKLOG.md) without an unrequested outward-facing public issue, and offered the issue path; (6) right-sized SOLO, ASCII-only, plain language, 0 corrections, scope confined to the directive. Weaknesses (honest): (a) low-to-moderate difficulty — git admin + one decision + a PR; the value is verification discipline, not depth; (b) the item-3 switch stumbled on the stale-branch/pull.rebase snag — recovered cleanly with no loss, but a smoother first pass would have gone straight to git fetch + git switch -C master origin/master (or reset) knowing local master was dormant; (c) batched the LabKey backlog add into this session (owner-directed mid-session, so legitimate, but a second small deliverable beyond the original 1/2/3). I reserve 9-10 for a higher-difficulty, single-deliverable session with a clean first pass.
