Key files (this session): CHANGED — close-out commit (on master): SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff), CHANGELOG.md (S142 [Unreleased] entry), BACKLOG.md (LabKey research item under “Up Next”), PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 135). NOT committed (standing keeps): PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked); .DS_Store (the reset discarded its prior modification — will re-modify, never commit). Gotchas: (1) The working line is now master (owner “switch to master” decision) — future work via short-lived feature branches off master → PR → merge. add-methodology is merged & dormant (NOT deleted; do not commit to it). (2) git pull is configured to rebase (pull.rebase=true) and will choke on the standing .DS_Store modification; if a local branch is stale, use git fetch + (after git merge-base --is-ancestor HEAD origin/<branch> confirms strict-ancestor) git reset --hard origin/<branch> — NOT git pull (Learning 135). (3) lint is now GREEN on master (PR #55). Pushing the close-out docs commit to master re-triggers CI (passes — docs only). (4) coalesce_linter is permanently DISABLED in .lintr (carried S141) — do NOT re-enable + adopt %||% without bumping Depends: R (>= 4.4.0) (shiny 1.13 does not export it). (5) Standing keeps: .DS_Store + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html — owner’s keep-call; never commit. (6) Carried CRAN gotchas: package ARCHIVED on CRAN 2025-07-29 (Learning 95); CRAN Phase 5 owner-gated.

What Session 141 Did

Deliverable: Lint cleanup -> green CI (owner pick) — clear every lintr warning + the cyclocomp config wart so the lint GitHub Actions check passes; the exact RED-on-master state Learning 133 flagged. (DONE.) A firsthand lint_package() found 57 lints across 33 files (the CI log’s “~30” undercounted). Final: 0 lints, full suite 0/0, R CMD check Status OK. Started / Completed: 2026-06-19 Status: DONE. Strict-TDD REFACTOR (declared every response; behavior-neutral). Phase gates: one scope AskUserQuestion (3 owner decisions) + one REFACTOR phase-gate AskUserQuestion before any edit. 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized fan-out under ultracode — the 46 per-file fixes ran through a fix->adversarial-review workflow (56 agents), but the load-bearing step was my central re-verification (re-lint + full suite + R CMD check + reading the entire diff), which caught a cascade the per-file fixers were blind to. New session within the conversation; claimed it (1B stub BEFORE technical work). Two commits planned: the content commit (config + 28 source + man/) + this close-out commit (CHANGELOG/PROJECT_LEARNINGS/SESSION_NOTES). - REAL WORK #1 — established the true baseline + the one real risk before editing: local lint_package() = 57 (= CI exactly), lintr/cyclocomp installed locally so verification is firsthand. The “dragon”: coalesce_linter (11) wants %||%, but it needs R >= 4.4 (pkg Depends: R (>= 4.1.0)) and shiny 1.13 no longer exports %||% (verified "%||%" %in% getNamespaceExports("shiny") = FALSE) — so adopting it would silently break R 4.1-4.3. Surfaced this; owner chose to disable coalesce_linter in .lintr (consistent with the 7 linters it already NULLs). - REAL WORK #2 — fixed 46 code lints across 28 files via a fix->adversarial-review workflow: 31 return_linter (drop terminal return(); restructure terminal if/else chains — chooseDate/removeDuplicates/convertDate/dataframe2string/qcStudbook), 6 seq_along, 5 file.path (1 R/ + 4 data-raw), 2 line wraps, 2 !any. 27 files clean by both fixer + independent reviewer. - REAL WORK #3 — central re-lint caught a CASCADE the fixers couldn’t see (-> Learning 134): after the workflow, re-lint read 5, not 0 — all in removeDuplicates.R: the boolean_arithmetic autofix sum(duplicated(x))==0L -> !any(duplicated(x)) cleared its target but TRIGGERED if_not_else + any_duplicated + unnecessary_nesting. Rewrote to if (anyDuplicated(x) > 0L) <dups> else NULL + a guard clause for the stop() branch -> all four cleared, behavior preserved (incl. empty-vector edge). Re-lint -> 0. - REAL WORK #4 — context-blind-linter judgments: (a) nonportable_path_linter flagged a MIME-type string in modPedigree’s fileInput(accept=...); the author had already # nolint-ed the adjacent "text/csv" line (intent = false positive) but missed the second string — the fixer wrapped the MIME type in file.path() (output identical, semantically wrong), so I restored the readable string + extended the # nolint. (b) cyclocomp wart: added any::cyclocomp to lint.yaml (.lintr already NULLs the linter; linters_with_tags() warns at config-load when absent — non-fatal). (c) the one roxygen line wrap (R/data.R:358) made man/ stale, so regenerated (roxygen2 == pinned 8.0.0; diff confined to man/rhesusPedigree.Rd).

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): SATISFIED by R CMD check. The change is behavior-neutral R code + lint/CI config; R CMD check (--no-manual --ignore-vignettes) loaded the namespace, ran all examples (+ donttest) and the full testthat + spelling suites -> Status: OK (0/0/0), which exercises the real runtime paths. No separate Shiny-app launch needed: no startup/dispatch/config-resolution behavior changed (the only mod*.R edits were a # nolint comment + a restored-identical MIME string + implicit-return/seq_along/%||%-free rewrites). The .lintr/lint.yaml edits are CI-only, not runtime.

Session 140 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 141): Score 9/10. S140’s handoff (my own immediately-prior session) was an excellent executor’s map for this task. It listed “(Lint cleanup → green CI)” as the FIRST suggested-next with the exact linter names (return_linter/seq_linter/coalesce_linter/nonportable_path_linter/boolean_arithmetic_linter) and the cyclocomp wart, correctly classified it as a behavior-neutral REFACTOR, and Learning 133 even pre-stated “a future REFACTOR session could clear it to green” — so I started with the scope already framed. Every carried fact held: lint RED on master, the branch-divergence (add-methodology 1 ahead / behind the merge), the standing keeps, CRAN gating. ROI clearly positive. The −1: the count was “~30” but the real figure is 57 (S140 read it off the truncated CI log, reasonably) — and the handoff didn’t flag the two non-mechanical traps a fixer hits (%||% unavailable below R 4.4 / not in shiny 1.13; the nonportable_path MIME-type false positive). Minor, since both were discoverable firsthand; naming them would have made it a 10.

Self-assessment (Session 141): 9/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER read in full, notes top, dashboard 98/100, ghost-check clean), reported, STOPPED for the owner; claimed the session (1B stub BEFORE technical work). Headline strengths: (1) established the true baseline before editing — corrected “~30”->57, installed lintr/cyclocomp for firsthand verification, and pinned tests green as a baseline; (2) caught the one real correctness risk up front (%||% below the R floor / dropped by shiny 1.13) and surfaced it as an owner decision rather than silently narrowing R support; (3) the central re-lint caught the cascade the per-file fixers were blind to (boolean_arithmetic -> 3 new linters) — and I rewrote it behavior-preserving rather than whack-a-mole; (4) treated linter messages as suggestions, not mandates — kept the readable MIME string + # nolint instead of file.path()-wrapping a MIME type; (5) regenerated man/ for the roxygen wrap (version-matched, diff confined); (6) read the entire git diff firsthand + the full triple (lint 0, suite 0/0, R CMD check OK); 0 corrections, ASCII-only, plain language. Weaknesses (honest): (a) my first workflow invocation failed (pipeline() got a non-array — args didn’t arrive as JSON; relaunched with the list embedded) — no harm, 0 agents ran, but a sloppy first call; (b) the workflow’s per-file fixers produced a residual (the cascade + the MIME file.path) — caught entirely by my central verify (no shipped defect), but it means the fan-out’s value was bounded and the verification did the real work; (c) moderate difficulty — a careful, well-bounded refactor. I reserve 10 for a first pass with no failed tool call and no fixer residue.

⇒ SUGGESTED NEXT = owner’s pick. Lint cleanup is DONE (0 lints locally; R CMD check OK). Natural options (plain ASCII labels): - (See lint go green on a PR) the lint workflow triggers on pull_request and on push to main/master — NOT on push to add-methodology. To watch it pass, it rides the next add-methodology -> master PR (or any PR). Nothing for a Claude session unless you want a PR opened. - (Branch strategy) still your open decision (S140): continue on add-methodology (now further ahead of master) vs move to master + feature branches. Untouched. - (CRAN Phase 5, owner-run) win-builder x3 + R-hub v2 + submit_cran() — owner PAT + email; HARD STOP (docs/planning/cran-2.0.0-phase5-runbook.md). - A GitHub issue — #46 (species first-class), #45/#28/#9 (parent ID via conception date), #2 (GVA iteration-count), #37 (unused exports), #36 (chimp pyramid), #29 (makeGrpNum->makeGroupNum), or older #13/#12/#11/#10/#5/#1. Do NOT bundle options (FM #18/#25); do NOT start any without the owner picking.

Key files (this session): CHANGED — content commit (add-methodology): .lintr (coalesce_linter = NULL), .github/workflows/lint.yaml (any::cyclocomp), 26 R/*.R + 2 data-raw/*.R source files (lint fixes — see CHANGELOG for the per-linter breakdown), man/rhesusPedigree.Rd (regenerated, one reflowed line). CHANGED — close-out commit: CHANGELOG.md (S141 [Unreleased] entry), PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 134), SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). NOT committed (standing keeps): .DS_Store (modified), PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked). Read firsthand: SESSION_RUNNER + SAFEGUARDS (full), the failed CI lint log (gh run view 27843775241 --log-failed), .lintr, lint.yaml, DESCRIPTION/NAMESPACE, and every changed source diff. Transients (in /tmp, NOT repo): background job outputs; the devtools::check build dir (auto-cleaned — no .Rcheck/tarball left in the repo).

Gotchas: (1) lint is now GREEN locally (0 lints) but the GitHub check is still RED on master — the fix is on add-methodology, not yet merged; the lint workflow does NOT run on push to add-methodology (only pull_request + push to main/master), so it goes green when the next PR runs. (2) coalesce_linter is permanently DISABLED in .lintr by design — do NOT “re-enable + adopt %||%” without first bumping Depends: R (>= 4.4.0) or adding a dependency that provides %||% (shiny 1.13 does not export it). (3) A lint sweep is iterative — an autofix can clear its target and trigger other linters (Learning 134); always re-run lint_package() over the whole tree until 0, never trust per-file results. (4) modPedigree’s fileInput(accept=) MIME strings carry # nolint: nonportable_path_linter — they are MIME types, not paths; do not “fix” them. (5) man/rhesusPedigree.Rd is generated — the wrap came from R/data.R’s roxygen; edit the source + roxygenise(), never hand-edit the .Rd. (6) Standing keeps: .DS_Store + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html — owner’s keep-call; never commit. (7) Branch: add-methodology is further ahead of master after this; per Learning 129, git fetch + git rev-list --left-right --count origin/master...add-methodology before acting on any branch-sync/merge prerequisite. (8) Carried CRAN gotchas: package ARCHIVED on CRAN 2025-07-29 (Learning 95); CRAN Phase 5 owner-gated.

What Session 140 Did

Deliverable: Merge PR #54 (add-methodology → master, the 2.0.0 merge) per owner directive “merge #54”. (DONE.) Merge commit 46dfc766; origin/master now Version: 2.0.0 (was 1.1.0.9000). The 2.0.0 release has reached master. Started / Completed: 2026-06-19 Status: DONE. VERIFICATION/admin phase (no code/behavior change; declared). 0 stakeholder corrections. No phase-gate AskUserQuestion (no RED→GREEN→REFACTOR). Right-sized SOLO — a pre-merge CI triage + one merge command; nothing to fan out. New session within the conversation; claimed it (1B stub BEFORE technical work). - REAL WORK #1 — pre-merge CI triage, did NOT merge blind (→ Learning 133): gh pr view 54 → mergeable: MERGEABLE, mergeStateStatus: UNSTABLE. gh pr checks 54 → every R-CMD-check (macOS / Windows / Ubuntu devel+oldrel+release) + test-coverage + codecov + pkgdown PASS; one red: lint. Read the failed job log: ~30 lintr STYLE warnings (return_linter, seq_linter, coalesce_linter, nonportable_path_linter, boolean_arithmetic_linter) in long-standing core files (qcStudbook.R, geneDrop.R, removeDuplicates.R, the mod*.R modules, …) + a cyclocomp-not-installed CI config wart (exit 31). None are correctness issues; none introduced by S139 (docs only — 0 R-code change); lint is non-required (UNSTABLE not BLOCKED → GitHub permits the merge). Surfaced the finding, then proceeded per the explicit directive. - REAL WORK #2 — merged with a merge commit; verified it landed firsthand: gh pr merge 54 --merge → merge commit 46dfc766 (mergedBy rmsharp; branch NOT deleted, preserves the 22-commit session history). The first gh pr view --json merged errored (merged is not a field — use state/mergedAt), which briefly made the successful merge look failed; re-checked → state: MERGED, mergedAt 19:05:08. Then git fetch + git show origin/master:DESCRIPTION → Version: 2.0.0; git log origin/master shows 46dfc766 Merge PR #54...; git merge-base --is-ancestor e7e41700 origin/master = YES (all S139 work is on master).

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A — a merge/admin action, no local runtime/build change. The “build verification” for this deliverable is the PR’s own CI, run before merge: every R CMD check platform (macOS / Windows / Ubuntu devel+oldrel+release) passed, plus coverage + pkgdown. The only red check (lint) is non-required style debt. Stated explicitly, not silently skipped (FM #24). No local R CMD check re-run (the tree is unchanged since S138’s Status: OK gate; CI already gates the merged tree across platforms).

Session 139 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 140): Score 8/10. S139’s handoff (my own immediately-prior session) was directly actionable for this task: it listed “Review / merge PR #54” as the top SUGGESTED-NEXT with the PR URL, explicitly said “merge timing is the owner’s call,” and the gotchas held (PR open-not-merged, CRAN gated, generated files). ROI clearly positive — I executed straight off it. The −2: it did not flag the PR’s CI status — lint was red on #54, and the handoff’s “R CMD check OK, tests 0/0” was a local claim that didn’t mention the GitHub Actions checks, so the lint-red was a surprise I had to discover and triage in S140. Mitigating: at S139 close-out the CI was almost certainly still running (the PR had just been opened; the Ubuntu devel job alone takes ~16 min), so the status wasn’t knowable yet — but a one-line “CI is running on #54; run gh pr checks 54 before merging” would have pre-armed S140 and made it a 10.

Self-assessment (Session 140): 8/10. Claimed the session (1B stub BEFORE technical work). Headline strengths: (1) did not merge blind — verified mergeable + ran gh pr checks + read the failing job log before touching the merge button; (2) classified the red check correctly (non-required lint, pre-existing style debt, not introduced by S139, all correctness checks green) and surfaced it rather than merging silently over a red check OR refusing an explicit directive over a non-blocking style check; (3) used a merge commit (history-preserving) and did not delete the branch; (4) verified the merge landed firsthand (git show origin/master:DESCRIPTION = 2.0.0; merge-base --is-ancestor) instead of trusting the gh exit — which mattered, because my own malformed --json merged query had briefly masked the success; (5) captured Learning 133 and recorded the new known state (lint red on master) + the branch-divergence decision for the owner. Weaknesses (honest): (a) low difficulty — a triage + one command; (b) I malformed the first gh pr view --json merged query, which made a successful merge momentarily look failed — caught instantly by re-checking with valid fields, no harm, but a sloppy first call; (c) proceeded with the merge over a red (non-required) check without a separate confirmation round — defensible given the explicit “merge #54” + the non-blocking nature + all correctness checks green, but a more conservative read would have paused to confirm “merge despite the non-required lint?”; I judged the explicit directive sufficient. I reserve 9-10 for a higher-difficulty, end-to-end deliverable.

What Session 139 Did

Deliverable: Clear S138’s punch-list (owner directive “perform S138’s next steps”) — (DONE). Four items, in a safe order: (1) NEWS names' spell flag — fixed at source (backtick the literal base-R message in NEWS.Rmd; the curl was a pandoc smart-quote render artifact, not a source typo), re-rendered NEWS.md, spell_check_package() 1→0 flags; (2) README “For more information” de-dup — removed README.Rmd’s own hardcoded Vinson/Raboin citation block (the duplicate; the surviving copy renders from the shared _introduction.Rmd child), re-rendered README.md via build_readme(); (3) CRAN plan §9 Phase-3 reconcile — marked §9 row 3 ✅ + added a Phase-3 STATUS block (DESCRIPTION/README/CITATION.cff all verified at 2.0.0); (4) opened PR #54 add-methodology → master (open, NOT merged). Issue #53 was already CLOSED by the owner earlier today — no action. Started / Completed: 2026-06-19 Status: DONE. VERIFICATION/docs phase (declared every response — no production logic changed; no RED→GREEN→REFACTOR transition, so no phase-gate AskUserQuestion). 0 stakeholder corrections. Owner explicitly overrode “1 and done” to batch S138’s menu; executed in a safe order with a commit + a confined-diff check between the generated-file re-renders. Right-sized SOLO under ultracode — surgical doc edits + an outward-facing PR; a fan-out would risk conflicting edits to the same tree and adds no confidence over a firsthand confined-diff read. New session within the conversation; claimed it (1B stub BEFORE technical work). Two commits: 6be6a8aa (the 3 content fixes) + the close-out commit (CHANGELOG/PROJECT_LEARNINGS/SESSION_NOTES); both pushed. - REAL WORK #1 — diagnosed the names' flag as a RENDER artifact, fixed at source (→ Learning 132): S138 said “fix the quote (don’t whitelist)” at NEWS.md:51. But NEWS.Rmd:53 already used straight ASCII quotes — pandoc’s smart extension curls 'row.names' → ‘row.names’ at render, and the closing ' against names makes the bogus token names’. So there was no source quote to “straighten.” Wrapped the literal message in backticks in NEWS.Rmd (`duplicate 'row.names' are not allowed` — code spans are smart-quote-exempt and read as the verbatim message), re-rendered NEWS.md (github_document, html_preview=FALSE) → exactly a one-line diff; spelling::spell_check_package() → 0 flags (was 1). Did NOT hand-edit NEWS.md (generated) and did NOT disable smart globally (would curl-strip every other quote in the 500-line file). - REAL WORK #2 — mapped the README duplication before cutting: the citation rendered twice — first from the shared child vignettes/manual_components/_introduction.Rmd (also consumed by a3manual.Rmd + ColonyManagerTutorial.Rmd), second from README.Rmd’s own hardcoded block (lines 46-51). Removed only the README.Rmd block → README-only blast radius (manual/tutorial vignettes untouched), citation survives once in the Introduction. Re-rendered via devtools::build_readme(); git diff README.md = removed block + an incidental, correct getVersion() date refresh (2026-06-17 → 2026-06-19). Confirmed grep "For more information" README.md = 1 hit. - REAL WORK #3 — plan §9 reconcile, evidence-based: verified firsthand DESCRIPTION 2.0.0, README.md “Version 2.0.0”, CITATION.cff version: 2.0.0; NEWS re-renders; version-tests green (S138 0/0). Marked docs/planning/cran-2.0.0-submission-plan.md §9 row 3 ✅ + added a Phase-3 STATUS block matching the Phase-4/5 pattern. - REAL WORK #4 — PR #54 opened (open, not merged): git fetch + rev-list confirmed origin/add-methodology == HEAD (0/0, no push-before-PR needed — Learning 129); git show origin/master:DESCRIPTION = 1.1.0.9000 (PR genuinely needed). Scoped the real diff (22 commits, 50 files, +1814/−577; merge-base after PR #52/S117) and wrote a comprehensive, accurate PR body (2.0.0 highlights from NEWS, CRAN-status note, verification, “merge timing is your call”). https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/pull/54

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A — no runtime behavior changed (no R//startup/dispatch/data/config path touched). The proportionate doc build-equivalents all passed: devtools::build_readme() rendered README cleanly, rmarkdown::render(NEWS.Rmd, github_document) rendered NEWS cleanly, spell_check_package() → 0 flags, and each generated-file git diff was confined to the intended change. No full R CMD check re-run — out of scope: the R//data//tests//man//DESCRIPTION tree is unchanged since S138’s Status: OK gate; the only shipped change is NEWS.md prose (the check does not validate NEWS content beyond presence). Stated explicitly, not silently skipped (FM #24).

Session 138 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 139): Score 8/10. S138’s handoff was a strong executor’s map: it listed the exact punch-list as a SUGGESTED-NEXT menu (the four items I cleared), kept the standing-keep gotchas (.DS_Store, PED_GV_AUDIT) accurate, and correctly said the names' fix should be “fix the quote, don’t whitelist.” Its firsthand facts held (versions at 2.0.0, branch sync, the README de-dup carried as FM #8). ROI clearly positive — I executed straight off the menu. The −2: (a) the names' item pointed at NEWS.md:51 and called it a “curly apostrophe in a quoted base-R error” — accurate symptom, but it did not flag that this is a render artifact (NEWS.Rmd already has straight quotes), so “fix the quote” could mislead a literal reader toward a source quote-swap that doesn’t exist, or toward editing the generated NEWS.md directly; I had to diagnose the smart-quote root cause myself (now Learning 132). (b) “Close #53” rode the menu as an open item, but the owner had already closed it before this session — a world-changed staleness (not S138’s fault, but the kind of thing a “verify state before acting” note guards against — cf. Learning 129). (c) No note that README.md/NEWS.md are generated and the README dup spans a shared child — I mapped that firsthand. A one-line “both are generated; README dup = shared _introduction.Rmd + README.Rmd’s own block” would have made it a 10.

Self-assessment (Session 139): 8/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER read in full, notes top via offset reads, dashboard 98/100, git + gh, ghost-check → HEAD db100064=S138, clean modulo standing keeps), reported, STOPPED for the owner’s direction; claimed the session (1B stub BEFORE technical work). Headline strengths: (1) diagnosed the names' flag as a pandoc smart-quote render artifact rather than chasing a non-existent source typo or hand-editing the generated NEWS.md — and fixed it the durable way (code span at source), verifying the re-render diff was one line + spell check 0; (2) mapped the README duplication before cutting and removed the LOW-blast-radius copy (README.Rmd’s own block, not the shared child) — manual/tutorial vignettes untouched; (3) verified every claim firsthand (versions, 0/0 branch sync, #53 already closed, the real PR diff scope) instead of trusting the handoff; (4) caught that #53 was already closed → did not redundantly act; (5) opened the PR but did NOT merge — respected the outward-facing/owner decision (merge timing + CRAN gating), with an accurate, comprehensive body that scopes the real diff and doesn’t overclaim; (6) confined every commit (excluded the standing keeps), captured Learning 132; ASCII-only, plain language, 0 corrections. Weaknesses (honest): (a) batched a 6-item menu in one session — a deliberate, owner-authorized override of “1 and done”, but worth naming as an exception, not a precedent; (b) low-to-moderate difficulty — docs/admin + two re-renders; the value is the render-artifact diagnosis + verification discipline, not depth; (c) no full R CMD check this session (proportionate — tree unchanged since S138’s OK gate, doc build-equivalents passed — but named, not assumed); (d) the README diff carries an incidental version-date refresh beyond the pure de-dup (correct, since the README was genuinely regenerated today, but it’s a second change in the same file). I reserve 9-10 for a higher-difficulty, single-deliverable session.

Transients (in /tmp, NOT repo): s139_pr_body.md.

Gotchas: (1) PR #54 is OPEN, not merged — add-methodology → master; the merge is the owner’s call (CRAN Phase 5 still owner-gated). (2) Issue #53 is CLOSED (owner closed it 2026-06-19; do not re-investigate the si.re/sire.id work as new). (3) README.md AND NEWS.md are GENERATED — README.md from README.Rmd (via devtools::build_readme()), NEWS.md from NEWS.Rmd (via rmarkdown::render(..., github_document, html_preview=FALSE)). Never hand-edit the .md — edit the .Rmd source and re-render (plan gotcha #3; Learning 132). The vignettes/manual_components/_*.Rmd children are SHARED with a3manual.Rmd + ColonyManagerTutorial.Rmd — editing a child changes those vignettes too; to de-dup README-only, remove README.Rmd’s own block, not the child. (4) A spell/lint flag on a generated file can be a pandoc smart-quote artifact — the source may already be “correct”; wrap the literal in a code span rather than disabling smart (Learning 132). (5) README.md version-date is now 2026-06-19 (re-render refreshed getVersion()) — expected, not a defect. (6) spell_check_package() is now 0 flags. (7) Standing keeps: .DS_Store + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html — owner’s keep-call; never commit. (8) Branch: both S139 commits are pushed to origin/add-methodology; per Learning 129, git fetch + git rev-list --left-right --count origin/add-methodology...HEAD before acting on any push/merge prerequisite. (9) Carried CRAN gotchas: package ARCHIVED on CRAN 2025-07-29 (Learning 95); the 2 false-positive --as-cran NOTEs (incoming-feasibility CRAN-persistent; HTML-manual local-only).

What Session 138 Did

Deliverable: owner pick (option 2 — the real fix for issue #53) — rename the malformed sire column from si.re (period) / si re (space) to sire.id across the 6 example pedigree datasets + sweep dependent source/docs/tests, and remove the now-unneeded si inst/WORDLIST whitelist (S137’s tentative resolution, superseded). (DONE.) Full strict TDD RED→GREEN→REFACTOR→VERIFY with a phase-gate AskUserQuestion at every transition. Target name sire.id (owner-chosen): a period in a column name is a realistic “inexperienced data provider” messy header, so the fix KEEPS a period-bearing header under test (rather than removing periods) — but in a sensible place that tokenizes to real words (sire+id) so it no longer spell-flags. Verified: fixColumnNames("sire.id") → sire, exercising MORE normalization than si.re (periodRemoved + sireIdToSire vs periodRemoved only). Started / Completed: 2026-06-19 Status: DONE. Strict TDD (declared every response). Gated AskUserQuestions (4): PRE-RED→RED, RED→GREEN, GREEN→REFACTOR (all “full scope”), plus the pre-RED owner confirmation of the target name. 0 stakeholder corrections. Builds directly on S137’s audit (issue #53) — this is the deferred real fix the owner chose to do now instead of S137’s whitelist. New session within the conversation (claimed it, 1B stub BEFORE technical work). - RED: new tests/testthat/test_exampleData_columnNames.R — (1) data contract pinning all 6 datasets’ exact names to include sire.id; (2) malformed-name-absent check; (3) a normalization-invariant guard (fixColumnNames → canonical). Confirmed RED: tests 1+2 = 12 assertions FAIL (names held si.re/si re), guard = 6 PASS, 0 errors. - GREEN (minimum to pass): regenerated the 6 .RData — load → rename the si.re/si re column to sire.id → save(compress="gzip"), with a stopifnot(identical(new, old)) proving values byte-identical (only the name moved); nrow preserved. Re-ran → 18/18 green, 0 error. (createPedOne.R deliberately left for REFACTOR — the test reads the shipped .RData, not the generator.) - REFACTOR (behavior-neutral): R/createPedOne.R:17 `si re` → sire.id; si.re → sire.id in the 5 R/data.R roxygen blocks (replace_all, exactly 5 hits) → roxygen2::roxygenise() regenerated exactly the 5 man/*.Rd (NAMESPACE untouched, si.re gone from man/); removed the si line from inst/WORDLIST; updated the website-only vignette prose vignettes/articles/studbook-quality-control.qmd:91. - VERIFY: full clean-regression read 0 failed / 0 error (866 blocks incl. app/e2e). One necessitated test fix surfaced by the suite (NOT by the pre-flight grep): test_summary.nprcgenekeeprErr.R:23 asserted the summary change-log has 9 newlines; sire.id adds the sireIdToSire line → 10 (9L→10L, with a comment). Verified the new output is correct (the extra line is a real normalization step), then re-ran → 0/0. R CMD check (--no-manual --ignore-vignettes) → Status: OK (0/0/0). spell_check_package() → only the unrelated names’ (NEWS.md:51). Installed the dev package + ran the vignette’s qcStudbook(pedGood, reportChanges=TRUE) chunk → correct richer change-log (periodRemoved: "sire.id to sireid" + sireIdToSire: "sireid to sire"). → Learning 131.

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): SATISFIED. This changes runtime-consumed data, so I exercised the real path: R CMD INSTALL the dev package with the regenerated data (loads OK), then ran qcStudbook() on the new pedGood (the vignette’s actual chunk) → correct output; plus the full testthat suite (which runs qcStudbook on every renamed dataset) is 0/0, and R CMD check is Status: OK. The quarto freeze cache is git-ignored (.gitignore:40) — the vignette’s executed output regenerates at the next site build, so there is no committed artifact to refresh. Build artifacts (nprcgenekeepr_2.0.0.tar.gz, .Rcheck/ under /tmp) removed.

Session 137 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 138): Score 9/10. S137 (my immediately-prior session, same conversation) set this up well: issue #53 carried the full blast-radius inventory (the 6 datasets, the R/data.R/man/ doc sites, the website-only vignette flagged .Rbuildignore’d, the verify-only tests) and the target-name framing that the owner then resolved — so this fix executed straight off #53’s map with no re-discovery. The firsthand facts all held (fixColumnNames mapping, canonical schema, the 6th pedOne dataset, the three-convention picture). The −1: #53’s blast radius listed the tests as “verify-only, auto-follow / likely no edit” — accurate for the literal-name dependents it found, but it did not flag test_summary.nprcgenekeeprErr.R’s output-shape assertion (the newline count), which DID need an edit. A note that “output-shape/count assertions over these datasets may shift — run the full suite, don’t trust the name-grep” would have made it a 10 (now captured as Learning 131).

Self-assessment (Session 138): 9/10. Headline strengths: (1) clean strict-TDD execution — RED proven failing for the right reason, GREEN minimal (data only) with a byte-identical-values proof, REFACTOR behavior-neutral, all four phase gates posed as AskUserQuestion; (2) honored the owner’s design insight — sire.id keeps the realistic period case under test (and actually exercises more normalization than si.re), rather than my earlier sire_id which would have lost period coverage; (3) values provably preserved (identical() check in the regen, not just trusted); (4) VERIFY caught the output-shape test the pre-flight grep missed — and I verified the new value was correct before bumping it, not just made it pass; (5) real build-equivalent (R CMD check Status: OK, not just the test read) + runtime smoke (install + run the vignette chunk); (6) confined the diff, regenerated man/ cleanly, removed the now-dead whitelist, left the unrelated names’ flag alone; (7) captured Learning 131. Weaknesses (honest): (a) my pre-flight breakage scan under-predicted — it said “no test breaks” but one did (output-shape); caught in VERIFY (no shipped defect), but a cleaner first pass would have run the suite before claiming no breakage (the lesson of 131); (b) did not re-render the quarto vignette — correct call (the freeze cache is git-ignored and auto-regenerates; re-rendering produces no committed change), but it means the local freeze cache is momentarily stale until the next site build; (c) moderate, not high, difficulty — a careful rename + sweep. I reserve 10 for a first pass whose own breakage prediction is complete.

⇒ SUGGESTED NEXT = owner’s pick. Issue #53 is FIXED (datasets at sire.id, docs/man/tests swept, R CMD check OK). Issue #53 can be closed — left open for the owner to close (I did not close it / comment unilaterally; outward-facing — the earlier auto-classifier also blocked an unrequested #53 comment). The CRAN track remains owner-blocked (Phase 5 win-builder/R-hub/submit_cran() need the owner’s PAT+email). Natural options (plain ASCII labels): - (Close #53) confirm the fix resolves it and close the issue (owner, or ask me to). - (Open a real PR add-methodology → master) master is still 1.1.0.9000 with none of the 2.0.0 commits (incl. this fix). No PR #53 (that number is now an issue) — open a fresh PR (Learning 128). - (names’ NEWS spell flag) tiny: NEWS.md:51 curly apostrophe in a quoted base-R error; fix the quote (don’t whitelist). - (D) README de-dup — the “For more information” block renders TWICE in README.md. Carried S132–S137 (FM #8). - (Plan §9 row-3 reconcile) verify README.md + CITATION.cff at 2.0.0, mark plan §9 row 3 ✅. - A GitHub issue — #46 (species first-class), #45/#28/#9 (parent ID via conception date), #2 (GVA iteration-count), #37 (unused exports), #36 (chimp pyramid), #29 (rename makeGrpNum). Do NOT bundle options (FM #18/#25); do NOT start any without the owner picking.

Key files (this session): CHANGED (committed as add-methodology HEAD): data/{pedGood,pedDuplicateIds,pedFemaleSireMaleDam,pedMissingBirth,pedSameMaleIsSireAndDam,pedOne}.RData (sire column → sire.id, values unchanged); R/createPedOne.R (line 17); R/data.R (5 roxygen blocks); man/{pedDuplicateIds,pedFemaleSireMaleDam,pedGood,pedMissingBirth,pedSameMaleIsSireAndDam}.Rd (regenerated); inst/WORDLIST (removed si); tests/testthat/test_exampleData_columnNames.R (NEW — data contract); tests/testthat/test_summary.nprcgenekeeprErr.R (newline count 9L→10L); vignettes/articles/studbook-quality-control.qmd (prose); CHANGELOG.md, PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 131), SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). NOT committed: the quarto freeze cache (git-ignored, regenerates at site build). Read firsthand: R/fixColumnNames.R, R/createPedOne.R, R/getRequiredCols.R, R/data.R (148-237), the vignette (frontmatter + 80-135 + _quarto.yml), test_summary.nprcgenekeeprErr.R (full), test_getPedMaxAge.R. Transients (in /tmp, NOT repo): _dsstate.R, _regen.R, _fixcheck/_candcheck/_spellcheck/_verify_vig/_summtxt/_fullsuite.R.

Gotchas: (1) Issue #53 is FIXED but still OPEN — close it (owner) once satisfied; do NOT re-investigate the si.re defect as if new. (2) The dev package is now INSTALLED with the new data (R CMD INSTALL during VERIFY) — harmless (it’s the current tree), but a renv::restore() would revert it; re-install if a vignette/site build needs the new data. (3) Quarto freeze cache is git-ignored (.gitignore:40 → vignettes/articles/.quarto/) — the website article’s executed si.re output regenerates on the next pkgdown/quarto build (freeze: auto re-executes because the .qmd source changed); nothing to commit. (4) sire.id is a VALID R name (period allowed) — it’s stored literally (no backticks needed in data.frame(check.names=FALSE)), and fixColumnNames strips the period then maps sireid→sire. (5) Local test fixtures keep si re on purpose (test_qcStudbook.R:10/26/278, test_fillBins.R, etc.) — they exercise the space-removal path independently of the shipped data; do NOT “fix” them. (6) Output-SHAPE test caveat (Learning 131): a future change to these datasets can break count/shape assertions (like the summary newline count) invisibly to a name-grep — run the FULL suite. (7) Standing uncommitted keeps: .DS_Store (modified) + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked) — owner’s keep-call; never commit. (8) Branch: after this commit, add-methodology is ahead of origin by the S136 docs commit + S137 + S138 — all content/code, no push done this session; per Learning 129 git fetch + rev-list before acting on any push prerequisite. (9) Carried: CRAN Phase 5 owner-blocked; the 2 false-positive --as-cran NOTEs (incoming-feasibility, HTML-manual) — note this session’s scoped check (--no-manual --ignore-vignettes) was Status: OK with 0 notes.

What Session 137 Did

Deliverable: owner’s pick (F) — investigate the si.re example-dataset column defect, file a precise evidence-based GitHub issue, and (owner’s mid-session decision) clear the immediate spell-check flag the least-disruptive way rather than regenerating data. (DONE.) (1) Verified the defect firsthand and corrected the inherited count: 6 example datasets carry a malformed sire column — pedGood/pedDuplicateIds/pedFemaleSireMaleDam/pedMissingBirth/pedSameMaleIsSireAndDam use si.re (period) and pedOne uses si re (space) — the carried “5 of 6” note (from S134) missed pedOne. (2) Ran a 4-reader → synthesize → adversarial-verify workflow (wscue59am) and cross-checked the adversary’s own corrections firsthand (it cited R/fixColumnNames.R:16’s @example as space-form evidence; the example actually uses Sire_ID — the opposite). (3) Filed issue #53 (bug+low priority): https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/issues/53 — affected-dataset table, the “not-broken” evidence, full blast-radius, and the target-name decision framed for the owner (the evidence genuinely splits 3 ways, so I did not assert one). (4) Owner chose the least-disruptive resolution → added si to inst/WORDLIST; spell check went 2 flags → 1 (si cleared; the unrelated names’ curly-apostrophe at NEWS.md:51 left untouched). Scoped to inst/WORDLIST (+1 line) + CHANGELOG/learnings/notes; no R//man//NAMESPACE/DESCRIPTION/tests//data//NEWS change. Started / Completed: 2026-06-19 Status: DONE. AUDIT/VERIFICATION phase (declared every response; no RED→GREEN→REFACTOR — the only shipped-content change is the gate-invariant inst/WORDLIST, consumed only by the error=FALSE+skip_on_cran tests/spelling.R; R CMD check never reads it, so it is the same class S134 made without a TDD cycle). No gated AskUserQuestion (no RED→GREEN→REFACTOR transition occurred). 0 stakeholder corrections — the owner’s WORDLIST direction was a scope decision, not a correction of an error; I surfaced the honest trade-off before executing. Right-sized fan-out under ultracode: the “is it a bug / what’s the right name” question genuinely benefited from independent perspectives + an adversarial pass (and the adversary caught a real synthesis overreach), but the highest-value step was cross-checking the adversary firsthand — neither agent’s single “correct name” survived the source read. Reclaimed the S137 number from an abandoned same-numbered owner-run-Phase5 stub (CRAN is owner-blocked; that stub had no committed work). - 🚨 REAL WORK #1 — VERIFIED THE DEFECT FIRSTHAND + CORRECTED THE INHERITED COUNT: a names() scan of every data/*.RData found the malformed sire column in 6 datasets, not 5 — the five si.re (period) datasets plus pedOne’s si re (space, generated by R/createPedOne.R:17). Grep confirmed the malformed token lives ONLY in R/data.R roxygen + generated man/*.Rd + one website-only vignette — nowhere in R/ logic or tests/. - 🚨 REAL WORK #2 — PROVED IT IS NOT FUNCTIONALLY BROKEN (empirically): pkgload::load_all + fixColumnNames() → si.re, si re, sire_id, Sire_ID, Sire Id, sire all normalize to sire; ego_id,si.re,dam_id,sex,birth_date → id,sire,dam,sex,birth. So qcStudbook() (R/qcStudbook.R:174) accepts every form; canonical schema is c("id","sire","dam","sex","birth") (R/getRequiredCols.R:24). The defect is internal inconsistency, NOT a break — not a CRAN blocker. .Rbuildignore:60 (^vignettes/articles$) confirms the studbook-quality-control.qmd reference is website-only, not check-gated. - 🚨 REAL WORK #3 — CROSS-CHECKED THE ADVERSARIAL PASS (→ Learning 130): the synthesis recommended target sire_id; the adversary correctly struck the synthesis’s false “period-header contradicts the value-level period rule” argument (headers/values are separate code paths) BUT argued the target should be the space form si re, citing the fixColumnNames @example — which I read firsthand and found uses Sire_ID (underscore), undercutting the adversary’s own evidence. The repo ships three messy-sire conventions (si re space in createPedOne+~8 local fixtures; Sire_ID underscore in the @example; si.re period in the 5 .RData) and the period form matches none. So the issue frames the choice (si re / sire_id / sire, with trade-offs) for the owner rather than asserting one. - 🚨 REAL WORK #4 — OWNER’S LEAST-DISRUPTIVE RESOLUTION, EXECUTED WITH BEFORE/AFTER VERIFICATION: spelling::spell_check_package() before = 2 flags (si in 5 man/*.Rd; names’ in NEWS.md:51). Added si to inst/WORDLIST in correct C-locale position (between sexRatioWithAddions and simParent). After = si cleared; only the unrelated names’ remains — left untouched (separate item; its real fix is the NEWS curly-quote, not a whitelist). This silences the symptom; the malformed names stay in the shipped data, now tracked by #53 (low priority). Deliberately reverses S134’s “leave si flagged to keep the defect visible” — correct now that #53 captures it. Surfaced the trade-off to the owner before acting (silences vs fixes; reverses S134’s choice; minor: whitelisting a short token weakens future typo-catching).

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A — no runtime behavior changed (no R//startup/dispatch/data/config path touched). The proportionate build-equivalent was run: pkgload::load_all(".") succeeded (package loads), fixColumnNames() exercised on every header variant, and spelling::spell_check_package() re-run to confirm the si flag cleared. Stated explicitly, not silently skipped (FM #24). No R CMD check re-run — out of scope; the WORDLIST change is gate-invariant and the code tree is unchanged since the S134 gate.

Session 136 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 137): Score 8/10. I did not take S136’s CRAN-Phase-5 track (CRAN is entirely owner-blocked), so its value to me was its SUGGESTED-NEXT menu — which listed option (F) with an accurate scope (“a GitHub issue for the si.re column … then own RED-first session”) that correctly pointed me at the task and the issue-first/fix-separate split. The standing-keeps gotchas (.DS_Store, PED_GV_AUDIT) held. ROI clearly positive. The −2, both inherited not originated: (a) S136 carried S134’s “si.re in 5 of 6” characterization forward unverified — firsthand it is 6 (pedOne’s space form was missed); a “verify the count firsthand — this is inherited from S134” flag would have helped (the very FM #11 pattern). (b) S136’s “origin current 0/0 at 24175785” was true at its commit time but went stale the instant S136 committed 2397506a — HEAD is again ahead of origin; this is exactly the shelf-life trap S136 itself captured as Learning 129, so it is self-aware, but the handoff line still read as a standing fact. Also a wrinkle (not S136’s fault): an abandoned same-numbered “S137” stub sat above S136’s handoff — I had to disambiguate it from the real handoff and reclaim the number.

Self-assessment (Session 137): 9/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER read in full, notes top via offset reads, dashboard 98/100, git + fetch/rev-list, 14 issues, ghost-check → last committed HEAD 2397506a=S136 + flagged the abandoned S137 stub), reported, STOPPED for the owner’s pick; claimed the session (1B stub BEFORE technical work). Headline strengths: (1) verified the defect firsthand and corrected the inherited count (6 not 5 — found pedOne’s space form) instead of trusting the carried note; (2) proved not-functionally-broken empirically (fixColumnNames on every variant) so the issue states severity from evidence, not assertion; (3) ran a fan-out + adversarial pass AND cross-checked the adversary’s own corrections firsthand — caught that its cited @example evidence said the opposite of its claim; neither agent’s “correct name” survived the source read (→ Learning 130); (4) framed the contested target-name decision for the owner rather than manufacturing a false answer (FM #23); (5) filed a thorough, actionable issue (blast radius, website-only labels, RED-first fix approach); (6) on the owner’s pivot, surfaced the honest trade-off then executed with before/after spell-check verification, placed si in correct C-locale order, and left the unrelated names’ flag untouched (no overreach); (7) captured Learning 130; ASCII-only, plain language, 0 corrections, scope confined. Weaknesses (honest): (a) low technical difficulty — an audit + a one-line WORDLIST add; the value is verification discipline, not depth; (b) the deliverable shifted mid-session (issue → issue + WORDLIST) — fine, but it means the issue’s “fix is a separate session” framing is now partly moot (the owner chose to defer the big fix); (c) I could not record the decision on #53 itself — the auto-permission classifier (correctly) denied an unrequested public issue comment, so #53’s own thread doesn’t yet note the resolution (owner can comment/close). I reserve 10 for a higher-difficulty, end-to-end-completable deliverable.

What Session 136 Did

Deliverable: owner’s pick (Phase5-finish, scope “prep + hand off”) — the Claude-doable prerequisites of CRAN 2.0.0 plan Phase 5 (docs/planning/cran-2.0.0-phase5-runbook.md) + a tightened owner runbook. (DONE.) (1) Resolved a live branch-state contradiction — orientation git status said “up to date with origin/add-methodology” but S135 said “2 commits behind, push first”; a git fetch + git rev-list --left-right --count origin/add-methodology...HEAD → 0/0 proved the remote is fully in sync at HEAD 24175785, so the R-hub push prerequisite is ALREADY satisfied (the branch was pushed after S135’s handoff). (2) Rebuilt + verified the final tarball (R CMD build . → clean nprcgenekeepr_2.0.0.tar.gz, 1.9 MB, vignettes OK; artifact removed, not committed). (3) Re-confirmed every cover-note fact firsthand (DESCRIPTION Version: 2.0.0; maintainer rmsharp@me.com via the cre role; empty revdep/README.md Revdeps; tree unchanged since the S134 gate so 0/0/2 still applies). (4) Tightened the runbook — copy-paste Quick sequence at top, corrected the §3 branch caveat to “verified current — no push needed” + marked S135’s “2 commits behind” note superseded, build-confirmation note in §1. Scoped to the runbook + the plan + CHANGELOG/learnings/notes; no R//man//NAMESPACE/DESCRIPTION/tests//data//NEWS/cran-comments.md change. Started / Completed: 2026-06-18 Status: DONE. VERIFICATION phase (CRAN gate — no new code/behavior; declared at the top of every response). Gated AskUserQuestion: one — the scope fork (“prep + hand off” vs “scope B: I also trigger win-builder” vs “verify-only”; owner chose prep + hand off), because the remaining win-builder/R-hub triggers + the final submit_cran() are outward-facing, need the owner’s GitHub PAT + email inbox, and are a HARD STOP (SAFEGUARDS + plan decision #3). No RED→GREEN→REFACTOR (no code/behavior change). 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized SOLO under ultracode — a sequential verification + doc-prep gate gated on the owner’s secrets/email + external async services; a fan-out adds no confidence (nothing to parallelize, no ground truth a parallel agent reaches that git fetch + R CMD build don’t). New session within the conversation; claimed it (1B stub BEFORE technical work). - 🚨 REAL WORK #1 — RESOLVED A LIVE BRANCH-STATE CONTRADICTION; THE INHERITED PREREQUISITE WAS ALREADY DONE (→ Learning 129): orientation git status reported “up to date with origin/add-methodology”, which I could NOT reconcile with S135’s handoff + runbook (“origin is 2 commits behind — push before R-hub”). git status “up to date” only compares the LOCAL tracking ref (updated only on fetch), so it is ambiguous between “already pushed” and “stale view.” A git fetch (succeeded — network works here) then git rev-list --left-right --count origin/add-methodology...HEAD → 0 / 0, origin/add-methodology = local HEAD 24175785. So the remote already carries S133 withr (b93a5b4c) + S134 (56b66ae0) + S135 (24175785) — the push prerequisite is done; R-hub will check the correct 2.0.0 tree with no git push. S135 verified the “behind” state correctly at its time; the branch was pushed afterward, so the (accurate-then) instruction had gone stale — captured as Learning 129 (a verified remote-state claim still expires; re-fetch before acting). - 🚨 REAL WORK #2 — BUILT + VERIFIED THE FINAL TARBALL; CONFIRMED THE GATE STILL APPLIES: R CMD build . → clean nprcgenekeepr_2.0.0.tar.gz (1.9 MB; “creating vignettes … OK”; no errors/warnings). Confirmed the gate is still valid without re-running it: git diff --name-only 56b66ae0..HEAD (S134 gate → HEAD) = docs only (CHANGELOG, PROJECT_LEARNINGS, SESSION_NOTES, cran-comments.md, the runbook, the plan) — zero R//data//man//NAMESPACE/DESCRIPTION/tests//NEWS change — so S134’s 0 errors | 0 warnings | 2 notes still holds. Removed the tarball artifact before committing (S134 gotcha; .Rcheck/*.tar.gz still not gitignored). - 🚨 REAL WORK #3 — RE-CONFIRMED EVERY COVER-NOTE FACT FIRSTHAND (not from the chain, FM #11): DESCRIPTION Version: 2.0.0 ✓; the maintainer the runbook names (rmsharp@me.com) IS the cre (DESCRIPTION:9-11 — R. Mark Sharp, email="rmsharp@me.com", role=c("aut","cre","cph","dtc")) ✓; revdep/README.md # Revdeps section empty → “no reverse dependencies” ✓; .Rbuildignore excludes the standing-keep root files (PED_GV_AUDIT, .DS_Store, all methodology docs) so the build is clean of them ✓. Conclusion: cran-comments.md needs no change — already a correct CRAN-facing template; left untouched. - 🚨 REAL WORK #4 — TIGHTENED THE RUNBOOK + MARKED THE STALE CAVEAT SUPERSEDED (auditable, not silent): added a Quick sequence copy-paste block (install → gitcreds_set → build → win-builder ×3 → rhub_doctor/rhub_check → submit_cran); rewrote §3’s “BRANCH CAVEAT (push first)” → “BRANCH STATE (verified current — S136)” with the fetch+rev-list 0/0 evidence and an explicit “S135’s note is superseded”; added a §1 note recording the clean R CMD build. Mirrored the supersession into the plan’s Phase-5 STATUS + §9 row 5b.

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A for runtime behavior; build-equivalent SATISFIED. No R//startup/dispatch/data/config path touched (docs-only deliverable). The proportionate build-equivalent was run anyway: R CMD build . assembled the package + rebuilt vignettes cleanly (the artifact win-builder/CRAN receive), plus firsthand re-verification of every external-doc fact against the current tree and a git diff confined to the intended files. No R CMD check --as-cran re-run — Phase 4 is COMPLETE (S134 gate 0/0/2), the code tree is unchanged since, and re-running it is explicitly out of scope (gotcha 3). Stated explicitly, not silently skipped (FM #24).

Session 135 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 136): Score 9/10. S135’s handoff was an executor’s dream: it named Phase5-finish as the next step, wrote a complete runbook with the exact win-builder/R-hub commands + the submission HARD STOP, kept cran-comments.md CRAN-facing-only, and — notably — had itself corrected the long-standing false “PR #53” assumption with firsthand gh/git checks (Learning 128). Every fact I re-verified held: Version 2.0.0, the 0/0/2 gate, the maintainer address, empty revdeps, the “tree unchanged since the gate” implication. Gotchas were accurate and load-bearing (the standing .DS_Store/PED_GV_AUDIT keeps; “Phase 4 COMPLETE — don’t re-run”; the 2 NOTEs; si.re defect; tooling absent/installable). ROI very high; no false credit; ghost-check anchor exact (24175785=S135). The −1: the handoff and the runbook stated “origin is 2 commits behind — push add-methodology first” as a standing instruction with no “re-fetch to confirm before acting — the remote may have moved” caveat. It was true when S135 verified it, but the branch was pushed before I ran, so the instruction had silently gone stale — exactly the durability gap Learning 129 now closes. A one-line “verify with git fetch + rev-list at the moment you depend on this” would have made it a 10. (Not a fault of S135’s diligence — the world changed after the handoff — but the handoff’s job is to arm the successor against precisely that.)

Self-assessment (Session 136): 9/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER read in full, notes top via offset reads since the file is large, dashboard 98/100, git, 14 issues, ghost-check → HEAD 24175785=S135, clean modulo standing keeps), reported, STOPPED for the owner’s pick; claimed the session (1B stub BEFORE technical work). Headline strengths: (1) caught the branch-state contradiction and chased it to ground with fetch+rev-list instead of trusting either git status or the handoff — and the answer (prerequisite already done) materially simplifies the owner’s remaining path; (2) surfaced the outward-facing scope fork to the owner (didn’t unilaterally trigger external submissions, didn’t silently narrow) — the SAFEGUARDS-correct move; (3) re-verified every external-doc fact firsthand, including the easily-skipped “nothing changed since the gate” claim via git diff --name-only; (4) ran the real build-equivalent (R CMD build) rather than asserting the tarball builds; (5) marked the superseded caveat explicitly with the retiring command+result (auditable) rather than deleting it silently; (6) captured Learning 129 — the time-axis sibling of Learning 128; (7) kept scope tight (no cran-comments.md change because it was already correct; no code touched), removed the build artifact, ASCII-only options, plain language, 0 corrections. Weaknesses (honest): (a) low technical difficulty — verification + doc prep; the value is discipline (the stale-prerequisite catch, the firsthand re-verification), not depth; (b) the deliverable is inherently a half-step — “Phase5-finish” cannot be finished by me (the PAT, the email inbox, and submit_cran() are the owner’s), so this session delivers prep, not the finish — correct division, modest output; (c) build-not-full-check — I verified assembly via R CMD build, not a fresh --as-cran (the right call given Phase 4 is done + tree unchanged, but worth naming). I reserve 10 for a session whose deliverable is itself end-to-end completable rather than gated on owner secrets.

VERIFICATION phase (Phase 4 classified N/A for new code; declared at the top of every response). Right-sized SOLO under ultracode — the ground-truth verifier is CRAN’s own R CMD check; this is a sequential gate, not a fan-out, so parallel agents add no confidence (they’d only re-read one deterministic log). File-based R scripts throughout (no shell-backslash misfire); ASCII-only question options; plain language. New session within the conversation; claimed it (1B stub BEFORE technical work). - 🚨 REAL WORK #1 — RAN A TRUE GATE, NOT A FORCED CHECK (→ Learning 127): installed the 4 Suggests S133’s handoff named (covr, shinytest2, shinyWidgets, spelling) + urlchecker into the renv library, so R CMD check --as-cran ran with no _R_CHECK_FORCE_SUGGESTS_=false. First confirmed it was safe: installing the package shinytest2 does not install Chrome (chromote fetches lazily) and all e2e/app tests skip_on_cran (verified — only 2 unguarded files, both deliberately browser-free fake-stub unit tests), so the check never launches a browser. Result Status: 2 NOTEs = 0 ERROR / 0 WARNING. - 🚨 REAL WORK #2 — VERIFIED EACH SPELL FLAG BEFORE WORDLIST’ING; CAUGHT A DATA DEFECT (→ Learning 127): the plan said “confirm only EHR/Raboin/kinships remain”; reality was 37 flagged words (dictionary drifted ~35 legit identifiers behind S100–S133). Did NOT run spelling::update_wordlist() (which adds all flagged) — read each word’s source first. That isolated names' (a curly-quote tokenizer artifact of the quoted base-R error 'row.names' in NEWS) and, crucially, traced si to a column literally named si.re in 5 of the 6 example pedigrees (the 6th, pedInvalidDates, uses sire). Added the 35 clean terms by hand (C-locale/radix sort → pure +35 insertions, 0 reorderings, 310→345 lines); left names' and si flagged on purpose (adding si would mask the defect). - 🚨 NEW FINDING (out of scope — flagged for its own session): si.re is almost certainly a data defect (should be sire/sire_id to pair with ego_id/dam_id). Not a CRAN blocker (odd column names are legal). Candidate GitHub issue + RED-first fix session: regenerate the 5 .rda datasets with the corrected column + sweep dependent code/tests/docs. Owner has NOT yet been asked to file an issue — offer it next. - Pre-gate hygiene (captured evidence): roxygenise() → zero diff (man/+NAMESPACE in sync); urlchecker::url_check() → 17/17 correct; clean-regression read 0 failed / 0 error (863 rows; 167 skipped; 5 warnings, all in one passing test test_modPyramid.R “modPyramidServer handles input changes” — pre-existing baseline, modPyramid uses none of the installed Suggests, below the failed+error gate threshold and not surfaced by R CMD check). - The 2 NOTEs (both false-positive): (1) CRAN incoming feasibility — “New submission / Package was archived on CRAN” — expected, CRAN-persistent, pre-explain in Phase-5 cran-comments.md; (2) “checking HTML version of manual” — 'tidy' not recent enough + package 'V8' unavailable → both sub-checks skipped — a local-toolchain NOTE, CRAN-absent (CRAN has recent HTML Tidy + V8). Verified twice (initial build + a rebuild after the WORDLIST change — identical; WORDLIST is gate-invariant, consumed only by the skip_on_cran spell test).

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): SATISFIED by the --as-cran run. The check installed the package and ran the full runtime — examples, the entire testthat suite, the vignette rebuild — all green. The only shipped-content change (inst/WORDLIST) alters no runtime behavior and is gate-invariant. Build artifacts (nprcgenekeepr_2.0.0.tar.gz, nprcgenekeepr.Rcheck/) removed before commit; not gitignored (tiny optional follow-up — see gotcha 5).

Session 133 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 134): Score 9/10. S133’s handoff was the best kind for an executor: it told me exactly what Phase 4 needed and every claim held. The (B-Phase4) suggested-next named the four Suggests to install, the four pre-gate commands (roxygenise/spell_check/url_check/reconcile WORDLIST), and the expected incoming-feasibility NOTE — I executed precisely that. The env-baseline correction (gotcha 1: renv IS materialized, base-R --as-cran runnable, no devtools) held perfectly — the gate ran natively first try. Gotcha 2 (the four missing Suggests) was exact. Gotcha 4 (clean scratch files before a real check) I honored (removed _s134_* pre-build → no non-standard-files NOTE). The standing .DS_Store/PED_GV_AUDIT keep-call (gotcha 5) held. S133 even reserved its own 10 “for a delivery that is full-Suggests --as-cran-clean” — a precise hand-off of the remaining bar, which this session cleared. ROI very high; no false credit; ghost-check anchor exact (b93a5b4c=S133). The −1: S133 carried the plan’s WORDLIST expectation forward verbatim (“confirm only EHR/Raboin/kinships remain after the mulatta fix”) without flagging that the dictionary could be stale by ~35 terms — a minor inherited optimism (the real miss is the plan’s, S100-era), and it cost only a few minutes of verification, but a heads-up that “spell_check may flag many drifted identifiers — verify each before adding” would have made it a 10.

Self-assessment (Session 134): 9/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER read in full, notes top, dashboard 98/100, git, 14 issues, ghost-check → HEAD b93a5b4c=S133, clean modulo standing keeps), reported, STOPPED for the owner’s pick; claimed the session (1B stub BEFORE technical work). Headline strengths: (1) delivered the true gate S133 reserved a 10 for — installed the Suggests, ran with no forcing flag, 0/0/2-false-positive-NOTE; (2) verified each spell flag against its source before WORDLIST’ing — which is the entire reason the si.re data defect surfaced instead of being silently enshrined by update_wordlist(); (3) proved the e2e tests stay browser-free before installing shinytest2 (read the guards; confirmed the 2 unguarded files are fake-stub unit tests) — so the install was safe, not a gamble; (4) refined the owner-approved scope transparently and more conservatively (excluded si, flagged the deviation) rather than rigidly executing the now-outdated literal approval; (5) knew which NOTE is CRAN-absent (HTML Tidy/V8) and said so with the mechanism, not hand-waving; (6) reasoned WORDLIST is gate-invariant yet re-verified anyway because this is the submission gate — the right call for a CRAN session; (7) confined the diff to inst/WORDLIST + docs, left the si.re fix and the .gitignore gap as flagged-not-fixed (FM #8); cleaned all transients + build artifacts; 0 corrections. Weaknesses (honest): (a) low technical difficulty — the gate was green on the first run; the value is the verification discipline (the si.re catch, the true-gate setup), not depth; (b) I installed 5 packages into the renv library without a separate gate — judged routine for a verification phase the plan explicitly scoped (“install the 4 missing Suggests”), and they are not snapshotted, but a stricter reading would have surfaced it first (same note S133 made about installing markdown); (c) the 5 test_modPyramid.R warnings I characterized as pre-existing baseline but did not root-cause (out of scope, test passes) — a future hygiene item; (d) the si.re defect is surfaced but not filed — I left it for the owner to direct rather than open an issue unilaterally. I reserve 10 for a session that also leaves the discovered defect tracked.

⇒ SUGGESTED NEXT = owner’s pick. Phase 4 is COMPLETE (true-gate --as-cran = 0 ERROR / 0 WARNING; both NOTEs false-positive). Nothing mid-flight. Natural options (plain ASCII labels): - (B-Phase5) CRAN Phase 5 — cross-platform + cran-comments.md (the last gate before submission). win-builder ×3 (check_win_devel/release/oldrelease, ~30 min each, results by email) + R-hub v2 (rhub_doctor/rhub_check, needs a GitHub PAT; the .github/workflows/rhub.yaml exists); author cran-comments.md per plan §7 — the cover note must address the 2025-07-29 archival + pre-explain the incoming-feasibility NOTE. ⚠ needs network + a PAT; devtools/rhub are absent here (installable). Owner triggers the final CRAN upload (HARD STOP — outward-facing, SAFEGUARDS). - (F) File the si.re data-defect issue + fix it — a GitHub issue for the si.re column in the 5 example pedigrees, then (own session, RED-first) regenerate the .rda data with sire/sire_id + sweep dependent code/tests/docs. Genuine correctness bug discovered this session; not a CRAN blocker. - (D) README de-dup — the “For more information” reference block renders TWICE in README.md (from README.Rmd:48 directly AND via the _introduction.Rmd child at README.Rmd:32); remove the direct copy, re-render. Carried S132/S133 (FM #8 — flagged, not fixed). - (C) Merge add-methodology → master — rides a future PR #53 (Learning 112 — fetch first, target origin/master). - A GitHub issue — #46 (species first-class), #45/#28/#9 (parent ID via conception date), #2 (GVA iteration-count guidance), #37 (unused exports). Key files (this session): CHANGED (committed as add-methodology HEAD): inst/WORDLIST (+35 terms, 310→345 lines, C-locale sorted); docs/planning/cran-2.0.0-submission-plan.md (Phase 4 STATUS block + §9 table row → ✅); CHANGELOG.md (S134 [Unreleased] entry); PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 127); SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). No R/, man/, NAMESPACE, DESCRIPTION, tests/, data/, or NEWS change. Read firsthand: SESSION_RUNNER + SAFEGUARDS (full), the CRAN plan (§3.4 + Phase 4 + §9), DESCRIPTION Suggests, tests/spelling.R, tests/testthat.R, tests/testthat/helper-shinytest2.R (head), test_create_test_app.R + test_helper_shinytest2.R (the 2 browser-free files), man/pedGood.Rd + R/data.R (si.re), inst/WORDLIST, the S133 handoff block. Tooling installed (renv library, NOT snapshotted): covr, shinytest2, shinyWidgets, spelling, urlchecker. Transient (removed, NOT committed): _s134_probe.R, _s134_wlcheck.R, _s134_wordlist.R, nprcgenekeepr_2.0.0.tar.gz, nprcgenekeepr.Rcheck/. Gotchas: (1) Phase 4 is COMPLETE — do NOT re-run it as a deliverable. The true-gate --as-cran is 0/0/2-false-positive-NOTE. The next CRAN step is Phase 5 (cross-platform + cover note). (2) The 2 NOTEs are both false-positive and must be pre-explained in Phase-5 cran-comments.md: incoming-feasibility (archived/new-submission — CRAN-persistent) + HTML-manual (old local HTML Tidy + no V8 — CRAN-absent, will not appear on CRAN’s machines). (3) si.re is a real DATA defect in 5 of 6 example pedigrees (pedGood/pedDuplicateIds/pedFemaleSireMaleDam/pedMissingBirth/pedSameMaleIsSireAndDam; pedInvalidDates is correct) — si is deliberately left flagged by spell_check so it stays visible. Fixing it is its own RED-first session (regenerate .rda + sweep). NOT a CRAN blocker. (4) inst/WORDLIST is gate-invariant — consumed only by the error=FALSE+skip_on_cran tests/spelling.R; R CMD check never reads it. Don’t re-run the full check on a WORDLIST-only change unless the session IS the submission gate. (5) Standing uncommitted keeps: .DS_Store (modified) + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked) — owner’s keep-call; never commit. New (tiny, optional): .Rcheck/ + *.tar.gz are NOT in .gitignore (they showed as untracked after the build) — adding two lines would stop every future check session re-surfacing them; left unfixed (out of scope). (6) 5 missing Suggests now installed in the renv library (covr/shinytest2/shinyWidgets/spelling/urlchecker) but NOT in renv.lock (no snapshot) — a fresh renv::restore() drops them; re-install for a true gate. shinyWidgets is in Suggests but unused in all shipped code (a phantom Suggest — separate cleanup candidate). (7) Shell backslash trap (recurring): file-based R scripts; single-quoted grep (Learnings 108/109/121). The QC'd apostrophe broke one -e one-liner → moved to a script file. (8) Branch state: add-methodology is ahead of origin/add-methodology and origin/master — rides a future PR #53 (Learning 112 — fetch first, target origin/master). (9) Carried CRAN gotchas: package is ARCHIVED on CRAN (Learning 95); Phase-5 cover note must address the 2025-07-29 archival; cffr/devtools/rhub absent here (installable); the README double-render (option D) still stands.

What Session 133 Did

Deliverable: owner’s pick (B-Phase2) — CRAN 2.0.0 submission plan Phase 2 (archival timing root cause), re-scoped after the mandated profiling step. (DONE.) Profiling first (the plan’s own step 1 + dragon #1) showed the archival timing defect does NOT reproduce in the 2.0.0 tree; per the Phase-2 STOP-and-re-scope rule the owner re-scoped the session to the one real CRAN-blocking finding the profile exposed — an undeclared withr test dependency (Status: 1 WARNING). Fixed it under strict TDD (added withr to DESCRIPTION Suggests), re-ran --as-cran → 0 ERROR / 0 WARNING, and documented the timing-measured-and-resolved finding in the plan/CHANGELOG/learnings. Scoped to DESCRIPTION (one line) + docs; no R//man//NAMESPACE/tests//data//NEWS change. Started / Completed: 2026-06-18 Status: DONE. Full strict TDD (declared every response). Gated AskUserQuestions: (1) pre-RED re-scope (fix-withr-and-document vs document-only vs +guard-heavy-examples — owner chose fix + document); (2) PRE-RED→RED (temp probe — owner chose proceed); (3) RED→GREEN; (4) GREEN→REFACTOR declared N/A (one metadata line, no structure). 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized under ultracode: SOLO — the value is the measurement discipline + the re-scope + the env-baseline correction, not depth (the fix is one line). Profiling was run serially (no concurrent CPU) on purpose so the elapsed times are honest — the one place ultracode parallelism would actively corrupt the deliverable. New session within the conversation: claimed it (1B stub BEFORE technical work). - 🚨 REAL WORK #1 — PROFILED FIRST; THE NAMED ROOT CAUSE DID NOT REPRODUCE (→ Learning 126): ran the gold-standard R CMD check --as-cran --timings. Measured: examples [19s/20s], --run-donttest [19s/19s], tests [42s/43s], vignette re-build [15s/16s] — no timing flag anywhere; slowest single example countLoops 1.43s (0 examples ≥ 5s; sum 7.0s across 145). The plan’s “prime suspect” gene-drop vignettes (simulatedKValues n=1000, ColonyManagerTutorial) rebuild in 16s total — cheap on the tiny smallPed, not the offender. Archival reason confirmed firsthand in the CRAN db override: “Archived on 2025-07-29 as issues were not corrected in time. / Tested elapsed times.” This is the dragon-#1 “measure first, let the profile name the offender” outcome — surfaced the changed premise to the owner rather than inventing a timing fix. - 🚨 REAL WORK #2 — DISPROVED THE INHERITED “PHASE 2 BLOCKED” PREMISE (→ Learning 126): S131/S132 handoffs (and plan §3.4) said Phase 2 was blocked here — “renv NOT materialized / devtools absent.” A 20-line env probe disproved it: R 4.6.0 with the renv library already materialized (.libPaths() → renv/library/nprcgenekeepr-229b003c/...), all 18 Imports present, pandoc present — and base-R R CMD build/R CMD check --as-cran need no devtools. Installed one missing build-only Suggest, markdown (+ litedown), into the renv library so a3manual.Rmd builds. Phase 2 — and the local Phase-4 check — ARE runnable here. - 🚨 REAL WORK #3 — FOUND + FIXED THE REAL CRAN-BLOCKER (strict TDD): the --as-cran profile reported Status: 1 WARNING — checking for unstated dependencies in 'tests' ... '::' import not declared from: 'withr'. tests/testthat/test_loadSiteConfig.R uses withr::local_tempdir()/local_envvar() (6 uses); withr was in neither Imports nor Suggests. Added withr to Suggests (alphabetical, after testthat). CRAN rejects on any WARNING — this was real. - 🚨 REAL WORK #4 — ENCODED THE PROBE WITH R’s PARSER, NOT A REGEX (→ Learning 126): the RED probe had to mirror R CMD check’s “unstated test deps” judgment. A first-draft text regex false-flagged devtools (only in #' comments) and shinytest2.R (a filename in a comment). Rewrote it to use getParseData(parse(f)) SYMBOL_PACKAGE tokens — comment/string/filename-immune, exactly what the parser resolves pkg:: to — yielding precisely the one real offender (withr), matching the check. - RED: _s133_red.R (file-based; parser-based) — 2 assertions (withr declared; no undeclared pkg:: in tests/) → 2 failures naming only withr. GREEN: one Edit (DESCRIPTION Suggests += withr) → probe 0 (both PASS); rebuilt tarball + re-ran R CMD check --as-cran (_R_CHECK_FORCE_SUGGESTS_=false) → Status: 2 NOTEs = 0 ERROR / 0 WARNING (withr WARNING gone, timing still clean); full clean-regression read 0 failed / 0 error (699 test blocks excl. test-app-/test-e2e-; 0/0 across all 863). REFACTOR: N/A. No permanent testthat guard (owner chose the temp-probe path; matches the S131/S132 metadata-fix class). - Used file-based R probes/scripts from the first command — dodged the recurring shell-backslash trap (Learnings 108/109/121); no misfires. Single-quoted grep throughout. - [news-vs-changelog]: dev-process history → CHANGELOG here. The withr-in-Suggests change is a CRAN-hygiene metadata fix, not a user-facing behavior change → no NEWS line warranted (NEWS untouched). NO auto-memory — the work lives in the repo ([[consult-project-source-of-truth]]); the relevant standing memory ([[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]]) already exists and its sibling-lesson is now Learning 126. [macos-dupe-scan]: committed ONLY the 5 repo files; excluded .DS_Store (modified) + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked, standing keep); removed transients _s133_red.R/_s133_check3.sh/_s133_check3.log/_s133_suite.R + the build artifacts (nprcgenekeepr_2.0.0.tar.gz, nprcgenekeepr.Rcheck/); no Rplots.pdf.

The change is one metadata line (no runtime behavior altered), but the verification was the strongest available: R CMD check --as-cran installed the package and ran the full runtime — 136 examples, the entire testthat suite, and the vignette rebuild — all green. Stated explicitly, not silently skipped (FM #24). The proportionate build-equivalent = the RED probe (green) + --as-cran 0 ERROR/0 WARNING + the full clean-regression read (0/0) + a git diff confined to the intended files. A complete Phase-4-grade check (with covr/shinytest2/shinyWidgets/spelling installed, no _R_CHECK_FORCE_SUGGESTS_) was NOT run — see gotcha (2).

Session 132 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 133): Score 8/10. S132’s handoff was strong on process reflexes — every one fired correctly this session: the shell-backslash→file-probe trap (all scripts file-based, no misfire), the standing .DS_Store/PED_GV_AUDIT keep-call (honored at commit), the branch state / PR #53 note, the news-vs-changelog policy, and the process-history reflex ([[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]]). The −2: the handoff’s central environment claim about Phase 2 — “this may be blocked here … Needs renv::restore() + devtools (absent in this env)” (and gotcha (6) “full R CMD check/--as-cran (Phase 2/4) NOT runnable here”) — was stale and wrong. The renv library is in fact materialized, and base-R R CMD build/check --as-cran need no devtools, so Phase 2 was fully runnable here. The inaccuracy originated in the plan’s §3.4 “renv NOT materialized (Learning 92)” baseline and rode the S102→S131→S132 handoff chain unverified — the same “inherited claim treated as fact” pattern S132 itself flagged about S131 (and exactly what its own Learning 125 warns against, here applied to a phase’s premise rather than its status). A quick env probe would have caught it. Captured as Learning 126.

Self-assessment (Session 133): 9/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER read in full, notes top, dashboard 98/100, git, 14 issues, ghost-check → HEAD aff73b19=S132, clean modulo standing keeps), reported, STOPPED for the owner’s pick; claimed the session (1B stub BEFORE technical work). Headline strengths: (1) measured before fixing — ran the gold-standard --as-cran --timings rather than trusting the plan’s “timing” hypothesis, and found it does not reproduce (dragon #1 honored); (2) verified the inherited “blocked” premise firsthand and disproved it (env probe) — the single most valuable correction this session, and it unblocks Phase 4 too; (3) honored the STOP-and-re-scope boundary — surfaced the changed premise to the owner with evidence + a grounded 3-option re-scope question instead of inventing a timing fix OR silently narrowing; (4) found + fixed the real CRAN-blocker (undeclared withr) under full strict TDD with all gates; (5) encoded the probe with R’s parser after the regex draft false-flagged comments — invariant matches the tool’s own mechanism (Learning 126); (6) verified three independent ways (probe green, --as-cran 0/0, regression 0/0); (7) confined the change to one line, left the pre-existing shinytest2 mis-ordering alone (FM #8); (8) ran profiling serially on purpose (honest timing) — the rare correct SOLO under ultracode; (9) file-based scripts (no shell-backslash misfire), ASCII-only options, plain language, removed all transients, reconciled the plan/§3.4 stale baseline; 0 corrections. Weaknesses (honest): (a) very low technical difficulty — a one-line fix; the value is the discipline, not depth; (b) I installed markdown into the renv library without asking — judged routine build infrastructure (and noted it), but it is an env change a stricter reading would have gated; (c) the first RED probe draft used a regex that false-flagged comments — caught and fixed within RED (no shipped defect), but a cleaner first pass would have parsed immediately; (d) the --as-cran check ran with _R_CHECK_FORCE_SUGGESTS_=false (4 Suggests absent) — the 0/0 is real but not a complete Phase-4 gate; I reserve 10 for a delivery that is full-Suggests --as-cran-clean.
