The owner picked “CRAN Phase 1 static hygiene”; orientation found Phase 1 was already executed by S102 (commit a3cf3623) — verified firsthand against the live tree (all §3.2/§3.3 items in place; ..Rcheck gone; LICENSE + LICENSE.md both 2017-2026). The only remaining §3.2 item is the optional DESCRIPTION DOI (plan marks <https:> acceptable). A tree-wide mulatto sweep showed S102 fixed the typo only in DESCRIPTION; it survived in four more tracked places — README.Rmd:48 (→ shipped README.md, twice), the shipped vignette child vignettes/manual_components/_introduction.Rmd:48 (built into a3manual.Rmd), CITATION.cff:16, and _pkgdown.yml:16 (website description: field; the last found by a close-out git ls-files grep after the dir-scoped RED probe missed it — owner-approved scope+1). Scoped to those 4 sources + the regenerated README.md + the plan + CHANGELOG; no R//man//NAMESPACE/DESCRIPTION/NEWS/data//test change. Started / Completed: 2026-06-18 Status: DONE. Full strict TDD (declared every response). Gated AskUserQuestions: (1) pre-RED scope (re-scope: typo-fix-tree-wide vs doc-reconcile-only vs typo-only — owner chose tree-wide); (2) PRE-RED→RED (temp probe vs permanent guard — owner chose proceed with temp probe); (3) RED→GREEN; (4) GREEN→REFACTOR declared N/A (prose content, no structure). 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized under ultracode: SOLO — a 5-line typo fix + a re-render + doc reconciliation; the value is the process-history catch (avoided re-doing a done phase), the downstream-sink completeness, and the tracked-scoped invariant probe, not depth. New session within the conversation: claimed it (1B stub BEFORE technical work). - 🚨 REAL WORK #1 — PROCESS-HISTORY CHECK CAUGHT A DONE PHASE BEING RE-PICKED (→ Learning 125): the plan said Phase 1 “open” and S131’s gotcha named DESCRIPTION:23 as still carrying the typo — both stale. git log --oneline -- .Rbuildignore DESCRIPTION → a3cf3623 … CRAN Phase 1 static hygiene (S102), and a read of CHANGELOG §“2026-06-17 CRAN Phase 1 (Session 102)” confirmed S102 did the entire §3.2/§3.3 set. Verified each item against the live tree (DESCRIPTION:22 'mulatta'; VignetteBuilder: knitr :79; Config at :81–83 after Package:; both man/appServer.Rd/man/appUI.Rd have \value; both LICENSE files 2017-2026; no ..Rcheck). Saved the session from re-doing settled work — memory [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]] in action. - 🚨 REAL WORK #2 — A SOURCE-ONLY FIX SURVIVED IN ITS DOWNSTREAM SINKS: S102 fixed the typo in DESCRIPTION (canonical) but the same species name lives in 5 sinks — a hand-maintained mirror (CITATION.cff abstract; cffr absent so it drifted), a generated file (README.md from README.Rmd), a child-include rendered TWICE into README.md (README.Rmd:48 direct + _introduction.Rmd via the child= at README.Rmd:32), that same child shipping into the a3manual.Rmd vignette, and a website-config mirror (_pkgdown.yml:16 description:, published to the pkgdown site). The plan’s own location list (“DESCRIPTION:23 + CITATION.cff:15”) missed the README, vignette, AND _pkgdown.yml sinks. The tree-wide sweep found README/vignette; the _pkgdown.yml sink was caught only by the close-out git ls-files | grep belt-and-suspenders pass (the RED probe’s dir-scoped sweep had missed it) — see the self-assessment weakness + Learning 125. - 🚨 REAL WORK #3 — SCOPED THE INVARIANT PROBE TO git-TRACKED FILES: the probe’s first tree-wide sweep flagged vignettes/a3manual.html|md — but those are untracked, git-ignored local render litter (stale Jan-20 builds), not committed content, absent from a clean CRAN tarball. Re-scoped the sweep to git ls-files so the invariant means “no mulatto in committed/shipped content”; the litter dropped out. Left the litter as-is (regenerates clean on the next vignette build). - RED: _s132_red.R (file-based; 8 per-file assertions on README.Rmd/README.md/_introduction.Rmd/CITATION.cff + 1 tracked tree-wide sweep) → 9 failures on the current tree, all for the right reason. GREEN: 4 Edits (README.Rmd:48, _introduction.Rmd:48, CITATION.cff:16, _pkgdown.yml:16) + pkgload::load_all + rmarkdown::render(github_document, html_preview=FALSE) for README.md → probe 0 (9/9); the _pkgdown.yml edit followed the close-out git ls-files | grep catch (owner-approved scope+1) and was confirmed by a final all-tracked-content sweep = 0 mulatto (only nprcgenekeepr_notes.txt:46, which describes the fix, remains — correctly left). REFACTOR: N/A. No permanent testthat test (prose; owner chose the temp-probe path in the PRE-RED→RED gate). - Used file-based R probes/render/suite scripts from the first command — dodged the recurring shell-backslash trap (Learnings 108/109/121); no misfires. Single-quoted grep throughout. - [news-vs-changelog]: dev-process history → CHANGELOG here. The typo fix touches shipped README/vignette content but is a spelling correction, not a user-facing feature/behavior change — fold into the CRAN Phase 3 NEWS only if a NEWS line is warranted (likely NOT; the DESCRIPTION half wasn’t NEWS’d either). NO auto-memory — the work lives in the repo ([[consult-project-source-of-truth]]); the relevant standing memory ([[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]]) already exists and fired correctly. [macos-dupe-scan]: committed ONLY the repo files; excluded .DS_Store (modified) + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked, standing keep); removed transients _s132_red.R/_s132_render.R/_s132_suite.R; no README.html/figure litter (html_preview=FALSE), no Rplots.pdf.

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A — content/documentation change, no runtime behavior altered (no R//startup/dispatch/data path touched; only prose in README/vignette/CITATION + the plan/CHANGELOG). Stated explicitly, not silently skipped (FM #24). The proportionate build-equivalent = the README re-render (SAFEGUARDS “Verify the Build Equivalent” for doc projects) + the invariant probe (9/9) + the full clean-regression read (0 failed / 0 error) + a git diff confined to the intended files. Full R CMD check not run (devtools absent).

Session 131 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 132): Score 8/10. S131’s handoff was excellent on what S131 did (the 2.0.0 bump) and on carried baselines: the shell-backslash→file-probe trap (used file-based scripts, no misfire), README-from-Rmd + html_preview=FALSE (re-rendered README cleanly), the standing .DS_Store/PED_GV_AUDIT keep-call, the branch state, and the tooling gaps (cffr/devtools absent → I hand-edited CITATION.cff exactly as it predicted). The ghost-check anchor was exact (e24a53a2=S131). ROI high; no false credit. The −2: the suggested-next option I picked, (B-Phase1), rested on a stale premise the handoff propagated without re-verifying — it described Phase 1 as a to-do list of items S102 had already completed ~16 sessions earlier, and gotcha (5) specifically named DESCRIPTION:23 as still carrying the mulatto typo when S102 had fixed it. The inaccuracy originated in the never-updated S101 plan and rode the whole S102→S131 handoff chain, so it is not uniquely S131’s fault — but a handoff that offers “resume the CRAN plan / do Phase 1” as the headline next-step owes a one-line “verify Phase 1 isn’t already done — S102 may have executed it” caveat. My process-history check (memory) caught it; without that memory the session would have re-done settled work. Captured as Learning 125.

Self-assessment (Session 132): 8/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER read in full, notes top, dashboard 98/100, git, 14 issues, ghost-check → HEAD e24a53a2=S131, clean modulo standing keeps), reported, STOPPED for the owner’s pick; claimed the session (1B stub BEFORE technical work). Headline strengths: (1) checked process history before executing — the single most valuable move this session; found Phase 1 was done by S102 and verified every item against the live tree rather than trusting the plan’s “open” status (Learning 125 + memory); (2) surfaced the changed premise to the owner with a grounded 3-option scope question instead of barreling into re-work OR silently narrowing — the genuine fork was the owner’s to decide; (3) found the downstream sinks the plan missed (README direct + child-include twice, the a3manual vignette, the CITATION mirror) via a tree-wide sweep, completing a typo fix that had looked “done” since S102; (4) scoped the invariant probe to git-tracked files after RED surfaced the untracked render litter — the probe now means “shipped content,” not “working-tree content”; (5) confined the diff — README.md changed in exactly the 2 typo lines, no date churn, no render litter (verified before/after git status); (6) reconciled the drift transparently — marked Phase 1 done in the plan + §9 table, and corrected S131’s false “Phase 1 open” line with a visible “(Correction by S132: …)” annotation rather than a silent rewrite; (7) full clean-regression read 0/0; file-based scripts (no shell-backslash misfire); 0 corrections; plain language; ASCII-only question options; removed all temp scripts; flagged (did not fix) the README reference-block duplication (FM #8). Weaknesses (honest): (a) I under-scoped the RED probe — its tree-wide sweep used a curated dir list (R//man//vignettes/ + README + DESCRIPTION) and MISSED _pkgdown.yml, the 5th sink; it was caught only by a close-out git ls-files | grep belt-and-suspenders pass (no shipped defect — caught pre-commit, owner-approved the +1 — but the probe re-created the very blind spot the typo exploited; the main −1, now Learning 125’s sharpened lesson: sweep ALL tracked files, not a curated subset); (b) very low technical difficulty — a 6-line typo fix; the value is the process-history catch + the sink completeness, not depth; (c) full R CMD check NOT run (devtools absent) — the probe + full suite + confined diff + render is the proportionate equivalent, not the full check; (d) the optional DESCRIPTION DOI item remains (deliberately deferred per the plan’s “low / <https:> acceptable”). I reserve 9–10 for a delivery whose invariant probe is correctly scoped on the first pass and that is full-check-verified.

⇒ SUGGESTED NEXT = owner’s pick. Phase 1 is COMPLETE (S102 + this S132 typo tail); the typo is gone from all tracked/shipped content; the plan + CHANGELOG now state Phase 1 done. Nothing mid-flight. Natural options (plain ASCII labels): - (B-Phase2) CRAN Phase 2 — archival ROOT CAUSE (example/test/vignette timing under --as-cran); ⚠ here-be-dragons / critical path; profile first. Needs renv::restore() + devtools (absent in this env) — so this may be blocked here and better run where devtools is available. - (B-Phase4) CRAN Phase 4 — full R CMD check --as-cran gate. Needs devtools + renv::restore() (absent here). - (D) README de-dup — the “For more information see” reference block renders TWICE in README.md (from README.Rmd:48 directly AND via the _introduction.Rmd child at README.Rmd:32); remove the direct copy, keep the child, re-render. Small structural cleanup (flagged this session, FM #8 — not fixed). - (E) Optional DESCRIPTION DOI — convert DESCRIPTION:20 PMC <https:> URL to <doi:...> (needs a DOI lookup for the Vinson/Raboin 2015 JAALAS paper). Plan marks this low / optional. - (C) Merge add-methodology → master — rides a future PR #53 (Learning 112 — fetch first, target origin/master). - A GitHub issue — #46 (species first-class), #45/#28/#9 (parent ID via conception date), #2 (GVA iteration-count guidance), #37 (unused exports). Do NOT bundle options together (FM #18/#25); do NOT start any of them without the owner picking.

Key files (this session): CHANGED (committed this session as add-methodology HEAD): README.Rmd (line 48 *Macaca mulatto*→*Macaca mulatta*); vignettes/manual_components/_introduction.Rmd (line 48, same); CITATION.cff (line 16 ''mulatto''→''mulatta''); _pkgdown.yml (line 16 'Macaca' 'mulatto'→'mulatta', website description); README.md (re-rendered — 2 typo lines only, lines 58 + 226); docs/planning/cran-2.0.0-submission-plan.md (Phase 1 STATUS: COMPLETE blockquote + §9 table row ✅); CHANGELOG.md (S132 [Unreleased] entry + corrected S131’s “Phase 1 open” line); PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 125); SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). No R/, man/, NAMESPACE, DESCRIPTION, NEWS, data/, or test change. Read firsthand: the CRAN plan (§3.1/§3.2/§3.3 + Phase 1 + §9), DESCRIPTION, .Rbuildignore, man/appServer.Rd/man/appUI.Rd, LICENSE/LICENSE.md, CITATION.cff, README.Rmd (+ render mechanism), _introduction.Rmd, CHANGELOG S102 entry, git log on the Phase-1 files. Transient (removed, NOT committed): _s132_red.R, _s132_render.R, _s132_suite.R.

Gotchas: (1) CRAN Phase 1 is DONE (S102 + S132) — do NOT re-flag the .Rbuildignore cruft, DESCRIPTION typo, \value docs, VignetteBuilder, Config order, or LICENSE year as open; all verified in place. The plan + CHANGELOG now say so. (2) The species typo is gone from all TRACKED content — but the untracked, git-ignored vignettes/a3manual.html/a3manual.md still carry the old mulatto (stale local renders); they are NOT committed and NOT in a clean CRAN tarball — they regenerate clean on the next vignette build. Do not “fix” them by hand; do not commit them. (3) README.md is generated from README.Rmd which child-includes vignettes/manual_components/_introduction.Rmd (README.Rmd:32) — NEVER hand-edit README.md; edit the sources + re-render with pkgload::load_all + rmarkdown::render(output_format = github_document(html_preview = FALSE)) (avoids README.html litter). The reference block renders TWICE (direct + child) — see option (D). (4) CITATION.cff is build-ignored (.Rbuildignore:67) so it does NOT ship — its abstract mirrors DESCRIPTION and would be regenerated by cffr::cff_write() (cffr ABSENT → hand-edited here, as S131 did for version/date). (5) Optional DOI (DESCRIPTION:20 PMC <https:>) is the ONLY remaining Phase-1 item; plan marks it low / <https:> acceptable — leave unless a future session converts it. (6) Tooling gaps: devtools/usethis/cffr ABSENT; rmarkdown/knitr/roxygen2/pkgdown/pkgload/quarto PRESENT — full R CMD check/--as-cran (Phase 2/4) NOT runnable here; run those where devtools is available. (7) Shell backslash trap (recurring) — file-based R probes; single-quoted grep -E (Learnings 108/109/121). (8) Process-history reflex — before resuming any plan phase, git log/grep CHANGELOG to confirm it isn’t already done (Learning 125; memory [[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work]]). (9) Standing uncommitted keeps: .DS_Store (modified) + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked) — owner’s keep-call. (10) Branch state: after this commit add-methodology is 13 ahead of origin/add-methodology (S120–S132) and carries 15 commits not on origin/master (S118–S132) — rides a future PR #53 (Learning 112). (11) Carried CRAN gotchas: the package is ARCHIVED on CRAN (Learning 95); renv explicit-snapshot omits Suggests; Phase 2 (archival timing) is the here-be-dragons critical path.

What Session 131 Did

Deliverable: owner’s pick (B-cont) — CRAN 2.0.0 submission plan Phase 3b: the version-consistency half of Phase 3. Bumped DESCRIPTION:4 Version 1.1.0.9000 → 2.0.0; re-rendered README.md (its version line auto-tracks getVersion()); set CITATION.cff version (stale at 1.0.7) → 2.0.0 + new date-released: '2026-06-18'; ran the two version-dependent tests. (DONE.) Scoped to DESCRIPTION + README.md + CITATION.cff; no R//man//NAMESPACE/data//test/NEWS change. This closes Phase 3 — the package is now version-consistent at 2.0.0 (resolving the intentional Phase-3a NEWS-says-2.0.0 / DESCRIPTION-says-1.1.0.9000 mismatch). Started / Completed: 2026-06-18 Status: DONE. Full strict TDD (declared every response). Gated AskUserQuestions: (1) pre-RED scope — version-consistency only vs +permanent guard test (owner chose version-consistency only); (1b, same call) the carried maintainer-judgment NEWS Minor→Major promotion (owner chose defer both); (2) PRE-RED→RED; (3) RED→GREEN; (4) GREEN→REFACTOR declared N/A (mechanical metadata bump, no structure). 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized under ultracode: SOLO — three mechanical edits; the value is the disciplined TDD framing of a no-new-behavior change, verifying the §3.1 inventory against the live tree, the litter-free render, and the whole-tree dangling-pointer sweep, not depth. New session within the conversation: claimed it (1B stub BEFORE technical work). - 🚨 REAL WORK #1 — RED BOUND TO THE VERSION-CONSISTENCY INVARIANT, not a behavior (→ Learning 124): a version bump has no new behavior, and both version-dependent tests are dynamic (read packageVersion()), so the bump makes nothing go red-then-green. The honest RED surface is the invariant the change must establish: _s131_red.R parsed DESCRIPTION (read.dcf), CITATION.cff (version: line), and README.md (rendered version line) and asserted all three == 2.0.0, the stale 1.1.0.9000 / 1.0.7 are gone, and CITATION has a date-released — 7 assertions, 7 failures on the current tree, 0 after the edits + re-render. (Metadata-bump cousin of RED-binding to a rendered NEWS.md / man/.Rd.) - 🚨 REAL WORK #2 — VERIFIED THE PLAN’S §3.1 INVENTORY AGAINST THE LIVE TREE before editing: a git grep confirmed exactly three bumpable hits (DESCRIPTION:4, README.md:8, CITATION.cff:11) and nothing hidden in R//man//tests//data-raw//inst/ (no inst/CITATION). README.Rmd:10 + the appUI About panel are on the §3.1 “NO edit — auto-tracks getVersion()” list and were correctly left. - 🚨 REAL WORK #3 — LITTER-FREE RENDER + CONFINED DIFF: re-rendered via rmarkdown::render(output_format = github_document(html_preview = FALSE)) (suppresses the NEWS.html-style preview litter at the source, improving on Learning 123’s clean-up-after). Snapshotted git status before/after: the ONLY new modified file was README.md, and its diff is confined to the single version line (1.1.0.9000 (2026-06-01) → 2.0.0 (2026-06-17)) — no figures, no README.html. - 🚨 REAL WORK #4 — WHOLE-TREE SWEEP FOUND DANGLING POINTERS the retitle left: a closing git grep for the old strings found, out of the locked scope, ROADMAP.md:6 “see NEWS.md 1.1.0.9000” (now dangling — S130 retitled that section to 2.0.0), stale “(Version 1.1.0.9000)” prose at CLAUDE.md:18, and a now-resolved TODO at nprcgenekeepr_notes.txt:5. Triaged the rest as historical-keep (inst/extdata/submission.txt + meeting_notes.html 1.0.7 logs — §3.1 “must NOT be bumped”). Flagged the three dev-doc items for follow-up; did not touch them (FM #8 — owner locked three files). - RED: _s131_red.R (7 assertions) → 7 failures. GREEN: two deterministic Edits (DESCRIPTION, CITATION.cff) + the README re-render; probe → 0; test_getVersion.R (7 checks) + test_appUI_version.R (3 checks) green at 2.0.0; full clean-regression read 191 files, 0 failed / 0 error. REFACTOR: N/A. No permanent testthat test (owner declined the optional CITATION-vs-DESCRIPTION guard; version consistency is checked by CRAN + the existing dynamic tests). - Used file-based R probes from the first command — dodged the recurring shell-backslash trap (Learnings 108/109/121); no misfires. - [news-vs-changelog]: dev-process history → CHANGELOG here. The user-facing release-note content was Phase 3a (NEWS, S130) — Phase 3b is the version bump, which getVersion()/the About tab/NEWS already surface; no new NEWS line warranted (NEWS untouched by owner decision). NO auto-memory — the work lives in the repo ([[consult-project-source-of-truth]]). [macos-dupe-scan]: committed ONLY the repo files; excluded .DS_Store (modified) + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked, standing keep); the _s131_red.R probe was TEMPORARY (removed before commit); no Rplots.pdf, no README.html.

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): SATISFIED — a real runtime path was exercised. Unlike the recent doc-only sessions (S126–S130, all N/A), the version bump does change runtime-visible output: the appUI() About tab shows the version dynamically. test_appUI_version.R renders appUI() and asserts the About panel now displays “Version 2.0.0” (and not the stale “Version 1.0.8”) — that IS the runtime confirmation (FM #24 avoided, not by claiming N/A but by running the path). Proportionate build-equivalent = probe 7/7 + both version tests green + full suite 0/0 + README re-render + git diff confined to the three files. Full R CMD check not run (devtools absent).

Session 130 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 131): Score 9/10. S130’s (B-cont) option pre-named this deliverable with surgical precision: the exact files + actions (DESCRIPTION:4 1.1.0.9000→2.0.0; regenerate README.md via rmarkdown::render since build_readme absent; CITATION.cff stale at 1.0.7 → 2.0.0 hand-edit since cffr absent; run test_getVersion.R + test_appUI_version.R), the load-bearing facts (both version tests are dynamic → won’t break; tooling gaps devtools/usethis/cffr absent vs rmarkdown/knitr present), and the teed-up maintainer-judgment NEWS-promotion question — which I posed verbatim. Every one held firsthand. The intentional NEWS/DESCRIPTION mismatch it flagged as “both must land before Phase 4” was exactly the thing this session resolved. ROI very high; no false credit. The −1: S130 retitled the NEWS 1.1.0.9000 section to 2.0.0 but did not sweep for inbound references to the old section title — leaving a dangling “NEWS.md 1.1.0.9000” pointer at ROADMAP.md:6 (and stale CLAUDE.md:18 prose) that this session had to surface. A retitle is best-placed to catch its own dangling pointers; that they’re .Rbuildignored dev docs makes it minor, not a gap. (Now captured as Learning 124’s “a version retitle leaves dangling pointers” reflex.)

Self-assessment (Session 131): 9/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER read in full, notes top, dashboard 98/100, git, 14 issues, ghost-check → HEAD 04990155=S130, clean modulo standing keeps), reported, STOPPED for the owner’s pick; claimed the session (1B stub BEFORE technical work). Headline strengths: (1) read the plan + the actual files before scoping (Learning 6) — §3.1 inventory, §6 spec, §8 owner decisions, DESCRIPTION/CITATION/README/both test files/getVersion source — not from the handoff’s compressed description; (2) verified §3.1 against the live tree with my own git grep (planning-discipline inventory), confirming exactly three bumpable hits; (3) chose the right RED surface for a no-new-behavior change — the version-consistency invariant, not an artificial behavior test — and gated both genuine owner decisions before RED; (4) rendered litter-free (html_preview=FALSE) and confined the diff to the version line, verified by before/after git status; (5) satisfied Phase-3E by running the real path (appUI() About-tab render), not by declaring N/A; (6) swept the whole tree post-fix and triaged every lingering old-version hit (bumpable / historical-keep / dangling-pointer), flagging the dev-doc dangling pointers without expanding scope (FM #8); (7) full clean-regression read 191/0/0; file-based probe (no shell-backslash misfire); 0 corrections; plain language; ASCII-only question options; removed the temp probe. Weaknesses (honest): (a) very low technical difficulty — three mechanical edits + a re-render; the value is the TDD framing, the inventory verification, and the sweep, not depth; (b) full R CMD check NOT run (devtools absent) — the probe + version tests + full suite + confined diff is the proportionate equivalent; (c) Phase 3 is now complete and consistent, but the package is not yet CRAN-clean — Phases 1 (static hygiene incl. the 'mulatto' typo), 2 (archival timing), 4 (--as-cran gate), 5 (cross-platform + submit) remain. I reserve 10 for a delivery that is also full-check-verified.

Gotchas: (1) Phase 3 is COMPLETE (3a + 3b) — package is version-consistent at 2.0.0 (NEWS, DESCRIPTION, README, CITATION). Do NOT re-flag the version mismatch; it is resolved. (2) README.md is generated from README.Rmd via getVersion() — NEVER hand-edit its version line; re-render with load_all + rmarkdown::render(output_format = github_document(html_preview = FALSE)) (the html_preview=FALSE avoids README.html litter — Learning 124). (3) Dates are provisional — README’s (2026-06-17) is getVersion()/sessioninfo-derived (NOT literal today); CITATION date-released is 2026-06-18 (today, matching the NEWS heading). Reconfirm both at the actual submission (Phase 5); do not force them to match. (4) Dangling/stale refs the retitle left (out of scope, flagged): ROADMAP.md:6 “NEWS.md 1.1.0.9000” → should read 2.0.0; CLAUDE.md:18 “(Version 1.1.0.9000)”; nprcgenekeepr_notes.txt:5 TODO now resolved — see option (D). (DONE.) Scoped to NEWS.Rmd + NEWS.md; no DESCRIPTION/R//man//data//test/README/CITATION change — version bump + README/CITATION regen + version-dependent tests are the deferred Phase 3b. Instead wrote the new section to a scratch file and spliced it in with R by heading boundaries (grep "^# nprcgenekeepr 1\.1\.0\.9000" … "^# nprcgenekeepr 1\.0\.8 ", recombine head/new/blank/tail). Prior history (1.0.8 → 1.0.4.9003 …) left untouched. The REFACTOR re-splice keyed on the new 2.0.0 heading the same way. - 🚨 REAL WORK #2 — MERGED THE DOUBLED SECTION + DROPPED INTERNALS + FOLDED THE DELTA: merged the doubled topic-block + Major/Minor-block (dragon #4) into one deduped section; dropped the 9 internal codes (NEW-xx/PED-x/XARCH-x) keeping (#NN); dropped §6.3 developer-internal mechanics (calcFE/FG/FEFG de-dup, mod*.R/moduleServer mechanics, runQcStudbook/processQcStudbookResult/shouldShowChangedColsTab, safeExecute/logModuleEvent/table-makers, “Testing Improvements”, internal test/CI fixes); folded in S123/S124 (rhesus data-type re-exports, Minor+flag), S125 (addGenotype allele coercion, Minor), and one omnibus “Documentation” Minor bullet for the shipped help/data-doc fixes (S112/S114–S117/S120–S122/S126–S127). S113/S118/S119/S128/S129 = tooling/VCS/test/website-only → correctly NOT surfaced. - 🚨 REAL WORK #3 — ADVERSARIAL 3-LENS VERIFY (workflow), then tighten: lens A completeness (vs OLD git show HEAD:NEWS.md + plan §6.3 + CHANGELOG S112–S129) = 0; lens B accuracy (every named fn exists/exported per NAMESPACE/R/; behavior + #NN cross-checked) = 0; lens C dedup/style = 0 dupes, 6 style nits (verbose bullets vs §6.2 one-sentence). REFACTOR tightened the 5 verbose Major bullets to one terse sentence each (semicolons for compound items); a post-tighten token sweep confirmed every user-facing fact survived; probe stayed 7/7. - RED: _s130_red.R (bound to rendered NEWS.md, 1 precondition + 6 assertions) → 6 failures on the current file. GREEN: splice + rmarkdown::render(github_document) (pandoc 3.1.1) → probe 0 (7/7). REFACTOR: re-splice tightened section, re-render → probe 0 (7/7), facts preserved. No permanent testthat test (NEWS is prose; verified by render + probe + adversarial read). - Used file-based R probes/splices from the first command — dodged the recurring shell-backslash trap (Learnings 108/109/121); no misfires. - Date provisional: the (20260618) heading date is today’s; reconfirm/adjust at the actual submission (Phase 5). - [news-vs-changelog]: dev-process history → CHANGELOG here. The NEWS rewrite IS the user-facing release-note work (the artifact this whole [news-vs-changelog] policy points at) — that is precisely what Phase 3 folds in. NO auto-memory — the work lives in the repo ([[consult-project-source-of-truth]]). [macos-dupe-scan]: committed ONLY NEWS.Rmd/NEWS.md + close-out files; excluded .DS_Store (modified) + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked, standing keep); removed transients _s130_red.R/_s130_splice.R/_s130_resplice.R/_s130_new_section.md and the github_document NEWS.html preview litter; no Rplots.pdf.

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A — documentation-content change, no runtime behavior altered (no R//startup/dispatch/data path touched; only NEWS prose). Stated explicitly, not silently skipped (FM #24). The proportionate build-equivalent = the rmarkdown::render of NEWS.md (SAFEGUARDS “Verify the Build Equivalent” for doc projects) + the rendered-artifact-bound probe (7/7) + the 3-lens adversarial verify + a git diff confined to the two NEWS files. Version-dependent tests not run (no version bump yet — Phase 3b). Full R CMD check not run (devtools absent).

Session 129 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 130): Score 9/10. S129 named option (B) with an unusually load-bearing framing: “Phase 3 (NEWS rewrite + version bump → 2.0.0; fold in S112–S129; the user-facing entries are S123/S124’s data-type changes and S125’s addGenotype behavior change — S126/S127/S128/S129 are doc/test/website-only and likely warrant no NEWS line).” That pre-classification of the delta was exactly right and saved real time — I confirmed it firsthand and it held. Every carried baseline held: the branch state (10 ahead of origin/add-methodology, 12 not on origin/master), the standing .DS_Store/PED_GV_AUDIT keep-call, the shell-backslash→file-probe trap (used file-based splices/probes, no misfire), and the CRAN gotchas (NEWS.md from NEWS.Rmd; devtools absent). ROI very high; no false credit. The −1: S129 (naturally — it was the vignette session) did not flag the Phase-3 tooling gaps (cffr/devtools/usethis absent) or that plan line 154 sanctions splitting Phase 3 — both of which shaped this session’s scope. Those are plan-level facts I surfaced this session, not S129’s job; a maximal handoff for a “resume the CRAN plan” pointer might have noted “check Phase-3 tooling availability first.” Minor.

Self-assessment (Session 130): 9/10. Oriented fully (read the plan’s Phase 3 + §6 spec, the current doubled NEWS, the S112–S129 CHANGELOG, tooling, version-test surface), reported the scope honestly, gated the split decision; claimed the session (1B stub BEFORE technical work). Headline strengths: (1) read the implementations before scoping (Learning 6) — found the doubled section (dragon #4), the tooling gaps, and the version-test dynamism before proposing scope; (2) deterministic splice rather than a fragile 178-line Edit (Learning 123); (3) bound RED to the rendered NEWS.md so the probe also proves the re-render ran; (4) folded the delta correctly, classifying ship-vs-internal per each handoff’s news-vs-changelog note and collapsing 12 doc-fix sessions into one omnibus bullet; (5) adversarially verified the user-facing artifact (3 lenses: completeness vs git show HEAD: + plan + CHANGELOG, accuracy vs NAMESPACE/R/, dedup/style) BEFORE tightening, then token-swept to prove the tighten dropped no fact; (6) surfaced the genuine classification fork (data-type Major vs Minor) to the owner rather than deciding silently; (7) confined the diff to the two NEWS files; cleaned the NEWS.html render litter; 0 corrections; plain language; ASCII question options. Weaknesses (honest): (a) my first draft was too verbose (the adversarial verify’s 6 style nits) — I should have written to the §6.2 terse spec on the first pass rather than tightening in REFACTOR; (b) Phase 3 is incomplete by design (split) — the version bump + README/CITATION + version tests remain (Phase 3b), so 2.0.0 is not yet self-consistent across files (NEWS says 2.0.0, DESCRIPTION still 1.1.0.9000 — intended, both land before Phase 4); (c) full R CMD check not run (devtools absent). I reserve 10 for a delivery that lands the terse spec first-pass and is full-check-verified.

(7) Both version-dependent tests are dynamic (test_getVersion.R checks structure; test_appUI_version.R reads packageVersion() and asserts “1.0.8” absent) — the bump won’t break them; run them in 3b to confirm. (8) Maintainer-judgment items flagged for the owner: the secondQuartile change and the rhesus data-type change are under Minor — owner may promote to Major. What Session 129 Did

Deliverable: owner’s pick (A3) — corrected the Studbook Quality Control vignette’s pedGood column description at vignettes/articles/studbook-quality-control.qmd:90-91 (carried from S116). (DONE — reworded the lead-in from “The shipped pedGood data set has deliberately messy headers (ego_id, si.re, dam_id, birth_date):” to “…has five columns – four with deliberately messy headers (ego_id, si.re, dam_id, birth_date) and an already-canonical sex that QC leaves untouched:”. A single 3-line prose edit.) Website-only article (vignettes/articles/ is .Rbuildignored) — no R//man//NAMESPACE/DESCRIPTION/NEWS/data//test/CRAN-vignette change. Started / Completed: 2026-06-18 Status: DONE. Full strict TDD (declared every response). Two gated AskUserQuestions for the genuine fork + the mandatory transitions: (1) pre-RED approach — rewrite the lead-in vs append a clause (owner chose rewrite the lead-in); (2) PRE-RED→RED; (3) RED→GREEN; (4) GREEN→REFACTOR declared N/A (prose content, structure preserved). 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized under ultracode: SOLO — a 3-line prose fix; the value is catching that the carried “omits sex” framing was imprecise, binding the RED probe to the live object, and rendering to verify the prose now matches the chunk. New session within the conversation: claimed it (1B stub BEFORE technical work). - 🚨 REAL WORK #1 — THE CARRIED ONE-LINER WOULD HAVE PRODUCED A WRONG FIX (→ Learning 122): the carried nit said “omits the sex column,” which reads as “add sex to the parenthetical.” A probe on the live object refuted that literal fix: names(pedGood) = ego_id, si.re, dam_id, sex, birth_date, and the article’s own changes chunk renames exactly the FOUR non-sex columns — sex is absent from changedCols because it already uses the canonical name. Adding sex to the “deliberately messy headers” list would have mislabeled it AND contradicted the chunk’s rendered output two lines below. The accurate fix conveys “five columns: four messy + an already-clean sex.” (S116’s richer framing “the dataset is 5 columns” was buried ~350 lines down in the notes; the compressed carried one-liner lost it.) - 🚨 REAL WORK #2 — BOUND THE RED PROBE TO THE LIVE OBJECT + SOURCE .qmd (Learning 122): a vignette has no generated man/.Rd to bind to (unlike Learnings 119/120). The probe parses the lead-in paragraph from the .qmd and asserts 3 ground-truth preconditions (names(pedGood) has 5 cols incl. sex; the 4 listed headers == pedGood’s non-sex columns) + 2 RED assertions (lead-in mentions sex; states “five”) → 2 failures / 3 passing on current prose; 0 failures (all 5 pass) after the edit. Binding to names(pedGood) means the test fails if the data ever diverges from the prose. - 🚨 REAL WORK #3 — RAN THE REAL BUILD-EQUIVALENT (render) AND CLEANED ITS LITTER: pkgdown::build_article("articles/studbook-quality-control") rendered clean; verified in the HTML that (a) the new “has five columns – four with deliberately messy headers” sentence is present, and (b) changedCols still shows exactly the 4 renames with no sex to X (prose now matches what the chunk demonstrates). The render writes HTML to the gitignored pkgdown_site/ but LITTERED the tracked tree with pkgdown/ (favicons), vignettes/articles/studbook-quality-control.rmarkdown (Quarto intermediate), and an auto vignettes/articles/.gitignore — snapshotted git status before, removed all three after, re-checked the tree is back to the intended set. - RED: _s129_red.R (parse the .qmd lead-in, 5 assertions) → 2 failures on current prose. GREEN: the 3-line rewrite at lines 90-92; probe → 0 failures; pkgdown render clean + content verified. REFACTOR: N/A (prose, structure preserved). No permanent testthat test added (matches the S120–S127 doc-fix class; testthat does not execute this website-only article). - Used file-based R probes from the first command — dodged the recurring shell-backslash trap (Learnings 108/109/121); no misfires. - [news-vs-changelog]: dev-process history → CHANGELOG here. Website-only article — does NOT ship to CRAN, so NO NEWS.md line warranted (unlike S123–S125’s user-facing changes). NO auto-memory — the work lives in the repo ([[consult-project-source-of-truth]]). [macos-dupe-scan]: committed ONLY the repo files; excluded .DS_Store (modified) + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked, standing keep); the two _s129_*.R probe files were TEMPORARY (removed before commit); cleaned the render litter (pkgdown/, *.rmarkdown, the auto .gitignore); pkgdown_site/ left in place (gitignored); no Rplots.pdf.

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A — documentation-content change, no runtime behavior altered (no R//startup/dispatch/data path touched; only vignette prose). Stated explicitly, not silently skipped (FM #24). The proportionate build-equivalent = the pkgdown render (SAFEGUARDS “Verify the Build Equivalent” for doc projects) + the live-object-bound source probe + a git diff confined to the one .qmd. Full testthat suite NOT run — no R//test/data/ change, and testthat does not execute the website-only article. Session 128 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 129): Score 9/10. S128 listed A3 as suggested-next option #1 with the exact file + line (studbook-quality-control.qmd:91) and the “carried from S116” pointer, which led me to S116’s richer framing (the dataset is 5 columns; “one-line article fix”). Every carried baseline/gotcha held firsthand: the standing .DS_Store/PED_GV_AUDIT keep-call (#7), the branch state (9 ahead of origin/add-methodology, #8), the shell-backslash trap (#5 — I used file-based probes + single-quoted grep, no misfire), and the “si.re/si re are INTENTIONAL raw fixtures, don’t clean them” gotcha (#4 — confirmed si.re is the real column; my fix kept the parenthetical verbatim). Ghost-check anchor (afd0d31d = S128) was exact. ROI high; no false credit. The −1: S128’s compressed one-liner “omits the sex column” is imprecise — taken literally it points at the WRONG fix (add sex to the messy-headers list), because sex is already canonical and is not a messy header. The accurate “5 columns; sex is already clean” framing existed only in S116’s note ~350 lines down. A maximal carried nit would have preserved “don’t just add sex to the list — it’s already clean.” Small lead-quality miss, not a gap — “carried from S116” is the right pointer and verifying ground truth was mine to do.

Self-assessment (Session 129): 9/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER read in full, notes top, dashboard 98/100, git, 14 issues, ghost-check → HEAD afd0d31d=S128, clean tree modulo standing keeps), reported, STOPPED for the owner’s pick; claimed the session (1B stub BEFORE technical work). Headline strengths: (1) caught that the carried one-liner was imprecise — proved firsthand that sex is NOT a messy header and that the literal “add sex” fix would contradict the chunk’s own output, then surfaced the genuine approach fork and gated it with two accurate options (neither mislabeling sex); (2) bound the RED probe to the live object (names(pedGood)) + the source paragraph, the strongest binding available for a vignette (no generated man/.Rd); (3) ran the real build-equivalent (pkgdown render) and verified BOTH the new prose AND the consistency of the executed changes chunk (no sex rename), not just that it compiled; (4) found and cleaned the render’s tracked-tree litter (pkgdown/, *.rmarkdown, auto .gitignore) by snapshotting git status before/after; (5) confined the diff to one .qmd; (6) correctly classified Phase-3E as N/A and said so explicitly (not FM #24); (7) file-based probes from command #1, no shell-backslash misfire; 0 corrections; plain language; ASCII-only question options; removed all temp probes. Weaknesses (honest): (a) the pkgdown render littered the tree and I had to discover + clean it mid-flight — rendering to an isolated temp dir up front would have avoided the extra step (now captured as Learning 122 for the next session); (b) full R CMD check and full testthat NOT run — proportionate (no code/test/data change; the article is .Rbuildignored), but it means the verification is the render + probe, not a full check; (c) very low technical difficulty — a 3-line prose edit; the value is the correctness-nuance catch, the live-object-bound probe, and the render verification, not depth. I reserve 10 for a harder delivery that is also full-check-verified.

Right-sized under ultracode: SOLO — a 12-line deletion; the value is the firsthand no-op proof, the file sweep that found the un-named third block, and correct TDD-phase reasoning, not depth. New session within the conversation: claimed it (1B stub BEFORE technical work). - 🚨 REAL WORK #1 — PROVED THE NO-OP BY WHOLE-FRAME IDENTITY, not class checks (→ Learning 121): a file-based probe confirmed rhesusPedigree ships id/sire/dam as character and birth as Date; identical(as.character(col), col) = TRUE for all three, identical(as.Date(birth), birth) = TRUE, and the airtight check identical(frame-with-all-4-conversions, frame-without) = TRUE. One identity check licenses deleting every block drawn from that object (both pedOne and pedDF). - 🚨 REAL WORK #2 — THE HANDOFF’S LINE-RANGES WERE A LEAD, NOT A CENSUS: a robust ERE sweep (grep -rEn) found a THIRD identical-class block the S127 handoff never named — test_getPotentialParents.R:116-119 (pedDF, NEW-53 mutation test), same file as the named pedOne block. Surfaced it as the genuine scope fork (gated → owner chose all three). Also classified out two look-alikes that are NOT the class: test_fillBins.R:22 (as.Date inside age arithmetic) and test_modGeneticValue.R:1274 (as.character inside expect_setequal) — coercions inside expressions/assertions, not redundant self-assignments. Left them. - 🚨 SELF-CAUGHT MISFIRE — THE SHELL-BACKSLASH TRAP EXTENDS TO GREP: my first sweep used double-quoted BRE (...pedOne\$...\|...) and matched NOTHING (macOS BSD grep ignores \| alternation / \$). Caught it immediately (“that grep hit the quoting trap, it proved nothing”) and redid with single-quoted grep -rEn ERE — found all blocks. Same class as Learnings 108/109; now also recorded for grep (Learning 121). - RED: none (refactor-only). GREEN baseline: both test files pass (21 + 39 checks). REFACTOR: deleted the 12 no-op lines; re-ran both files (pass, identical counts) and the full suite (0/0). No new test added — the deletion is guarded by the existing suite staying green. - Used file-based R probes from the first command — dodged the recurring R-probe shell-backslash trap (the grep misfire above was the BRE-grep cousin, self-corrected). - [news-vs-changelog]: dev-process history → CHANGELOG here. Internal test cleanup — fold into the CRAN Phase 3 NEWS rewrite at 2.0.0 only if a NEWS line is warranted (almost certainly NOT — no user-facing change). NO auto-memory — the work lives in the repo ([[consult-project-source-of-truth]]). [macos-dupe-scan]: committed ONLY the repo files; excluded .DS_Store (modified) + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked, standing keep); the two _s128_*.R probe files were TEMPORARY (removed before commit; no Rplots.pdf).

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A — test-only change, no runtime/production behavior altered (no R//man//startup/dispatch path touched; only test-fixture setup simplified). Stated explicitly, not silently skipped (FM #24). The proportionate build-equivalent = both target files green + full-suite 0/0 + diff confined to the 12 intended deletions. No checkRd/NAMESPACE-diff needed (no R/ or man/ change). Full R CMD check not run (devtools absent).

Session 127 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 128): Score 9/10. S127 listed A8 as suggested-next option #1 with file:line references (test_getPotentialParents.R:4-7 + test_modPotentialParents.R:120-125), characterized it correctly as “behavior-preserving; re-run suite,” and gotcha #4 repeated it. The no-op CAUSE was fully traceable through the handoff chain (S123 re-exported rhesusPedigree with canonical types). Every carried gotcha held: the shell-backslash trap (#6) steered me to file-based R probes for the no-op proof — and, fittingly, its BRE-grep cousin bit my first sweep, exactly the class it warns about; the standing .DS_Store/PED_GV_AUDIT keep-call (#9); the branch state (8 ahead of origin/add-methodology, #10). ROI high; no false credit. The −1: S127 named only TWO of the THREE identical-class blocks — it missed test_getPotentialParents.R:116-119 (pedDF), in the SAME file it did cite — and the test_modPotentialParents.R range “120-125” was loose (the actual conversions are 121-124; 120 is the assignment, 125 is fromCenter). A line list from a sweep, not from the predecessor’s two prior handoffs, would have caught the third block. Small lead-quality miss, not a gap — “re-run suite” is the right discipline and the sweep was mine to run.

Self-assessment (Session 128): 9/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER read in full, notes top, dashboard 98/100, git, 14 issues, ghost-check → HEAD 6f96726f=S127, clean), reported, STOPPED for the owner’s pick; claimed the session (1B stub BEFORE technical work). Headline strengths: (1) proved the no-op by whole-frame identical(), the airtight behavior-preservation proof, not a weaker per-column class check; (2) swept the file myself and found the un-named third block (pedDF), then classified two look-alikes OUT — completeness the handoff’s line list lacked; (3) correct TDD-phase reasoning for a pure refactor — recognized there is NO RED phase (the green suite is the net) and neither invented one nor skipped the scope + GREEN→REFACTOR gates; (4) gated the genuine scope fork with grounded options and let the owner decide breadth; (5) proved zero regressions (both files green with identical counts, full suite 0/0, diff confined to exactly 12 deletions); (6) correctly classified Phase-3E as N/A for a test-only change and said so explicitly (not FM #24); (7) self-caught the BRE-grep misfire and corrected it in one step; 0 corrections; plain language; ASCII-only question options; removed all temp probes. Weaknesses (honest): (a) my first grep used double-quoted BRE and matched nothing — a self-caught misfire that cost one extra command; I should have reached for single-quoted -E immediately given the standing shell-backslash gotcha; (b) full R CMD check NOT run (devtools absent) — the targeted-files + full-suite + confined-diff is the proportionate equivalent, not the full check; (c) very low technical difficulty — a 12-line deletion; the value is the no-op proof, the sweep completeness, and the phase reasoning, not depth. I reserve 10 for a delivery that is also full-check-verified with no process misfire.

⇒ SUGGESTED NEXT = owner’s pick. (A8) is DONE — all three redundant no-op blocks removed; the file is internally consistent and the deferred REFACTOR class is closed. Nothing mid-flight. Do NOT bundle options together (FM #18/#25); do NOT start any of them without the owner picking.

Key files (this session): CHANGED (committed): tests/testthat/test_getPotentialParents.R (removed two no-op blocks: lines 4-7 pedOne + 116-119 pedDF, 8 lines); tests/testthat/test_modPotentialParents.R (removed one no-op block: lines 121-124 pedOne, 4 lines); CHANGELOG.md (S128 [Unreleased]); PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 121); SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). No R/, man/, NAMESPACE, DESCRIPTION, NEWS, or data/ change. Read firsthand: both test files (the pedOne + pedDF setup blocks), and the no-op probe results on the live rhesusPedigree. Transient (removed, NOT committed): _s128_noop.R, _s128_suite.R.

Gotchas: (1) A8 is DONE — all three redundant no-op blocks removed; do not re-flag. (2) Two look-alikes are NOT the class — test_fillBins.R:22 (as.Date inside age arithmetic) and test_modGeneticValue.R:1274 (as.character inside expect_setequal) are coercions inside expressions, not redundant self-assignments — leave them. (3) The no-op holds only because rhesusPedigree ships canonical types (character id/sire/dam, Date birth; S123/Learning 116). The fixtures now rely on the object’s types directly — if those ever regress, the fixtures inherit the regression. (4) si.re/si re are INTENTIONAL raw fixtures (carried) — do NOT “clean” them. (5) Shell backslash trap extends to grep (Learning 121) — use single-quoted grep -rEn (ERE), NOT double-quoted BRE \|/\$ (macOS BSD grep silently matches nothing); and write R probes to a FILE + Rscript. (6) A REFACTOR-only deliverable has NO RED phase (Learning 121) — the existing green suite is the safety net; run the pre-REFACTOR scope gate + GREEN→REFACTOR gate only. (7) Standing uncommitted: .DS_Store (modified) + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked) — owner’s keep-call. (8) Branch state: after this commit add-methodology is 9 ahead of origin/add-methodology (S120–S128) and carries 11 commits not on origin/master (S118–S128) — rides a future PR #53 (Learning 112). (9) Carried CRAN gotchas: NEWS.md from NEWS.Rmd; renv explicit-snapshot omits Suggests; the package is ARCHIVED on CRAN (Learning 95); full R CMD check not run this session (devtools absent).

Probing refuted that: BOTH candidate CSVs carry the same obfuscated ids as the bundled object (BRI2MW, 677E7M, …), so neither is pre-obfuscation data — the un-obfuscated source is simply not shipped. inst/extdata/obfuscated_rhesus_mhc_ped.csv is 375×8 and value-identical to rhesusPedigree across all 8 columns; rhesusPedigree_fromCenter.csv is value-identical only on the 8 shared columns but is 375×9 (extra fromCenter column) — a superset. Cited the exact-shape twin (obfuscated_rhesus_mhc_ped.csv), consistent with the sibling genotypes doc (S126). - 🚨 REAL WORK #2 — BOUND THE RED PROBE TO THE GENERATED man/.Rd (Learning 119, reused): the probe parses man/rhesusPedigree.Rd’s sole \emph{} token and asserts the phantom is absent (man + R/data.R), the cited file is present (man + R/data.R), exists in inst/extdata, and is value-identical to the object → 4 failures (A1–A4) against the current doc (A5/A6 preconditions passed); 0 after swap + regen. GREEN: swapped \emph{rhesusPedigree.csv}→\emph{obfuscated_rhesus_mhc_ped.csv} at R/data.R:358; regenerated man/ via the rd roclet only; probe → 0 failures. I reserve 10 for a delivery that is also full-check-verified.

Weaknesses (honest): (a) full R CMD check NOT run (devtools absent) — the checkRd + load_all + suite + NAMESPACE-diff + probe is the proportionate equivalent, not the full check; (b) very low technical difficulty — a one-token fix; the value is the firsthand verification, the artifact-bound probe, and the class sweep, not depth. I reserve 10 for a delivery that is also full-check-verified.

⇒ SUGGESTED NEXT = owner’s pick. (A10) is DONE — the rhesusGenotypes @source now cites a real, value-identical file. Nothing mid-flight. Natural options (plain ASCII labels): - (A11) Sibling @source phantom (ships, small — newly surfaced this session): R/data.R:358 cites a nonexistent rhesusPedigree.csv. Determine the correct citation — candidate real sources rhesusPedigree_fromCenter.csv (the obfuscation source) or obfuscated_rhesus_mhc_ped.csv (likely value-identical obfuscated export — verify value-identity first) — fix the roxygen, regen man/rhesusPedigree.Rd. Same shape as A10; reuse the man-\emph-parse RED probe (Learning 119). - (A8) Adjacent REFACTOR cleanup (carried from S123): remove the now-redundant as.character/as.Date no-op conversions in test_getPotentialParents.R:4-7 + test_modPotentialParents.R:120-125. Behavior-preserving; re-run suite. - (A3) Vignette nit: vignettes/articles/studbook-quality-control.qmd:91 omits the sex column (carried from S116). - (B) Resume the CRAN plan — Phase 3 (NEWS rewrite + version bump → 2.0.0; fold in S112–S126, incl. S123/S124’s user-facing data-type changes and S125’s user-facing addGenotype behavior change). - (C) Merge add-methodology → master — after this commit add-methodology is 7 ahead of origin/add-methodology (S120–S126) and carries 9 commits not on origin/master (S118–S126); rides a future PR #53 (Learning 112 — fetch first, target origin/master, PR mechanism). Do NOT bundle (A11)/(A8)/(A3)/(B) together (FM #18/#25); do NOT start any of them without the owner picking.

Key files (this session): CHANGED (committed): R/data.R (1 roxygen token: \emph{rhesusGenotypes.csv}→\emph{obfuscated_rhesus_mhc_breeder_genotypes.csv} at the rhesusGenotypes doc, line 345); man/rhesusGenotypes.Rd (regenerated — 1-line source diff); CHANGELOG.md (S126 [Unreleased]); PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 119); SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). No NAMESPACE, DESCRIPTION, NEWS, data/, or code change. Transient (removed, NOT committed): _s126_verify.R, _s126_red.R, _s126_regen.R, _s126_suite.R.

Gotchas: (1) The rhesusGenotypes @source is now FIXED — do not re-flag it; it cites obfuscated_rhesus_mhc_breeder_genotypes.csv, which exists and is value-identical. (2) NEW: the sibling rhesusPedigree @source (R/data.R:358) cites a nonexistent rhesusPedigree.csv — same phantom class (A11); candidate real sources rhesusPedigree_fromCenter.csv or obfuscated_rhesus_mhc_ped.csv (verify value-identity before citing). ExamplePedigree.csv (:18) is fine (exists). (3) An audit “vs live objects” canNOT catch a @source phantom (Learning 119) — provenance citations need a filesystem check (cited file exists + value-identical), and the RED probe should parse the GENERATED man/.Rd. (4) Redundant no-op conversions remain in test_getPotentialParents.R:4-7 + test_modPotentialParents.R:120-125 — carried deferred REFACTOR (A8). (5) si.re/si re are INTENTIONAL raw fixtures (carried) — do NOT “clean” them. (6) Shell backslash trap (recurring) — write R probes to a FILE and Rscript it. (7) Markdown IS enabled in roxygen — keep prose free of stray markdown. (8) rd-roclet-only regen (roxygen2::roxygenise(roclets="rd")) keeps NAMESPACE byte-identical. (9) Standing uncommitted: .DS_Store (modified) + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked) — owner’s keep-call. (10) Branch state: after this commit add-methodology is 7 ahead of origin/add-methodology (S120–S126) and carries 9 commits not on origin/master (S118–S126) — rides a future PR #53 (Learning 112). (11) Carried CRAN gotchas: NEWS.md from NEWS.Rmd; renv explicit-snapshot omits Suggests; the package is ARCHIVED on CRAN (Learning 95); full R CMD check not run this session (devtools absent).

What Session 125 Did

What Session 125 Did

Deliverable: owner’s pick (A9) — hardened addGenotype() so its integer allele encoding is consistent regardless of whether the two allele columns arrive as character or factor. (DONE — coerced both allele columns to character at the top of the function; factor input now produces output identical to character input, verified on the real bundled rhesusGenotypes: 35-allele dictionary codes 10001–10035, max distinct codes per allele = 1.) This realizes the latent code fragility S124 surfaced and flagged (Learning 117): genoDict[genotype[, 2L]] / [, 3L] indexes a name-keyed integer vector by a factor → R uses the factor’s INTEGER CODES not its labels → encoding inconsistent between the two columns (and between callers). First CODE fix in the S118–S125 run (S118–S122 were doc/prose; S123–S124 were DATA). Scoped to R/addGenotype.R + tests/testthat/test_addGenotype.R + man/addGenotype.Rd; no NAMESPACE/DESCRIPTION/NEWS/data//checkGenotypeFile.R change (NAMESPACE proven byte-identical). Started / Completed: 2026-06-17 Status: DONE. Full strict TDD (declared every response). Two gated AskUserQuestions for the genuine forks + the 3 mandatory transitions: (1) pre-RED scope — addGenotype-only vs also-harden-checkGenotypeFile (owner chose addGenotype-only / point-of-use); (2) PRE-RED→RED; (3) RED→GREEN; (4) GREEN→REFACTOR. The coerce-at-top-vs-coerce-at-index implementation detail was ground-truth-determined (coerce-at-top is strictly more robust — it also fixes the version-fragile c()-on-factors dict build) → presented as the gate baseline, NOT re-gated (Learning 113). 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized under ultracode: a read-only Explore breadth scan to confirm scope (callers / sibling-pattern / checkGenotypeFile behavior) with all load-bearing claims verified firsthand, then SOLO TDD (the fix is a deterministic 2-line coercion — a fan-out for the edit would be theater). New session within the conversation: claimed it (1B stub BEFORE technical work). - 🚨 REAL WORK #1 — THE SCOPE DECISION TURNED ON THE CALL GRAPH, NOT THE DOC (→ Learning 118): addGenotype’s roxygen says genotype “is to be provided by checkGenotypeFile so it is not checked” — which reads as “harden the gate.” The breadth scan + firsthand reads refuted that as the complete answer: addGenotype has DIRECT callers that bypass checkGenotypeFile (the roxygen example passing rhesusGenotypes, test_addGenotype.R:17, test_geneDrop.R:28), so a gate-only fix leaves them exposed. The only fix that closes the bug for ALL callers is at the point of use. Also confirmed: checkGenotypeFile is a pure structural validator (no type coercion), and no sibling genotype function shares the dict[factorColumn] pattern (getGVGenotype/getGenotypes/getGenoDefinedParentGenotypes/hasGenotype are clean) → scope stayed narrow. - 🚨 REAL WORK #2 — THE RED FIXTURE HAD TO MAKE PER-COLUMN CODES DIVERGE FROM THE GLOBAL SORT: the naive fixture (both columns the same allele set in sorted order) does NOT reproduce the bug — per-column factor codes coincidentally align with the global sort(unique()) positions. The minimal trigger is first_name = c("a","b"), second_name = c("b","c") as factors: globally a→10001, b→10002, c→10003, but the per-column codes (a,b→1,2 and b,c→1,2) make allele “b” encode as 10002 in first but 10001 in second. Designing it required tracing the exact index arithmetic, not just “make it a factor.” Character input is the oracle. - 🚨 REAL WORK #3 — COERCE AT THE TOP, NOT ONLY AT THE INDEX: the breadth agent suggested wrapping only the two lookups (genoDict[as.character(genotype[, 2L])]). That fixes the lookup but leaves the dict BUILD (sort(unique(c(col1, col2)))) relying on c()-on-factors (pre-R-4.1 dropped factor attrs → mis-named dict). Coercing both columns to character once at the top fixes build AND lookup in one place, version-robustly. - RED: two new tests in test_addGenotype.R — (1) factor-input output identical to character-input output (expect_identical on $first and $second); (2) allele “b” gets the same code as first and as second — confirmed 2 failed / 0 error on the unhardened function; the existing character-input test stayed green (4/0). GREEN: the 2-line coercion at the top of addGenotype; all 3 tests pass; full suite 0/0. REFACTOR: added a one-line @details roxygen note documenting the internal coercion; regenerated man/addGenotype.Rd (rd roclet only → NAMESPACE untouched). - Used file-based R probes from the first command — dodged the recurring shell-backslash trap (Learnings 108/109); no misfires. - [news-vs-changelog]: dev-process history → CHANGELOG here. This change SHIPS and is user-facing (addGenotype output changes for any factor-columned input) → fold into the CRAN Phase 3 NEWS rewrite at 2.0.0 (flagged, NOT edited now). NO auto-memory — the work lives in the repo ([[consult-project-source-of-truth]]). [macos-dupe-scan]: committed ONLY the repo files; excluded .DS_Store (modified) + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked, standing keep); the four _s125_*.R probe files were TEMPORARY (removed before commit; no Rplots.pdf).

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): DONE — this IS a runtime-behavior change. “Suite passes” was necessary but insufficient (the fix changes addGenotype output for factor input). Ran addGenotype(rhesusPedigree, rhesusGenotypes) on a factor-coerced copy of the real bundled object (375×12, 31 genotype rows): first/second came out identical to the character path; the combined dictionary is the full 35 alleles (codes 10001–10035); max distinct codes per allele = 1 (consistent encoding). Computed the consistency property at the genotype-row level (gr <- !is.na(first_name), aligned al/co vectors) to avoid the S124 short-logical-recycling trap (gotcha #11). Full R CMD check not run (devtools absent) — proportionate equivalent = checkRd 0 + load_all + full-suite 0/0 + NAMESPACE byte-diff + the factor-vs-character smoke on the real object.

Session 124 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 125): Score 9/10. S124 pre-named THIS deliverable precisely as (A9) and made it trivial to execute: it gave the exact bug mechanism (genoDict[genotype[, 2L]] indexes by integer codes not labels), the exact fix location (“coerce the allele columns to character inside addGenotype”), the exact test design hint (“feed a factor-columned genotype, assert consistent name-based codes”), and the exact numbers to expect (combined dict 35 alleles, codes 10001–10035 vs the buggy per-column 18/23). Every carried gotcha held: the shell-backslash→file-probe trap (I used file probes from command #1, no misfires), the consistency-check trap gotcha #11 (verify at the genotype-row level, NOT by indexing the 375-row output with a 31-row logical — S124 hit this itself and documented it; I followed the warning and avoided the round-trip), the standing .DS_Store/PED_GV_AUDIT keep-call, and the branch state (5 ahead of origin/add-methodology). ROI very high; no false credit; the bug characterization was accurate firsthand. The −1: S124’s A9 wording offered the fix location as “inside addGenotype (or in checkGenotypeFile)” without noting that direct callers bypass checkGenotypeFile, so the “or checkGenotypeFile” branch — taken literally — would NOT have fully closed the bug. It listed addGenotype first (correct lead), but I had to run the breadth scan to confirm the gate-only path was insufficient. A perfect handoff would have flagged the direct-caller asymmetry.

Self-assessment (Session 125): 9/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER read in full, notes top, dashboard 98/100, git, 14 issues, ghost-check → HEAD 9b713206 = S124, clean), reported, STOPPED for the owner’s pick; claimed the session (1B stub BEFORE technical work). Headline strengths: (1) the scope decision turned on the call graph, not the doc contract — proved (via breadth scan + firsthand reads) that direct callers bypass checkGenotypeFile, so the point-of-use fix is the only complete one, and that the bug is isolated (no sibling pattern) so scope stayed narrow; (2) designed a RED fixture that actually reproduces the bug — traced the index arithmetic to make per-column codes diverge from the global sort (the naive fixture would have passed even unfixed); (3) coerced at the source, not just the buggy line — also neutralized the version-fragile c()-on-factors dict build the breadth agent’s patch would have left; (4) verified everything firsthand (bug mechanism, caller types, checkGenotypeFile behavior) — used the Explore scan for completeness but re-derived its load-bearing claims; (5) real Phase-3E smoke on the actual bundled object (factor-coerced) with the consistency property computed correctly at the genotype-row level — sidestepped the S124 gotcha #11; (6) proved zero regressions (3/3 tests, full suite 0/0, checkRd 0, NAMESPACE byte-identical, diff confined to 3 files); (7) correct TDD discipline (declared every response; gated the genuine scope fork + the 3 transitions; did not re-gate the ground-truth-determined coerce-at-top detail — Learning 113); (8) file-based probes from command #1; 0 corrections; plain language; ASCII-only question options; removed all temp probes. Weaknesses (honest): (a) full R CMD check NOT run (devtools absent) — the checkRd + load_all + suite + NAMESPACE-diff + smoke is the proportionate build-equivalent, not the full check; (b) low technical difficulty — the fix is 2 lines; the value is the scope analysis, the reproducing fixture, and the realistic smoke, not depth; (c) I did not add an explicit NA-with-factor RED case (the coercion is NA-safe — as.character(factor-with-NA) preserves NA, and the character NA path is already covered by test_geneDrop.R — but a dedicated assertion would have made it airtight; owner-approved RED scope was the two tests). I reserve 10 for a delivery that is also full-check-verified.

⇒ SUGGESTED NEXT = owner’s pick. (A9) is DONE — addGenotype() is now robust to factor allele columns; the function-level fragility S124 flagged is CLOSED. Nothing mid-flight.
