Roxygen-source fix → man/rhesusGenotypes.Rd regen (same shape as the S120–S122 data-doc class). - (A8) Adjacent REFACTOR cleanup (carried from S123): remove the now-redundant as.character/as.Date no-op conversions in test_getPotentialParents.R:4-7 + test_modPotentialParents.R:120-125. Behavior-preserving; re-run suite. - (A3) Vignette nit: vignettes/articles/studbook-quality-control.qmd:91 omits the sex column (carried from S116). - (B) Resume the CRAN plan — Phase 3 (NEWS rewrite + version bump → 2.0.0; fold in S112–S125, incl. S123/S124’s user-facing data-type changes and S125’s user-facing addGenotype behavior change). - (C) Merge add-methodology → master — after this commit add-methodology is 6 ahead of origin/add-methodology (S120–S125) and carries 8 commits not on origin/master (S118–S125); rides a future PR #53 (Learning 112 — fetch first, target origin/master, PR mechanism). Do NOT bundle (A10)/(A8)/(A3)/(B) together (FM #18/#25); do NOT start any of them without the owner picking.

Key files (this session): CHANGED (committed): R/addGenotype.R (2-line character coercion at top + @details roxygen note); tests/testthat/test_addGenotype.R (2 new tests: factor==character output, cross-column consistency); man/addGenotype.Rd (regenerated — \details block, 1 hunk); CHANGELOG.md (S125 [Unreleased]); PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 118); SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). No NAMESPACE, DESCRIPTION, NEWS, data/, checkGenotypeFile.R, or other-function change. Read firsthand: R/addGenotype.R, R/checkGenotypeFile.R, R/fixGenotypeCols.R, R/modInput.R:375-414 (the Shiny call path), tests/testthat/test_addGenotype.R. Breadth scan (Explore): mapped all 3 callers, confirmed checkGenotypeFile does not coerce, confirmed no sibling dict[factorColumn] pattern. Transient (removed, NOT committed): _s125_red.R, _s125_green.R, _s125_refactor.R, _s125_phase3e.R.

Gotchas: (1) The addGenotype factor fragility is now FIXED — do not re-flag it; the function coerces allele columns to character at the top, so factor input is encoded consistently (and identically to character input). (2) checkGenotypeFile was intentionally NOT changed (owner chose addGenotype-only) — it remains a pure structural validator that does NOT coerce types; if a future task wants the documented “opener” contract enforced at the gate, that is a separate deliverable. (3) addGenotype has DIRECT callers that bypass checkGenotypeFile (roxygen example, test_addGenotype.R, test_geneDrop.R) — any future change to genotype handling must account for them, not just the Shiny gate path. (4) Consistency-check trap (carried, gotcha #11 from S124): verify addGenotype’s allele encoding at the genotype-row (31) level, NOT by indexing the 375-row merged output with a 31-row logical (recycles → spurious result). (5) rhesusGenotypes doc cites a nonexistent rhesusGenotypes.csv (R/data.R:345); real source is obfuscated_rhesus_mhc_breeder_genotypes.csv (option A10). (6) Redundant no-op conversions remain in test_getPotentialParents.R:4-7 + test_modPotentialParents.R:120-125 — carried deferred REFACTOR (A8). (7) si.re/si re are INTENTIONAL raw fixtures (carried) — do NOT “clean” them. (8) Shell backslash trap (recurring) — write R probes to a FILE and Rscript it. (9) Markdown IS enabled in roxygen — keep prose free of stray markdown. (10) Standing uncommitted: .DS_Store (modified) + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked) — owner’s keep-call. (11) Branch state: after this commit add-methodology is 6 ahead of origin/add-methodology (S120–S125) and carries 8 commits not on origin/master (S118–S125) — rides a future PR #53 (Learning 112). (12) Carried CRAN gotchas: NEWS.md from NEWS.Rmd; renv explicit-snapshot omits Suggests; the package is ARCHIVED on CRAN (Learning 95); full R CMD check not run this session (devtools absent).

What Session 124 Did

Deliverable: owner’s pick (A7) — re-exported the bundled rhesusGenotypes object so all three columns carry the canonical character type, preserving every value. (DONE — id/first_name/second_name all factor→character; dim 31×3, unique counts 31/18/23 and 0 NA preserved; values cross-checked identical to the shipping obfuscated_rhesus_mhc_breeder_genotypes.csv.) Same DATA + strict-TDD shape as S123’s rhesusPedigree re-export (Learning 116). Scoped to data/rhesusGenotypes.RData + new data-raw/rhesusGenotypes.R + new tests/testthat/test_rhesusGenotypes.R + the rhesusGenotypes id doc line in R/data.R + man/rhesusGenotypes.Rd; no NAMESPACE/DESCRIPTION/NEWS/.Rbuildignore/other-data-object/addGenotype.R change (^data-raw$ already build-ignored from S123; NAMESPACE proven byte-identical). Started / Completed: 2026-06-17 Status: DONE. Full strict TDD (declared every response). Four gated AskUserQuestions: (1) pre-RED scope — id-only vs all-three-columns (owner chose all three — full type-correctness); (2) PRE-RED→RED; (3) RED→GREEN; (4) GREEN→REFACTOR. One genuine fork (scope) + the 3 mandatory transitions; coerce-in-place and the data-raw/ convention were ground-truth/established-pattern-determined → not re-gated (Learnings 113/116). 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized under ultracode: a read-only 3-prong investigation workflow (provenance / dependents / test-patterns) to frame scope, with all key claims verified firsthand before authoring; then SOLO coercion + oracle verification (the work is a deterministic coercion — a fan-out for the edit would be theater). New session within the conversation: claimed it (1B stub BEFORE technical work). - 🚨 REAL WORK #1 — THE FIRSTHAND INVESTIGATION REFRAMED A7 (→ Learning 117): S123 flagged A7 as “de-factor id”. Probing showed three factor columns, and that id is the column that does NOT matter — merge(ped, genotype, by="id") coerces it either way and every consumer is id-agnostic (independently confirmed by the dependents scan). The columns that matter are the two allele columns: addGenotype()’s genoDict[genotype[, 2L]] indexes a name-keyed vector by a factor → R uses the factor’s INTEGER CODES not its labels → an inconsistent encoding (same allele → different code in first vs second). Character columns index by name → consistent (verified: same allele→same code = TRUE for character / FALSE for factor; combined dict 35 alleles, codes 10001–10035 vs the buggy per-column 10001–10018 / 10001–10023). The package’s own test_addGenotype.R already feeds stringsAsFactors=FALSE — the shipped factor object was the anomaly. So scope was a correctness decision, not cosmetic; gated it; owner chose all three. - 🚨 REAL WORK #2 — PROVENANCE confirmed COERCE-not-REGENERATE: data/rhesusGenotypes.RData has a single 2020 commit (31c4679d), no scripted generator, shares all 31 obfuscated ids with rhesusPedigree (re-deriving via non-deterministic obfuscatePed() would change ids + desync the sibling). New idempotent data-raw/rhesusGenotypes.R coerces the three columns and re-saves in place (compress="xz", 734→608 B). The value-identical inst/extdata/obfuscated_rhesus_mhc_breeder_genotypes.csv is an independent cross-check (all 3 columns identical). - 🚨 REAL WORK #3 — BUNDLED doc + man with the data change: reverted R/data.R:347 id doc factor→character (first_name/second_name make no type claim, untouched) and regenerated man/rhesusGenotypes.Rd (1-line diff) in the SAME deliverable so the data-doc-mismatch class (S114–S122) stays closed. - RED: test_rhesusGenotypes.R (22 assertions pinning types AND value-preservation — dim 31×3, names, unique 31/18/23, NA 0, membership spot-checks 0BNY0G/I67LRJ/K0M2RD, A004_B002/A008_B015b, A004_B048a/A002a_B069a), confirmed failing on the factor object (the 6 expect_type("character")/expect_false(is.factor()) assertions; value assertions already passed). GREEN: ran the coercion, the 1-line doc revert, regenerated man/. REFACTOR: N/A (own code clean; no redundant conversions introduced — no test references the bundled genotype object). - Used file-based R probes from the first command — dodged the recurring shell-backslash trap (Learnings 108/109); no misfires. - [news-vs-changelog]: dev-process history → CHANGELOG here. This change SHIPS and is user-facing (column types changed; the addGenotype example output changes) → fold into the CRAN Phase 3 NEWS rewrite at 2.0.0 (flagged, NOT edited now). NO auto-memory — the work lives in the repo ([[consult-project-source-of-truth]]). [macos-dupe-scan]: committed ONLY the repo files; excluded .DS_Store (modified) + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked, standing keep); the five _s124_*.R probe files were TEMPORARY (removed before commit; no Rplots.pdf).

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): DONE — this IS a runtime-behavior change. “Suite passes” was necessary but insufficient (the data change alters addGenotype output). Ran addGenotype() on the shipped corrected object directly, no conversions → 375×12, 31 genotype rows, codes spanning the combined 35-allele dictionary, encoding consistent (same allele→same code = TRUE); coercion idempotent (re-run is a no-op); CSV cross-check id/first_name/second_name all identical. (Note: my first consistency check printed a spurious FALSE — it indexed the 375-row merged result with a 31-row logical; recomputed correctly at the genotype-row level → TRUE. Caught and reconciled, not a defect.) Full R CMD check not run (devtools absent) — proportionate equivalent = checkRd 0 + load_all + full-suite 0/0 + NAMESPACE byte-diff + the consumer-on-shipped-object smoke test + the CSV cross-check.

Session 123 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 124): Score 8/10. S123 pre-named THIS deliverable precisely as (A7) and its gotcha #3 was load-bearing and accurate: “rhesusGenotypes is the next opaque .rda … shares ids with rhesusPedigree and is merged by addGenotype() (both-character merge is fine, but verify)”. Every carried gotcha held exactly — the data-raw/ pattern (.RData not .rda, save(..., compress="xz"), do NOT usethis::use_data, ^data-raw$ build-ignored), the shell-backslash→file-probe trap, the roxygenise(roclets="rd") regen method, the standing .DS_Store/PED_GV_AUDIT keep-call, and the branch state (4 ahead of origin/add-methodology; 6 not on origin/master). The “but verify” steer is exactly what surfaced the fuller picture. ROI high; no false credit. The −2: A7 was characterized as id-only / “match the convention” — cosmetic framing that missed that (a) the object has three factor columns and (b) the column it named (id) is the one that doesn’t matter, while the two it didn’t mention (first_name/second_name) carry a latent addGenotype correctness issue. S123 wasn’t tasked to deep-investigate rhesusGenotypes, and it did say “verify” — but the scope label under-sold the deliverable.

Self-assessment (Session 124): 9/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER read, notes top, dashboard 98/100, git, 14 issues, ghost-check → HEAD 19a47fab=S123, clean), reported, STOPPED for the owner’s pick; claimed the session (1B stub BEFORE technical work). Headline strengths: (1) the firsthand investigation REFRAMED the task — found the 3-column degradation and the addGenotype factor-indexing correctness issue, so the literal “de-factor id” (which would have shipped a half-degraded object still producing inconsistent codes) was avoided; (2) verified everything firsthand (structure, consistency property, no type-asserting tests, CSV value-identity) — never relied on the workflow’s relayed claims; the workflow independently CONFIRMED the allele-column finding; (3) gated the genuine fork (scope) as a correctness decision and did not re-gate the established coerce-in-place/data-raw pattern (Learnings 113/116); (4) value-preservation in the RED tests + a real Phase-3E smoke on the shipped object + an independent CSV cross-check; (5) bundled doc+man+test atomically; (6) proved zero regressions (new test 22/22, full suite 0/0, checkRd 0, NAMESPACE byte-identical, diff confined to intended files); (7) flagged (not fixed) the addGenotype factor-fragility and the doc-source nit (FM #8); (8) file-based probes from command #1; 0 corrections; plain language; removed all temp probes. Weaknesses (honest): (a) full R CMD check NOT run (devtools absent); (b) my first Phase-3E consistency check had an indexing bug (375-row result indexed by a 31-row logical) that printed a misleading FALSE — caught and corrected, but a closer first read would have avoided the round-trip; (c) the investigation workflow’s dependents agent overlapped my firsthand id-agnosticism check (it was still additive — it independently confirmed the allele-column issue). I reserve 10 for a delivery that is also full-check-verified and free of a self-inflicted round-trip.

⇒ SUGGESTED NEXT = owner’s pick. (A7) is DONE — rhesusGenotypes now ships all-character columns; the data-DOC and data-ARTIFACT classes for it are both closed. Nothing mid-flight. Natural options (plain ASCII labels): - (A9) Harden addGenotype() against factor inputs (CODE fix, strict TDD): the surfaced fragility — genoDict[genotype[, 2L]] mis-encodes ANY factor allele column (indexes by integer code, not label). Coerce the allele columns to character inside addGenotype (or in checkGenotypeFile). Has its own RED tests (feed a factor-columned genotype, assert consistent name-based codes). The data fix made the shipped example correct; the FUNCTION is still unhardened. - (A10) Data-doc nit (ships, small): R/data.R:345 cites a nonexistent source rhesusGenotypes.csv; the real value-identical export is obfuscated_rhesus_mhc_breeder_genotypes.csv. Roxygen-source fix → man/ regen (same shape as the S120–S122 data-doc class). - (A8) Adjacent REFACTOR cleanup (carried from S123): remove the now-redundant as.character/as.Date no-op conversions in test_getPotentialParents.R:4-7 + test_modPotentialParents.R:120-125. Behavior-preserving; re-run suite. - (A3) Vignette nit: vignettes/articles/studbook-quality-control.qmd:91 omits the sex column (carried from S116). - (B) Resume the CRAN plan — Phase 3 (NEWS rewrite + version bump → 2.0.0; fold in S112–S124, incl. S123/S124’s user-facing data-type changes). - (C) Merge add-methodology → master — after this commit add-methodology is 5 ahead of origin/add-methodology (S120–S124) and carries 7 commits not on origin/master (S118–S124); rides a future PR #53 (Learning 112 — fetch first, target origin/master, PR mechanism). Do NOT bundle (A9) with (A8)/(A10)/(B) (FM #18/#25); do NOT start the addGenotype hardening without the owner picking (A9) (a code + test change).

Key files (this session): CHANGED (committed): data/rhesusGenotypes.RData (re-exported — 3 columns coerced to character, values preserved, 734→608 B); data-raw/rhesusGenotypes.R (NEW — idempotent coercion script); tests/testthat/test_rhesusGenotypes.R (NEW — 22 type+value assertions); R/data.R (1 #' edit, id doc line ~347 factor→character); man/rhesusGenotypes.Rd (regenerated, 1-line diff); CHANGELOG.md (S124 [Unreleased]); PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 117); SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). No NAMESPACE, DESCRIPTION, NEWS, .Rbuildignore, addGenotype.R, or other-data-object change. Read firsthand: the live rhesusGenotypes structure (probe), R/addGenotype.R, R/data.R:338-354, tests/testthat/test_addGenotype.R, tests/testthat/test_rhesusPedigree.R (mirror), data-raw/rhesusPedigree.R (mirror), inst/extdata/obfuscated_rhesus_mhc_breeder_genotypes.csv. Transient (removed, NOT committed): _s124_probe.R, _s124_smoke.R, _s124_consistency.R, _s124_groundtruth.R, _s124_phase3e.R.

Gotchas: (1) data-raw/ pattern (carried): data objects are .RData (not .rda); re-export by overwriting in place — save(obj, file="data/X.RData", compress="xz") — and do NOT use usethis::use_data (writes a lowercase .rda duplicate). ^data-raw$ is already build-ignored. The new data-raw/rhesusGenotypes.R is idempotent (re-run is a no-op). (2) NEW — addGenotype() is FRAGILE to factor inputs (option A9): genoDict[genotype[, 2L]] indexes a name-keyed vector by a factor → uses INTEGER CODES not labels → inconsistent allele encoding. The shipped rhesusGenotypes is now character so the EXAMPLE is correct, but the FUNCTION is unhardened — do NOT assume it’s fixed. (3) addGenotype() emits first/second integer codes via a name-keyed dict (c() of the two allele columns → sort(unique()) → +10000L); character allele columns are REQUIRED for a consistent encoding. (4) rhesusGenotypes doc cites a nonexistent rhesusGenotypes.csv (R/data.R:345); real source is obfuscated_rhesus_mhc_breeder_genotypes.csv (option A10). (5) Redundant no-op conversions remain in test_getPotentialParents.R:4-7 + test_modPotentialParents.R:120-125 — carried deferred REFACTOR (A8). (6) si.re/si re are INTENTIONAL raw fixtures (carried) — do NOT “clean” them. (7) Shell backslash trap (recurring) — write R probes to a FILE and Rscript it. (8) Markdown IS enabled in roxygen — keep prose free of stray markdown. (9) Standing uncommitted: .DS_Store (modified) + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked) — owner’s keep-call. (10) Branch state: after this commit add-methodology is 5 ahead of origin/add-methodology (S120–S124) and carries 7 commits not on origin/master (S118–S124) — rides a future PR #53 (Learning 112). (11) Consistency-check trap: verify addGenotype’s allele encoding at the genotype-row (31) level, NOT by indexing the 375-row merged output with a 31-row logical (that recycles and prints a spurious FALSE). (12) Carried CRAN gotchas: NEWS.md from NEWS.Rmd; renv explicit-snapshot omits Suggests; the package is ARCHIVED on CRAN (Learning 95); full R CMD check not run this session (devtools absent).

What Session 123 Did

Deliverable: owner’s pick (A6) — re-exported the bundled rhesusPedigree object so its degraded columns carry canonical types matching examplePedigree, preserving every value. (DONE — id/sire/dam factor→character, birth factor-of-date-strings→Date, exit all-NA logical→Date all-NA; sex/gen/age already correct, left unchanged. All values preserved: dim 375×8, 375 unique ids, NA patterns intact, BRI2MW birth 1998-12-06, range 1970-07-03..2013-12-21.) This is the first real DATA change in the S118–S123 run — touches data/, adds tests, runs the full RED→GREEN→REFACTOR cycle. Scoped to data/rhesusPedigree.RData + new data-raw/rhesusPedigree.R + new tests/testthat/test_rhesusPedigree.R + the rhesusPedigree doc in R/data.R + man/rhesusPedigree.Rd + .Rbuildignore; no NAMESPACE/DESCRIPTION/NEWS/other-data-object change (NAMESPACE proven byte-identical). Started / Completed: 2026-06-17 Status: DONE. Full strict TDD (declared every response). Five gated AskUserQuestions: (1) pre-RED scope — Type-correctness vs full-canonical-match vs minimal (owner chose Type-correctness: fix the genuine type bugs, keep sex factor F,M and gen integer — no gratuitous widening); (2) pre-RED reproducibility — add a data-raw/ script vs in-place-only (owner chose add data-raw script); (3) PRE-RED→RED; (4) RED→GREEN; (5) GREEN→REFACTOR. The 3 transition gates are the project’s mandatory TDD contract; the 2 pre-RED questions were the genuine forks. The determined fixes (birth→Date, exit→Date, id/sire/dam→character — all ground-truth-determined) were the presented baseline, not re-gated (Learning 113). 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized under ultracode: a read-only 4-prong investigation workflow (provenance / target-structure / dependents / test-patterns) to frame the scope decision, then SOLO implementation + oracle verification (the work itself is a deterministic coercion — a fan-out would be theater). New session within the conversation: claimed it (1B stub BEFORE technical work). - 🚨 REAL WORK #1 — PROVENANCE forced COERCE-not-REGENERATE (→ Learning 116): the .rda has no reproducible generator — obfuscated from inst/extdata/rhesusPedigree_fromCenter.csv via obfuscatePed(), hand-saved 2020-02-02 (commit 31c4679d), never scripted/seeded. Obfuscation is non-deterministic, so re-deriving from the CSV would produce different obfuscated ids/dates than the shipped object (and desync sibling objects sharing those ids). The committed data-raw/rhesusPedigree.R therefore loads the existing object and coerces its TYPES while preserving all VALUES (idempotent), rather than regenerating. “Re-export” for an opaque artifact = value-preserving type coercion, not regeneration. - 🚨 REAL WORK #2 — INVESTIGATED BEFORE GATING to PRUNE UNSAFE OPTIONS (→ Learning 116): two plausible-sounding options were eliminated before the owner ever saw them — “re-derive from CSV” (non-deterministic) and “drop exit” (getPotentialParents.R:68-76,114 reads ba$exit; dropping breaks the object’s main consumer). Verified firsthand that no test asserts the current factor/logical types (grep: none) so the fix breaks nothing. The scope gate offered only safe choices. - 🚨 REAL WORK #3 — BUNDLED the doc + man with the data change: changing the object’s types without updating its R/data.R doc + regenerating man/ in the SAME session would have reopened the exact data-doc-mismatch class S114–S122 spent eight sessions closing. So one coherent deliverable: .rda + data-raw/ + doc reversal (id factor→character; birth factor of birth-date strings→Date vector; exit logical→Date) + man/ regen + tests + .Rbuildignore. - RED: test_rhesusPedigree.R (31 assertions pinning types AND value-preservation — dim, id-all-unique, sire/dam 124 NA, birth range + BRI2MW 1998-12-06, exit all-NA), confirmed failing on the degraded object (id/sire/dam integer-factors; birth factor — min errored “not meaningful for factors”; exit not S3). GREEN: ran the coercion (file 7449→4340 B via xz), updated the 3 doc lines, regenerated man/rhesusPedigree.Rd (3-line diff). REFACTOR: N/A (own code clean) — the now-redundant as.character/as.Date no-ops in test_getPotentialParents.R/test_modPotentialParents.R were flagged, not removed (adjacent scope, FM #8; owner chose skip). - Used file-based R probes from the first command — dodged the recurring shell-backslash trap (Learnings 108/109); no misfires. - [news-vs-changelog]: dev-process history → CHANGELOG here. This change SHIPS and is user-facing (column types changed) → fold into the CRAN Phase 3 NEWS rewrite at 2.0.0 (flagged, NOT edited now; more NEWS-worthy than the S114–S122 prose fixes). NO auto-memory — the work lives in the repo ([[consult-project-source-of-truth]]). [macos-dupe-scan]: committed ONLY the repo files; excluded .DS_Store (modified) + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked, standing keep); the seven _s123_*.R probe files were TEMPORARY (removed before commit; no Rplots.pdf).

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): DONE — this IS a runtime-behavior change. “Suite passes” was necessary but insufficient (the potential-parents tests do their own as.character/as.Date, now no-ops, which could mask a still-wrong type). The conclusive test ran getPotentialParents() on the SHIPPED corrected object directly, with no conversions (birth is Date, id is character) → ran cleanly, 50-element result; the suite’s positional dam/sire assertions pass. (Note: getPotentialParents() returns an unnamed positional list — the tests index by position, e.g. dams_1=element 1, dams_4=element 4 — so names(result) are NOT focal ids; an early naive spot-check on that was reconciled, not a defect.) Full R CMD check not run (devtools absent) — proportionate equivalent = checkRd 0 + load_all 162 + full-suite 0/0 + NAMESPACE byte-diff + the consumer smoke test.

Session 122 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 123): Score 9/10. S122 pre-named THIS deliverable precisely as option (A6), characterized the degradation firsthand and accurately (birth factor-of-strings, exit all-NA logical, id/sire/dam/sex factors), and correctly flagged it as a DATA change touching data/ + tests under strict TDD — a separate, larger deliverable than a doc fix (which is exactly how it played out). Every carried gotcha held: the near-identical examplePedigree/rhesusPedigree describe blocks (I anchored the id edit on the following sire line so I wouldn’t hit rhesusGenotypes’s identical id line at R/data.R:347), the roxygenise(roclets="rd") regen method, the shell-backslash→file-probe trap, the standing .DS_Store/PED_GV_AUDIT keep-call, and the branch state. ROI very high; no false credit. The −1: its suggested-next phrasing for A6 (“so birth is a real Date and exit holds real dates (or is dropped)”) contained one infeasible sub-option (there are NO exit dates in the source — exit is all-NA there too) and one unsafe sub-option (dropping breaks getPotentialParents). Right about the bug; slightly off on the proposed remedies — both resolved by this session’s investigation.

Self-assessment (Session 123): 9/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER read, notes top, dashboard 98/100, git, 14 issues, ghost-check → HEAD 17cd5922=S122, clean), reported, STOPPED for the owner’s pick; claimed the session (1B stub BEFORE technical work). Headline strengths: (1) investigated provenance + dependents BEFORE gating and pruned unsafe options (re-derive-from-CSV, drop-exit) so the owner chose only among safe ones; (2) chose coerce-in-place over regenerate — correct given the non-reproducible non-deterministic generator — and made it reproducible+idempotent via committed data-raw/; (3) verified everything firsthand (structure, BRI2MW date, gen/age types, no type-asserting tests, exit required) — never relied on the workflow’s relayed claims; (4) value-preservation in the RED tests (not just types) + a real Phase-3E smoke test on the shipped object (not the can-be-masked suite-passes signal); (5) bundled doc+man+tests with the data change so the data-doc-mismatch class stayed closed; (6) proved zero regressions (new tests pass, full suite 0/0, checkRd 0, NAMESPACE byte-identical, 162 exports, diff confined to intended files); (7) correct TDD discipline (declared every response; gated the genuine forks + the 3 mandatory transitions; did not re-gate ground-truth-determined fixes); (8) used file-based probes from command #1; 0 corrections; plain language; removed all temp probes. Weaknesses (honest): (a) full R CMD check NOT run (devtools absent) — so the new .rda’s compression NOTE and the ^data-raw$ build-ignore are unconfirmed by the actual build (xz is best-available, so a compression NOTE is unlikely, but unverified); (b) my first smoke-test spot-check (BRI2MW %in% names(pp)) was naive about the unnamed-positional-list result — caught and reconciled, but cost a round-trip a closer read of the test would have avoided; (c) the 4-prong investigation workflow had mild overlap (provenance/target-structure both read data.R). I reserve 10 for a delivery that is also full-check-verified.

⇒ SUGGESTED NEXT = owner’s pick. (A6) is DONE — rhesusPedigree now ships canonical types; the data-DOC and data-ARTIFACT classes for it are both closed. Nothing mid-flight. Natural options (plain ASCII labels): - (A7) Apply the same re-export to rhesusGenotypes (DATA, smaller): its id is still a factor (S122 documented it accurately as factor). The data-raw/ pattern now exists; de-factoring id→character would match the convention. Check first: rhesusGenotypes/rhesusPedigree share ids and addGenotype() merges by id — both character would still merge fine, but verify. Same TDD shape as A6. - (A8) Adjacent REFACTOR cleanup (small): remove the now-redundant as.character/as.Date no-op conversions in test_getPotentialParents.R:4-7 and test_modPotentialParents.R:120-125 (deferred from this session’s REFACTOR). Behavior-preserving; re-run suite. - (A3) Vignette nit: vignettes/articles/studbook-quality-control.qmd:91 omits the sex column (carried from S116). - (B) Resume the CRAN plan — Phase 3 (NEWS rewrite + version bump → 2.0.0; fold in S112–S123, incl. S123’s user-facing data-type change). - (C) Merge add-methodology → master — after this commit add-methodology is 4 ahead of origin/add-methodology (S120–S123) and carries 6 commits not on origin/master (S118–S123); rides a future PR #53 (Learning 112 — fetch first, target origin/master, PR mechanism). Do NOT bundle (A7) with (A8)/(B) (FM #18/#25); do NOT start the rhesusGenotypes re-export without the owner picking (A7) (data + test change).

Key files (this session): CHANGED (committed): data/rhesusPedigree.RData (re-exported — coerced types, values preserved); data-raw/rhesusPedigree.R (NEW — idempotent coercion script); tests/testthat/test_rhesusPedigree.R (NEW — 31 type+value assertions); R/data.R (3 #' edits in the rhesusPedigree block: id L361 factor→character; birth L370 factor-of-strings→Date vector; exit L371-372 logical→Date); man/rhesusPedigree.Rd (regenerated, 3-line diff); .Rbuildignore (added ^data-raw$); CHANGELOG.md (S123 [Unreleased]); PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 116); SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). Transient (removed, NOT committed): _s123_probe.R, _s123_verify.R, _s123_postcheck.R, _s123_regen.R, _s123_regress.R, _s123_smoke.R, _s123_smoke2.R.

Gotchas: (1) data-raw/ pattern is now established. Data objects are stored as .RData (not .rda); re-export by overwriting in place — save(obj, file="data/X.RData", compress="xz") — and do NOT use usethis::use_data (it writes a lowercase .rda, creating a DUPLICATE). data-raw/ is build-ignored (^data-raw$). (2) data-raw/rhesusPedigree.R is idempotent — re-running it on the already-corrected object is a no-op. (3) rhesusGenotypes is the next opaque .rda (id still factor) — option (A7); shares ids with rhesusPedigree and is merged by addGenotype() (both-character merge is fine, but verify). (4) getPotentialParents() returns an UNNAMED positional list — index by position (dams_1=elem 1, dams_4=elem 4); names(result) are NOT focal ids. (5) Redundant no-op conversions remain in test_getPotentialParents.R:4-7 + test_modPotentialParents.R:120-125 — deferred REFACTOR (A8), safe to remove. (6) si.re/si re are INTENTIONAL raw fixtures (carried) — do NOT “clean” them. (7) Shell backslash trap (recurring) — write R probes to a FILE and Rscript it. (8) Markdown IS enabled in roxygen — keep prose free of stray markdown. (9) Standing uncommitted: .DS_Store (modified) + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked) — owner’s keep-call. (10) Branch state: after this commit add-methodology is 4 ahead of origin/add-methodology (S120–S123) and carries 6 commits not on origin/master (S118–S123) — rides a future PR #53 (Learning 112). (11) Carried CRAN gotchas: NEWS.md from NEWS.Rmd; renv explicit-snapshot omits Suggests; the package is ARCHIVED on CRAN (Learning 95); full R CMD check not run this session (devtools absent).

What Session 122 Did

Deliverable: owner’s pick (A5), promoted to the full sweep — a one-pass factual-claim audit of all 24 data docs in R/data.R against their live objects (dims, column names/order, types, factor levels, counts, prose), fixing every confirmed discrepancy, then regenerating the 4 affected man/ pages. (DONE — 9 fixes across 4 objects, all oracle-verified: checkRd 0 problems ×4, NAMESPACE byte-identical, load_all 162 exports, full-suite regression 0 fail / 0 error, rendered .Rd ↔︎ source in sync.) Scoped to R/data.R + 4 data man/ pages; NAMESPACE/DESCRIPTION/data/ unchanged. Started / Completed: 2026-06-17 Status: DONE. TDD phase = N/A (roxygen prose — only #' comment lines changed, no executable R, no package logic or test surface; RED vacuous; declared every response, as S108–S121). Two gated AskUserQuestions — (1) scope (narrow 2-doc fix vs. full 24-doc audit; owner chose full, the deliverable Learning 114 had proposed); (2) the 2 data-artifact findings (document actual types now vs. leave docs + queue a data fix; owner chose document actual types now). The 7 unambiguous doc-bug fixes were ground-truth-determined → not separately gated (Learning 113 — over-gating avoided). 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized under ultracode as oracle ground truth computed once by me + a workflow (5 independent lens scanners for completeness over all 24 docs, then an adversarial refuter per unique discrepancy) → Learning 115. New session within the conversation: claimed it (1B stub BEFORE technical work). - The audit (12 candidates → 10 confirmed, 2 rejected): the adversarial pass earned its keep both ways — it independently re-derived and confirmed all 10 real mismatches AND rejected flagging rhesusPedigree sire/dam (they make NO type claim — “the male/female parent … NA for unknown”; “correcting” them to “factor” would invent a fix). Bounding scope is the skeptic’s symmetric job in a fix-audit. - The 9 fixes (each verified firsthand against the live object before authoring — Learning 109/111): examplePedigree \item{sex} "M","F","U" → "F","M","H","U" (live factor: 4 levels incl. zero-count H); \item{ancestry} “character vector or NA … free-form text” → “factor with levels: INDIAN, CHINESE, HYBRID, JAPANESE, OTHER, UNKNOWN …” (a closed 6-level factor, not free-form character). rhesusPedigree \item{id} “character” → “factor”; \item{sex} "M","F","U" → "F","M" (no U level); \item{birth} “Date vector” → “factor of birth-date strings (282 levels)”; \item{exit} “Date vector” → “logical vector, all NA (no exit dates recorded …)”. rhesusGenotypes \item{id} “character” → “factor”. qcBreeders @description + @source “A list of …” → “A character vector of …” (counts 3 males/26 females confirmed correct). Edited rhesusPedigree’s whole \describe block as one chunk because it is near-identical to examplePedigree’s — the shared lines (id -- character, Date vector of birth/exit) are correct in examplePedigree (id IS character; birth/exit ARE Date) and must NOT be touched; block-level disambiguation kept the edits surgical. - 🚨 2nd-order finding — DATA artifact (gated, doc fixed, DATA flagged for a future session): rhesusPedigree ships birth as a factor-of-strings (not Date) and exit as all-375-NA logical (no exit dates at all) — the obfuscation degraded these columns. Owner chose to document the actual types now (docs are now accurate to the shipped object), but the underlying DATA oddity may warrant a re-export (birth → real Date; exit → real dates or drop the column). That is a DATA change (touches data/, tests, TDD) — flagged as option (A6), NOT done this session (FM #8, “1 and done”). - Used file-based R probes from the first command — dodged the recurring shell-backslash trap (Learnings 108/109); no misfires. Oracle ground truth derived once by me (deterministic); the fan-out reasoned over it; R execution kept with the orchestrator to avoid concurrent-temp-file races. - [news-vs-changelog]: dev-process history → CHANGELOG here. These help pages SHIP → fold S122’s 9 fixes into the CRAN Phase 3 NEWS rewrite at 2.0.0 (flagged, NOT edited now). NO auto-memory — the work lives in the repo ([[consult-project-source-of-truth]]). [macos-dupe-scan]: committed ONLY the repo files; excluded .DS_Store (modified) + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked, standing keep); the eight _s122_*.R/.js probe/workflow files were TEMPORARY (removed before commit; no Rplots.pdf).

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A — STATED, not silently skipped. Roxygen/Rd prose changes NO package or app runtime behavior (no logic, dispatch, config, or test surface; NAMESPACE/DESCRIPTION/data/ unchanged — NAMESPACE proven byte-identical). Verification appropriate to the deliverable = per-page checkRd + load_all + the full-suite regression read + the audit/adversarial-verify workflow, all done. Full R CMD check not run (devtools absent; the above is the proportionate equivalent for a generated-doc/source change). NOT FM #24.

Session 121 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 122): Score 10/10. S121 pre-named THIS deliverable precisely as option (A5), gave the exact two flagged sex docs with line numbers (examplePedigree R/data.R:25; rhesusPedigree R/data.R:366) AND the exact wrong/right level sets (F,M,H,U; F,M), AND — crucially — explicitly suggested promoting it to a full 24-doc batch sweep (Learning 114), which is what the owner chose. The completeness/recurring-class framing it added was directly load-bearing: I didn’t have to rediscover that the bug class spans many docs; S121 told me to scan the whole family. Its gotchas (verify firsthand before authoring; shell-backslash → file-based probes; si.re/si re intentional; roxygenise regen method; standing .DS_Store/PED_GV_AUDIT keep-call; branch state) all held exactly. The two sex bugs were real and correctly characterized firsthand. ROI very high; no false credit; no inaccuracy; the predecessor literally pre-scoped the higher-ROI deliverable. A clean 10.

Self-assessment (Session 122): 9/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER read, notes top, dashboard 98/100, git, 14 issues, ghost-check → HEAD b117a9c8 = S121, clean), reported, STOPPED for the owner’s pick; claimed the session (1B stub BEFORE technical work). Headline strengths: (1) derived authoritative ground truth for all 24 objects once and verified every fix against the live object before authoring — never edited from a flag’s wording; (2) gated the two genuine forks (scope; data-artifact handling) and did NOT re-gate the 7 ground-truth-determined doc-bugs (Learning 113); (3) the completeness fan-out earned itself — 5 independent lenses + adversarial verify turned the single flagged sex bug into the full class-complete set (10 confirmed) and the adversarial pass bounded scope by rejecting sire/dam non-claims (→ Learning 115); (4) separated doc-bug from data-artifact — surfaced that rhesusPedigree birth/exit are degraded DATA, fixed the doc per the owner’s call, and flagged the DATA for a future session rather than silently enshrining or silently ignoring it; (5) surgical block-level disambiguation of the near-identical examplePedigree/rhesusPedigree describe blocks — left the genuinely-correct shared lines (id character, birth/exit Date in examplePedigree) untouched, verified by grep before AND after; (6) proved zero regressions (regen confined to 4 pages, NAMESPACE byte-identical, checkRd 0×4, load_all 162, suite 0/0); (7) used file-based probes from command #1; 0 corrections; plain language; declared TDD N/A + Phase-3E N/A with reasons; removed all temp probes. Weaknesses (honest): (a) full R CMD check NOT run (devtools absent) — checkRd + load_all + regression + NAMESPACE diff is the proportionate build-equivalent, not the full check; (b) low technical difficulty — the value is the audit rigor, the doc-bug/data-artifact split, and the scope-bounding, not depth; (c) the deliverable closes the data-DOC class but deliberately leaves the data-ARTIFACT (rhesusPedigree birth/exit) open — correct scope discipline, but it means the rhesus example object still ships with degraded columns until a future (A6). I reserve 10 for a delivery that is also full-check-verified.

⇒ SUGGESTED NEXT = owner’s pick. (A5)/the full data-doc audit is done — the 24 data docs are now factually accurate to their live objects (the recurring data-doc bug class S114–S122 is CLOSED at the doc level). Nothing mid-flight. Natural options (plain ASCII labels): - (A6) The rhesusPedigree DATA artifact (NEW — DATA change, not docs): birth ships as a factor-of-strings (not Date) and exit as all-NA logical (no exit dates). The docs now describe this accurately, but the owner may want to re-export rhesusPedigree so birth is a real Date and exit holds real dates (or is dropped). This touches data/ + tests under strict TDD — a separate, larger deliverable than a doc fix. - (A3) Vignette nit: vignettes/articles/studbook-quality-control.qmd:91 omits the sex column (carried from S116; separate artifact from man/). - (B) Resume the CRAN plan — Phase 3 (NEWS rewrite + version bump → 2.0.0; fold in S112–S122 doc repairs). - (C) Merge add-methodology → master — after this commit add-methodology is 3 ahead of origin/add-methodology (unpushed S120 + S121 + S122) and carries 5 commits not yet on origin/master (S118–S122); rides a future PR #53 (Learning 112 — fetch first, target origin/master, PR mechanism). Do NOT bundle (A6) with (A3)/(B) (FM #18/#25); do NOT start the rhesusPedigree data re-export without the owner picking (A6) (it is a data + test change, not a doc change).

Key files (this session): CHANGED (committed): R/data.R (9 #' edits across 4 data-doc blocks: examplePedigree sex+ancestry, rhesusPedigree id+sex+birth+exit, rhesusGenotypes id, qcBreeders description+source); man/examplePedigree.Rd, man/qcBreeders.Rd, man/rhesusGenotypes.Rd, man/rhesusPedigree.Rd (regenerated); CHANGELOG.md (S122 [Unreleased]), PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 115), SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). No NAMESPACE, DESCRIPTION, NEWS, test, CI-workflow, CRAN-vignette, or data/ change. Read firsthand: R/data.R (full file), every live data object’s structure (dims, column classes, factor levels), the 4 rendered .Rd diffs. Transient (removed, NOT committed): _s122_sexprobe.R, _s122_groundtruth.R, _s122_compact.R, _s122_confirm.R, _s122_audit_wf.js, _s122_regen.R, _s122_checkrd.R, _s122_regress.R.

Gotchas: (1) NEW — rhesusPedigree ships DEGRADED columns (option A6): birth is a factor-of-date-strings (282 levels), exit is logical with all 375 values NA, and id/sire/dam/sex are factors (stringsAsFactors-era load). The docs now describe this accurately; if the owner wants real Date birth/exit, that is a data/ re-export + test change, not a doc edit. (2) examplePedigree and rhesusPedigree have NEAR-IDENTICAL \describe blocks — id/sire/dam/sex/gen/birth/exit/age items share text, but examplePedigree’s are CORRECT (id character; birth/exit Date) while rhesusPedigree’s were wrong. When editing either, anchor on a UNIQUE block-level chunk (e.g. the trailing } after age for rhesusPedigree; the ancestry/status/recordStatus tail for examplePedigree) — a single-line \item match will hit the wrong block. Same trap for the \item{id}{-- character column of animal IDs} line (appears at examplePedigree:20 CORRECT, rhesusGenotypes:347 + rhesusPedigree:361 which were wrong). (3) si.re/si re are INTENTIONAL raw fixtures, NOT corruption (carried) — fixColumnNames normalizes them to sire; do NOT “clean” the data columns. (4) Regenerate with roxygen2::roxygenise(package.dir=<abspath>, roclets="rd") (devtools absent); baseline 8.0.0 so regens are clean — confirm via git diff --stat only intended pages changed and git diff --quiet -- NAMESPACE for import neutrality. (5) Shell backslash trap (recurring): write R probes to a FILE and run Rscript file.R. (6) Markdown IS enabled (Roxygen: list(markdown = TRUE)) — keep prose free of stray markdown syntax. (7) Standing uncommitted: .DS_Store (modified) + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked) — owner’s keep-call. (8) Branch state: after this commit add-methodology is 3 ahead of origin/add-methodology (unpushed S120 + S121 + S122) and carries 5 commits not yet on origin/master (S118–S122) — will ride a future PR #53 (Learning 112). (9) Carried CRAN gotchas remain valid: NEWS.md from NEWS.Rmd; renv explicit-snapshot omits ALL Suggests; the package is ARCHIVED on CRAN (Learning 95).

What Session 121 Did

Deliverable: owner’s pick (A4) — repaired the two newly-flagged same-class data-doc bugs S120 found and deliberately left (FM #8): (1) ped1Alleles \item{V2}/{V3}/{V4} all said “iteration 1” → now “iteration 2/3/4”; (2) missing space “column.Unknown dams” in the dam \item of examplePedigree/lacy1989Ped/rhesusPedigree → “column. Unknown dams” — then regenerated the 4 affected man/ pages. (DONE — both fixes adversarially CONFIRMED: 2 refuters returned refuted 0/2 each; rendered .Rd ↔︎ source in sync; repo-wide grep for “column.Unknown” returns NONE; checkRd 0 problems ×4; NAMESPACE byte-identical; load_all 162 exports; full-suite regression 0 fail / 0 error.) Scoped to R/data.R + 4 data man/ pages; NAMESPACE/DESCRIPTION/data/ unchanged. Started / Completed: 2026-06-17 Status: DONE. TDD phase = N/A (roxygen prose — only #' comment lines changed, no executable R line, no package logic or test surface; RED vacuous; declared every response, as S108–S120). No gated AskUserQuestion — both fixes were fully determined by ground truth (ped1Alleles has 4 distinct iteration columns; the parallel sire items were already correctly spaced), so per Learning 113 over-gating was avoided (the owner’s “(A4)” pick was the authorization). 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized under ultracode as SOLO edits + oracle (checkRd/load_all/regression/NAMESPACE byte-diff/rendered-.Rd diff) for structure, PLUS a workflow (2 adversarial refuters + 4 completeness scanners over all 24 data docs) for the non-oracle dimensions (Learning 113/114). New session within the conversation: claimed it (1B stub BEFORE technical work). - The two fixes (each verified against the live object before authoring — Learning 109/111): (1) ped1Alleles \item{V2}/{V3}/{V4} “iteration 1” → “iteration 2”/“3”/“4” (R/data.R 135/138/141; V1 at line 132 correctly stays “iteration 1”). Ground truth: dim(ped1Alleles)=554×6, columns V1,V2,V3,V4,id,parent; V1–V4 are 4 DISTINCT columns (all 6 pairwise identical()=FALSE; 290/554 rows differ); \item{parent} (R/data.R:146) says “each of the 4 gene dropping iterations”. (2) dam \item “column.Unknown dams” → “column. Unknown dams” (R/data.R 24/97/365), matching the already-correct parallel sire items (“column. Unknown sires”). - 🚨 REAL WORK — THE COMPLETENESS SCAN FOUND A 3RD SAME-CLASS BUG (→ Learning 114; FLAGGED, NOT fixed): the \item{sex} of BOTH examplePedigree (R/data.R:25) and rhesusPedigree (R/data.R:366) claims factor with levels: "M", "F", "U", but the live factors are F,M,H,U (examplePedigree — 4 levels, H count 0) and F,M (rhesusPedigree — 2 levels, NO U). Neither matches the doc. The scanner only flagged examplePedigree; I verified firsthand and found rhesusPedigree (identical doc text) is ALSO wrong — a per-group scanner is blind to a cross-group sibling (Learning 114). All other 24-doc factual claims (dims, column names, counts) verified correct. - Used file-based R probes from the first command — dodged the recurring shell-backslash trap (Learnings 108/109); no misfires. - [news-vs-changelog]: dev-process history → CHANGELOG here. These help pages SHIP → fold S121’s two fixes into the CRAN Phase 3 NEWS rewrite at 2.0.0 (flagged, NOT edited now). NO auto-memory — the work lives in the repo ([[consult-project-source-of-truth]]). [macos-dupe-scan]: committed ONLY the repo files; excluded .DS_Store (modified) + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked, standing keep); the four _s121_*.R probe files were TEMPORARY (removed before commit; no Rplots.pdf).

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A — STATED, not silently skipped. Roxygen/Rd prose changes NO package or app runtime behavior (no logic, dispatch, config, or test surface; NAMESPACE/DESCRIPTION/data/ unchanged — NAMESPACE proven byte-identical). Verification appropriate to the deliverable = per-page checkRd + load_all + the full-suite regression read + the adversarial verify/completeness workflow, all done. Full R CMD check not run (devtools absent; the above is the proportionate equivalent for a generated-doc/source change). NOT FM #24.

(2) rhesusGenotypes \details: garbled “There are object.” → “There are 31 rows and 3 columns.” (object is 31×3, 31 unique ids — consistent with the neighboring “Represents 31 animals” and roxygen’s auto-\format). (3) exampleNprcgenekeeprConfig @description: “…configuration file created the SNPRC.” → “…created at the SNPRC.” (inserted the missing locative preposition S117 had diagnosed). - 🚨 REAL WORK — THE COMPLETENESS CRITIC FOUND 2 MORE SAME-CLASS BUGS THE AUTHORIZED LIST MISSED (→ Learning 113; FLAGGED, NOT fixed): scanning all 24 \docType{data} blocks against their live objects surfaced two bugs of the same class outside the authorized three: (i) ped1Alleles — V2/V3/V4 \items all say “iteration 1” though identical(V1,V2/V3/V4)=FALSE (four distinct iterations) and the block’s id/parent items say “each of the 4 gene dropping iterations” — V2/V3/V4 should read iterations 2/3/4 (R/data.R 134–142, renders into man/ped1Alleles.Rd); (ii) missing space “column.Unknown dams are indicated with \code{NA}” in the dam item of examplePedigree/lacy1989Ped/rhesusPedigree (R/data.R 24 / 97 / 365) — the parallel sire items in each are correctly spaced (“column. Unknown sires…”). Both confirmed firsthand before flagging. The real target is origin/master (7a8433b3 = “Merge pull request #51”). After git fetch, add-methodology (55331c17) and origin/master were DIVERGED: 18 ahead / 3 behind, where the 3 “behind” are only the PR #41/#43/#51 merge bubbles (master accumulates them; add-methodology stays linear and never pulls them back). Fork point 14032640 (S100). So this was never a fast-forward. - 🚨 REAL WORK #2 — READ THE MERGE MECHANISM OUT OF THE HISTORY (→ Learning 112): the “Merge pull request #41/#43/#51 from rmsharp/add-methodology” run on origin/master, plus a linear feature branch, encodes a PR-based workflow (commit 6175fe66 even records “PR #51 merged to master”). So the correct action was a fresh PR #52, not a local --no-ff merge + direct push (which would deviate and may hit branch protection). Confirmed the outward mechanism + who-merges with the owner before acting. - Actions taken (outward): git push origin add-methodology → fast-forward ef1b86e8..55331c17 (published S112–S117 to the remote branch; origin/add-methodology now 55331c17). gh pr create --base master --head add-methodology → PR #52 (verified: base master, head add-methodology, 18 commits S101–S117, OPEN, MERGEABLE). Session 117 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 118): Score 8/10. S117 correctly named (C) merge to master as the real standing pending action and gave the unpushed-commit count (“6 ahead of origin/add-methodology (S112–S117)”) — directly load-bearing (I had to push exactly those 6 first). That single fact (PR workflow against origin/master, not a local ff) is the crux of executing the merge, and I had to discover it via git fetch + topology analysis. Accurate as far as it went (the local-master count was literally true), but it pointed at the wrong “master” — a documented trap now captured as Learning 112.

Self-assessment (Session 118): 9/10. Claimed the session (1B stub before any mutation). Headline strengths: (1) did NOT take the naive fast-forward bait — git branch -vv + git fetch + merge-base analysis revealed the local-master-is-stale red herring and the true divergence before any action (→ Learning 112); (2) read the PR-based merge mechanism out of the commit history (#41/#43/#51 bubbles + linear feature branch + the 6175fe66 coda) and matched it rather than inventing a local merge; (3) gated the outward, hard-to-reverse main-branch action with one AskUserQuestion (who-merges) despite an explicit instruction — owner chose push+open+owner-merges, and I stopped before merging exactly as asked; (4) pushed only the already-verified committed work and opened a well-formed PR (verified base/head/18-commits/MERGEABLE via gh pr view); (5) kept the PR a clean 18-commit S101–S117 record for the owner’s review by NOT pushing this session’s close-out commit (flagged below); (6) plain language; declared TDD N/A + Phase-3E N/A with reasons; 0 corrections. Weaknesses (honest): (a) low technical difficulty — the value is the topology diagnosis + mechanism-matching + the gate, not complexity; (b) I leave one deliberately-unpushed local commit (this close-out) — a clearly-flagged handoff item, not a silent dangling state; (c) the merge itself is not yet in master — by design (owner merges), so the deliverable is “PR opened,” not “merged.”

What Session 117 Did

Deliverable: owner’s pick (A2) — the data-doc short-@title rewrite: rewrote the roxygen TITLE of all 24 datasets in R/data.R from long “X is a <type> …” run-on sentences into short noun-phrase titles, moving the descriptive detail into @description, then regenerated the 24 data man/ pages. (DONE — verified by tools::checkRd() 0 problems on all 24 pages + NAMESPACE byte-identical (git diff --quiet) + load_all (162 exports) + full-suite regression read (0 fail / 0 error) + a 3-lens adversarial critic panel returning 0 block / 0 should-fix / 3 nits.) Scoped to R/data.R + 24 data man/ pages; NAMESPACE, DESCRIPTION, data/ unchanged. Started / Completed: 2026-06-17 Status: DONE. TDD phase = N/A (roxygen prose; no executable R line changed — only #' comments — so no package logic or test surface; RED vacuous; declared every response, as S108–S116). One gated pre-RED AskUserQuestion (two questions: scope = all 24 vs. worst-8; style = short noun phrase vs. name-led — owner chose all 24 + short noun phrase, grounded in a pedGood before/after preview). No TDD phase-transition gates (none apply). 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized under ultracode as SOLO edits + oracle (checkRd/load_all/tests) for the structural dimension, PLUS a 3-lens critic workflow for the NON-oracle quality dimension (title aptness/accuracy/consistency — which no oracle can judge) → Learning 111. New session within the conversation: claimed it (1B stub BEFORE technical work), did the full Phase 0 reads this same turn. - What changed (1 source file, 24 regenerated man/ pages, 0 new committed files): R/data.R — 24 data-doc blocks restructured: short noun-phrase title, blank line, then the moved descriptive sentence as @description; \describe/@examples/@source/structure-dumps preserved byte-for-byte. man/ (24 regenerated): exampleNprcgenekeeprConfig, examplePedigree, finalRpt, focalAnimals, lacy1989Ped, lacy1989PedAlleles, ped1Alleles, pedDuplicateIds, pedFemaleSireMaleDam, pedGood, pedInvalidDates, pedMissingBirth, pedOne, pedSameMaleIsSireAndDam, pedSix, pedWithGenotype, pedWithGenotypeReport, qcBreeders, qcPed, qcPedGvReport, smallPed, smallPedTree, rhesusGenotypes, rhesusPedigree. Plus CHANGELOG.md (S117 [Unreleased]), PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 111), SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). No NAMESPACE, DESCRIPTION, NEWS, test, CI-workflow, CRAN-vignette, or data/ change. - 🚨 REAL WORK #1 — VERIFIED EVERY COUNT BEFORE AUTHORING IT; FOUND qcPed SELF-CONTRADICTS (→ Learning 111): before moving any “N rows and M columns” sentence into a description, I checked all 10 count-bearing docs against the loaded objects. Exactly one was wrong: qcPed title said “277 rows and 6 columns” but the object is 280×8 — and roxygen’s auto-\format already read “280 rows and 8 columns”, so the page literally contradicted itself. Wrote the accurate 280×8 in the new description and flagged the correction (the S115/S116 verify-and-correct pattern). All 9 others (incl. ped1Alleles 554×6, the six error-set peds, pedWithGenotype 280×12) verified accurate, preserved verbatim. - 🚨 REAL WORK #2 — PAIRED SOLO+ORACLE WITH AN ADVERSARIAL PANEL FOR THE SUBJECTIVE DIMENSION (→ Learning 111): checkRd/load_all/tests prove the rewrite RENDERS and is structurally clean, but cannot judge whether a title is APT/accurate/non-duplicative. So I ran a 3-lens critic workflow (accuracy / completeness / style+consistency), each lens grounding itself in git show HEAD:R/data.R + the loaded objects. Result 0 block / 0 should-fix / 3 nits: (a) examplePedigree’s \describe documents \item{recordStats} but the actual column is recordStatus — PRE-EXISTING (identical in HEAD), in a \describe block I only touched at the title → flagged, not fixed (FM #8), verified real against the data first; (b) smallPed “Small hypothetical pedigree (17 animals)” was a confusable near-duplicate of lacy1989Ped “Small hypothetical pedigree (Lacy 1989)” → applied the disambiguation to “Hypothetical 17-animal pedigree” (improving my own un-previewed draft); (c) “studbook” vs “pedigree” for the 6 QC fixtures → kept studbook (owner approved it in the style preview; it’s defensible — studbook input fixtures that represent pedigrees, feeding qcStudbook), noted for future. - ped1Alleles made well-formed: two ## Copyright comment lines interrupted its roxygen block (between title and @format); relocated them above the block so it is contiguous. Regenerated .Rd carries title + @description + @format cleanly. - Used file-based R probes from the first command — dodged the recurring shell-backslash trap (Learnings 108/109) that bit S112–S114; no misfires this session. - [news-vs-changelog]: dev-process history → CHANGELOG here. These help pages SHIP → fold S117’s new titles into the CRAN Phase 3 NEWS rewrite at 2.0.0 (flagged, NOT edited now). NO auto-memory — the work lives in the repo ([[consult-project-source-of-truth]]). [macos-dupe-scan]: committed ONLY the repo files; excluded .DS_Store (modified) + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked, standing keep); the five _s117_*.R probe/script files were TEMPORARY (removed before commit).

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A — STATED, not silently skipped. Roxygen/Rd prose changes NO package or app runtime behavior (no logic, dispatch, config, or test surface; NAMESPACE/DESCRIPTION/data/ unchanged — proven NAMESPACE byte-identical). Verification appropriate to the deliverable = the per-page checkRd + load_all + the full-suite regression read + the adversarial quality panel, all done. Full R CMD check not run (devtools absent; the above is the proportionate equivalent for a generated-doc/source change). NOT FM #24.

Session 116 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 117): Score 9/10. S116 pre-named THIS deliverable as suggested-next option (A2) (“give the data docs proper short @titles with detail moved to @description — e.g. finalRpt‘s 4-sentence run-on; the error-set docs’ titles are now their full first sentence”), so I located the scope instantly. Its gotchas held exactly and saved real time: the shell-backslash trap → write file-based R probes (I used file-based probes from command #1, zero misfires); regen with roxygen2::roxygenise(package.dir=<abspath>) (devtools absent), baseline 8.0.0 so the regen confined to my intended pages; the NAMESPACE byte-identical check (git diff --quiet) as the import-neutrality oracle; and — critically — si.re/si re are INTENTIONAL raw fixtures, do NOT “clean” them (I preserved them and explicitly told the critic panel not to flag them). The standing .DS_Store/PED_GV_AUDIT keep-call held. ROI very high. The −1: S116’s A2 description referenced only finalRpt + the error-set docs as examples without noting the full set is 24 data docs sharing one consistent “X is a…” anti-pattern (I had to inventory the scope myself), and it could not foresee the latent qcPed count error (277×6 vs 280×8) or the examplePedigree recordStats→recordStatus drift — both discoverable only by probing/critic this session. Accurate within what a flag could know; no false credit.

Self-assessment (Session 117): 9/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER in full, notes top, dashboard 98/100, git, 14 issues, ghost-check → HEAD 674b772d = S116, clean), reported, STOPPED for the owner’s pick; claimed the session (1B stub BEFORE technical work). Headline strengths: (1) inventoried the true scope (24 data docs = 24 .rda = 24 \docType{data} pages, complete and self-contained) before posing the gated AskUserQuestion, which carried a concrete pedGood before/after preview + ASCII-only labels ([[ascii-only-in-question-options]]); (2) verified every count-bearing claim against the live object before authoring — caught qcPed’s self-contradiction and corrected it (Learning 111, extends 109); (3) right-sized SOLO+oracle for structure but fanned out a 3-lens adversarial panel for the non-oracle quality dimension — the legitimate, non-theater use of a critic workflow (it validated the rewrites AND surfaced a real near-duplicate + a pre-existing content bug) → Learning 111; (4) respected the owner-decision boundary — kept the owner-approved “studbook” when the panel merely preferred “pedigree”, but freely improved my own un-previewed smallPed draft; (5) held scope discipline — FLAGGED (did not fix) the recordStats→recordStatus drift, the garbled rhesusGenotypes fragment, and the created the SNPRC wording (FM #8), verifying the first against the data; (6) proved zero regressions (checkRd 0 ×24, NAMESPACE byte-identical, load_all 162, suite 0/0); (7) used file-based probes from command #1 (no backslash misfires); 0 corrections; plain language; declared TDD N/A + Phase-3E N/A with reasons; removed all temp probes. Weaknesses (honest): (a) full R CMD check NOT run (devtools absent) — checkRd + load_all + regression + NAMESPACE diff is the proportionate build-equivalent, not the full check; (b) the work is moderate-difficulty — the value is the verify-before-authoring rigor + the solo/panel split, not deep technical complexity; (c) I left a within-doc term split (studbook title / “pedigree” description) standing for the 6 fixtures — defensible and owner-approved, but a reader could notice it. I reserve 10 for a delivery that is also full-check-verified with no residual known stylistic split in touched files.

⇒ SUGGESTED NEXT = owner’s pick. (A2) is done; nothing mid-flight. All 24 data-doc titles are now short noun phrases. Natural options (plain ASCII labels): - (A) Adjacent content bugs flagged this session (ships, gated): examplePedigree \describe says \item{recordStats} but the column is recordStatus (verified against the data); rhesusGenotypes’s garbled “There are object.” fragment; exampleNprcgenekeeprConfig’s “created the SNPRC” (missing “at”). Low-risk roxygen-source fixes → man/ regen → sequence with CRAN. - (A3) Vignette accuracy nit (ships, gated): S116’s still-open item — vignettes/articles/studbook-quality-control.qmd:91 lists (ego_id, si.re, dam_id, birth_date), omitting the sex column. One-line article fix. - (B) Resume the CRAN plan — Phase 2b (vignette rebuild timing) or Phase 3 (NEWS rewrite + version bump → 2.0.0; fold in S112–S117’s shipping doc repairs, incl. the 24 new data-doc titles). - (C) Merge add-methodology → master — after this commit 6 ahead of origin/add-methodology (S112–S117) and ~183 ahead of master; the real standing pending action is the merge to master. Key files (this session): CHANGED (committed): R/data.R; the 24 man/*.Rd data pages listed above; CHANGELOG.md, PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 111), SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). Read firsthand: R/data.R (full, all 24 blocks), DESCRIPTION (confirmed Roxygen: list(markdown = TRUE) — focalAnimals’s _id_/__examplePedigree__ are intentional markdown), man/ped1Alleles.Rd (HEAD, to see how roxygen parsed the ##-interrupted block), the loaded data objects (exact dims/colnames for every count-bearing doc), the 3 spot-checked regenerated .Rd (ped1Alleles/qcPed/finalRpt title+description). Transient (removed, NOT committed): _s117_probe.R, _s117_counts.R, _s117_regen.R, _s117_checkrd.R, _s117_regress.R, _s117_critic.R.

Gotchas: (1) Adjacent content bugs FLAGGED, NOT fixed (FM #8) — option (A): examplePedigree’s \describe item is recordStats but the actual column is recordStatus (verified 3694×12, last col recordStatus); rhesusGenotypes’s description still contains the garbled “There are object.” (intentionally preserved this session); exampleNprcgenekeeprConfig says “created the SNPRC”. (2) si.re/si re are INTENTIONAL raw fixtures, NOT corruption (carried from S116) — fixColumnNames normalizes them to sire; do NOT “clean” the data columns. The data-doc column lists (set by S116) were preserved byte-for-byte. (3) qcPed is 280×8 — its title formerly claimed 277×6 (now corrected in the description); roxygen’s auto-\format is a free oracle for shape claims (it already read 280×8). (4) studbook vs pedigree term split — the 6 QC fixtures are titled “…studbook…” (owner-approved preview) while their descriptions say “…full pedigree…”; a future pass could unify to “pedigree” if preferred (panel’s nit). (5) Shell backslash trap (recurring): write R probes to a FILE and run Rscript file.R; simple Rscript -e expressions (no regex/backslashes) are fine. (6) Regenerate with roxygen2::roxygenise(package.dir=<abspath>, roclets="rd") (devtools absent); baseline 8.0.0 so regens are clean — confirm via git diff --stat that only intended pages changed, and git diff --quiet -- NAMESPACE for import neutrality. (7) Markdown IS enabled (Roxygen: list(markdown = TRUE)) — titles must avoid stray markdown syntax (mine are plain phrases + parens, safe); _id_/__examplePedigree__ in focalAnimals are intentional markdown emphasis, preserved. (8) Standing uncommitted: .DS_Store (modified) + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked) — owner’s keep-call; a stray Rplots.pdf can appear from Rscript plot calls — none this session. What Session 116 Did

Deliverable: owner’s pick (A) — repaired the three adjacent doc/data-doc bugs S115 flagged but deliberately left (FM #8): (1) the \code{pedgood} wrong-case cross-reference in all six error-set data docs (dataset is pedGood); (2) the data-doc column-list inaccuracy across the qc data family (owner chose full accuracy doc-fix); (3) the wrapped @importFrom roxygen 8.0.0 lint (owner chose all 10 files) — then regenerated the affected man/. (DONE — verified by NAMESPACE byte-identical + per-page checkRd HEAD-vs-working (0 new) + load_all (162) + full-suite regression read (0 fail / 0 error) + 0 wrapped tags remaining.) Scoped to R/data.R + 10 R/ source files + 6 data man/ pages; NAMESPACE, DESCRIPTION, data/ unchanged. Started / Completed: 2026-06-17 Status: DONE. TDD phase = N/A (roxygen/data-doc prose + @importFrom source reformatting; no executable R line changed — only #' comment lines — so no package logic or test surface; RED vacuous; declared every response, as S108–S115). Two gated pre-RED AskUserQuestions — (i) item-2 data-vs-doc fork (owner: full-accuracy docs); (ii) item-3 3-vs-10-file scope, posed after I discovered the scope was 10 files not 3 and the bug was cosmetic not a hazard (owner: all 10). No TDD phase-transition gates (none apply). 0 stakeholder corrections — two mid-session owner questions (“what does wrapped @importFrom mean?”; “does that make some lines >80 chars?”) were answered (no; longest is 80), not corrections. Right-sized under ultracode as SOLO with firsthand probes + deterministic dry-run script + byte-level oracles (a fan-out re-running grep/checkRd/git diff would be theater — Learnings 108/109; [[consult-project-source-of-truth]]) → Learning 110. New session within the conversation: claimed it (1B stub BEFORE technical work), did the full Phase 0 reads this same turn. - What changed (11 source files, 0 new committed files): R/data.R — item 1: \code{pedgood} -> \code{pedGood} (×6, all error-set cross-ref lists); item 2: column lists corrected to actual data — sire -> si.re in pedGood/pedDuplicateIds/pedFemaleSireMaleDam/pedMissingBirth/pedSameMaleIsSireAndDam, and pedInvalidDates (ego_id, sire, dam_id, sex, birth_date) -> (id, sire, dam, sex, birth) plus \code{birth_date} -> \code{birth}. 10 R/ source files (item 3) — appServer.R, appUI.R, modBreedingGroups.R, modGeneticValue.R, modInput.R, modORIPReporting.R, modPedigree.R, modPotentialParents.R, modPyramid.R, modSummaryStats.R: 20 wrapped @importFrom tags each split into multiple single-line @importFrom pkg ... tags <=80 chars. man/ (6 regenerated): pedDuplicateIds.Rd, pedFemaleSireMaleDam.Rd, pedGood.Rd, pedInvalidDates.Rd, pedMissingBirth.Rd, pedSameMaleIsSireAndDam.Rd. Plus CHANGELOG.md (S116 [Unreleased]), PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 110), SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). No NAMESPACE, DESCRIPTION, NEWS, test, CI-workflow, CRAN-vignette, or data/ change. - 🚨 REAL WORK #1 — DISPROVED MY OWN INHERITED CLAIM WITH A REVERTED REGEN ORACLE (→ Learning 110): I initially restated S115’s item-3 flag as a “latent NAMESPACE-drop hazard.” A fully-reverted roxygenise() probe (git checkout -- man/ NAMESPACE DESCRIPTION after) disproved it: roxygen 8.0.0 prints ✖ @importFrom must be only 1 line long for all wrapped tags but still captures every continuation line — NAMESPACE came back byte-identical (140 importFrom, 0 removed). It’s cosmetic lint, not a regression. Reported the correction to the owner before touching anything (verify-the-claim applies to your OWN characterization). - 🚨 REAL WORK #2 — THE FLAG UNDERCOUNTED SCOPE 3× (→ Learning 110): S115 named 3 mod*.R files; a file-based detector scanning every @importFrom in R/ found 20 wrapped tags across 10 files, incl. non-mod appServer.R/appUI.R. Surfaced the 3-vs-10 boundary to the owner (a flag’s file list is a sample, not an inventory). - 🚨 REAL WORK #3 — THE OBVIOUS FIX WAS INVERTED; si.re IS AN INTENTIONAL FIXTURE (→ Learning 110): si.re (and pedOne’s si re) looked like a corrupted sire to rename in the data. Tracing it through the consumer: R/fixColumnNames.R:22,25 strips spaces then periods (si re -> si.re -> sire), and the end-to-end probe qcStudbook(pedGood) returns canonical columns id, sire, dam, .... So si.re is a deliberate raw studbook-input fixture (make.names("si re") at .rda creation) exercising the QC normalizer — renaming would gut it. Correct fix = the docs, not the data. Posed the data-vs-doc fork; owner chose full-accuracy doc-fix. - Used file-based R probes from the first command — dodged the recurring shell-backslash trap (Learnings 108/109) that bit S112–S114; no misfires this session. - [news-vs-changelog]: dev-process history → CHANGELOG here. These help pages SHIP → fold S116’s corrections into the CRAN Phase 3 NEWS rewrite at 2.0.0 (flagged, NOT edited now). NO auto-memory — the work lives in the repo ([[consult-project-source-of-truth]]). [macos-dupe-scan]: committed ONLY the repo files; excluded .DS_Store (modified) + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked, standing keep); the four _s116_*.R probe/script files were TEMPORARY (removed before commit).

Session 115 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 116): Score 9/10. S115 pre-named THIS deliverable precisely as option (A), listing all three sub-items with the file and the wrong phrase for each (\code{pedgood} wrong-case in the six error-set docs; the si.re-vs-sire data/doc mismatch framed as a gated data-vs-doc fork; the mod*.R @importFrom 8.0.0 errors). Location was instant — zero rediscovery. Its gotchas held exactly: the shell-backslash trap warning (→ I wrote file-based probes from command #1, no misfires); regen with roxygen2::roxygenise(package.dir=<abspath>) (devtools absent), baseline 8.0.0 so the regen confined to my 6 pages; the standing uncommitted .DS_Store/PED_GV_AUDIT keep-call. ROI very high. The −1: S115’s flag (a) undercounted item 3 — it named 3 mod*.R files, but the wrapped-@importFrom issue is 20 tags across 10 files (incl. appServer.R/appUI.R), and it implied a correctness problem without noting it’s cosmetic (NAMESPACE-neutral); and (b) framed item 2 as “si.re vs sire” without noting si.re is an intentional raw fixture (renaming would be wrong). Both are discoveries only execution could make — accurate within what a flag could know; no false credit.

Self-assessment (Session 116): 9/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER in full, notes top, dashboard 98/100, git, 14 issues, ghost-check → HEAD 1ddfb38e = S115, clean), reported, STOPPED for the owner’s pick; claimed the session (1B stub BEFORE technical work). Headline strengths: (1) disproved my OWN inherited “hazard” claim with a cheap reverted regen oracle and corrected the record to the owner before editing (→ Learning 110); (2) inventoried the flagged pattern across the whole repo — caught the 3→10-file undercount — rather than trusting the flag’s file list; (3) traced si.re through fixColumnNames/qcStudbook and found the obvious fix was inverted (it’s a fixture; fix docs not data); (4) posed the two genuine owner-forks as separate evidence-grounded AskUserQuestions with recommendations + ASCII-only labels ([[ascii-only-in-question-options]]); (5) proved item-3 neutrality with the byte-level oracle (NAMESPACE git diff --quiet) and matched the authors’ ≤80-char style (multiple single-line tags, dry-run-audited script per Learning 108) rather than one mega-line; (6) verified end-to-end — checkRd HEAD-vs-working 0-new, load_all 162, regen confined to 6 pages, full-suite regression 0/0; (7) held scope discipline — FLAGGED (did not fix) the vignette si.re/missing-sex nit and the deferred A2 short-title rewrite; (8) answered both mid-session owner questions accurately (incl. catching my own grep -n measurement artifact); 0 corrections; plain language; declared TDD N/A + Phase-3E N/A with reasons; removed all temp probes. Weaknesses (honest): (a) I initially propagated S115’s “hazard” framing into my own report before the probe corrected it — right outcome, but I should have probed the tool’s behavior before characterizing it; (b) the work is intrinsically low-difficulty — the value is the verification rigor + the three corrections (nature/scope/inversion), not technical depth; (c) full R CMD check NOT run (devtools absent) — the NAMESPACE/man diff + checkRd + load_all + regression read is the proportionate build-equivalent, not the full check. I reserve 10 for a delivery that probes-before-characterizing from the start and is also full-check-verified.

Key files (this session): CHANGED (committed): R/data.R; R/appServer.R, R/appUI.R, R/modBreedingGroups.R, R/modGeneticValue.R, R/modInput.R, R/modORIPReporting.R, R/modPedigree.R, R/modPotentialParents.R, R/modPyramid.R, R/modSummaryStats.R; man/pedDuplicateIds.Rd, man/pedFemaleSireMaleDam.Rd, man/pedGood.Rd, man/pedInvalidDates.Rd, man/pedMissingBirth.Rd, man/pedSameMaleIsSireAndDam.Rd; CHANGELOG.md, PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 110), SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). Gotchas: (1) si.re/si re are INTENTIONAL raw fixtures, NOT corruption — fixColumnNames strips spaces then periods so both normalize to sire; qcStudbook(pedGood) returns canonical columns. The data docs now say si.re to match the actual data. Do NOT “clean” the data column (it would gut the QC-demo fixture and a test family that round-trips names(pedGood)). (2) Wrapped @importFrom is COSMETIC lint, not a NAMESPACE hazard — roxygen 8.0.0 captures continuation lines fine (proven byte-identical); the 20 errors are now 0. (3) pedInvalidDates is the anomaly of the error-set family — its data uses already-canonical columns (id, sire, dam, sex, birth), unlike the raw (ego_id, si.re, dam_id, ..., birth_date) of the other five; its doc now matches. (4) Newly-flagged, NOT fixed (FM #8): vignette studbook-quality-control.qmd:91 omits the sex column (option A3); S114’s data-doc short-title rewrite still deferred (A2). (5) Shell backslash trap (recurring): write R probes to a FILE and run Rscript file.R; also grep -n prepends LINENUM: which inflates length($0) line-length checks — read files directly when measuring width. (6) Regenerate with roxygen2::roxygenise(package.dir=<abspath>) (devtools absent); baseline 8.0.0 so regens are clean — confirm via git diff --stat that only intended pages changed, and git diff --quiet -- NAMESPACE for import-neutral source edits. (7) Standing uncommitted: .DS_Store (modified) + PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked) — owner’s keep-call; a stray Rplots.pdf can appear from Rscript plot calls — none this session. (8) Branch state: after this commit add-methodology is 5 ahead of origin/add-methodology (S112–S116) and ~182 ahead of master — the pending action is the merge to master. (9) Carried CRAN gotchas remain valid: NEWS.md from NEWS.Rmd; renv explicit-snapshot omits ALL Suggests; pkgdown/quarto/devtools dev-lib-only; RSelenium undeclared; the package is ARCHIVED on CRAN (Learning 95).

R/convertSexCodes.R title Converts sex indicator for an individual to a standardized codes → Converts a sex indicator for an individual to a standardized code (number agreement). R/data.R (3 blocks): focalAnimals “containing the of animal Ids” → “containing the animal Ids”; pedMissingBirth “8 rows and 5 columns (…, birth_date) … with no errors” → “8 rows and 4 columns (ego_id, sire, dam_id, sex) … that is missing the birth_date column”; pedSameMaleIsSireAndDam “… with no errors” → “… in which the same male animal is listed as both a sire and a dam”. man/ (5 regenerated): findPedigreeNumber.Rd, convertSexCodes.Rd, focalAnimals.Rd, pedMissingBirth.Rd, pedSameMaleIsSireAndDam.Rd. The regenerated .Rd’s auto-\format already read “4 columns” — independent corroboration. pedSameMaleIsSireAndDam row o3 has dam_id = s1 (a male) and s1 sires o1/o2 (same male is both sire and dam; 5 columns correct). focalAnimals is 1 column (id)/327 rows. findPedigreeNumber source confirmed to assign a connected-component number, not a generation. - 🚨 REAL WORK #2 — A MID-SESSION OWNER CONSTRAINT WAS ALREADY SATISFIED (→ Learning 109): owner required pedMissingBirth to “retain the characteristic of not having a Birth column.” The deliverable touched only documentation, so the data object was never at risk; confirmed post-edit (4 columns, birth_date absent) and reported, rather than manufacturing a change. - Used file-based R probes from the first command — dodged the recurring shell-backslash trap (Learning 108) that bit S112–S114. - [news-vs-changelog]: dev-process history → CHANGELOG here. These help pages SHIP → fold S115’s corrections into the CRAN Phase 3 NEWS rewrite at 2.0.0 (flagged, NOT edited now).
