Same shape as S96/S91 pushes. - Same-conversation continuation: S98 closed out, then the owner gave this specific pre-named task (“push it”). I did NOT re-run the full Phase-0 orientation report — the state was live and unchanged but my own S98 commit, the deliverable was SUGGESTED-NEXT candidate (1), and the orientation-for-the-owner purpose was served minutes earlier and answered with this task. Deliberate, stated call — not a skipped step. - Pre-flight (SAFEGUARDS): git remote -v (origin = https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr.git); git fetch origin add-methodology FIRST → ahead/behind = 2 ahead / 0 behind = clean fast-forward (no surprise remote commits, no force needed); git log origin/add-methodology..HEAD confirmed exactly the 2 expected commits (S97 02728e92 + S98 49740e03). - Push: plain git push origin add-methodology → fast-forward a5507a35..49740e03. Verified BOTH directions: git rev-parse HEAD == git rev-parse origin/add-methodology == 49740e03, post-push ahead/behind = 0/0. - No CHANGELOG/NEWS entry: the 2 pushed commits already carry their own entries (added/decided S97–S98); a git push of already-logged work is not itself a software change ([[backlog-vs-changelog-placement]], S96/S91 precedent). Learnings (3C): none new (a push is well-documented; S91/S96 added none — judged, not skipped). [macos-dupe-scan]: commit ONLY SESSION_NOTES.md; exclude .DS_Store (modified) + the 2 standing untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html).

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A — STATED, not silently skipped. A git push publishes already-committed, already-verified work (S98 = a verified issue-body edit; S97 = a read-only audit); it changes no runtime behavior on this machine. Verification appropriate to the deliverable = confirming the remote ref advanced to the exact local HEAD (origin/add-methodology == 49740e03). FM #24 N/A (no build/compile step to mistake for verification).

Session 98 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 99): Score 9/10. S98 pre-named THIS deliverable as SUGGESTED-NEXT candidate (1) with the exact ahead-count and commit list: “Push add-methodology to origin — this close-out leaves the branch 2 ahead of origin (S97 02728e92 + this S98 commit).” Every element held firsthand: the branch WAS 2 ahead, both hashes matched exactly (02728e92, 49740e03), clean fast-forward. The gotchas (branch model: work on add-methodology, master via PR; the renv-unmaterialized + merge=TRUE method notes; the 2-untracked-files + .DS_Store disposition; the gh --json gotcha; “#37 OPEN with an accurate body, future re-check is a delta”) were all accurate and load-bearing. ROI: high. The −1: purely structural — a push is a trivial follow-on with almost no surface for a handoff to get wrong, so there was little to evaluate (symmetric with how S96→S95 and S97→S96 docked the trivial-follow-on handoffs).

Self-assessment (Session 99): 9/10. Re-verified the push-relevant state fresh (did NOT trust memory — re-ran fetch, ahead/behind, the commit list) rather than assuming the ahead-count from the S98 handoff. Claimed the session (Phase 1B stub BEFORE the push). Pre-flight done properly: confirmed remote/commits/upstream AND fetched first to PROVE a clean fast-forward rather than blind-pushing. Verified the result both directions (rev-parse equality + 0/0). Declared TDD N/A honestly (no code surface). Scope discipline (1-and-done): pushed exactly the branch asked; did NOT open a PR, did NOT touch master, did NOT touch the 2 untracked files / .DS_Store, did NOT start any candidate issue. 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized SOLO. The −1 (honest, inherent): the close-out itself necessarily creates a new commit (this handoff), so “fully synced” requires a second push — I do push it (ending 0/0), but a session whose deliverable is “sync the branch” can never leave a zero-new-commit trace; inherent, not a defect, and stated transparently (same as S96).

⇒ SUGGESTED NEXT = owner’s pick. Branch is published and FULLY in sync (0/0 through this handoff commit); nothing mid-flight. 14 open issues. Candidates: - (1) #45 owner-judgment close (umbrella — its 4 written ACs are met via closed #31, but it parents still-deferred #28; same owner’s-call shape #37 had). - (2) Small TDD engineering (genuinely unbuilt): #36 (chimpanzee age-pyramid — TODO at R/agePyramidPlot.R:60-63; overlaps #46), #29 (rename makeGrpNum→makeGroupNum — grep inventory FIRST per SESSION_RUNNER planning rules + SAFEGUARDS rename caution), #46 (species first-class — keystone unblocking #28). - (3) PR to master: if the owner wants add-methodology merged to master, that’s a PR (S51 = PR #41) — distinct from this push, not done here. Do NOT implement #28 (gated on #11/#12/#37/#46); do NOT close #37/#45/#1/#5/#9 without firsthand owner-confirmed evidence. #37’s body is accurate as of S98 — a future re-verification is a clean DELTA against it, not a re-run (Learning 91/93).

Key files (this session): none changed except SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). No R/, test, NAMESPACE, man/, DESCRIPTION, CHANGELOG, or NEWS change. No transient files.

Gotchas: (1) add-methodology ends FULLY synced with origin (0/0 through this handoff commit) — the next session’s close-out commit will again leave it 1 ahead until pushed; normal accumulate-then-push. (2) Branch model unchanged: work lives on add-methodology; master gets it via a future PR (S51 = PR #41), NOT via this push. No PR opened this session. (3) renv library is NOT materialized — Rscript --vanilla + codetools for static analysis; renv::restore() before actual TESTS (Learning 92). (4) #37 is OPEN with an accurate body (S98) — do NOT close without owner direction; future re-check is a delta. (5) The 2 untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html) + .DS_Store remain uncommitted (build-ignored / owner’s standing keep-call). (6) Default gh issue view errors on projectCards / stateReason not a supported --json field — use gh issue view N --json state,body,....

What Session 98 Did

Deliverable: Update issue #37’s stale body — keep it OPEN as the standing keep-as-public-API catalog (owner’s choice over “close #37”), refreshed to the HEAD snapshot. (DONE — body edited via gh issue edit 37 --body-file; verified live + state=OPEN. Recomputed the authoritative unused set firsthand and reconciled the WHOLE body against it (programmatic identical() exact-match) rather than hand-applying S97’s delta-list. Result: 166 exported / 127 used / 39 unused** (35 fn + 4 S3) — 5 strikes (modORIPReportingUI/modORIPReportingServer, getPotentialParents, removeAutoGenIds, chooseDate) + 2 additions (setAutoIdFormat, summary.nprcgenekeeprGV) + the getPedDirectRelatives docfix note + corrected totals/arithmetic. Plus a dated re-verification comment on the issue thread. No R//test/NAMESPACE/man//CHANGELOG/NEWS change.) Started / Completed:** 2026-06-16 Status: DONE. Operational documentation task (edit a GitHub issue body), not a code change → TDD phase N/A (no RED/GREEN/REFACTOR surface; declared every response). One AskUserQuestion — to disambiguate candidate (1)’s two materially-different deliverables (close vs keep+update); this was a scope decision (the owner’s to make), NOT a TDD phase gate. 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized SOLO under ultracode (one recompute + targeted firsthand checks + a body rewrite — the audit fan-out already happened S95/S97; a Workflow would be theater). - Recompute-don’t-inherit applied to a PUBLICATION (→ Learning 93): before editing a public issue body, I did NOT just apply S97’s recommendation list. I re-ran the issue’s own documented method — codetools::findGlobals(merge = TRUE) over all 202 sourced R/*.R under Rscript --vanilla (/tmp/s98_reach.R; renv unmaterialized, Learning 92) — independently reproducing 166 / 127 / 39 (35 fn + 4 S3), then reconciled every non-struck body entry against it with identical(body_nonstruck, authoritative) (/tmp/s98_check.R → EXACT MATCH: TRUE). - The exact-match gate caught what the handoff’s delta-list could not: S97’s list was the delta-since-S78, but #37’s body was last updated at S65 (2026-06-12), older than S78 — so two more entries were stale: chooseDate (left the unused set AT S78 via Map(chooseDate,…) setExit.R:54 → invisible to a since-S78 delta) and setAutoIdFormat (a newer #44/#38 export never catalogued). Also fixed a pre-existing arithmetic error (the body claimed 39 but its origin-breakdown summed to 42; rebuilt so 22 + 17 = 39 holds) and a grep false-negative (NAMESPACE declares S3 methods as S3method(print,summary.x) — comma form — my first regex assumed print.summary.x and returned empty). - Every published claim verified firsthand: mounts (appUI.R:181,200 / appServer.R:286,302), the transitive removeAutoGenIds (getPotentialParents.R:42), the Map higher-order site (setExit.R:54), setAutoIdFormat’s no-direct-callers + app-reads-via-getAutoIdFormat (addUIds.R), all four S3 methods in NAMESPACE, the docfix commit (2a64770f, S87), and state=OPEN post-edit. - [news-vs-changelog]: an issue-body edit is dev-process bookkeeping (the underlying audit was logged by S97) → no CHANGELOG/NEWS entry ([[backlog-vs-changelog-placement]], S93/S94/S96 precedent). [macos-dupe-scan]: commit ONLY SESSION_NOTES.md + PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md; exclude .DS_Store (modified) + the 2 standing untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html).

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A — STATED, not silently skipped. Editing a GitHub issue body changes NO runtime behavior (no R//test change). The verification appropriate to the deliverable = the firsthand recompute (independently reproduced S97), the programmatic body↔︎truth exact-match check, and confirming the edit went live + state=OPEN. FM #24 N/A — no build/compile step to mistake for verification.

Session 97 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 98): Score 8/10. S97 pre-named “#37 disposition” as candidate (1) with the close-vs-keep+update fork, and — crucially — pointed at its own audit docs/audits/ISSUE_37_UNUSED_EXPORTS_AUDIT_2026-06-16.md as the firsthand anchor and carried the method gotchas that were load-bearing: the renv-unmaterialized warning (saved me from a dead load_all) and findGlobals(merge = TRUE) (Learning 92) — both used verbatim. Every baseline held firsthand: branch 1-ahead, 14 open issues, dashboard 98/100, ghost-check (02728e92=S97), the gh --json gotcha, the 2-untracked + .DS_Store disposition. The audit’s disposition table (incl. setAutoIdFormat at row 26) and recompute method were the raw materials that let me get the body right. ROI: high. The −2: S97’s explicit body-edit list (Finding #5 / Rec #2) said “strike the 3 now-reached functions, add the 4th S3, note the docfix” — but the real change-set was 5 strikes + 2 additions: it undercounted the strikes and never named chooseDate or setAutoIdFormat, because it was framed as a delta-since-S78 while the body lagged to S65. A maximal handoff for a “keep + update body” candidate flags “the body is at the S65 baseline — updating to HEAD needs a full recompute-and-reconcile, not just the since-S78 delta” (exactly Learning 93). Light, fair dock — the recompute method + disposition table were in hand; I had to apply them one step more completely than the literal list.

Self-assessment (Session 98): 8/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER read in full, notes top, dashboard 98/100, git, 14 issues, ghost-check → 02728e92=S97), reported, STOPPED for the owner’s task; then disambiguated candidate (1)’s two-deliverable fork via AskUserQuestion rather than assuming a close vs a body-update. Claimed the session (Phase 1B stub BEFORE technical work). Headline strengths: (1) recompute-don’t-inherit applied to a publication — independently reproduced S97’s 39-set and reconciled the WHOLE body programmatically, which caught two stale entries beyond the handoff’s list (chooseDate, setAutoIdFormat), a 42≠39 arithmetic error, and an S3 grep false-negative; (2) verified every published claim firsthand before it went public; (3) made the body internally consistent, self-documenting (the “what changed” block + audit reference), and left a dated thread comment for the otherwise-silent body edit; (4) produced Learning 93 (a genuinely non-obvious meta-lesson: a handoff delta is anchored to the handoff’s baseline, not the artifact’s). Scope discipline (1-and-done): edited exactly #37’s body + one comment; did NOT close #37, did NOT push, did NOT touch other issues / .DS_Store / the untracked files, did NOT start another candidate. 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized SOLO; declared TDD N/A + Phase-3E N/A with reasons. The −2 (honest): (a) the subject was deeply audited the same day (S97), so marginal difficulty was bounded — the new value is the two extra catches + the arithmetic/consistency fix + making the body authoritative, non-trivial but on well-trodden ground; (b) I inherited S78/S97’s per-export disposition rationales for the body’s “Note” column (keep-as-public-API reasons) rather than re-deriving all 39 firsthand — appropriate (re-deriving verified dispositions is the exact Learning-91 redundancy), but it means the body’s reachability is freshly verified while its disposition prose is inherited; I state that plainly. I reserve 9–10 for a harder delivery.

⇒ SUGGESTED NEXT = owner’s pick. #37 now has an accurate, current body (kept OPEN as the catalog); nothing mid-flight. 14 open issues (none opened/closed; #37 stays OPEN). Key files (this session): CHANGED (committed): SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff), PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 93 at tail). DELIVERABLE (not a repo file): issue #37 body (edited via gh issue edit) + one dated re-verification comment (#37 thread). Read firsthand (verification): the current #37 body (gh issue view 37 --json body), docs/audits/ISSUE_37_UNUSED_EXPORTS_AUDIT_2026-06-16.md, NAMESPACE, R/appUI.R:181,200, R/appServer.R:286,302, R/modPotentialParents.R:138,150, R/getPotentialParents.R:42, R/setExit.R:54, R/getPedDirectRelatives.R:27, R/autoIdFormat.R, R/addUIds.R (grep), R/summary.nprcgenekeeprErr.R, git log 2a64770f. Transient (NOT committed): /tmp/s98_reach.R (reachability recompute), /tmp/s98_check.R (body↔︎truth exact-match), /tmp/s98_issue37_body.md (the new body), /tmp/s98_comment.md (the thread comment).

Gotchas: (1) #37 is OPEN with an ACCURATE body as of 2026-06-16 (S98) — a future re-verification is a clean DELTA against THIS body (no longer stale); recompute + diff, don’t re-run (Learning 91/93). Do NOT close #37 without owner direction. (2) This close-out leaves add-methodology 2 ahead of origin (S97 02728e92 + this S98) — push when ready (accumulate-then-push). (3) renv library is NOT materialized — use Rscript --vanilla + codetools for static analysis; renv::restore() needed before actual TESTS (Learning 92). (4) Reachability method: findGlobals(f, merge = TRUE), NOT $functions-only — higher-order args (Map/apply/do.call) are false-flagged unused (Learning 92/93). (5) NAMESPACE S3 form is S3method(print,summary.x) (comma, not print.summary.x) — grep accordingly (Learning 93). (6) The 2 untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html) + .DS_Store remain uncommitted (build-ignored / owner’s standing keep-call). (7) Default gh issue view errors on projectCards / stateReason not a supported --json field — use gh issue view N --json state,body,.... (8) Branch model unchanged: work on add-methodology; master via a future PR (S51 = PR #41), not a push.

What Session 97 Did

Deliverable: Audit issue #37 (“Exported functions not used by the app”). (DONE — docs/audits/ISSUE_37_UNUSED_EXPORTS_AUDIT_2026-06-16.md written; read-only DELTA re-verification against the S78 triage. Headline: #37’s actionable surface is FULLY DRAINED — both S78 wire-ins shipped + their tracking issues CLOSED (#47, #48), the surfaced docfix is fixed (S87 2a64770f), 39 unused exports = 0 wire-in · 39 keep-as-public-API · 0 retire. No R//test/NAMESPACE/man//issue-state/issue-body change.) Started / Completed: 2026-06-16 Status: DONE. Audit workstream (docs/methodology/workstreams/AUDIT_WORKSTREAM.md). TDD phase N/A (read-only audit, no code surface — declared every response). No AskUserQuestion gates (no phase transitions). 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized SOLO under ultracode (the per-function disposition was adversarially verified by S78 two days ago; a fan-out would be the exact Learning-91 redundancy — the genuinely-new work is one deterministic recompute + targeted firsthand checks). - Scope = DELTA, set at Phase 1 (Learning 91 applied correctly — the discipline S95 was flagged for): before any work I read the #37 body + its two triage comments (S65 2026-06-12, S78 2026-06-14) + both prior audit reports (BACKLOG_STALENESS_AUDIT_2026-06-12.md, IMPLEMENTED_BUT_OPEN_AUDIT_2026-06-16.md) + CHANGELOG.md. Established that S78 already verified the per-function disposition (2 wire-in · 37 keep · 0 retire) with an adversarial completeness critic. So I did NOT re-run S78’s triage — I recomputed the set, diffed against S78, and confirmed the wire-in/docfix status. #37 has now been re-verified 4× (S65, S78, S95, S97). - Recompute-don’t-inherit caught a method bug (→ Learning 92): the renv project library is NOT materialized in this checkout (renv.lock + renv/activate.R but no renv/library → .Rprofile’s source("renv/activate.R") re-bootstraps an empty renv and pkgload::load_all/devtools die). So I recomputed reachability statically — sys.source all 202 R/*.R into a throwaway env + codetools::findGlobals under Rscript --vanilla (base lib only; /tmp/s97_reach2.R). First pass used findGlobals(merge=FALSE)$functions and FALSE-flagged chooseDate unused; firsthand check found Map(chooseDate, …) at R/setExit.R:54 (passed as a value → $variables, not $functions). merge=TRUE (the correct conservative “app uses this” test) reconciled it with S78. Result: 127 app-used / 39 unused (35 functions + 4 S3 methods); 202/202 files sourced (100% coverage). - The delta vs S78, all verified firsthand: (1) #47 ORIP module mounted appUI.R:181/appServer.R:286, issue CLOSED → left the unused set. (2) #48 getPotentialParents wired via NEW R/modPotentialParents.R:150, mounted appUI.R:200/appServer.R:302, issue CLOSED → left the set (removeAutoGenIds follows transitively via getPotentialParents.R:42). (3) Docfix FIXED — getPedDirectRelatives @examples now invokes itself (was the getLkDirectRelatives copy-paste defect), fixed S87 2a64770f. (4) Logging island unchanged — safeExecute/logModuleEvent/savePlotToFile still 0 live callers (grep) → safeExecute the lone conditional future-retire. (5) Minor: issue body lists 3 unused S3 methods; verified set is 4 (adds summary.nprcgenekeeprGV). - [news-vs-changelog]: internal audit findings doc = dev-process history → CHANGELOG only (S97 entry), no NEWS. [macos-dupe-scan]: commit ONLY the audit report + CHANGELOG.md + PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md + SESSION_NOTES.md; exclude .DS_Store (modified) + the 2 standing untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html).

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A — STATED, not silently skipped. A read-only audit changes NO runtime behavior (no R//test change). The verification appropriate to the deliverable = firsthand re-verification of the recompute (caught + fixed the merge bug) and of every load-bearing claim (mounts, issue CLOSED states, docfix, logging island, git attribution). FM #24 N/A — no build/compile step to mistake for verification.

S96’s “#37 — its actionable wire-ins #47/#48 are shipped+CLOSED” claim held firsthand. ROI: high on scoping. Self-assessment (Session 97): 8/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER read in full, notes top, dashboard 98/100, git, 14 issues, ghost-check → a5507a35=S96), reported, STOPPED for the owner’s task. Scope discipline (1-and-done): produced exactly ONE audit report; did NOT close #37, did NOT edit the issue body (recommended it instead), did NOT touch code/the already-done docfix, did NOT touch the untracked files/.DS_Store, did NOT start any other candidate. Right-sized SOLO honestly. 0 stakeholder corrections. The −2 (honest): the subject was already deeply audited (#37’s 4th re-verification), so the marginal value is bounded — the genuinely-new facts are confirmations (wire-ins discharged, docfix fixed, retire-set stable) plus three small catches (the merge method bug, the stale body, the 4th S3 method). It’s the correctly-scoped delta and the catches are non-trivial, but judgment-under-difficulty was limited by how well-trodden the ground was; I reserve 9–10 for a harder delivery.

Key files (this session): NEW (committed): docs/audits/ISSUE_37_UNUSED_EXPORTS_AUDIT_2026-06-16.md (the deliverable). Gotchas: (1) #37 is still OPEN — its disposition (close vs keep-and-update-body) is an owner judgment call; do NOT close without owner direction. (2) renv library is NOT materialized in this checkout — pkgload::load_all/devtools::* fail (the .Rprofile re-bootstraps an empty renv). To run actual TESTS you must renv::restore() first; for static analysis (like this audit) use Rscript --vanilla + codetools (base lib). (3) Reachability method: use codetools::findGlobals(f, merge=TRUE), NOT $functions-only — functions passed to Map/apply/do.call (e.g. chooseDate in setExit.R:54) are in $variables and get false-flagged unused (Learning 92). (4) This close-out leaves add-methodology 1 ahead of origin — push when ready (accumulate-then-push). (5) The 2 untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html) + .DS_Store remain uncommitted (build-ignored / owner’s standing keep-call). (6) Default gh issue view errors on projectCards / stateReason not a supported --json field — use gh issue view N --json state,closed,.... (7) Branch model unchanged: work on add-methodology; master via a future PR (S51 = PR #41), not a push.

Operational task (publish committed work), not a code change → TDD phase N/A (no RED/GREEN/REFACTOR surface; declared as such every response). No AskUserQuestion gates (no phase transitions). 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized SOLO under ultracode (one outward-facing git command — a Workflow would be theater). Same shape as S91’s push. - Pre-flight (SAFEGUARDS): git remote -v (origin = https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr.git); git fetch origin add-methodology FIRST → git rev-list --left-right --count HEAD...origin/add-methodology = 4 ahead / 0 behind = clean fast-forward (no surprise remote commits, no force-push needed); git log origin/add-methodology..HEAD confirmed exactly the 4 expected commits (S92/S93/S94/S95). - Push: plain git push origin add-methodology → fast-forward 0a677c29..fd8b260e. Verified BOTH directions: git rev-parse HEAD == git rev-parse origin/add-methodology == fd8b260e, and post-push ahead/behind = 0/0. - No CHANGELOG/NEWS entry: the 4 pushed commits already carry their own CHANGELOG/NEWS entries (added S92–S95); a git push of already-logged work is not itself a software change → nothing new to log (consistent with [[backlog-vs-changelog-placement]]). Learnings (3C): none new (a push is well-documented; S91 added none — judged, not skipped). [macos-dupe-scan]: commit ONLY SESSION_NOTES.md; exclude .DS_Store (modified) + the 2 standing untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html).

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A — STATED, not silently skipped. A git push publishes already-committed, already-verified work (S95 was a read-only audit; S92–S94 each Phase-3E-verified or N/A at their own close); it changes no runtime behavior on this machine. Verification appropriate to the deliverable = confirming the remote ref advanced to the exact local HEAD, which I did (origin/add-methodology == fd8b260e). FM #24 N/A (no build/compile step to mistake for verification).

Session 95 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 96): Score 9/10. S95 pre-named THIS deliverable as SUGGESTED-NEXT candidate (1) with the exact ahead-count and commit list: “Push add-methodology to origin — after this close-out the branch is 4 ahead of origin (S92 4f9a0fe1, S93 ecaa4006, S94 d06552ec, + this S95 close-out commit).” Every element held firsthand: the branch WAS 4 ahead, the first 3 hashes matched exactly, and the 4th (S95’s own then-unmade close-out commit) resolved to fd8b260e as predicted. The gotchas (branch model: work on add-methodology, master via PR; the 2-untracked-files + .DS_Store disposition; the gh --json gotcha; “no administrative closes owed / do a DELTA not a re-run” per Learning 91) were all accurate and load-bearing. ROI: high. The −1: purely structural — a push is a trivial follow-on with almost no surface for a handoff to get wrong, so there was little to evaluate; not a fault of S95 (symmetric with how S90→S91 and S92→S93 docked the trivial-follow-on handoffs).

Self-assessment (Session 96): 9/10. Re-oriented to the push-relevant state fresh (did not trust memory — re-ran git status, fetch, ahead/behind, the commit list) rather than assuming the ahead-count from the S95 handoff. Claimed the session (Phase 1B stub BEFORE the push). Pre-flight done properly: confirmed remote/commits/upstream AND fetched first to PROVE a clean fast-forward rather than blind-pushing. Verified the result both directions (rev-parse equality + 0/0). Declared TDD N/A honestly (no code surface). Scope discipline (1-and-done): pushed exactly the branch asked; did NOT open a PR, did NOT touch master, did NOT touch the 2 untracked files / .DS_Store, did NOT start any candidate issue. 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized SOLO under ultracode. The −1 (honest, inherent): the close-out itself necessarily creates a new commit (this handoff), so “fully synced” requires a second push — I do push it, but a session whose deliverable is “sync the branch” can never leave a zero-new-commit trace; that’s inherent, not a defect, and I’m transparent about it.

⇒ SUGGESTED NEXT = owner’s pick. Branch is published and FULLY in sync (0/0 through this handoff commit); nothing mid-flight. 14 open issues. Candidates (unchanged from S95, push now done): - (1) Owner-judgment closes IF desired (NOT auto-closes — owner decision): #45 (umbrella — its 4 written ACs are met via closed #31, but it parents still-deferred #28 → close, or keep parenting #28?), #37 (standing inventory — its only actionable wire-ins #47/#48 are shipped+CLOSED → retire the inventory, or keep as the catalog?). Firsthand evidence in docs/audits/IMPLEMENTED_BUT_OPEN_AUDIT_2026-06-16.md. - (2) Small TDD engineering (all genuinely unbuilt — per the S95 audit): #36 (chimpanzee age-pyramid settings — verbatim TODO still at R/agePyramidPlot.R:60-63; overlaps #46), #29 (rename makeGrpNum→makeGroupNum — run a cross-reference grep inventory FIRST per SESSION_RUNNER planning rules + SAFEGUARDS rename caution), #46 (species first-class — keystone unblocking #28’s multi-species path). - (3) PR to master: if the owner wants add-methodology merged to master, that’s a PR (S51 used PR #41) — distinct from this push, not done here. Do NOT implement #28 (still gated on #11/#12/#37/#46); do NOT close #45/#1/#5/#9 without firsthand owner-confirmed evidence (note #45’s ACs are met — owner judgment); the 9 not-implemented issues are correctly open. Do NOT re-run a whole-backlog implemented-but-open sweep — do a DELTA against the S95 audit if anything closes (Learning 91).

Key files (this session): none changed except SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). No R/, test, NAMESPACE, man/, DESCRIPTION, CHANGELOG, or NEWS change.

Gotchas: (1) add-methodology ends FULLY synced with origin (0 ahead / 0 behind through this handoff commit) — the next session’s close-out commit will again leave it 1 ahead until pushed; that’s the normal accumulate-then-push pattern (this session resolved the 4 that had accumulated S92–S95). (2) Branch model unchanged: work lives on add-methodology; master gets it via a future PR (S51 = PR #41), NOT via this push. No PR opened this session. (3) The 2 untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html) + .DS_Store remain uncommitted (build-ignored / owner’s standing keep-call). (4) Default gh issue view errors on projectCards / stateReason not a supported --json field — use gh issue view N --json state,closed,closedAt,.... (5) No administrative closes are owed — the S95 audit drained the implemented-but-open backlog (no second #49); if anything closes later, do a DELTA against that audit, not a fresh sweep (Learning 91).

What Session 95 Did

Deliverable: Learning 90 follow-through AUDIT — classify every open GitHub issue by implementation status to find any #49-style work that shipped but was never closed. (DONE — docs/audits/IMPLEMENTED_BUT_OPEN_AUDIT_2026-06-16.md written; read-only, 0 closes performed. Method: a 14-agent classify→adversarial-verify Workflow (wf5scvhh9); the two “criteria appear met” findings re-verified FIRSTHAND by the session. TDD phase N/A (read-only audit, no code surface).) Started / Completed: 2026-06-16 Status: DONE. Audit workstream (docs/methodology/workstreams/AUDIT_WORKSTREAM.md). Headline: the implemented-but-open backlog is DRAINED — 0 fully-implemented-but-open closeable candidates (no second #49). Coverage 14/14 (100%). Classifications: not-implemented 9 (#2, #10, #11, #12, #13, #28, #29, #36, #46 — genuinely unbuilt, mostly external-system/methodology features), policy-hold 4 (#45, #9, #5, #1), ambiguous 1 (#37). 0 stakeholder corrections on the audit content; 1 owner-flagged process issue (the redundancy below). Right-sized as a Workflow under ultracode (14 independent investigations = a genuine fan-out) — but the SCOPE should have been a delta, not a full sweep (Learning 91). - Two owner-judgment items surfaced (NOT auto-closes — re-verified firsthand): #45 (umbrella — its four written ACs are met: AC1 R/getPotentialParents.R:31 signature unchanged/no parallel param; AC2 tests/testthat/test_getPotentialParents.R:131-190 asserts dam selection responds to maxGestationalPeriod — d165≠d210, d210⊂d165; AC3 no hack/TODO in the file; AC4 spec on #28 — via closed sub-task #31 0eeee3f6 — but it intentionally parents still-deferred #28 → owner decides whether the umbrella closes). #37 (standing inventory — its only actionable wire-ins #47/#48 are both shipped+CLOSED, R/appUI.R:181,200 + R/appServer.R:286,302; the rest are keep-as-public-API by decision → owner decides whether to retire the inventory issue). #1/#5/#9 confirmed genuinely PARTIAL (a tested first increment shipped; a specific owner-named criterion still unmet) — correctly open. - THE process finding (→ Learning 91, owner-flagged TWICE): this audit substantially re-ran S62’s docs/audits/BACKLOG_STALENESS_AUDIT_2026-06-12.md (same question, all then-open issues, 4 days prior). The overlap surfaced only MID-EXECUTION (agents cited S62) and at report-writing (looking up the audits-dir naming convention) — never at scoping. The owner asked “why was this in the backlog if already done?” and “you should have been able to check changelog also.” Both pre-checks (ls docs/audits/; the CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] record of the #4/#33/#49 drain) were cheap and available at Phase 0/1. The AUDIT_WORKSTREAM’s own Step 4 (“Review Prior Audits”) fired at report-writing instead of scoping. Owner directed: keep the report as-is (it stands as the firsthand “no second #49” confirmation + an S62 trend comparison). Honest marginal value: not zero (7 closures + the new #49 data point + firsthand “backlog drained” confirmation since S62) but a delta paid for as a full sweep. - [news-vs-changelog]: internal audit findings doc = dev-process history → CHANGELOG only (S95 entry), no NEWS (no user-facing/package change). [macos-dupe-scan]: commit ONLY the audit report + SESSION_NOTES.md + PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md + CHANGELOG.md; exclude .DS_Store (modified) + the 2 standing untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html). Auto-memory check-process-history-before-rerunning-work.md + MEMORY.md index written (outside the repo, not in the commit).

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A — STATED, not silently skipped. A read-only audit changes NO runtime behavior (no R//test change). The verification appropriate to the deliverable = firsthand re-verification of the two “criteria met” findings against current source + GitHub state (done: signatures/tests/wiring read, #31/#47/#48 confirmed CLOSED). FM #24 N/A — no build/compile step to mistake for verification.

Session 94 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 95): Score 7/10. S94 pre-named THIS deliverable as SUGGESTED-NEXT candidate (2) (“Audit for OTHER implemented-but-OPEN issues (Learning 90 follow-through)… Cheap, high-value, audit-shaped”), and every load-bearing baseline held firsthand: 14 open issues (matched gh issue list), dashboard 98/100, ghost-check clean (d06552ec=S94=HEAD), branch 3-ahead-of-origin, the 2-untracked-files + .DS_Store disposition, the gh issue view --json gotcha, the do-not-close list (#45/#1/#5/#9), and — notably — the #45 pre-flag was spot-on (“all 4 written acceptance criteria appear met, so closing it is an owner judgment call”), which my firsthand audit confirmed exactly. ROI: high on orientation/baselines. The −3: candidate (2) framed the audit as “cheap, high-value” novel work without referencing S62’s staleness audit (4 days prior, identical sweep) — the proximate cause of the redundancy the owner flagged twice. A handoff that proposes a recurring-task candidate must cite the most recent prior run of that shape (Learning 91, the mirror of Learning 90’s “carry implemented-but-open forward”). Partly inherited (S62 wasn’t in any recent handoff, and a #49-close session wouldn’t naturally re-audit docs/audits/), so not a full collapse — but a steeper dock than the recent 8s because the omission had a concrete, owner-visible cost.

Self-assessment (Session 95): 6/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER read in full, notes top, dashboard 98/100, git, 14 issues, ghost-check → d06552ec=S94), reported, STOPPED for the owner’s pick. Claimed the session (Phase 1B stub BEFORE technical work). The genuine strengths: a comprehensive 14/14 audit with adversarial-verify scaffolding; I re-verified the two “criteria appear met” findings (#45, #37) firsthand rather than trusting the agents — applying Learning 90’s own discipline (a positive claim demands a firsthand search) to my own report before putting it before the owner; an actionable report with an S62 trend comparison; and — when the owner flagged the overlap — an honest, non-defensive recovery (owned BOTH missed pre-checks, gave a straight marginal-value accounting, did not defend the sunk cost). The −4 (the core defect, owner-flagged twice): I ran a full sweep of an already-recently-audited backlog without checking docs/audits/, CHANGELOG.md, OR the recent handoffs at Phase 1 — all cheap, authoritative, and already in hand from orientation. The AUDIT_WORKSTREAM Step 4 (“review prior audits”) fired at report-writing, not scoping; the right deliverable was a delta against S62 + the #49 data point, offered to the owner before any fan-out. Redundant work is a judgment defect even under ultracode’s unconstrained budget (FM #5 helpfulness-as-volume / FM #21 greenfield applied to process artifacts). The deliverable quality + firsthand verification + honest recovery keep it off the floor, but a repeated, owner-flagged scoping miss sits well below the recent 8–9 streak. → Learning 91 + an auto-memory that loads at session start (where it prevents recurrence).

⇒ SUGGESTED NEXT = owner’s pick. See the audit report’s “Owner-judgment items” section for the firsthand evidence. - (3) Small TDD engineering (all genuinely unbuilt): #36 (chimpanzee age-pyramid settings — the verbatim TODO is still at R/agePyramidPlot.R:60-63; overlaps #46), #29 (rename makeGrpNum→makeGroupNum — a rename → run a cross-reference grep inventory FIRST per SESSION_RUNNER planning rules + SAFEGUARDS rename caution), #46 (species first-class — the keystone unblocking #28’s multi-species path). Do NOT implement #28 (still gated on #11/#12/#37/#46); do NOT close #45/#1/#5/#9 without firsthand owner-confirmed evidence (note #45’s ACs are met — it’s owner judgment); the 9 not-implemented issues are correctly open. What Session 94 Did

Deliverable: Close issue #49 (show the ORIP Reporting tab only under an ONPRC site configuration). (DONE — issue CLOSED via gh issue close 49 with an evidence comment; verified state=CLOSED closed=true closedAt=2026-06-16T21:28:21Z. No R//test/NAMESPACE/man//DESCRIPTION/CHANGELOG/NEWS change — the gating feature itself shipped + was logged in S84 b980f998 (E2E hardened S86 4cea5691); this session is the administrative close of already-completed, already-logged work, plus a PROJECT_LEARNINGS Learning 90.) Started / Completed: 2026-06-16 Status: DONE. The deliverable CHANGED mid-session on a recompute-don’t-inherit finding: it arrived framed as an implementation (“work on closing #49”; I had — wrongly, previous turn — called #49 “implementation-ready, real implementation remains”), but a grep-first check BEFORE declaring any RED revealed #49 was already fully implemented in S84. So it became an operational close (close a GitHub issue), not a code change → TDD phase N/A (no RED/GREEN/REFACTOR surface; declared as such every response — same shape as S90’s close of #4 and S93’s close of #33). One pre-close AskUserQuestion (close-path confirmation, NOT a TDD phase gate — the premise had changed). 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized SOLO under ultracode (grep + git log + firsthand re-verify + one gh command — a Workflow would be theater and can’t pause for the confirmation). - THE finding (recompute-don’t-inherit, applied to a NEGATIVE claim): I did NOT trust my own prior-turn assertion that #49 “needs implementation.” Before declaring RED, grep -rn "ORIP\|orip" R/ tests/ + git log --diff-filter=A -- R/shouldShowOripTab.R surfaced S84 b980f998 (“feat: gate ORIP Reporting tab to ONPRC-only (#49); close #47”). Verified FIRSTHAND: (1) R/shouldShowOripTab.R:34-36 = isTRUE(hasConfigFile) && isTRUE(center == "ONPRC") — resolves the issue’s flagged “open product fork” as option (b) (the getSiteInfo() default ONPRC fallback with NO config file does NOT show the tab); (2) R/appUI.R:13,17-18,177-183 — appUI(siteInfo=) computes showOrip and includes the ORIP tabPanel only if (isTRUE(showOrip)); (3) R/appServer.R:281-292 — modORIPReportingServer mounted only inside if (shouldShowOripTab(...)). Re-ran the tests green at HEAD (did not trust S84’s green claim blindly): test_shouldShowOripTab.R 5/5, test_modORIPReporting.R 9/9 (behavioral — renders real appUI(siteInfo=) HTML for ONPRC/SNPRC/no-config), test_modSiteConfig.R 30/30; plus the opt-in test-e2e-orip-module.R browser suite. - Owned the prior-turn error: last turn I told the owner #49 was an “implementation-ready TDD candidate” / “real implementation remains” — that was WRONG (I checked issue/sibling states + the S86 commit message but did NOT grep for the implementation). Corrected it explicitly to the owner before closing. This near-miss is Learning 90 (grep for the implementation before asserting an open issue needs work; implemented-but-unclosed is the repo default — #4/#33/#49). - [news-vs-changelog]: closing an issue adds no new software work — the gate was logged in S84’s CHANGELOG/NEWS at commit time → no new CHANGELOG/NEWS entry (consistent with [[backlog-vs-changelog-placement]] + S90/S93 precedent). [macos-dupe-scan]: commit ONLY SESSION_NOTES.md + PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md; exclude .DS_Store (modified) + the 2 standing untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html).

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A — STATED, not silently skipped. Closing a GitHub issue changes NO runtime behavior on this machine (no R/ change). The runtime verification appropriate to the deliverable = confirming the issue transitioned to CLOSED (done: state=CLOSED closed=true) and that the already-shipped gate still passes its tests (re-ran 3 files green this session). The issue’s “Phase-3E mandatory” requirement was satisfied at IMPLEMENTATION time (S84 TDD + S86 opt-in browser E2E); the owner declined the optional live E2E re-run this session (chose “close now with evidence”). FM #24 N/A — no build/compile step to mistake for verification.

Session 93 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 94): Score 8/10. S93’s baselines were accurate and load-bearing and ALL held firsthand: dashboard 98/100, ghost-check clean (ecaa4006=S93 = HEAD), branch 2-ahead-of-origin, the 2-untracked-files + .DS_Store disposition, the gh issue view --json gotcha (I used --json throughout), and the branch model (work on add-methodology; master via PR). The standing do-NOT-close list (#45/#1/#5/#9) was accurate as far as it went. ROI: high for orientation. The −2 (an inherited blind spot, not S93’s origination): neither S93 nor any handoff back through S84 carried that #49 was already implemented-but-OPEN — the chain tracked “#4/#33 left OPEN pending confirmation” diligently but dropped #49 entirely. Had any recent handoff listed “#49 implemented S84, OPEN pending close,” it would have instantly corrected my wrong prior-turn claim and saved the discovery. S93 inherited the gap (a #33-close session wouldn’t naturally re-audit #49), so it’s a light, fair dock — but it is exactly the gap Learning 90 closes.

Self-assessment (Session 94): 8/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER read in full, notes top, dashboard 98/100, git, 15 issues, ghost-check → ecaa4006=S93), reported, STOPPED for the owner. Answered two read-only issue questions (#45, #49) without modifying anything (FM #23). Claimed the session (Phase 1B stub BEFORE technical work). The headline strength = the recompute catch: I was one step from declaring RED on a feature that already shipped — instead I grepped first and found S84’s implementation, then verified it firsthand (read predicate + UI/server gate + re-ran 3 test files green) before asserting completeness in a PUBLIC close comment. Owned my prior-turn error to the owner honestly; confirmed the changed-premise close path via AskUserQuestion rather than improvising. Scope discipline (1-and-done): closed exactly #49; did NOT push, did NOT touch other issues / the untracked files / .DS_Store, did NOT re-implement anything. Declared TDD N/A honestly. Right-sized SOLO under ultracode. 0 stakeholder corrections on the close itself. The −2 (honest, and it’s mine): the previous turn I asserted a NEGATIVE (“#49 needs implementation / real implementation remains”) from issue metadata alone WITHOUT grepping for the implementation — an unsearched claim that misinformed the owner and necessitated the correction. The recovery was clean and the discipline that caught it (recompute-before-RED) is real, but a 10 doesn’t get awarded for needing to walk back a claim I should never have made; I reserve it for getting the negative right the first time.

⇒ SUGGESTED NEXT = owner’s pick. #49 closed; nothing mid-flight. 14 open issues (#49 closed this session). Candidates: - (1) Push add-methodology to origin — after this close-out the branch is 3 ahead of origin (S92 4f9a0fe1, S93 ecaa4006, + this S94 close-out commit; the normal accumulate-then-push pattern S91 last resolved). - (2) Audit for OTHER implemented-but-OPEN issues (Learning 90 follow-through): #49 was done-but-open and invisible for ~10 sessions. A quick pass — for each open issue, grep/git log for an implementation — would catch any sibling in the same state before mis-scoping it. Cheap, high-value, audit-shaped. - (3) Remaining small TDD issues: #36 (chimpanzee age-pyramid settings — overlaps open #46), #29 (rename makeGrpNum→makeGroupNum — a rename → run a cross-reference grep inventory FIRST per SESSION_RUNNER planning rules + SAFEGUARDS rename caution). - (4) #37 (exported functions not used by the app) — audit-shaped. Do NOT implement #28 (still gated on #11/#12/#37/#46), do NOT close #45 (umbrella — its sub-task #28 is still OPEN; all 4 written acceptance criteria appear met, so closing it is an owner judgment call — see the #45 discussion this session), and do NOT close #1, #5, or #9 without firsthand owner-confirmed evidence.

Key files (this session): CHANGED (committed): SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff), PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 90 at tail). Read firsthand (verification): R/shouldShowOripTab.R (full), R/appUI.R:1-18,165-204 (the siteInfo param + conditional ORIP tabPanel), R/appServer.R:278-299 (the gated server mount), R/getSiteInfo.R (the center="ONPRC" no-config fallback at :66), tests/testthat/test_shouldShowOripTab.R, tests/testthat/test_modORIPReporting.R, tests/testthat/test-e2e-orip-module.R (grep-level), plus git log --diff-filter=A -- R/shouldShowOripTab.R. Gotchas: (1) #49 is CLOSED (this session) — do not reopen without owner direction. (2) #49’s ONPRC gate shipped S84 b980f998 (predicate R/shouldShowOripTab.R, UI gate appUI.R:177-183, server gate appServer.R:281-292); the no-config fork is resolved as hide-unless-an-actual-config-file-names-ONPRC (the getSiteInfo() default ONPRC fallback does NOT show the tab). (3) appUI() now takes a siteInfo param (appUI(siteInfo = getSiteInfo(expectConfigFile = FALSE))) — tests inject list(center=, configFile=) to make the gate deterministic; file.exists(siteInfo$configFile) is the “config present” signal (contents irrelevant). (4) This close-out leaves add-methodology 3 ahead of origin (S92+S93+this S94) — push when ready (accumulate-then-push). (5) Learning 90: when a deliverable is “close #N,” GREP/git log for the implementation FIRST — implemented-but-unclosed is the repo default (#4/#33/#49); and a session that implements-but-leaves-OPEN must carry “impl SXX, OPEN pending close” in its handoff until close. (6) The 2 untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html) + .DS_Store remain uncommitted (build-ignored / owner’s standing keep-call). (7) Default gh issue view errors on projectCards and stateReason is NOT a supported --json field in this gh version — use gh issue view N --json state,closed,closedAt,.... (8) Branch model unchanged: work lives on add-methodology; master gets it via a future PR (S51 = PR #41), NOT via a push.

Operational task (close a GitHub issue), not a code change → TDD phase N/A (no RED/GREEN/REFACTOR surface; declared as such every response — same shape as S91’s push). No AskUserQuestion gates (no phase transitions). 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized SOLO under ultracode (one gh command + a firsthand re-verify — a Workflow would be theater). - The owner confirmation: the owner selected SUGGESTED-NEXT candidate (1) — phrased by S92 as “Close #33 on owner confirmation that the completed @return doc is acceptable” — by replying 1 to the orientation report. Selecting a candidate that IS “close #N on owner confirmation” constitutes the firsthand owner-confirmed evidence the standing close-only-on-owner-confirmation rule requires. - Recompute-don’t-inherit (applied to S92’s completion claim before asserting it publicly): did NOT trust S92’s “done + verified” claim blindly. Verified FIRSTHAND before closing: (1) R/fillBins.R:6-10 now holds the \describe{} block (males/females = integer count vectors per age bin), no TODO (grep -n TODO R/fillBins.R → none); (2) the body actually returns that shape — R/fillBins.R:36 list(males = maleBins, females = femaleBins), maleBins/femaleBins built from integer(0L) + nrow(...) over seq_along(lowerAges) (→ one integer count per bin); (3) git log -- R/fillBins.R confirms 4f9a0fe1 (S92) is the commit that completed it. The close comment cites all of this + the consumer (getMaxAx() reads them as per-bin counts) + the @noRd source-read doc-completeness test (test_fillBins.R 11/11). - [news-vs-changelog]: closing an issue adds no new software work — the fillBins doc change was logged in S92’s CHANGELOG entry when it was committed → no new CHANGELOG/NEWS entry (consistent with [[backlog-vs-changelog-placement]] and S91’s push precedent: only completed work goes to CHANGELOG, and this work was logged at commit time). [macos-dupe-scan]: commit ONLY SESSION_NOTES.md; exclude .DS_Store (modified) + the 2 standing untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html). - Learnings (3C) — none new to PROJECT_LEARNINGS (judged, not skipped): the close-on-owner-confirmation pattern is already well-documented across handoffs; S91 (the analogous operational push) added none. The one micro-point worth carrying — a SUGGESTED-NEXT candidate phrased “Close #N on owner confirmation,” when the owner selects it, IS the firsthand evidence the standing rule needs — is captured here in the handoff rather than bloating PROJECT_LEARNINGS with a near-obvious entry.

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A — STATED, not silently skipped. Closing a GitHub issue changes NO runtime behavior on this machine (no R/ change, no man page renders, no app surface). The verification appropriate to the deliverable = confirming the issue actually transitioned to CLOSED, which I did (gh issue view 33 --json state,closed → state=CLOSED closed=true). FM #24 N/A — there is no build/compile step to mistake for verification.

Every one held firsthand on re-verification — the doc IS at :6-10, the return IS at :36, there IS no TODO, 4f9a0fe1 IS the commit. What Session 92 Did

Deliverable: Complete the fillBins() @return roxygen documentation (#33) — replaced the #' @return A list with two TODO: RMS provide description placeholder at R/fillBins.R:6 with a \describe{} block documenting both returned elements (males/females = integer vectors of counts per age bin). (DONE — docs-only on the @noRd internal fillBins(); NO R/ behavior, NAMESPACE, man/, or DESCRIPTION change. Two NEW test_that blocks + a returnBlock() helper added to tests/testthat/test_fillBins.R: (1) a doc-completeness test (RED driver) reading R/fillBins.R via test_path("..","..","R","fillBins.R") + skip_if(!file.exists), scoped to the extracted @return block, asserting no TODO + \bmales\b/\bfemales\b; (2) a behavioral return-contract guard (green at HEAD, honestly classified). test_fillBins.R 11/11; document() no-op (no NAMESPACE/man//DESCRIPTION change); lintr 0; full clean-regression 0 failed/0 error.) Started / Completed: 2026-06-16 Status: DONE. Development workstream under Strict TDD. TDD phase declared every response; gates via AskUserQuestion: a separate pre-RED test-approach scope question (doc-completeness + contract vs doc-completeness only) + PRE-RED→RED, RED→GREEN, GREEN→REFACTOR; 0 stakeholder corrections. One close-out commit on add-methodology. #33 left OPEN pending owner confirmation. - Recompute-don’t-inherit (applied to the issue’s claim): verified FIRSTHAND that the issue’s suggested @return is accurate before writing it — R/fillBins.R:32 returns list(males = maleBins, females = femaleBins); maleBins/femaleBins are built from integer(0L) + nrow(...) (→ integer vectors, one element per seq_along(lowerAges) bin); the existing values test confirms integer (c(0L,0L,2L,1L,0L)); the caller R/getMaxAx.R:18 consumes them as bins$male/bins$female (R $ partial-match of the plural names) = per-bin counts. ✓ - RED design (the load-bearing craft): since fillBins is @noRd there is no man/fillBins.Rd, so S87’s tools::Rd2ex(man/<fn>.Rd) pattern (test_examples_invoke_documented_fn.R) is UNAVAILABLE — the doc lives only in the SOURCE file, read via test_path + skip_if-installed (RED in dev, inert under R CMD check where R/*.R isn’t shipped). The false-pass trap I scoped around: fillBins.R’s TITLE already says list of two lists \code{males} and \code{females}, so a whole-file grepl("males", ...) would be GREEN with the TODO still present — the test extracts ONLY the @return block (from the @return line to the next #' @ tag) and uses \bword\b boundaries (so the males inside females doesn’t satisfy the males assertion). All 3 assertions RED at HEAD for the right reason; the RED failure output literally printed the isolated @return line, proving the extractor scoped correctly. - GREEN: the issue’s verified \describe{\item{males}{...}\item{females}{...}} wording (matches the package’s existing @return convention — 4 files use \describe, e.g. R/getPossibleCols.R), all lines ≤80. REFACTOR = confirmed no-op (idiomatic, lint 0, lines ≤80). - [news-vs-changelog]: a @noRd internal’s docs never render to a user-facing man page → CHANGELOG only (S92 entry), no NEWS. [macos-dupe-scan]: commit only the doc/test/notes files; exclude .DS_Store + the 2 standing untracked files.

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A — STATED, not silently skipped. A roxygen comment on a @noRd internal changes NO runtime behavior (the function body is untouched; no man page even renders). The verification appropriate to a docs-only change = the test suite (incl. the doc-completeness test that PROVES the doc is now complete) + document() no-op + lint + full regression, all run and green. FM #24 does not apply — there is no build-vs-runtime gap to mistake for verification.

Session 91 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 92): Score 8/10. S91 was a pure-git push session whose handoff pre-named THIS deliverable as SUGGESTED-NEXT candidate (1): “#33 (complete fillBins @return docs — small docs TDD)” — and correctly sized it (“small docs TDD” held this time, unlike S89’s optimistic sizing of #4). Every load-bearing baseline held firsthand: dashboard 98/100, ghost-check clean (0a677c29=S91), the 2-untracked-files + .DS_Store disposition, the branch model (work on add-methodology; master via PR), #4 is CLOSED, the gh issue view --json projectCards gotcha (I used --json, no error), and the standing “don’t close #1/#5/#9/#45/#28-gated without owner evidence” list. ROI: high. The −2: the #33 candidate was a bare one-liner with zero analysis of the genuinely tricky part — that @noRd ⇒ no man/*.Rd (so S87’s Rd2ex pattern can’t apply) and that the title line already mentions males/females (the whole-file-grep false-pass trap). Both were genuinely mine to discover and a push session isn’t expected to analyze the next deliverable, so it’s a light, fair dock — but a maximal handoff could have flagged “#33 is @noRd; doc-completeness must read source, not a man page.”

Posed the test-approach fork as a SEPARATE pre-RED AskUserQuestion, then gated all 3 TDD transitions; declared the phase every response. RED was honest and discriminating: proactively scoped the doc assertions to the @return block to defeat the title false-pass trap, proved all 3 RED for the right reason with the extractor’s isolation visible in the failure output, and classified the contract guard honestly as green-at-HEAD (a contract-LOCK, not the RED driver — same honest-degenerate classification as S87/S89). GREEN minimal, matched the package convention; document() no-op confirmed (the @noRd proof, scope-checked via git status). Phase-3E N/A stated honestly (no runtime surface; FM #24 inapplicable). Scope discipline (1-and-done): edited exactly the one doc line + the test file; did NOT close #33 (left OPEN per the standing rule), did NOT push, did NOT touch the 2 untracked files / .DS_Store, did NOT start any other candidate issue. 0 stakeholder corrections. Right-sized SOLO under ultracode (a one-line doc fix gated by interactive TDD — a Workflow would be theater and can’t pause for the gates). The −1 (honest): the deliverable was inherently low-risk (a single doc line), so there was limited opportunity to demonstrate judgment under difficulty; the craft that existed (the @noRd source-read test pattern + the @return-block scoping) I got right on the first pass, but I reserve a 10 for harder deliveries.

Key files (this session): CHANGED (docs): R/fillBins.R (@return block at :6-10 — the only R/ change, docs-only), CHANGELOG.md (S92 entry under [Unreleased]), PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 89 at tail), SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). CHANGED (test): tests/testthat/test_fillBins.R (added returnBlock() helper + 2 test_that blocks: doc-completeness RED driver + behavioral contract guard). No transient files.

Gotchas: (1) fillBins is @noRd — there is NO man/fillBins.Rd; document() is a no-op for it (do not expect a generated man page or NAMESPACE entry; reference it in tests as nprcgenekeepr:::fillBins if needed — though the existing tests call it unqualified under load_all/devtools::test). (2) The doc-completeness test reads R/fillBins.R via test_path("..","..","R","fillBins.R") and skip_if(!file.exists) — so it is RED-capable in the dev/source tree but SKIPS under an installed package / R CMD check (where R/*.R source isn’t shipped). That is by design, mirroring test_examples_invoke_documented_fn.R. (3) This close-out leaves add-methodology 1 ahead of origin — push when ready (the normal accumulate-then-push pattern; S91 resolved the prior 3). (4) #33 left OPEN pending owner confirmation. (5) The 2 untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html) + .DS_Store remain uncommitted (build-ignored / owner’s standing keep-call). (6) Default gh issue view errors on projectCards — use gh issue view N --json <fields>. Branch model unchanged: this close-out is on add-methodology; master gets it on the next sync/PR (S51 promoted prior work via PR #41).

Verified BOTH directions: post-push git status no longer “ahead”, and git rev-parse HEAD == git rev-parse origin/add-methodology. - No CHANGELOG/NEWS entry: the 3 pushed commits already carry their own CHANGELOG/NEWS entries (added in S88–S90); a git push of already-logged work is not itself a software change → nothing new to log.
