Do NOT implement #28 (still gated on #11/#12/#37/#46), do NOT close #45, and do NOT close #1, #5, #9, or any OPEN issue without firsthand owner-confirmed evidence.

Key files (this session): NEW (committed): R/loadSiteConfig.R (exported helper), tests/testthat/test_loadSiteConfig.R (5 tests / 8 expectations), man/loadSiteConfig.Rd. CHANGED (code): R/appServer.R (config observer → one-line shared$config <- loadSiteConfig() ~:58-64; @seealso loadSiteConfig added to roxygen :24), NAMESPACE (export), man/appServer.Rd (seealso). CHANGED (docs): NEWS.Rmd→NEWS.md (#50 bug-fix bullet), CHANGELOG.md (S85 entry), PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 83 at tail), SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). GitHub: #50 CLOSED (owner-confirmed 2026-06-16; fix d440e504, Phase-3E-verified) — close comment on the issue. Read firsthand (unchanged): R/getSiteInfo.R (tolerant parser :30-88; requires all 7 params when a file exists), R/getParamDef.R (stop()s on a missing param), R/getTokenList.R (handles comments/blank/multi-line/parens/quotes), R/getConfigFileName.R (HOME-based :16-22), R/modInput.R (config param :228, UNUSED in body), R/modPedigree.R (config param :198, UNUSED in body), inst/extdata/example_nprcgenekeepr_config (documented multi-line format, center=SNPRC), tests/testthat/test_getSiteInfo.R (covers only the no-file DEFAULT path). Transient (NOT committed): /tmp/s85_home/.nprcgenekeepr_config, /tmp/s85_appdir/app.R, /tmp/news_before_s85.md.

Gotchas: (1) #50 is CLOSED (owner-confirmed 2026-06-16; fix d440e504, Phase-3E-verified). (2) shared$config is now a getSiteInfo() named list (was a read.table data.frame) when a config file is present, else NULL. Safe TODAY because both consumer modules ignore the config param — but if a FUTURE change makes a module actually USE config, it now receives the named-list shape, not a data.frame. (3) loadSiteConfig() fails SOFT: a missing OR malformed config → NULL + a futile.logger::flog.warn to the nprcgenekeepr logger (no R warning/error surfaced to the user). A partial config (missing one of the 7 required params) also no longer crashes — getParamDef’s stop() is caught → NULL. (4) Phase-3E HOME-override recipe (reusable): temp app.R does Sys.setenv(HOME=<dir with .nprcgenekeepr_config>) BEFORE shinyApp(appUI(), appServer); AppDriver$new(<appdir>); NOT_CRAN=true; install dev first. The DOCUMENTED example_nprcgenekeepr_config now boots clean — no longer needs S84’s stripped single-line workaround. (5) Test idiom: withr::local_tempdir() + withr::local_envvar(c(HOME=tmp)) (withr is available via testthat); name the config file by basename(getConfigFileName(Sys.info())[["configFile"]]) for OS-correctness. (6) object_usage_linter false-positives a brand-new package function (“no visible global function”) until devtools::install(); re-lint after install. (7) CLAUDE.md + appUI.R:216 still say “Version 1.0.8” (DESCRIPTION 1.1.0.9000) — stale since S56. (8) The 2 untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html) + .DS_Store remain uncommitted (build-ignored / owner’s standing keep-call). (9) Default gh issue view errors on projectCards — use gh issue view N --json <fields>. Branch model unchanged: this close-out is on add-methodology; master gets it on the next sync/PR (S51 promoted prior work via PR #41).

What Session 84 Did

Deliverable: (1) Owner-confirmed close of #47 (ORIP wire-in accepted), then (2) implement #49 — gate the “ORIP Reporting” tab to show only for ONPRC — under Strict TDD. (BOTH DONE — #47 CLOSED; #49 = NEW exported predicate + parameterized appUI + gated server mount; new tests 5/5 + 4/4; full suite 0 failed/0 error; lint 0; Phase-3E AppDriver 3/3 config scenarios PASS. Discovered a pre-existing config-observer crash → filed NEW #50, did NOT fix it.) Started / Completed: 2026-06-15 Status: DONE. Development workstream under Strict TDD. TDD phase declared every response; 3 phase gates via AskUserQuestion (PRE-RED→RED, RED→GREEN, GREEN→REFACTOR) + a separate pre-RED scope/approach question (the product fork AND the gating mechanism, posed together before RED); 0 stakeholder corrections. One close-out commit on add-methodology. - Owner-authorized first action — #47 CLOSED: the owner’s “1” was the firsthand confirmation the standing close-rule requires; closed #47 with a comment referencing S83 commit 6fd16715 + the 13/13 Phase-3E smoke, noting ONPRC-gating is tracked in #49. (Not a workstream deliverable — a clerical close; #49 is the substantive work.) - Two pre-RED decisions (owner, via one AskUserQuestion): (a) product fork → Hide unless a real ONPRC config — show only when an actual config file exists AND center=="ONPRC"; the getSiteInfo() default fallback (center="ONPRC" with no file, true in dev/CI) hides the tab. (b) approach → Build-time conditional tabPanel (not dynamic insertTab) — appUI() is evaluated ONCE at shinyApp(appUI(), appServer) construction and the deployment center is fixed per server, so the tab’s presence is a per-deployment CONSTANT, not a per-session reactive (dynamic insertTab is for DATA-driven tabs like Error List). - RED: NEW tests/testthat/test_shouldShowOripTab.R (5 pure-predicate cases) + rewrote tests/testthat/test_modORIPReporting.R to INJECT a siteInfo list — tab PRESENT under ONPRC+existing-file, ABSENT under SNPRC / non-existent-file — plus a deparse(appServer) assertion that the server mount is gated on shouldShowOripTab. All failed for the right reason (undefined function / unused siteInfo arg / un-gated mount); rest of suite unaffected (only test files changed). - GREEN (3 edits): NEW exported R/shouldShowOripTab.R = isTRUE(hasConfigFile) && isTRUE(center == "ONPRC") (mirrors shouldShowChangedColsTab; isTRUE(==) not identical → names/NA/NULL/length-safe); parameterized appUI(siteInfo = getSiteInfo(expectConfigFile = FALSE)) with if (isTRUE(showOrip)) tabPanel("ORIP Reporting", …); gated the modORIPReportingServer mount in appServer.R on the same predicate. document() → NAMESPACE export + man/shouldShowOripTab.Rd + man/appUI.Rd. REFACTOR = confirmed no-op (kept the sibling inline-call idiom; no helper extraction — would break the appServer structural test’s intent). - Verification: new tests 5/5 + 4/4 (9 expectations); full clean-regression read 0 failed / 0 error; lintr 0 lints on all 5 changed files (the object_usage warning for the brand-new fn cleared after devtools::install(), like its installed siblings). - [news-vs-changelog]: user-facing (visible-tab behavior change) → BOTH NEWS.Rmd→NEWS.md (ORIP bullet augmented with the ONPRC-gating note; rendered from SOURCE, diff = ONLY that bullet) AND CHANGELOG.md (S84 entry). [macos-dupe-scan]: git add only source/test/doc/notes; excluded .DS_Store + the 2 standing untracked files.

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): PERFORMED — PASS (3/3 config scenarios). AppDriver boots of the installed app, each via a generated app.R that Sys.setenv(HOME=<temp dir with .nprcgenekeepr_config>) BEFORE shinyApp(appUI(), appServer) (controls the real getSiteInfo→config path in-process): ONPRC config → ORIP tab present + navigable (active pane = ORIP), all 5 module outputs register values, 4 siblings intact; SNPRC config → tab ABSENT (UI + server), siblings intact; no config → tab ABSENT, siblings intact. 0 ORIP-namespaced ERROR-level logs (the 5 oripReporting lines are INFO shiny:value registrations; the only errors are the pre-existing app-wide shinyBS noise). First app boot ever done WITH a config file present → surfaced pre-existing bug #50 (below).

Discovered (out of scope) → NEW #50: the pre-existing config-loading observer (appServer.R:63, from 6457a3a3, 2025-12-29) uses read.table(configFile, header=TRUE, sep="="), which cannot parse the documented config format (example_nprcgenekeepr_config: comments, blank lines, multi-line quoted values) and crashes the modular app on boot whenever such a file is present — latent in dev/CI only because no config file exists there. NOT fixed (unrelated to #49); sidestepped for the smoke with a stripped single-line key=value config both read.table AND getParamDef accept; filed #50 with root cause + repro + suggested fix.

Session 83 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 84): Score 9/10. S83 pre-named THIS deliverable as SUGGESTED-NEXT candidate (2): “Implement #49 (ONPRC-gate the ORIP tab) … resolve the ‘show or hide when no config file (default ONPRC)’ product fork first (likely via AskUserQuestion). Design options recorded on #49 … Phase-3E must confirm present-under-ONPRC / absent-under-SNPRC.” Every load-bearing element held firsthand: the product fork was pre-flagged (saved me from discovering it cold), the issue carried evidence-based design options, and the baselines (dashboard 98/100, the dynamic-tab reference appServer.R:163-242, getSiteInfo.R:66 default ONPRC, the AppDriver Phase-3E recipe + shinyBS-noise separation, gh --json gotcha, 2-untracked-files disposition, stale version, branch model) were all accurate. Zero time lost on what to build or the core seams. The −1: S83 leaned toward the dynamic insertTab approach (“most consistent with the codebase”) — a minor over-steer I had to evaluate and override, because the firsthand fact that appUI() is built ONCE makes build-time gating both simpler and semantically correct for a per-deployment constant; and S83 couldn’t pre-load the pure-predicate test idiom, the getSiteInfo-requires-all-7-params strictness, or the #50 observer crash (only surfaces on a config-present boot). All genuinely the implementer’s to find. ROI: very high.

Self-assessment (Session 84): 9/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER in full, notes top, dashboard 98/100, git, 18 issues, ghost-check → none: 6fd16715 = S83 close-out), reported, STOPPED for the owner’s pick. Honored “1 then 2” without bleeding scope: closed #47 as the owner-authorized clerical action, made #49 the substantive deliverable, claimed the session (Phase 1B stub BEFORE technical work). Resolved BOTH pre-RED forks (product + approach) via a SEPARATE AskUserQuestion before RED, then gated all 3 TDD transitions via AskUserQuestion with exact next-phase actions; declared the TDD phase every response. RED clean & isolated (failures for the right reason; rest of suite untouched since only test files changed). GREEN minimal (3 edits + document()), mirroring the sibling pure-predicate idiom with a names/NA/NULL-safe predicate. Recompute-don’t-inherit: read appUI/appServer/getSiteInfo/getConfigFileName/the dynamic-tab test/the example config firsthand, and verified appUI()-built-once + the config strictness firsthand rather than trusting the handoff’s approach steer. Did NOT treat build-clean as runtime-clean (FM #24): real AppDriver Phase-3E across 3 config scenarios via the HOME-override-in-app.R recipe. Scope discipline (FM #23 + 1-and-done): discovered the #50 observer crash, did NOT fix it, filed it, sidestepped minimally; did NOT touch the stale version string, the 2 untracked files, or push. Caught my own Phase-3E check-script flaw (naive grepl-as-error metric) and verified the 5 “errors” were INFO-level value registrations rather than accepting a false FAIL. 0 stakeholder corrections. The −1 (honest): (a) my FIRST Phase-3E run crashed because I used the documented example-config format, not anticipating the appServer observer’s read.table strictness — cost one extra AppDriver round-trip (though it productively surfaced #50; a faithful “what does the observer do with a config file” pre-check would have predicted it); (b) the check-script error metric should have classified by log level from the start; (c) build-time gating freezes the decision at app construction (correct for a per-deployment config, but a deliberate trade-off worth naming).

⇒ SUGGESTED NEXT = owner’s pick. #49 is implemented, verified, and runtime-smoke-tested; #47 is CLOSED; nothing mid-flight. 18 open issues (NEW #50 filed; #47 closed → net +0). Candidates: - (1) Fix #50 (config-observer crash) — real and serious: the modular app crashes on boot for ANY deployment whose config file uses the documented format. Real TDD: RED = parse example_nprcgenekeepr_config through the observer’s path and assert no error / correct shared$config; GREEN = parse via getSiteInfo/getParamDef (single source of truth) or tryCatch+warn; Phase-3E = boot WITH a documented-format config present (use this session’s HOME-override app.R recipe). - (2) Durable opt-in E2E for the ORIP tab — tests/testthat/test-e2e-orip-module.R mirroring test-e2e-potential-parents-module.R (S82): must set an ONPRC config (the tab is now ONPRC-gated) — the E2E app.R can use the HOME-override recipe with a read.table-safe single-line config. Small TDD/test-only. - (3) Docfix sweep — getPedDirectRelatives @examples bug (R/getPedDirectRelatives.R:27 calls getLkDirectRelatives, not itself; one-line roxygen RED→GREEN) ± dedicated tests for kinshipMatrixToKValues/getAncestors. Small TDD. - (4) Housekeeping: CLAUDE.md + the appUI.R About tab (R/appUI.R:216) still say “Version 1.0.8” vs DESCRIPTION 1.1.0.9000 (carried since S56). - (5) Push add-methodology to origin (now ahead by ~37 after this close-out; S51 promoted prior work via PR #41). Do NOT implement #28 (still gated on #11/#12/#37/#46), do NOT close #45/#50, and do NOT close #1, #5, #9, or any OPEN issue without firsthand owner-confirmed evidence.

Key files (this session): NEW (committed): R/shouldShowOripTab.R (exported pure predicate), tests/testthat/test_shouldShowOripTab.R (5 cases), man/shouldShowOripTab.Rd. CHANGED (code): R/appUI.R (added siteInfo param + showOrip + conditional ORIP tabPanel ~:165-172), R/appServer.R (gated modORIPReportingServer mount ~:284-296), tests/testthat/test_modORIPReporting.R (rewritten: 4 injected-siteInfo cases), NAMESPACE (export), man/appUI.Rd. CHANGED (docs): NEWS.Rmd→NEWS.md (ORIP bullet ONPRC note), CHANGELOG.md (S84 entry), PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 82), SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). GitHub: #47 CLOSED (owner-confirmed); NEW #50 OPEN (config-observer crash, enhancement). Read firsthand (unchanged): R/getSiteInfo.R (default center “ONPRC” :66, requires all 7 params when file present), R/getConfigFileName.R (~/.nprcgenekeepr_config), R/shouldShowChangedColsTab.R (exported pure-predicate precedent), tests/testthat/test_appServer_dynamicTabs.R (the pure-predicate + appUI-grepl + deparse-grepl idiom), inst/extdata/example_nprcgenekeepr_config (center=SNPRC; documented multi-line format). Transient (NOT committed): /tmp/s84_orip_phase3e.R, /tmp/s84_orip_logcheck.R.

Gotchas: (1) The ORIP tab gate is BUILD-TIME — decided when appUI()/appServer first run (per-deployment constant). Correct because the config is per-server, but it does NOT react to a mid-session config change. (2) appUI() now takes a siteInfo param (defaulted to getSiteInfo(expectConfigFile=FALSE)) — tests inject it; production callers (runModularApp, inst/shinytest/app.R) call appUI() with no args (unchanged behavior). (3) Pre-existing bug #50: the appServer config observer’s read.table(sep="=") crashes the app on boot with a DOCUMENTED-format config file. For ANY app-boot-with-config test, use a stripped single-line key=value config (all 7 params: center, baseUrl, schemaName, folderPath, queryName, lkPedColumns, mapPedColumns — multi-values SPACE-separated, no quotes/commas/comments) that both read.table and getParamDef accept. (4) Phase-3E HOME-override recipe: generate a temp app.R doing Sys.setenv(HOME=<temp dir with .nprcgenekeepr_config>) BEFORE shinyApp(appUI(), appServer), then AppDriver$new(<that dir>) (NOT_CRAN=true + install dev first). One AppDriver per config scenario. (5) Classify AppDriver logs by LEVEL/text, not namespace mention — shiny:value <ns>-<output> lines are INFO registrations (success), not errors; genuine errors are error-level minus the pre-existing app-wide shinyBS is not defined noise. (6) object_usage_linter false-positives a brand-new package function (“no visible global function”) until devtools::install(); re-lint after install to confirm clean. (7) CLAUDE.md + appUI.R:216 still say “Version 1.0.8” (DESCRIPTION 1.1.0.9000) — stale since S56. (8) The 2 untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html) + .DS_Store remain uncommitted (build-ignored / owner’s standing keep-call). (9) Default gh issue view errors on projectCards — use gh issue view N --json <fields>. Branch model unchanged: this close-out is on add-methodology; master gets it on the next sync/PR (S51 promoted prior work via PR #41).

What Session 83 Did

Deliverable: Implement #47 — mount the existing-but-unwired modORIPReporting module pair as a new “ORIP Reporting” tab. (DONE — 2 production edits + 2 wiring tests; full suite + lint clean; Phase-3E AppDriver smoke 13/13 PASS; #47 left OPEN pending owner-confirmed close; owner-clarified ONPRC-gating deferred to NEW #49.) Started / Completed: 2026-06-15 Status: DONE. Development workstream under Strict TDD. TDD phase declared every response; 3 phase gates via AskUserQuestion (PRE-RED→RED, RED→GREEN, GREEN→REFACTOR) + a separate PRE-RED UX-fork question (tab placement, per the S79 precedent); 0 stakeholder corrections. One close-out commit on add-methodology. - NEW tests/testthat/test_modORIPReporting.R — 2 wiring tests / 4 expectations mirroring test_modGvAndBgDesc.R’s mount idiom: (1) as.character(appUI()) must contain the oripReporting- namespace AND “Office of Research Infrastructure Programs”; (2) deparse(appServer) must contain modORIPReportingServer AND oripReporting. No module unit tests (the module is already tested in test_modSiteConfig.R:98-122). - Wiring (2 edits): appUI.R — tabPanel("ORIP Reporting", icon("file-invoice"), modORIPReportingUI("oripReporting")) after Summary Statistics (owner-chosen placement via AskUserQuestion); appServer.R — modORIPReportingServer("oripReporting", pedigree = reactive(shared$currentPedigree), geneticValues = reactive(shared$geneticValues), siteConfig = reactive(getSiteInfo(expectConfigFile = FALSE))) after the Summary Statistics module. - siteConfig wiring: passed the explicit reactive(getSiteInfo(expectConfigFile = FALSE)) — functionally identical to the module’s NULL fallback (the module internally calls the same), but matches the issue’s stated plumbing + the sibling “pass reactives” convention. - Verification: RED = 4/4 fail for the right reason (markers absent at HEAD; assertion failures, not errors), confirmed before GREEN. GREEN = 4/4 pass; full clean-regression read 0 failed / 0 error (even including the test-app-/test-e2e- baseline files); lintr 0 lints on all 3 changed files; document() no-op (module already @export’d — NAMESPACE/man unchanged). REFACTOR = confirmed no-op (edits already idiomatic). - [news-vs-changelog]: user-facing (a new visible tab) → BOTH NEWS.Rmd→NEWS.md (new “Shiny application” bullet under 1.1.0.9000, rendered from the .Rmd SOURCE then diffed against a pre-edit render to confirm only the ORIP bullet changed) AND CHANGELOG.md (S83 entry). The module was previously in NEWS only as an existing module file (NEWS.Rmd:94), never a reachable feature. [macos-dupe-scan]: git add only the source/test/doc/notes files; excluded .DS_Store + the 2 standing untracked files. - Owner-clarified follow-up → NEW #49: the tab ships always-visible this session; mid-session the owner clarified ORIP reporting is ONPRC-specific and the tab should be gated on an Oregon-specific config — deferred to #49 (out of scope, 1-and-done). I answered the owner’s “is visibility config-dependent?” as a QUESTION (FM #23) firsthand from the code (no — static tabPanel, always mounted; only the displayed center reflects config, defaulting to “ONPRC” via getSiteInfo.R:66 when absent), changed nothing, then filed the issue when it surfaced a real requirement.

Gotchas: (1) #47 is implemented but stays OPEN pending owner-confirmed close (standing rule). (2) The ORIP tab ships ALWAYS-VISIBLE; the owner wants it ONPRC-gated → tracked in NEW #49. Do NOT treat the always-visible v1 as the final intended behavior. (3) Phase-3E mount-smoke recipe (reusable): NOT_CRAN=true Rscript + devtools::install(quick=TRUE) first + AppDriver$new(system.file("shinytest", package="nprcgenekeepr")); navigate via app$set_inputs(mainNavbar="<Tab Title>"); grep logs for YOUR namespace (the shinyBS is not defined errors are pre-existing app-wide noise). SCRIPT GOTCHA: app$get_html(".tab-pane.active") returns MULTIPLE nodes (every nested module tabset has its own active pane) — collapse with paste(collapse=" ") or assert app$get_value(input="mainNavbar"); a length-N vector into &&/isTRUE halts the run. (4) NEWS.md is GENERATED from NEWS.Rmd (github_document) — edit the .Rmd SOURCE and rmarkdown::render("NEWS.Rmd", output_file="NEWS.md"); verify sync by diffing a fresh render. Never hand-edit NEWS.md. (5) siteConfig=NULL and siteConfig=reactive(getSiteInfo(expectConfigFile=FALSE)) are functionally identical (module falls back to the same call) — I passed the explicit reactive for issue-fidelity. (6) CLAUDE.md + the appUI.R About tab still say “Version 1.0.8” (DESCRIPTION 1.1.0.9000) — stale since S56. (7) The 2 untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html) + .DS_Store remain uncommitted (build-ignored / owner’s standing keep-call). (8) Default gh issue view errors on projectCards — use gh issue view N --json <fields>. Branch model unchanged: this close-out is on add-methodology; master gets it on the next sync/PR (S51 promoted prior work via PR #41).

What Session 82 Did

Deliverable: Durable opt-in E2E test for the Potential Parents tab — tests/testthat/test-e2e-potential-parents-module.R (4 blocks) mirroring the sibling test-e2e-*-module.R pattern, driving the shipped #48 feature end-to-end in a real browser. (DONE — 4/4 blocks PASS against the installed app (7/7 expectations); skips cleanly in the normal suite; 0 regressions; lint clean. Closes the literal full-browser-chain gap S80/S81 both flagged.) Started / Completed: 2026-06-15 Status: DONE. Development workstream (test backfill) under Strict TDD — TDD phase declared every response; 3 phase gates via AskUserQuestion (PRE-RED→RED full-strictness, RED→GREEN full-run, GREEN→REFACTOR lint+tidy); 0 stakeholder corrections. Regression backfill of already-shipped behavior ⇒ no production code (no R/, man/, NAMESPACE, NEWS.md changes). One close-out commit on add-methodology. - NEW tests/testthat/test-e2e-potential-parents-module.R — 4 opt-in AppDriver blocks (each skip_if_not_installed("shinytest2"/"chromote") + skip_on_cran() + the create_test_app() opt-in gate + on.exit(app$stop())): (1) tab accessible (assert_active_pane); (2) controls present (gestation input / Find button / Download CSV); (3) CRUX — upload_and_wait(rhesusPedigree_fromCenter.csv) → Pedigree Browser → Potential Parents → set maxGestationalPeriod=210 → click Find → assert #potentialParents-statusMessage matches “Found candidate parents for 50 animal” + #potentialParents-resultsTable matches “of 50 entries” + app$get_download("potentialParents-downloadParents") CSV = 50 rows & header id,nSires,nDams,sires,dams; (4) graceful degradation — upload_and_wait(ExamplePedigree.csv) → Find → assert #potentialParents-statusMessage matches “colony-origin”. File-level const PP_EXPECTED_CANDIDATES <- 50L is the regression lock (used in 3 assertions). - The “50” lock independently verified BEFORE the browser run through the app’s exact pipeline (read.csv → runQcStudbook(minParentAge=2, reportChanges=TRUE)$cleaned → setPopulation(_, NULL) → findPedigreeNumber/findGeneration → getPotentialParents(_, minParentAge=2.0, maxGestationalPeriod=210L)): 50 on BOTH the full processedPedigree AND the pedigreeData (generated-unknowns removed) variant — so the count is robust to which object the module feeds. Matches S81’s live “50”. The module exports pedigree = reactive(pedigreeData()) (modPedigree.R:344) — i.e., shared$currentPedigree is the FILTERED pedigree, not processedPedigree; both give 50, so the distinction was immaterial here. - Verification chain: independent count-check (50) → devtools::install(quick=TRUE) (the E2E drives the INSTALLED copy; confirmed modPotentialParentsServer exported + fixture installed) → NOT_CRAN=true NPRC_RUN_E2E=true single-file run → ....... = 7/7 PASS, 0 fail/skip/error → clean-regression read (test_dir) 0 failed / 0 error / 0 true offenders with my file 4 skipped in the normal (E2E-off) run → lintr 0 lints. - No helper extraction (REFACTOR scope held): the sibling test-e2e-* files deliberately repeat the skip/AppDriver boilerplate; extracting it would diverge from the established idiom, so the file matches its siblings in shape. Lint clean ⇒ no edits needed in REFACTOR. - [news-vs-changelog] → CHANGELOG only (test-only addition, no R/ code, no user-facing feature; the feature landed in NEWS.md at S80). [macos-dupe-scan]: git add only the test file + SESSION_NOTES.md + PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md + CHANGELOG.md; excluded .DS_Store and the 2 standing untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html).

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): PERFORMED — PASS. The browser E2E run IS the runtime verification (the strongest kind): real Chrome via chromote, real reactive graph, real file upload → QC → pedigree → compute → DT render → CSV download. All 4 blocks pass against the installed app, including the populated 50-animal table, the CSV download (50 rows, correct columns), and the no-fromCenter graceful-degradation warning. shinytest2 + chromote are installed in this environment, so this ran for real — NOT skipped. (NOT_CRAN=true is required or AppDriver aborts “On CRAN” under Rscript; install dev code first — Learning 78.)

Self-assessment (Session 82): 9/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER in full, notes top, dashboard 98/100, git, 17 issues, ghost-check → none: 868a4975 = S81 close-out), reported, STOPPED for the owner’s pick. Claimed the session (Phase 1B stub BEFORE technical work — grounding reads are read-only orientation). Declared the TDD phase every response and gated all 3 transitions via AskUserQuestion with the exact next-phase actions spelled out. Honest TDD framing up front: classified this as a regression backfill of shipped behavior (no production code), surfaced that at the PRE-RED→RED gate, and mapped RED→GREEN→REFACTOR onto it truthfully (RED = author assertions with teeth that self-skip when opt-in is off; GREEN = run against the shipped feature + independently confirm the baked value first; REFACTOR = lint/tidy). Recompute-don’t-inherit: read the module, the helper, two sibling e2e tests, the server wiring, the QC/pedigree chain, and the module’s pedigree return firsthand. Did NOT treat build/skip-clean as runtime-clean (FM #24): actually ran the browser E2E (4/4 blocks, 7/7 expectations) — the strongest Phase-3E. Independently verified the regression value (50) through the real pipeline BEFORE the browser run, so the E2E passed first try. Assertions have teeth: the “50 animal”/“of 50 entries”/50-row-CSV checks only pass when the feature actually returns 50; a broken/empty feature renders the empty-state and fails them (not the body-grepl tautology the helper comments warn against). Right-sized SOLO — a contained single-file test backfill with interactive TDD gates is not a breadth fan-out; a Workflow would have been theater (Learning 70/77 right-size heuristic, contained side) — and that call holds even with ultracode “on”, because the project methodology + documented learnings govern. Scope discipline: no R/ changes, no helper extraction (preserved the sibling idiom), did NOT touch the stale version string, the 2 untracked files, did NOT push or close issues. 0 stakeholder corrections. The −1 (honest): (a) the deliverable adds no new user-facing value — though it closes the literal full-browser-chain gap S80/S81 both flagged; (b) I spent an extra read tracing which reactive the module exports (processedPedigree vs pedigreeData) before finding both yield 50 — a more targeted first read could have saved one round-trip; (c) the table assertion couples to DT’s “of N entries” info-text format (mirrors the existing pedigree e2e test’s “of 375 entries”, so an accepted, idiomatic coupling — but a DT phrasing change would require updating both).

⇒ SUGGESTED NEXT = owner’s pick. The E2E test ships, is runtime-verified, and skips cleanly in CI; nothing mid-flight. 17 open issues (none closed this session — test-only). Key files (this session): NEW (committed): tests/testthat/test-e2e-potential-parents-module.R (4 opt-in blocks; const PP_EXPECTED_CANDIDATES <- 50L; CRUX block ~:73, graceful-degradation block ~:136). CHANGED (docs): CHANGELOG.md (S82 entry), PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 80 at tail), SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). Read firsthand (unchanged): R/modPotentialParents.R (UI controls :65-109; server button/eventReactive :141; statusMessage branches :161-194; downloadHandler :206), tests/testthat/helper-shinytest2.R (create_test_app opt-in :200, create_app_driver :235, navigate_to_tab :258, assert_active_pane :415, upload_and_wait :150, get_html_safe/click_element_safe/get_test_data_path, E2E_TIMEOUT), tests/testthat/test-e2e-pedigree-module.R (the upload→navigate→assert-populated-DT pattern, :60-89 “of 375 entries”), tests/testthat/test-e2e-genetic-value-module.R (the per-block static-surface idiom), R/appServer.R:244-295 (shared$currentPedigree observer :252-255; module wiring :291-295), R/modInput.R:251-302 (readDataFile exact reader), R/modInput.R:407-423 (qcStudbook/runQcStudbook QC call), R/modPedigree.R:260-311 (processedPedigree→pedigreeData), R/modPedigree.R:343-347 (module exports pedigree = pedigreeData()), tests/testthat/test_getPotentialParents.R (unit fixture builds raw rhesusPedigree+fromCenter, no QC). Transient (NOT committed): none (all checks were inline Rscript -e).

Gotchas: (1) The new E2E test is OPT-IN (NPRC_RUN_E2E=true) and drives the INSTALLED package — to run it: devtools::install() dev code first, then NOT_CRAN=true NPRC_RUN_E2E=true Rscript -e '...test_file(...)'. Without NOT_CRAN=true AppDriver aborts “On CRAN”; without install the installed copy may be stale (Learning 78). (2) shinytest2 + chromote ARE installed here — the browser E2E genuinely runs; don’t assume “no Chrome → skip”. (3) PP_EXPECTED_CANDIDATES <- 50L is a regression lock — if the fixture or getPotentialParents ever changes the count, this fails by design; re-derive via the pipeline (read.csv → runQcStudbook(...)$cleaned → setPopulation → getPotentialParents(_, 2.0, 210L)) and update. Both processedPedigree and pedigreeData yield 50 today. (4) The table assertion uses DT’s “of 50 entries” info text (mirrors the pedigree e2e’s “of 375 entries”); a DT phrasing change would require updating it. (5) shared$currentPedigree = the FILTERED pedigreeData() (modPedigree.R:344), auto-populated by the observer at appServer.R:252-255 once the studbook is ready — but blocks 3/4 still visit Pedigree Browser first to faithfully mirror S81’s manual flow and dodge any output-suspension flakiness. (6) ExamplePedigree.csv is the right no-fromCenter graceful-degradation input (QC-clean, no colony-origin field); the populated path needs rhesusPedigree_fromCenter.csv. (7) CLAUDE.md + appUI.R:198 still say “Version 1.0.8” (DESCRIPTION 1.1.0.9000) — stale since S56. (8) The 2 untracked files + .DS_Store remain uncommitted (build-ignored / owner’s standing keep-call). (9) Default gh issue view errors on projectCards — use gh issue view N --json <fields>. Branch model unchanged: this close-out is on add-methodology; master gets it on the next sync/PR (S51 promoted prior work via PR #41).

What Session 81 Did

Deliverable: Owner-confirmed close of #48 (the getPotentialParents “Potential Parents” tab shipped by S80), gated on a live owner click-through verified end-to-end. To make the populated path reachable, staged a purpose-built clean fromCenter example dataset (every shipped pedigree example is a deliberate input-error QC fixture — owner-confirmed). (DONE — click-through PASS (graceful-degradation + populated 50-animal table + CSV download); inst/extdata/rhesusPedigree_fromCenter.csv added; #48 CLOSED.) Started / Completed: 2026-06-15 Status: DONE. Verification + issue-management workstream (TDD phase = N/A declared every response — added an example dataset + diagnosis, no production logic/tests). One close-out commit on add-methodology. No R/, tests/, man/, .lintr, or NEWS.md changes. - The click-through-first call (owner’s pick) earned its keep. Provided the step-by-step script (Part A graceful-degradation on a standard file; Part B populated path on the new file). The owner ran it live and caught a real defect in my staged file that my headless pre-check had blessed → I fixed and re-verified against the app’s EXACT ingest path before close. - NEW inst/extdata/rhesusPedigree_fromCenter.csv (375 animals = nprcgenekeepr::rhesusPedigree + fromCenter = TRUE; unknown parents written as literal NA matching ExamplePedigree.csv). Verified through the real pipeline (app reader → runQcStudbook → setPopulation → getPotentialParents): QC clean, fromCenter preserved, 50 animals with candidates. Owner chose the rhesusPedigree source (vs full-ExamplePedigree / modify-in-place) via AskUserQuestion. - Defect caught by the click-through (FM #24 — replication diverged from the app): my first file wrote unknowns as empty cells (write.csv(na="")); the app’s text reader (modInput.R:274 = read.table(..., fill=TRUE, quote="\"") with default na.strings="NA") reads "" as the empty string, so "" landed in both the sire and dam columns → correctParentSex.R:71 intersect(sires,dams) flagged “Subject(s) listed as both sire and dam” (the empty offending id = the “cell empty” the owner saw). My pre-check had used na.strings=c("","NA"), silently converting ""→NA and masking it. Rewrote with na="NA" and re-verified with the app’s exact reader → clean. - Diagnosed two non-defects firsthand from the code (not guesses): (1) the “Column name case changed: fromCenter → fromcenter” warning is cosmetic — fixColumnNames.R:20 lowercases all headers (logging the caseChange), :61 restores fromcenter→fromCenter; the cleaned studbook keeps fromCenter, which is exactly what getPotentialParents (case-sensitive names(ped)=="fromCenter") needs. Same noise hits recordStatus/geographicOrigin. (2) “No data available in table” = the empty Errors tab DT placeholder (no errors). The earlier repeat of “both sire and dam” was a stale upload — cleared once the owner re-read the rewritten file. - [news-vs-changelog] → CHANGELOG only (verification + a test-support example dataset + issue close = dev-process history; the feature already landed in NEWS.md at S80; no R/ code changed). [macos-dupe-scan]: git add only inst/extdata/rhesusPedigree_fromCenter.csv + SESSION_NOTES.md + PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md + CHANGELOG.md; excluded .DS_Store and the 2 standing untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html).

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): PERFORMED — PASS (owner-run live click-through). The strongest possible runtime verification: real browser, real reactive graph, real file upload. Part A (graceful degradation): ExamplePedigree.csv (QC-clean, no fromCenter) → Potential Parents → Find → empty-state warning “This dataset has no colony-origin (‘fromCenter’) field…” — proves the tab mounts, the button fires, no crash. Part B (populated): rhesusPedigree_fromCenter.csv → QC pass → Pedigree Browser populates → Potential Parents → Find → 50-animal sortable table (status “Found candidate parents for 50 animal(s)…”) → CSV downloaded as potential_parents_2026-06-15.csv (matches paste0("potential_parents_", Sys.Date(), ".csv")). Existing tabs intact.

Session 80 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 81): Score 8/10. S80 pre-named THIS exact deliverable as SUGGESTED-NEXT candidate (1): “Owner-confirm close #48 … Optionally first do a manual/owner click-through of the live tab with real data.” That, plus the documented data seams (fromCenter-absent → NULL → empty-state), the baselines (dashboard 98/100, 18 open issues, the gh issue view --json gotcha, the 2-untracked-files disposition, the stale CLAUDE.md version, the branch model), and the headless-smoke recipe, meant zero time lost on what to do. The −2: S80’s own tests fabricate rhesusPedigree$fromCenter <- TRUE — so S80 knew the feature needs a fromCenter column — yet the handoff said “click-through … with real data” without flagging that no shipped studbook cleanly yields a fromCenter pedigree (the example files are deliberate input-error fixtures), so a live populated click-through requires staging a clean fixture. I discovered that firsthand (and the empty-cell NA trap, and the cosmetic case-change warning — none pre-loaded). All genuinely the verifier’s to find, but a one-line “stage a clean fromCenter file; shipped examples are error fixtures” would have saved a round-trip. ROI: high.

Self-assessment (Session 81): 8/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER in full, notes top, dashboard 98/100, git, 18 issues, ghost-check → none: 74e0f77a = S80 #48 impl), reported, STOPPED for the owner’s pick. Claimed the session (Phase 1B stub BEFORE technical work). Declared TDD = N/A every response (no production logic/tests — an example dataset + diagnosis). Recompute-don’t-inherit honored hard: read modPotentialParents, getPotentialParents, the full input→QC→pedigree→currentPedigree flow (modInput/runQcStudbook/getPedigree/modPedigree/appServer), correctParentSex, fixColumnNames, runModularApp firsthand. Surfaced the genuine data fork via AskUserQuestion (new-file source), 0 design corrections. Did the live click-through FIRST (owner’s call) — which CAUGHT the empty-cell defect unit/headless checks had blessed; I diagnosed the “both sire and dam”, the stale-upload repeat, and the cosmetic case-change warning from the code, not by guessing. Closed #48 only on owner confirmation (standing rule). Right-sized SOLO — a contained diagnose-and-stage with interactive owner round-trips, not a breadth fan-out (a Workflow would have been theater; Learning 70). The −2 (honest): (a) I shipped a defective fixture to the owner first because my pre-flight used na.strings=c("","NA") instead of the app’s default na.strings="NA" — a faithful replication of the app’s reader up front (FM #24) would have caught it before the owner saw it; (b) a couple of round-trips (stale-upload confusion, case-change alarm) were partly pre-emptable by telling the owner up front to re-select after a file rewrite and that the case-change warning is cosmetic.

⇒ SUGGESTED NEXT = owner’s pick. #48 is CLOSED (verified + owner-confirmed); nothing mid-flight. 17 open issues (#48 closed this session). Candidates: - (1) Durable opt-in E2E test for the Potential Parents tab — tests/testthat/test-e2e-potential-parents-module.R mirroring test-e2e-*-module.R (opt-in via NPRC_RUN_E2E, helper-shinytest2.R): now you can drive it with the shipped inst/extdata/rhesusPedigree_fromCenter.csv — load it, navigate, Find, assert the table populates, exercise the CSV download. Small TDD/test-only session; closes S80’s literal full-browser-chain gap. (S80 candidate 2.) - (2) ORIP wire-in (#47, low priority) — mount-only, 2 edits + Phase-3E (headless-smoke recipe in Learning 78). - (3) Docfix sweep — getPedDirectRelatives @examples bug (R/getPedDirectRelatives.R:27 calls getLkDirectRelatives, not itself) ± dedicated tests for kinshipMatrixToKValues/getAncestors. Small TDD. - (4) UX polish (optional, file an issue first): the cosmetic “fromCenter → fromcenter” caseChange warning fires for every canonical camelCase column that fixColumnNames lowercases-then-restores (fromCenter, recordStatus, geographicOrigin) — confusing to users. Could suppress the warning for restored columns. Minor. - (5) Housekeeping: CLAUDE.md + appUI.R:198 still say “Version 1.0.8” vs DESCRIPTION 1.1.0.9000 (carried since S56). (Verify any check “fix” reproduces first — the only standing check NOTE has been the transient timestamp one.) - (6) Push add-methodology to origin (now ahead by ~34 after this close-out; S51 promoted prior work via PR #41). Do NOT implement #28 (still no location source; gated on #11/#12/#37/#46), do NOT close #45/#47, and do NOT close #1, #5, #9, or any OPEN issue without firsthand evidence.

Key files (this session): NEW (committed): inst/extdata/rhesusPedigree_fromCenter.csv (375 animals; fromCenter=TRUE; unknowns as literal NA; the Potential Parents demo/click-through fixture). CHANGED (docs): CHANGELOG.md (S81 entry), PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 79 at tail), SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). GitHub: #48 CLOSED (owner-confirmed; close comment posted). Read firsthand (unchanged): R/modPotentialParents.R (server fromCenter empty-state :176), R/getPotentialParents.R (fromCenter gate :38), R/appServer.R:244-295 (shared$currentPedigree flow), R/modInput.R (readDataFile:251-296 exact reader; QC call :407-423), R/runQcStudbook.R (cleaned=NULL on any error :160), R/correctParentSex.R:71 (intersect(sires,dams) → “both sire and dam”), R/fixColumnNames.R:20,61 (lowercase-then-restore), R/getPedigree.R, R/modPedigree.R:260-310 (pedigree auto-produces from studbook), R/runModularApp.R (in-namespace appUI()/appServer). Transient (NOT committed): /tmp/s81_*.R.

Gotchas: (1) The shipped pedigree example files are deliberately error-laden input-error QC fixtures (jmac csv: 2 hard parent-age errors; jmac-edited: a “.” in an ID; xlsx: invalid dates; ExamplePedigree: QC-clean but no fromCenter) — none reaches the populated Potential Parents path. The clean demo file is inst/extdata/rhesusPedigree_fromCenter.csv. (2) Replicate the app’s EXACT reader when pre-verifying example data: text path = read.table(..., fill=TRUE, quote="\"") with default na.strings="NA" (modInput.R:274) — empty cells stay "" (NOT NA). Write unknown parents as literal NA (write.csv(..., na="NA")). Using na.strings=c("","NA") in a check masks the empty-cell “both sire and dam” bug (FM #24). (3) The “fromCenter → fromcenter” caseChange warning is cosmetic — fixColumnNames.R:20 lowercases all headers (logs it), :61 restores fromCenter; the cleaned studbook keeps fromCenter. (4) processQcStudbookResult()$hasErrors does NOT include the sireAndDam category — the app’s Errors tab renders the raw errorLst$sireAndDam directly; inspect the errorLst components, not just hasErrors, when judging “is it clean.” (5) Run the dev app with the new tab via pkgload::load_all("."); runModularApp() (builds from appUI()/appServer in-namespace — no reinstall; an older installed copy lacks the tab and the line-61 restore). (6) #48 is CLOSED (owner-confirmed). (7) CLAUDE.md + appUI.R:198 still say “Version 1.0.8” (DESCRIPTION 1.1.0.9000) — stale since S56. (8) The 2 untracked files + .DS_Store remain uncommitted (build-ignored / owner’s standing keep-call). (9) Default gh issue view errors on projectCards — use gh issue view N --json <fields>. Branch model unchanged: this close-out is on add-methodology; master gets it on the next sync/PR (S51 promoted prior work via PR #41).

What Session 80 Did

Deliverable: Implemented #48 — the getPotentialParents Shiny wire-in (new top-level “Potential Parents” tab) under Strict TDD (full RED → GREEN → REFACTOR, every transition gated via AskUserQuestion). Turns the shipped #31 package-API logic into user-visible app value — the build-from-scratch second session S79’s Learning 77 predicted. (DONE — module + 14 tests + 2 wiring edits shipped; check() clean; Phase-3E headless smoke PASS. #48 left OPEN pending owner-confirmed close.) Started / Completed: 2026-06-14 / 2026-06-15 Status: DONE. Implementation/bug-fix workstream under Strict TDD. TDD phase declared every response (PRE-RED → RED → GREEN → REFACTOR); 3 phase gates via AskUserQuestion (PRE-RED→RED, RED→GREEN, GREEN→REFACTOR), 0 stakeholder corrections. One close-out commit on add-methodology. - NEW R/modPotentialParents.R: pure flattenPotentialParents() (list-of-lists|NULL → df id,nSires,nDams,sires,dams; toString() collapse; NULL/empty → 0-row; @noRd internal); modPotentialParentsUI() (numeric maxGestationalPeriod default 210, “Find Potential Parents” button, DT::DTOutput sortable table, downloadButton, uiOutput status/empty-state); modPotentialParentsServer(id, pedigree = NULL, minParentAge = 2.0) (eventReactive(findParents) → getPotentialParents → flatten; DT::renderDT; downloadHandler CSV; graceful degradation). Both mod functions @export. - NEW tests/testthat/test_modPotentialParents.R: 14 tests / 43 expectations — helper (cols, NULL/empty→0-row, multi-animal flatten, empty-sires→""/0, write.csv round-trip), UI (4 namespaced controls + “Find Potential Parents” + default 210 + shiny.tag), server via shiny::testServer (happy path on rhesus fixture → BRI2MW present; no-fromCenter→empty; no-unknowns→empty; return-shape). All pass; lint clean (0). - Wiring (2 edits): appUI.R — tabPanel("Potential Parents", icon("search"), modPotentialParentsUI("potentialParents")) after Breeding Groups; appServer.R — modPotentialParentsServer("potentialParents", pedigree = reactive(shared$currentPedigree)) after the Breeding Groups module. - Data seams honored (from #48): no fromCenter col → getPotentialParents returns NULL → empty-state (not error); minParentAge does NOT flow to shared so it stayed a server param default 2.0 (QC value) — owner ratified only the gestation UI input, so no second UI input added (scope held); empty results → empty-state message. - Verification: GREEN devtools::check() 0/0/0; post-REFACTOR check() 0 errors / 0 warnings / 1 NOTE (unable to verify current time — transient environmental clock note, NOT code); full clean-regression read 0 real failed/error (847 rows); lintr clean on both new files; document() regenerated man/modPotentialParents*.Rd + NAMESPACE (flattenPotentialParents stayed internal). - [news-vs-changelog]: user-facing feature → BOTH NEWS.md (new “Shiny application” bullet under 1.1.0.9000) and CHANGELOG.md (S80 process entry). First code session since S74, so NEWS gets an entry (S76–S79 were design/process → CHANGELOG-only). [macos-dupe-scan]: git add only the source/test/doc/notes files; the 2 standing untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html) excluded + untouched.

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): PERFORMED — PASS. Booted the assembled app headlessly via shinytest2::AppDriver against inst/shinytest/app.R (= shinyApp(appUI(), appServer)). Gotchas to reuse: set NOT_CRAN=true (AppDriver aborts “On CRAN” under Rscript otherwise); devtools::install() the dev package FIRST (the e2e app drives the installed copy); the e2e suite is opt-in (NPRC_RUN_E2E=true) and skips in test()/check(). Confirmed: app boots with full server init (incl. the new module), the tab + all 4 controls mount, default 210 set, navigation to the tab works, existing tabs (Breeding Groups, Genetic Value Analysis) intact, and the module’s outputs (statusMessage/resultsTable/downloadParents) register error-free. The only log “errors” are pre-existing shinyBS is not defined JS reference errors (app-wide, used by other tabs’ popovers — NOT from this module; separated by grepping the logs for the potentialParents- namespace). Not done (honest boundary): the literal single-chain browser E2E (upload a file → create pedigree → click → see table → download the CSV) — the compute/table/CSV path is runtime-verified by testServer on a real fromCenter rhesus pedigree (the real reactive graph, not mocks) and the integration mount by AppDriver; the data-loaded full-browser interaction is split across those two rather than one E2E.

Session 79 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 80): Score 9/10. S79 pre-named THIS exact deliverable as SUGGESTED-NEXT candidate (1) with remarkable precision: “Implement #48 … a real TDD code session (RED→GREEN→REFACTOR with phase gates) building R/modPotentialParents.R (numeric maxGestationalPeriod input default 210, ‘Find Potential Parents’ button, results table, CSV download) + tests/testthat/test_modPotentialParents.R + the 2 wiring edits (appUI.R tab + appServer.R server call), with a mandatory Phase-3E runtime smoke test … Start from #48’s task list + data seams.” Every element held firsthand: the exact file names, the exact controls, the exact 2 wiring edits, the Phase-3E mandate, the data seams (fromCenter→NULL, reuse 2-yr minParentAge, empty-state), and the baselines (dashboard 98/100, 18 issues, gh --json gotcha, 2-untracked-files disposition, stale CLAUDE.md version, branch model). Zero time spent discovering what to build or where the seams are — I went straight from orient → grounding reads → gates. What helped most: #48’s ratified forks + documented seams meant the design was settled; I only had to choose implementation-level details (helper extraction, DT-for-sortable, minParentAge-as-param). The −1 (structural per-issue ceiling, not a fault): the handoff couldn’t pre-load the Phase-3E toolchain gotchas (NOT_CRAN, install-first, opt-in e2e, shinyBS log noise) or that minParentAge doesn’t reach shared (forcing the param choice) — all genuinely the implementer’s to discover. What was wrong: nothing material. ROI: very high.

Self-assessment (Session 80): 9/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER in full, notes top, dashboard 98/100, git, 18 issues, ghost-check → none: 760561b7 = S79 close-out), reported, STOPPED for the owner’s pick. Claimed the session with a Phase 1B stub BEFORE writing any test/impl code (grounding reads are read-only orientation). Declared the TDD phase every single response and gated all 3 transitions via AskUserQuestion with the exact next-phase actions spelled out (per the project’s phase-gate contract). RED was clean and isolated — 14 tests failing for the right reason (undefined functions), verified the rest of the suite stayed green before GREEN. GREEN was minimal and honored the ratified scope (no second UI input). Recompute-don’t-inherit: read the function, both wiring files, the module idiom, the test idiom, the minParentAge exposure, and the existing fixture firsthand. Did NOT treat build-clean as runtime-clean (FM #24): actually launched the app and ran a headless mount smoke, distinguishing my module’s logs from pre-existing shinyBS noise. Extracted a pure helper (flattenPotentialParents) so the contract is deterministically testable and the table + CSV can’t diverge (→ Learning 78). Scope discipline: did NOT close #48 (owner-confirmed close), did NOT touch the stale CLAUDE.md/appUI version string, the 2 untracked files, or push. Right-sized SOLO — a focused implementation across ~6 files with interactive TDD gates is not a breadth fan-out; a Workflow would have been theater (Learning 70/77 right-size heuristic, contained side). Honest 9 — a complete, runtime-verified vertical slice shipping genuine user-facing value with full TDD discipline and 0 corrections. The −1 (honest): (a) the post-REFACTOR check() carries a transient environmental NOTE (clock; the GREEN check was 0/0/0); (b) I covered the data-loaded full-browser E2E via testServer (compute/table/CSV) + AppDriver (mount/boot/nav) rather than one literal upload→click→download chain, and did NOT add a durable opt-in e2e test mirroring the per-module test-e2e-* pattern (left as a clearly-scoped follow-up).

Key files (this session): NEW: R/modPotentialParents.R (helper flattenPotentialParents :18; UI :66; server :138), tests/testthat/test_modPotentialParents.R (14 tests), man/modPotentialParentsServer.Rd, man/modPotentialParentsUI.Rd. CHANGED (code): R/appUI.R (Potential Parents tabPanel after Breeding Groups, ~:171-176), R/appServer.R (module server call after Breeding Groups, ~:291-295), NAMESPACE (2 exports). CHANGED (docs): NEWS.Rmd (SOURCE: added the Shiny “Potential Parents” entry + back-ported the missing NEW-47/48/49) → regenerated NEWS.md via rmarkdown::render() (owner-flagged mid-session sync fix), CHANGELOG.md (S80 entry), PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 78 at tail), SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). Read firsthand (unchanged): R/getPotentialParents.R (full; return shape :117-121), R/modORIPReporting.R (downloadHandler idiom), R/modGvAndBgDesc.R (minimal module), R/modInput.R:122 (minParentAge textInput, NOT propagated to shared), tests/testthat/test_modPyramid.R (testServer idiom), tests/testthat/test_modFounderStats.R (pure-helper idiom), tests/testthat/test_getPotentialParents.R (fixture), tests/testthat/helper-shinytest2.R:200 (create_test_app opt-in gate), inst/shinytest/app.R. Transient (NOT committed): /tmp/s80_smoke.R, /tmp/s80_logs.R, /tmp/s80_check*.log, /tmp/s80_install.log.

Gotchas: (1) #48 is implemented but stays OPEN pending owner-confirmed close (don’t close without owner sign-off — the standing rule). (2) Phase-3E headless-smoke recipe (reusable for #47 and future wire-ins): NOT_CRAN=true Rscript + devtools::install() dev code first + AppDriver$new(system.file("shinytest", package="nprcgenekeepr")); grep logs for YOUR namespace, not “error” (the shinyBS is not defined errors are pre-existing app-wide noise). (3) minParentAge is a server param (default 2.0), NOT a UI input — it does not flow through shared (modInput’s minParentAge reactive isn’t propagated to appServer’s shared); a future “let the user set minParentAge in this tab” would need either a new UI input or threading it through shared. (4) maxGestationalPeriod = MAX (210 rhesus, ~165 typical), documented “integer” but NOT type-checked — carried; the UI numericInput prefills 210. (5) Species-keyed gestation stays #46 — v1 is single-species rhesus; do NOT pull #46 in. (6) The post-REFACTOR check() NOTE (unable to verify current time) is environmental (clock/network), not a defect — the GREEN check was 0/0/0; don’t chase it. (7) flattenPotentialParents is internal (@noRd) — accessible in tests via bare name under load_all; not part of the public API (keeps #37’s export surface from growing). (8) Default gh issue view errors on projectCards — use gh issue view N --json <fields>. (9) CLAUDE.md + appUI.R:198 still say “Version 1.0.8” (DESCRIPTION 1.1.0.9000) — stale, carried since S56. (10) The 2 untracked files remain — build-ignored / owner’s standing keep-call. (11) NEWS.md is GENERATED from NEWS.Rmd (github_document) — edit the .Rmd source and rmarkdown::render("NEWS.Rmd"), never hand-edit NEWS.md. This session found prior sessions had hand-edited NEWS.md, leaving NEWS.Rmd missing NEW-47/48/49 (now fixed). Verify sync with diff <(Rscript -e 'rmarkdown::render("NEWS.Rmd", output_file="/tmp/n.md", quiet=TRUE)') NEWS.md. Branch model unchanged: this close-out is on add-methodology; master gets it on the next sync/PR (S51 promoted prior work via PR #41).

What Session 79 Did

Deliverable: Resolved the three UX/design forks for the getPotentialParents wire-in (the #37 integration of the S74 #31 logic, scoped under umbrella #45) with the owner via AskUserQuestion, and recorded the ratified design on a new tracking issue #48 so a following session can implement under TDD. Separately, deprioritized #47 (ORIP) per owner direction. (DONE — #48 filed with the 3 ratified forks; register-ratified link comment on #45; #47 labeled low priority; #45 left OPEN; open issues 17 → 18.) Started / Completed: 2026-06-14 Status: DONE. Design/decisions session (no production code or tests; TDD phase = N/A declared every response — same classification as S76/S77/S78). One close-out commit on add-methodology. No R/, tests/, man/, .lintr, or NEWS.md changes. - The question vs. the action (FM #23): the owner’s “explain getPotentialParents wire-in” was a QUESTION — I explained firsthand (read getPotentialParents.R + appUI.R + appServer.R), did NOT implement, and recommended a design-step-first path. Only on “Yes” (proceed with that path) did I claim the session (Phase 1B stub BEFORE technical work) and resolve the forks. Kept the design step SEPARATE from implementation (FM #18 → Learning 77). - Owner decisions (via AskUserQuestion, 3 forks, all applied as recommended, 0 corrections): (1) maxGestationalPeriod source = a numeric input prefilled with the rhesus default 210, user-overridable per run (v1 single-species per #45; species-keyed gestation stays #46); (2) location = a new top-level “Potential Parents” tab (peer to the 8 existing module tabs); (3) trigger/output = a “Find Potential Parents” button → sortable table (animal → candidate sires/dams) → CSV download (the app’s downloadHandler idiom). - Firsthand grounding (recompute-don’t-inherit): getPotentialParents.R read in full — in-colony (fromCenter) animals with ≥1 unknown parent; gestation-derived dam window :83-85, exclusion :106, sire-at-conception :66-71; zero production callers (only its test + inst/extdata/trulyUnknownParents.R). appUI.R — 8 mounted module tabs (modXxxUI("id") in tabPanels). appServer.R:104-293 — the modXxxServer("id", <reactives>) wiring + the shared$currentPedigree/shared$geneticValues flow. fromCenter producers — convertFromCenter + qcStudbook:229-230/:281-283. Data seams confirmed for the implementer: fromCenter-absent → function returns NULL (UI must degrade gracefully); minParentAge has NO signature default (reuse QC’s 2-yr config); empty-results → empty-state, not error. Noted #45’s body line-map predates S74 (stale). - Deprioritization (#47 ORIP): repo had no priority labels + no BACKLOG.md (Issues are the backlog) → created a low priority label and applied it to #47. ORIP’s module already exists (a mount-only wire-in), correctly deferred behind the larger build-from-scratch #48 (→ Learning 77). - The outward-facing work: #48 created (enhancement, body /tmp/s79_ppwire_issue.md — transient) with the 3 ratified forks, the from-scratch module/test/wiring task, the data seams, the #28/#46 out-of-scope boundary, and the mandatory Phase-3E smoke test; register-ratified link comment on #45 (#45-issuecomment-4704597167, additive — FM #22). Verified: #48 OPEN, #45 OPEN (3 comments), #47 labeled low priority, open count 18. Nothing closed. - [news-vs-changelog] → CHANGELOG only (resolving design forks + filing an issue is dev-process history → NOT NEWS.md; no BACKLOG.md file — open work lives in GitHub Issues). [macos-dupe-scan]: git add only SESSION_NOTES.md + CHANGELOG.md + PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md; the 2 standing untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html) excluded + untouched.

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A and stated — no runtime behavior changed (filed 1 issue, posted 1 comment, created 1 label, edited build-ignored docs; no R/ code). The implementation session that builds #48 owns the mandatory Phase-3E smoke test.

What Session 77 Did

Deliverable: Ratified the 8 [OPEN] items in #28’s §13 open-decisions register (the S76 colocation data-model spec) via owner sign-off, and recorded the decisions back onto #28 (every item [OPEN] → [DECIDED]). (DONE — ratification comment posted on #28; register-ratified link on #45; new issue #46 filed for the corrected species dependency; #28/#45 left OPEN; open issues 15 → 16.) Started / Completed: 2026-06-14 Status: DONE. Design/grooming/decisions session (no production code or tests; TDD phase = N/A declared every response — same classification as S73/S76). One close-out commit on add-methodology. No R/, tests/, man/, .lintr, or NEWS.md changes. - Method (Workflow under ultracode, Learning 70 breadth side + new Learning 75): a verify-and-sharpen workflow (wf_b8035a53-5be) — 8 parallel item-verifiers (one per register item, each re-reading the spec’s code claims firsthand against the working tree and adversarially stress-testing its [REC]) → an adversarial completeness critic. The critic earned its keep: it reduced an over-asked 8-item register to 4 genuine owner-decisions (items 1/2/3 + item 6’s provenance half), classified the rest as ratifiable corollaries/deferrals, and surfaced two §7 policy gaps absent from §13. - Two firsthand corrections to the S76 spec (→ Learning 75a): (a) item 2’s written [REC] is unsound — getPotentialParents.R:46-48’s inclusive-OR pUnknown filter means a both-unknown infant’s own birth-time location is a valid dam-side anchor (only the sire side is anchorless), so the decision flipped from the spec’s “require ≥1 known parent” to dam-side colocation only; (b) §10 mis-cited #36 (“Add chimpanzee-specific age pyramid plot settings”, a display ticket) as the “make species first-class” prerequisite — first-class species was un-ticketed, now filed as #46. - Owner decisions (via AskUserQuestion, 2 staggered rounds — item 2 deliberately placed in round 2 so it could be informed by item 1’s answer; all ratified as recommended, 0 corrections): (1) missing-dam = mother–infant co-housing model + required no-default species-tunable postnatalCoHousingWindow + gestation-presence, strictly soft-rank (hard-filter rejected); (2) both-unknown = dam-side only via focal anchor; (3) output = additive-only invariant locked, carrier deferred; (4) coherent obfuscation (one shared per-animal delta + alias FK-remap, built WITH #28); (5) v1 single-species, #28 ⊥ first-class species; (6) pull-date provenance stamp (full bitemporal deferred); (7+8) defer flat-file & POSIXct (LabKey-only, Date res); §7 QC policy (open-start = no overlap; contradictory rows = QC-warn). - The outward-facing work: ratification comment on #28 (#28-issuecomment-4703716881, body /tmp/s77_ratify28.md — transient) annotating every item [DECIDED-S77]/[SIZING] with the two corrections inline + sizing notes for the implementer; register-ratified link comment on #45 (#45-issuecomment-4703717807); #46 created (enhancement, body /tmp/s77_species_issue.md — transient). Verified: #28 OPEN (3 comments), #45 OPEN (2 comments), #46 OPEN, open count 16. Nothing closed. - [news-vs-changelog] → CHANGELOG only (ratifying a register on an issue + filing an issue is dev-process history → NOT NEWS.md; no BACKLOG.md file — open work lives in GitHub Issues). [macos-dupe-scan]: git add only SESSION_NOTES.md + CHANGELOG.md + PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md; the 2 standing untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html) excluded + untouched.

Gotchas: (1) #28’s register is FULLY RATIFIED (all 8 items + §7 policy [DECIDED-S77]) — don’t re-ask. The spec now has both a ratified data model (S76) and ratified inference semantics (S77). But #28 stays OPEN and gated for IMPLEMENTATION on a concrete location source (#11 Oracle = demographic-only/unspecified; #12 ARMS = empty stub), app wiring (#37), and — for multi-species — #46. Do NOT implement #28; “ready to size” ≠ “ready to build.” (2) Two spec corrections are now on the record — item 2’s both-unknown [REC] was overturned (dam-side colocation IS recoverable via the focal-animal anchor); #36 was mis-cited (it’s age-pyramid display) → first-class species is now #46. (3) Sizing notes for the #28 implementer (recorded on #28): postnatalCoHousingWindow is a NEW required, species-dependent param (no default, mirrors maxGestationalPeriod); obfuscateDate.R:49-57’s per-element re-draw-to-floor must become a per-animal draw — this changes existing obfuscation output and will break current obfuscatePed/obfuscateDate test expectations (budget for it); the scored-output carrier/columns are deferred until #37 + items 1/2 are concrete. (4) #46 is NEW (open count 15 → 16) — the real first-class-species dependency, distinct from #36’s display scope. (5) maxGestationalPeriod is the MAX (210 rhesus, ~165 typical), documented “integer” but NOT type-checked — carried. (6) getPotentialParents is exported but UNWIRED (#37) — the spec’d feature delivers package-API value, not app value, until integration. (7) Default gh issue view errors on projectCards — use gh issue view N --json <fields>. (8) CLAUDE.md still says “Version 1.0.8” (DESCRIPTION 1.1.0.9000) — stale, carried since S56. (9) The 2 untracked files remain — build-ignored / owner’s standing keep-call. Branch model unchanged: this close-out is on add-methodology; master gets it on the next sync/PR (S51 promoted prior work via PR #41).

No R/, tests/, man/, .lintr, or NEWS.md changes. - Method (Workflow under ultracode, Learning 70 breadth heuristic): a 2-phase workflow (wf_b938c215-f7a) — 5 parallel facet readers (conception primitive / pedigree data-model+ingestion / LabKey-Oracle-ARMS sourcing / existing location+temporal constructs / colocation semantics+domain-fit) → an adversarial completeness critic. The critic earned its keep (→ Learning 74): it caught an inter-reader contradiction (Facet B “species novelCol survives” vs Facet D “species dropped”), flagged Facet E’s 3-case inference model as invented design presented as findings, surfaced the obfuscation-coherence constraint neither issue mentioned, and produced a clean owner-decision list + 13-section outline. - Recompute-don’t-inherit (Learning 70/72c/73 → 74b): re-read every load-bearing file firsthand before the outward-facing post — getPotentialParents.R (full, post-S74; #45’s :72-94 map was stale), getPossibleCols, getDateColNames, getSiteInfo (live default /ONPRC/EHR; roxygen /SNPRC is stale), getDemographics, obfuscateDate, obfuscatePed, fixColumnNames, qcStudbook:281-283 (resolved the species contradiction firsthand — novelCols survive). - Owner decisions (via AskUserQuestion, 4 questions, applied exactly): temporal model = Date-ranged residency intervals (day resolution); grain = configurable, finest-available default; effect = soft rank with fallback; v1 scope = source-agnostic model + optional location arg, byte-identical when location = NULL (not hard-blocked on #11/#12). - The outward-facing work: spec comment on #28 (#28-issuecomment-4703570168, body /tmp/s76_spec28.md — transient) with a [DECIDED]/[REC]/[OPEN] status legend, a data dictionary, the overlap predicate, ingestion design mirroring getDemographics/getSiteInfo, a null/partial-coverage matrix, the obfuscation-coherence requirement (§9), an open-decisions register, and implementation acceptance criteria gated on #11/#12/#37/#36. Criterion-#4 link comment on #45 (#45-issuecomment-4703572531). Verified: #28 state=OPEN, 2 comments, last comment is the spec; nothing closed. - [news-vs-changelog] → CHANGELOG only (S76 entry under [Unreleased]; a spec recorded on an issue is dev-process history → NOT NEWS.md; no BACKLOG.md file — open work lives in GitHub Issues). (4) Obfuscation interplay is a real constraint — obfuscatePed jitters each Date column independently (±30 d) and covers only the pedigree; any location-date obfuscation must be coherent per-animal + remap the location FK via the alias map, else overlap math breaks (spec §9). (5) maxGestationalPeriod is the MAX (210 rhesus, ~165 typical), documented “integer” but NOT type-checked — carried from S74. (6) getPotentialParents is exported but UNWIRED (#37) — the spec’d feature delivers package-API value, not app value, until integration. What Session 74 Did

Deliverable: Implement #31 — replace the dam-exclusion “hack” (fixed ±½-year birth window) in getPotentialParents with a principled gestation-derived window driven by the existing maxGestationalPeriod parameter (owner-decided S73: reuse/extend that param, NO new parameter). The near-term, low-lift sub-task of umbrella #45. (DONE — feature shipped; devtools::check() 0/0/0; full suite 0/0; #31 left OPEN, awaiting owner confirm to close.) Started / Completed: 2026-06-13 Status: DONE. Code session under strict TDD: RED→GREEN→REFACTOR(skipped) — a separate pre-RED scope/approach decision + all three transition gates (PRE-RED→RED, RED→GREEN, GREEN→REFACTOR-skipped) posed via AskUserQuestion; TDD phase declared atop every response. 0 stakeholder corrections. One close-out commit on add-methodology. - The change (ONE rule, one line of logic): a female who delivered another offspring within maxGestationalPeriod days of the focal birth cannot have gestated the focal animal (a female bears one offspring at a time), so she is excluded as a candidate dam. The births window (getPotentialParents.R:83-85) now uses pUnknown$birth[i] ± maxGestationalPeriod instead of ± (dYear / 2L) (182.5 d). The :106 dam exclusion (potentialDams[!id %in% births$dam, ]) and :92-95 comment block had the TODO “hack” replaced with a principled explanation; the @param maxGestationalPeriod roxygen (now ~:17-27) documents it as the conservative MAX gestation (e.g. 210 for rhesus vs ~165 typical), its dual sire-retention + dam-exclusion use, and the intentional sire(conception)/dam(birth) exit-check asymmetry. - Owner decision (pre-RED, via AskUserQuestion): minimal principled change — only the births window; document the exit-check asymmetry as correct (NOT changed); leave the preferential band untouched (proven a no-op); recompute fixtures with per-delta justification; keep maxGestationalPeriod=210L in tests. Applied exactly as chosen. - Recompute-don’t-inherit (Learning 70/72 applied to a code change → Learning 73): in PRE-RED I read issue #31/#45 + getPotentialParents.R + test_getPotentialParents.R firsthand, then built a throwaway read-only oracle (/tmp/s74_explore.R, never touching R/) to quantify the behavior change BEFORE writing tests — every fixture delta derived independently + justified biologically, not from running the production change. - TDD trail (RED→GREEN): RED edited ONLY test_getPotentialParents.R — recomputed exact-set fixtures (dams_1 drops 0B7XRI/PHCADH; dams_4 drops 1SIP4V/DMI0QY/HV7LZ3, each with a justification comment citing the conflicting-offspring offset) + added 2 driving tests (a hand-verifiable synthetic-exclusion pedigree + a differential-responsiveness test, acceptance criterion #2). Confirmed RED: 4 expected failures for the right reasons; the 4 unaffected tests passed. GREEN: the one-line window change + comment + roxygen + devtools::document() → all green. - Verification: test file 21/21; full suite 0 failed / 0 error (real + total, excl test-app-/test-e2e-); lint 0 on both changed files; devtools::check() 0 errors / 0 warnings / 0 NOTES (cleaner than the S71/S72-predicted spelling NOTE — it did not fire; my new doc words are all dictionary words, spell-check clean). [news-vs-changelog] → BOTH (behavior change to an exported function is user-facing): NEWS.md NEW-49 + CHANGELOG.md S74 under [Unreleased]. [macos-dupe-scan]: explicit git add of only the 7 modified files; the 2 standing untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html) excluded + untouched.

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A and stated — getPotentialParents is exported but UNWIRED (zero app/production callers; only its test + inst/extdata/trulyUnknownParents.R), so there is no Shiny runtime path to smoke-test. Its runtime behavior is exercised directly by the suite (the synthetic + differential + recomputed-characterization tests all call the function). No app wiring changed.

Session 73 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 74): Score 9/10. S73 pre-named THIS deliverable as SUGGESTED-NEXT candidate (1) and pre-loaded essentially the entire risk surface: the exact lines (getPotentialParents.R:72-94), the “BEHAVIOR CHANGE, not a pure refactor” flag, the exact-set expect_identical fixture caveat (recompute + justify, don’t silently regenerate), the owner-decided “reuse/extend maxGestationalPeriod, no new param,” the load-bearing reframing (the param already exists, applied sire-side at :62 — so #31 extends it to the dam side, not adds it fresh), the 210L vs ~165 d rhesus reconciliation prompt, the sire/dam exit-check asymmetry note (:62 vs :69), the unwired-function gotcha (#37), the species caveat, and the gh issue view --json gotcha. Every anchor held firsthand — I spent ~zero time discovering what to do or where the risk lived. The −1 is the structural per-issue depth ceiling (same shape as S69–S72): the handoff names where to start, not the answers this session’s research produced — it could not pre-compute the 5 exact fixture deltas, nor pre-prove that the preferential-band move is a no-op or that the exit-check asymmetry is biologically correct (so “reconcile if appropriate” resolved to “document, don’t change”). One minor framing nit in the safe direction: it phrased “reconcile 210L vs actual rhesus gestation” as if a code change might be needed, when it’s a documentation/interpretation matter (resolved by documenting 210 as the conservative max). No inaccuracies cost time. ROI: very high — best-prepared implement handoff in the recent chain.
