Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER in full, session-notes top, dashboard 98/100, git, all 16 issues, ghost-check → none), reported, STOPPED for the owner’s pick. Claimed the session with a Phase 1B stub BEFORE technical work. Declared the TDD phase atop every response; posed a separate pre-RED scope decision + all three transition gates via AskUserQuestion — the project’s phase-gate convention, fully exercised. Recompute-don’t-inherit: read the issue + function + tests firsthand and built a read-only oracle to quantify the change before writing tests. RED was honest: 4 expected failures confirmed for the right reasons; the new exact fixtures were derived from the independent oracle + per-delta biological justification (NOT from running the production change — that would be circular), and backed by two from-first-principles driving tests (synthetic-exclusion + differential-responsiveness) → Learning 73. GREEN minimal: one-line window change + principled comment + roxygen; the :92-93 TODO resolved. Verification depth: full suite (0/0) + lint (0) + check() (0/0/0) + spell-check clean. Scope discipline: proved the preferential-band move is a no-op and documented the asymmetry as correct rather than changing either to “look responsive” (FM #8 resisted); did NOT close #31, touch the stale CLAUDE.md version, the 2 untracked files, or push. Right-sized SOLO (contained single-function change, directly checkable via an oracle + suite + check() — Learning 70/72 breadth heuristic; the read-only exploration was research I needed in my own context, not a fan-out). Stakeholder corrections: 0. Honest 9 — a clean, fully-verified, 0-correction behavior change with a justified fixture recomputation and a genuinely new methodology contribution. The −1 (honest): species-specific gestation remains a documented dependency I did not advance (correctly out of scope), and the function stays unwired (#37) so this delivers package-API value, not app value yet — both correctly out of scope but worth naming so the next session doesn’t expect user-visible effect.

⇒ SUGGESTED NEXT = owner’s pick. #31 is IMPLEMENTED (feature shipped, check()-green); nothing mid-flight. 16 open issues. Candidates: - (1) Close #31 — the gestation-derived dam window is done and check-green; close with a pointer to the S74 commit (would drop 16 → 15 open). I deliberately did NOT self-close (no owner confirm; closing is outward-facing/irreversible — same S68→S69 / S71→S72 discipline). A clean close candidate, NOT re-verification work; verify the shipped code against #31’s asks + #45 acceptance criteria 1–3 before closing (Learning 69). - (2) Spec #28’s data model (the deferred sub-task under #45) — write the colocation data-model spec (grain: cage/room/enclosure; point-in-time vs overlapping range; source query) onto #28, gated on #11/#12. A design/grooming session; do NOT implement (no location data source exists in the package). - (3) Act on the integration gap #37 — getPotentialParents (and other exported-but-unwired functions) deliver no app value until wired in; #45 notes this gates user-visible value for #31. - (4) Housekeeping: (a) CLAUDE.md says “Version 1.0.8” but DESCRIPTION is 1.1.0.9000 (one-line fix, carried since S56); (b) the spelling.Rout.save baseline NOTE S71/S72 flagged — but my check() this session returned 0 NOTES, so it may be flag/environment-dependent; verify it actually reproduces before “fixing” it. - (5) Push add-methodology to origin (now ahead by ~27 after this close-out; S51 promoted prior work via PR #41). Do NOT close #45 / #28 (umbrella + deferred sub-task), and do NOT close #1, #5, #9, or any OPEN issue without firsthand evidence.

Key files (this session): CHANGED R/getPotentialParents.R (births window :83-85 now ± maxGestationalPeriod; :92-95 comment principled, TODO resolved; @param roxygen ~:17-27 expanded — conservative-max meaning + dual use + intentional asymmetry), man/getPotentialParents.Rd (regenerated), tests/testthat/test_getPotentialParents.R (recomputed dams_1/dams_4 with per-delta justification comments + 2 NEW test_that blocks: synthetic-exclusion ~:120-165, differential-responsiveness ~:166-189), NEWS.md (NEW-49, Pedigree-curation section), CHANGELOG.md (S74 under [Unreleased]), PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 73 at tail), SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). The 2 standing untracked files left untouched. TRANSIENT (read-only oracles, NOT committed): /tmp/s74_explore.R, /tmp/s74_synth.R.

Gotchas: (1) #31 is IMPLEMENTED but still OPEN — close (with a commit pointer) only on owner confirm; feature done + check-green = a clean close candidate (16 → 15), not re-verification. (2) The change is exactly ONE rule: births exclusion window = ± maxGestationalPeriod (was ± dYear/2L = 182.5). Only the births filter changed — the preferential band (births_plus_minus_one) and the sire/dam exit-checks are UNCHANGED by design: the band move was proven a behavioral no-op (the wider exclusion at :106 removes the overlap region before the preferential intersection runs), and the asymmetry (exit ≥ birth − gestation sire vs exit ≥ birth dam) is biologically correct (sire→conception, dam→birth) and now documented — do not “reconcile” them into a change. (3) Behavior change to exact-set fixtures: dams_1 dropped 0B7XRI(−193 d)/PHCADH(+195 d); dams_4 dropped 1SIP4V(+183 d)/DMI0QY(+192 d)/HV7LZ3(−192 d) — each justified by an other-offspring birth within ±210 d (comments in the test file). If a future session changes the tests’ maxGestationalPeriod=210L, these fixtures move again. (4) maxGestationalPeriod is the MAX (conservative upper bound), NOT the typical gestation — 210 for rhesus vs ~165 typical; the roxygen documents this. Do NOT “fix” 210→165. (5) getPotentialParents is exported but UNWIRED (only its test + inst/extdata/trulyUnknownParents.R) — #31 delivers package-API value, not app value, until #37 integration. (6) devtools::check() returned 0/0/0 this session — the pre-existing spelling.Rout.save NOTE (S71/S72) did NOT appear; may be flag/environment-dependent. Don’t assume it’s gone; verify if it matters. (7) Default gh issue view errors on projectCards — use gh issue view N --json <fields>. (8) CLAUDE.md still says “Version 1.0.8” (DESCRIPTION 1.1.0.9000) — stale, carried since S56. (9) The 2 untracked files remain — build-ignored / owner’s standing keep-call. Branch model unchanged: this close-out is on add-methodology; master gets it on the next sync/PR (S51 promoted prior work via PR #41).

What Session 73 Did

Deliverable: Consolidate the parent-ID cluster (#31 + #28) into one umbrella design issue, grounded in a firsthand map of the parent-identification subsystem (getPotentialParents.R), with a decided disposition for the two originals. (DONE — umbrella #45 created OPEN; #31 + #28 kept OPEN as distinct, cross-linked sub-tasks (NOT duplicates). Open issues 15 → 16.) Started / Completed: 2026-06-13 Status: DONE. Grooming/design session (no production code or tests; TDD phase = N/A declared every response — same classification as S70’s #44 consolidation). One close-out commit on add-methodology. No R/, tests/, man/, .lintr, or NEWS.md changes. - Firsthand subsystem map (Workflow under ultracode): 2-phase workflow (wf_3acaf054-98d) — phase 1: 4 parallel facet readers (core fn / callers-integration / gestation+location data-infra / tests); phase 2: an adversarial completeness critic over the combined inventory. The critic earned its keep (Learning 70 pattern): it overturned the readers’ overstatements and produced the load-bearing verdict — the two issues share only a thin scalar primitive (conception date = birth − gestation) and have severe lift asymmetry, so they are NOT duplicates. - Recompute-don’t-inherit (Learning 70 → 72c): before the outward-facing create/link I re-read the load-bearing files firsthand. This corrected the critic itself: it claimed “no species column in the data model,” but inst/extdata/deidentified_jmac_ped.csv DOES carry a species column (+ latent multi-species intent: #36, agePyramidPlot.R:60). I published the narrower accurate fact in #45, not the absolute. Reading the core file myself surfaced the single most scope-shaping fact (→ 72b): getPotentialParents already takes maxGestationalPeriod, applied sire-side only (:62) — so #31 is “extend the existing gestation quantity to the dam side,” not “add gestation fresh.” - Owner decisions (via AskUserQuestion, 3 questions): (Disposition) linking umbrella, BOTH #31+#28 kept OPEN (not dedup-closed); (Scope) narrow — getPotentialParents candidate-selection only (species discriminator + #28 data model + #37 integration documented as out-of-scope deps/cross-links); (Gestation source) reuse/extend the existing maxGestationalPeriod, NO parallel parameter. Applied exactly as chosen. - The outward-facing work: created #45 (enhancement, body from /tmp/s73_umbrella_body.md) with the verified current-state map, the decided design, acceptance criteria, the two sub-tasks (#31 near-term low-lift; #28 deferred/data-dependent), and an explicit out-of-scope/cross-link section (#9 GVA, #37 integration, #36 species, #11/#12 data). Commented on #31 and #28 to link them to #45 as distinct sub-tasks. Verified: #45/#31/#28 all state=OPEN; open count 16 (was 15). - [news-vs-changelog] → CHANGELOG only (S73 entry under [Unreleased]; issue consolidation = dev-process history, not a user-facing release note → NOT NEWS.md; no BACKLOG.md file — open work lives in GitHub Issues, nothing to prune). [macos-dupe-scan]: git add only SESSION_NOTES.md + CHANGELOG.md + PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md; the 2 standing untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html) excluded + untouched.

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A and stated — no runtime behavior changed (created 1 GitHub issue + 2 link comments + edited build-ignored docs; no R/ code). The current-state facts #45 documents were read directly off the working-tree source, not a stale install.

Session 72 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 73): Score 9/10. S72 pre-named THIS deliverable as SUGGESTED-NEXT candidate (1) with the exact two issue numbers (#31 + #28), accurate one-line summaries of each (#31 gestational length replacing the hack; #28 timestamped colocation at birth − gestation), the common touch-point (getPotentialParents.R), and the correct classification + flag (“a grooming/design session… Workflow-worthy if it spans the subsystem — Learning 70”). Every baseline held firsthand: dashboard 98/100, 15 open issues (matched), the standing rules (don’t close an OPEN issue without firsthand evidence, [news-vs-changelog], [macos-dupe-scan], the branch model, the 2-untracked-files disposition, the stale CLAUDE.md version). The candidate was directly actionable — zero time spent discovering what to do. The −1 is the recurring per-issue depth-calibration ceiling (same as S69/S70): the candidate named the two issues but could not pre-load the landscape underneath — that the shared primitive is already half-built as maxGestationalPeriod, that the two are NOT duplicates (severe lift asymmetry), that #28 needs an absent data model, that the function is unwired. That is exactly what this session’s research was for → structural, not a fault. One minor mis-frame in the safe direction: “directly analogous to the #44 consolidation” subtly implied the same close-as-duplicate disposition, which the evidence overturned (→ Learning 72a). No inaccuracies that cost time. ROI: high.

Self-assessment (Session 73): 9/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER in full, session-notes top, dashboard 98/100, git, all 15 issues, ghost-check → none), reported, STOPPED for the owner’s pick. Claimed the session with a Phase 1B stub BEFORE technical work; declared TDD phase (N/A, with reason) every response. Used a Workflow + completeness critic (right call — multi-issue subsystem consolidation; Learning 70 breadth heuristic). Recompute-don’t-inherit extended to the critic: firsthand verification corrected the critic’s species overstatement and surfaced the maxGestationalPeriod-already-exists reframing by reading the core file myself — did NOT publish subagent findings unverified. Let the EVIDENCE decide the disposition (linking umbrella, both open) rather than force-fitting S70’s close-as-dup pattern (→ Learning 72a). Surfaced 3 owner decisions via AskUserQuestion; applied exactly as chosen. Scope discipline: created #45 + linked #31/#28; did NOT close anything, touch code, the stale CLAUDE.md version, the 2 untracked files, or push. Stakeholder corrections: 0. Honest 9 — a clean, fully-verified, 0-correction consolidation with a genuinely distinct methodology contribution (consolidation ≠ deduplication). The −1 (honest): I confirmed the species column is in the raw CSV headers but did NOT trace whether ingestion (getPossibleCols/toCharacter) retains it into the internal pedigree — I stated the verified-narrow fact, but a full trace would have resolved “is species-specific gestation even reachable today” for the #31 implementer (captured as Learning 72c’s honest −1).

Here be dragons: it is a BEHAVIOR CHANGE — test_getPotentialParents.R:46-53 asserts exact dam/sire sets (dams_1/dams_4/sires_1/sires_4) via expect_identical, so the expected-set fixtures must be recomputed + justified (not silently regenerated); reconcile the tests’ 210L vs actual rhesus gestation (~165 d); consider the sire/dam exit-check asymmetry (:62 adjusted vs :69 not). - (2) Spec #28’s data model (the deferred sub-task) — write the colocation data-model spec (grain: cage/room/enclosure; point-in-time vs overlapping range; source query) onto #28, gated on #11/#12. Key files (this session): GitHub: #45 (NEW umbrella, OPEN, body from /tmp/s73_umbrella_body.md — transient), #31 + #28 (OPEN, link comments #31-issuecomment-4700139567 / #28-issuecomment-4700139590). Verified-but-unchanged source read this session: R/getPotentialParents.R (full — :24 signature, :62 sire maxGestationalPeriod use, :69 dam unadjusted exit, :72-94 the dam ±182.5-day “hack” + TODO), tests/testthat/test_getPotentialParents.R:46-53 (exact-set expect_identical assertions), inst/extdata/deidentified_jmac_ped.csv (header carries a species column), R/getProductionStatus.R:48-109 (housing static scalar — the only “location-ish” construct; confirms #28’s data model is absent). Gotchas: (1) #45 is the umbrella (OPEN); #31 + #28 are its OPEN sub-tasks — NOT duplicates, do NOT close them as dups (owner-decided linking consolidation). (2) The shared primitive is ALREADY half-built: getPotentialParents already takes maxGestationalPeriod, applied sire-side only (:62); #31 = extend it to the dam side, NOT add it fresh. Owner decided: reuse/extend that param, NO new parameter. (3) #31 is a BEHAVIOR CHANGE — test_getPotentialParents.R:46-53 asserts exact dam/sire sets via expect_identical; recompute fixtures under TDD with justification, don’t silently regenerate. The tests’ maxGestationalPeriod = 210L already exceeds actual rhesus gestation (~165 d) — reconcile what the value means. (4) #28 needs a timestamped-colocation data model that does NOT exist in the package — blocked on #11 (Oracle) / #12 (ARMS); spec the grain before sizing. Do NOT size #28 as a peer to #31. (5) getPotentialParents is experimental + UNWIRED — only test_getPotentialParents.R + inst/extdata/trulyUnknownParents.R call it; user value needs #37 integration. (6) Species: canonical fixtures (rhesusPedigree/examplePedigree) have NO species column, but some input pedigrees do (deidentified_jmac_ped.csv) + latent multi-species intent (#36) — a species-keyed gestation lookup is a dependency, not present; NOT verified whether ingestion retains the column into the internal pedigree (open for the #31 implementer). (7) Default gh issue view errors on projectCards — use gh issue view N --json <fields>. (8) CLAUDE.md still says “Version 1.0.8” (DESCRIPTION 1.1.0.9000) — stale, carried since S56. (9) The 2 untracked files remain — build-ignored / owner’s standing keep-call. What Session 71 Did

Deliverable: Implement GitHub issue #44 (full umbrella scope) and its #38 sub-task — configurable auto-generated unknown-ID format (default "U%04d", byte-identical back-compat with no config) from a single source of truth: exported getAutoIdFormat()/setAutoIdFormat() + an internal isGeneratedUnknownId() predicate replacing all 7 detection literals across 4 files + 2 generators; case-handling reconciled to case-sensitive. (DONE — feature shipped; #44 + #38 implemented + devtools::check()-green, awaiting owner confirm to close.) Started / Completed: 2026-06-13 Status: DONE. Code session under strict TDD: RED→GREEN→REFACTOR(skipped) — all three transition gates posed via AskUserQuestion, plus a separate pre-RED scope/approach decision (3 questions). TDD phase declared atop every response. 0 stakeholder corrections. One close-out commit on add-methodology. - Owner decisions (pre-RED, via AskUserQuestion, 3 questions): (Scope) full #44; (Detection) prefix-only, byte-identical (startsWith(id, <literal prefix before first %>) — preserves today’s exact matching, incl. the existing over-match of real prefix-IDs); (Case rule) case-sensitive (match generation’s uppercase prefix; reconciles reportGV+obfuscateId, which were insensitive). All applied exactly as chosen. - Recompute-don’t-inherit (Learning 70 applied to a feature): re-read the 8 load-bearing files firsthand to verify S70’s subsystem map against the working tree BEFORE declaring RED — confirmed the 2 generators + 7 detection sites + the case-divergence, rather than trusting the handoff. - Evidence-based decision aid (→ Learning 71b): grepped the test corpus and found the lowercase "u001" in test_obfuscateId.R:28 — the exact datum that makes “reconcile case-sensitivity” a concrete, test-breaking fork. The case-rule AskUserQuestion cited that specific test, so the owner chose against evidence. - New code: R/autoIdFormat.R — getAutoIdFormat() = getOption("nprcgenekeepr.autoIdFormat", "U%04d") + setAutoIdFormat(format) (validates: single non-NA char, non-empty literal prefix, sprintf-template-consuming-one-integer; returns previous invisibly) [both exported]; internal getAutoIdPrefix() + isGeneratedUnknownId() (case-sensitive, NA-preserving like the startsWith/stri_sub it replaces → a true drop-in) [@noRd]. - Threaded through (all 7 literals + 2 generators centralized): addUIds(ped, format = getAutoIdFormat()) mints via sprintf(format, …) (≡ default); obfuscateId mints with getAutoIdPrefix() + size - nchar(prefix) and guards via the predicate; removeAutoGenIds (×3), modPedigree.R:290 display filter, reportGV.R:137-140 founder counts all route detection through the single predicate. removeAutoGenIds’s “use a function call” TODO resolved (docstring updated). qcStudbook unchanged (its addUIds(sb) picks up the option via the default — smallest blast radius). modPedigree.R:112 tooltip notes configurability. - TDD trail (RED→GREEN): RED wrote 2 NEW test files (test_autoIdFormat.R, test_removeAutoGenIds.R) + edited test_obfuscateId.R:27-32 to the case-sensitive contract, and confirmed red (new fns undefined → error; obfuscate test fails vs current insensitive code). GREEN: the new file + 5 edits + devtools::document(). Only ONE existing test changed (test_obfuscateId); test_addUIds/test_qcStudbook/test_modPedigree pass UNCHANGED = the byte-identical back-compat proof (→ Learning 71a). - Verification: Full suite 0 failed / 0 error / 159 skip (985 results; the 5 warnings are pre-existing test_modPyramid.R baseline). devtools::check() exit 0, 1 NOTE — the spelling.R comparison vs a stale spelling.Rout.save; attributed to pre-existing baseline (spell_check_package + check word list flag 0 of my identifiers among 54 words → not introduced/worsened; left as housekeeping, → Learning 71d). Lint clean under load_all (the options() setter carries # nolint: undesirable_function_linter per the getPyramidPlot.R precedent — a public permanent setter). Exhaustive grep confirmed no remaining auto-ID "U" literal in R/ (only the unrelated sex-factor level M/F/U/H). [news-vs-changelog] → BOTH (user-facing feature): NEWS.md NEW-48 + CHANGELOG.md S71 under [Unreleased].

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): DONE via the test suite. The runtime-behavior changes — the modPedigreeServer displayUnknownIds filter (:290) and reportGV() founder counts — are exercised by test_modPedigree*/reportGV tests within the green suite (testServer-level verification, same basis as S68). The new public API is a pure option get/set, tested directly in test_autoIdFormat.R. No separate interactive runModularApp() launch needed.

Session 70 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 71): Score 9/10. S70 pre-named AND fully spec’d THIS deliverable as SUGGESTED-NEXT candidate (1): the exact work (configurable default-preserving format, the isGeneratedUnknownId() predicate replacing the literals, get/setAutoIdFormat, case reconciliation, test updates) AND the three “here be dragons” — byte-identical back-compat, case-sensitivity reconciliation, and “obfuscateId is a second generator that’s easy to miss.” All three dragons were the REAL load-bearing risks (byte-identical was criterion #1; case reconciliation forced the one test change; obfuscateId’s mint genuinely needed special handling). #44 itself (S70’s artifact) carried the firsthand current-state map, acceptance criteria, the “tests that bake in U” list, and an explicit out-of-scope section — every anchor (addUIds.R:47,54, obfuscateId.R:38-43, the 7 sites, getRecordStatusIndex as the model, the qcStudbook ordering) held firsthand. The gotchas were load-bearing and correct: gotcha #3 (second producer) shaped my GREEN; gotcha #2 (U-prefix-only scope) kept me from widening; gotcha #5 (gh issue view --json) I used. I spent ~zero time discovering what to do or where the risks were. The −1: #44’s “tests that bake in U … will need updating” listed four test files, but only one actually needed changing — under the byte-identical default the other three pass unchanged (which is itself the back-compat proof). A conservative over-count in the safe direction, but it slightly mis-framed the blast radius. No inaccuracies that cost time. ROI: very high — best-prepared handoff in the recent chain.

Self-assessment (Session 71): 9/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER in full, session-notes top, dashboard 98/100, git, all 17 issues, ghost-check → none), reported, STOPPED for the owner’s pick. Claimed the session with a Phase 1B stub BEFORE technical work. Declared the TDD phase atop every response and posed a separate pre-RED scope/approach decision (3 questions) + all three transition gates (PRE-RED→RED, RED→GREEN, GREEN→REFACTOR-skipped) via AskUserQuestion — the project’s phase-gate convention, fully exercised. Recompute-don’t-inherit: verified S70’s map firsthand before scoping. Evidence-based decision aids: found the "u001" fixture datum so the case-rule choice cited the exact test that flips. TDD held: RED confirmed red before any R/ code; GREEN minimal; the only existing test changed was the deliberate case one; 3 of 4 “U”-baking tests passed unchanged = the byte-identical proof. Verification depth: full suite (0/0) + devtools::check() (exit 0, NOTE attributed to pre-existing baseline, not reflexively “fixed”) + lint clean + exhaustive completeness grep. Scope discipline: did NOT touch the out-of-scope conventions, the stale CLAUDE.md version, the 2 untracked files; did NOT fix the baseline spelling NOTE (FM #8 resisted); did NOT push. Right-sized SOLO (contained 6-file feature, directly checkable — Learning-70 breadth heuristic; contrast S70’s workflow-worthy subsystem map). Stakeholder corrections: 0. The honest −1: the chosen prefix-only/byte-identical approach satisfies removeAutoGenIds’s TODO only in letter (detection is now a centralized function call) — its deeper goal (“so that actual IDs that start with U are possible”) is not achieved and would need the declined prefix+digits option; this was an explicit owner tradeoff, but it means a known limitation (real “Ulysses”-style IDs are still over-matched, same as before) ships with the feature. Documented as gotcha #3. A clean, fully-verified, 0-correction feature with new exported API + man pages + NEWS + 2 new test files + green check() → honest 9.

⇒ SUGGESTED NEXT = owner’s pick. #44 + #38 are IMPLEMENTED (feature shipped, check()-green); nothing mid-flight. 17 open issues. Candidates: - (1) Close #44 + #38 — the configurable-auto-ID feature is done and check-green; close both with a pointer to the S71 commit (would drop 17 → 15 open; #38 = the generation sub-task delivered, #44 = the umbrella delivered). I deliberately did NOT close them (no owner confirm; closing is outward-facing/irreversible — same S68→S69 discipline). This is a clean close candidate, NOT re-verification work. - (2) Consolidate the parent-ID cluster (#31 + #28) into an umbrella — both touch getPotentialParents.R, both about identifying a missing parent (#31 via gestational length replacing the “hack”; #28 via timestamped colocation). Directly analogous to the #44 consolidation; a grooming/design session (carried from S70). - (3) Act on #37’s surface — 39 genuinely-unused exports cluster into (i) sim-kinship, (ii) modORIPReporting* (defined, never mounted), (iii) obfuscation; each a “wire-in-or-retire” issue. - (4) Housekeeping: (a) CLAUDE.md says “Version 1.0.8” but DESCRIPTION is 1.1.0.9000 (one-line fix, carried since S56); (b) the pre-existing spelling.Rout.save baseline NOTE (regenerate the .save or expand inst/WORDLIST) — surfaced this session, not introduced by it. - (5) Push add-methodology to origin (now ahead by ~24 after this close-out; S51 promoted prior work via PR #41). Do NOT re-open #26/#32 (closed as dupes of #44), and do NOT close #1, #5, #9, or any OPEN issue without firsthand evidence.

Key files (this session): NEW R/autoIdFormat.R (getAutoIdFormat/setAutoIdFormat exported; getAutoIdPrefix/isGeneratedUnknownId @noRd), tests/testthat/test_autoIdFormat.R, tests/testthat/test_removeAutoGenIds.R, man/getAutoIdFormat.Rd, man/setAutoIdFormat.Rd. CHANGED R/addUIds.R (format= param + sprintf), R/obfuscateId.R (prefix mint + predicate guards, :32,38,53-54), R/removeAutoGenIds.R (×3 predicate + TODO resolved), R/modPedigree.R (:290 filter, :112 tooltip), R/reportGV.R (:137-140), NAMESPACE (+2 exports), man/addUIds.Rd + man/removeAutoGenIds.Rd (regenerated), tests/testthat/test_obfuscateId.R:27-32. NEWS.md (NEW-48), CHANGELOG.md (S71), PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 71 at tail), SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). The 2 standing untracked files left untouched. TRANSIENT: /tmp/s71_check.log.

Gotchas: (1) #44 + #38 are IMPLEMENTED but still OPEN — close (with a commit pointer) only on owner confirm; feature is done + check-green = a clean close candidate (17 → 15), not re-verification. (2) Single source of truth = getOption("nprcgenekeepr.autoIdFormat", "U%04d") via getAutoIdFormat()/setAutoIdFormat(); detection = getAutoIdPrefix() (literal before first %) + startsWith, case-SENSITIVE, NA-preserving. (3) Prefix-only detection still over-matches real IDs starting with the prefix (e.g. “Ulysses”) — SAME as before (byte-identical, by owner choice). removeAutoGenIds’s TODO is satisfied only in letter (centralized function call); its deeper “real U-prefixed IDs possible” goal is NOT achieved and would require the declined prefix+digits option (^prefix[0-9]+$). (4) Case reconciliation: reportGV+obfuscateId are now case-SENSITIVE (were insensitive) — test_obfuscateId.R:27-32 updated accordingly (lowercase "u001" → real ID); a real lowercase-"u" animal ID is now treated as real everywhere (the intended fix). (5) Out-of-scope conventions untouched — textual "UNKNOWN" (unknown2NA), recordStatus (addParents/getRecordStatusIndex); don’t widen into them. (6) Pre-existing spelling NOTE — devtools::check() yields 1 NOTE (spelling.R vs stale spelling.Rout.save); 0 of my words flagged; housekeeping candidate, not this session’s regression. (7) obfuscateId mint uses size - nchar(prefix) — a multi-char configured prefix needs size > nchar(prefix). (8) setAutoIdFormat() uses options() permanently (it IS the public setter) — carries # nolint: undesirable_function_linter; do not “fix” to withr::with_options. (9) CLAUDE.md still says “Version 1.0.8” (DESCRIPTION 1.1.0.9000) — stale, carried since S56. (10) The 2 untracked files remain — build-ignored / owner’s standing keep-call. Branch model unchanged: this close-out is on add-methodology; master gets it on the next sync/PR (S51 promoted prior work via PR #41).

What Session 70 Did

Deliverable: Consolidate the ID cluster — GitHub issues #26 / #32 / #38 (auto-generated unknown-ID format/detection) — into one umbrella design issue, grounded in a firsthand subsystem map, with a decided disposition for the three originals. (DONE — umbrella #44 created OPEN; #26 + #32 CLOSED as duplicates; #38 kept OPEN + linked. Open issues 18 → 17.) Started / Completed: 2026-06-13 Status: DONE. Grooming/design session (no production code or tests; TDD phase = N/A declared every response — same non-code classification as S57/S61–S67/S69). One close-out commit on add-methodology. No R/, tests/, man/, .lintr, or NEWS.md changes. - Firsthand subsystem map (Workflow under ultracode): ran a 2-phase workflow — phase 1: 4 parallel facet readers (generation / detection / callers-config / tests); phase 2: an adversarial completeness critic fed the combined inventory. The critic earned its keep: it overturned the mappers’ claims that addUIds is “the only synthesizer” and that “no centralized predicate exists,” surfacing that the ID cluster spans three independent unknown/auto-gen conventions coupled only by ordering in qcStudbook.R:188→198→199 (unknown2NA → addUIds → addParents): (1) textual "UNKNOWN" (unknown2NA.R, case-insensitive); (2) the "U%04d" prefix — 2 producers (addUIds.R:47,54 + obfuscateId.R:38-43) and 7 detection sites in 4 files with inconsistent case-handling (stri_sub/startsWith sensitive vs grepl("^U", ignore.case=TRUE)), no centralized predicate; (3) recordStatus="added" (addParents.R:43-61) with the already-centralized getRecordStatusIndex() predicate (the model #2 should imitate). - Verify-before-publish (the load-bearing discipline): before the irreversible/outward-facing actions, re-read the 8 load-bearing files firsthand (addUIds, removeAutoGenIds, addParents, getRecordStatusIndex, obfuscateId, unknown2NA, the qcStudbook ordering, the modPedigree.R:112 tooltip) to confirm the three-convention thesis + the two-generator claim in source — did NOT publish subagent findings unverified (→ Learning 70). - Owner decisions surfaced via AskUserQuestion (after the owner asked to clarify the first pass and steered “U default, configurable other prefix”): (Configurable) prefix + number format, default "U%04d"; (Scope) the "U"-prefix convention only (textual-UNKNOWN + recordStatus documented as out-of-scope); (Originals) close #26 + #32 as duplicates, keep #38; (Form) umbrella issue only (no docs/ note, since scope is narrow). Applied exactly as chosen. - The outward-facing work: created #44 (enhancement) with the verified current-state map, a single-source-of-truth design (getAutoIdFormat()/setAutoIdFormat() per #38 + an internal isGeneratedUnknownId() predicate replacing the 8 literals + case-sensitivity reconciliation), acceptance criteria (byte-identical back-compat with no config; a non-default-format generate→detect round-trip test; removeAutoGenIds has no unit test today → add one), the “U”-baking tests, and an explicit out-of-scope section. Closed #26 + #32 (--reason "not planned") with comments pointing to #44; commented on #38 to link it. Verified: #44 OPEN; #26/#32 state=CLOSED, closed=true; #38 OPEN; open count 17. - [news-vs-changelog] → CHANGELOG only (S70 entry under [Unreleased]; issue consolidation = dev-process history, not a user-facing release note → NOT NEWS.md; no BACKLOG.md file exists — open work lives in GitHub Issues, nothing to prune). [macos-dupe-scan]: will git add only SESSION_NOTES.md + CHANGELOG.md + PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md; the 2 standing untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html) excluded + untouched. - Honest correction logged: my AskUserQuestion “Originals” option copy said closing 2 = “18 → 16” — it forgot the umbrella is itself a new open issue, so the true net is 18 → 17. Caught at verification, corrected in the issue list + here.

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A and stated — no runtime behavior changed (created/closed GitHub issues + edited build-ignored docs; no R/ code). The current-state facts #44 documents were read directly off the working-tree source (the 8 files above), not a stale install.

Gotchas: (1) #35 is now CLOSED (completed) — don’t re-open or re-verify; the close comment maps shipped code to its acceptance criteria. (2) getDescendantPedigree is the descendants-only primitive — getProbandPedigree=ancestors(up), getDescendantPedigree=descendants(down), getPedDirectRelatives=both+collaterals, getOffspring/getParents=single-level. (3) The trim is strict-lineal by design — a displayed descendant’s other parent (mate) is intentionally NOT pulled in; that is the approved Option-A semantics, not a bug. (4) CLAUDE.md still says “Version 1.0.8” (DESCRIPTION is 1.1.0.9000) — stale, carried since S56; candidate (4) above. (5) The 2 untracked files remain — build-ignored / owner’s standing keep-call. (6) No BACKLOG.md file exists — open work lives in GitHub Issues, not a backlog doc. Branch model unchanged: this close-out is on add-methodology; master gets it on the next sync/PR (S51 promoted prior work via PR #41).

⟢ Carried — Session 68 (implement #35 descendants, DONE; full detail below):

What Session 68 Did

Deliverable: Implement GitHub issue #35 — include descendants in the Pedigree Browser’s “Trim pedigree” filtering (ancestor-inclusion was already done per S67). Strict TDD, Option A (strict lineal) per owner. (DONE — feature shipped; #35 implemented, awaiting owner confirm to close.) Started / Completed: 2026-06-13 Status: DONE. First real code session after the S57–S67 non-code run → genuine RED→GREEN→REFACTOR(skipped) with all three phase gates posed via AskUserQuestion, plus a separate pre-RED Option A/B approach decision. 0 stakeholder corrections. One close-out commit pending on add-methodology. - The approach (owner-chosen via AskUserQuestion, pre-RED): Option A — strict lineal. Trimmed pedigree = focal ∪ transitive ancestors ∪ transitive descendants; no collaterals (siblings/cousins/mates). (Option B = reuse getPedDirectRelatives, which adds collaterals = whole connected pedigree — declined.) - New code: R/getDescendantPedigree.R — new exported transitive-offspring closure (repeat { getOffspring → break when nothing new }), the downward mirror of getProbandPedigree. R/modPedigree.R:292-307 — trim block now unions the ancestor closure (trimPedigree(probands, ped, removeUninformative=FALSE, addBackParents=FALSE)) with the new descendant closure: ped <- ped[ped$id %in% union(ancestors$id, descendants$id), ]. UI help text (:124-126) changed from the “include only relatives” over-promise S67 flagged → “the ancestors and descendants of the focal animals”; docstring (:166-167) updated to match. - TDD trail (RED then GREEN): RED wrote 9 failing assertions and CONFIRMED red — test_getDescendantPedigree.R errored (could not find function) + the 2 new integration tests failed ("GC" %in% ped$id false under ancestors-only). GREEN: created the helper + union edit + docstring/help-text + devtools::document() (man page + NAMESPACE export) + NEWS.md NEW-47. - The regression catch (→ Learning 68): the full-suite clean-regression read surfaced a SECOND stale test in a different file — test_modPedigree.R:419 (“trims pedigree based on focal animals”) asserted the OLD ancestors-only result (nrow==3, expect_false("D")) for focal {A,C}; D is a child of focal A → now a correctly-included descendant. Updated it to the new contract (nrow==4, {A,B,C,D} in, half-sib E still out) with reasoning. I’d only updated the obvious _processing file in RED; the regression read (CLAUDE.md Build/Test/Verify) is the backstop that caught the one RED missed. - Verification: Full suite 972 tests / 0 failed / 0 error / 159 skip. Lint clean — the lone object_usage_linter warning (“no visible global function definition for ‘getDescendantPedigree’”) proven an install-staleness artifact: 0 warnings once linted with pkgload::load_all() (resolves against installed namespace; the new fn is dev-only until reinstall). [news-vs-changelog] → BOTH (user-facing feature): NEWS.md NEW-47 + CHANGELOG.md S68 under [Unreleased]. BACKLOG.md has no #35/descendant ref (grep-verified — nothing to prune).

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): DONE via shiny::testServer. The deliverable changes the modPedigreeServer pedigreeData reactive — the 2 integration tests in test_modPedigree_processing.R (+ the updated test_modPedigree.R trim test) drive that exact reactive through the real server pipeline (processedPedigree → trim union) and confirm the new behavior is active (descendant GC included, collaterals C/E excluded). That is the deterministic runtime verification for a Shiny-module change; no separate interactive runModularApp() browser launch was needed (testServer exercises the changed code path directly).

Session 67 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 68): Score 9/10. S67 pre-named AND fully spec’d THIS deliverable as SUGGESTED-NEXT #1: it documented the Option A/B fork, the exact anchors (R/modPedigree.R:292-302, R/trimPedigree.R:51, R/getProbandPedigree.R:24-40 = upward closure, R/getPedDirectRelatives.R:46-59 = both-directions-incl-collaterals, getOffspring/getParents = single-level), and the load-bearing insight “there is NO transitive descendants-only helper — Option A must add one.” Every anchor held firsthand; the Option A preview I showed the owner came essentially straight from S67’s spec, making PRE-RED→GREEN nearly mechanical. Its gotchas held: trimPedigree has no ancestors=/descendants= params (true); the UI help text over-promises “relatives” (true — I fixed it); the direction cheat-sheet (exact). The −1: S67 listed test_modPedigree.R as a test home but did not flag that its line-419 trim test asserts the old ancestors-only contract and will break under the descendants change — the recurring “names where to start, not the full downstream impact” gap (the same calibration ceiling S65→#37 / S64→#8 noted). Minor, and arguably out of scope for a re-scope session; the full-suite read (which S67 also pointed me to) caught it. ROI: very high — best-prepared handoff in the recent chain.

Self-assessment (Session 68): 9/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER in full, session-notes top, dashboard 98/100, git, all 19 issues), reported, STOPPED for the owner’s pick. Claimed the session with a Phase 1B stub BEFORE technical work. Declared the TDD phase atop every response (PRE-RED→RED→GREEN→REFACTOR-skipped) and posed all three transition gates + the separate pre-RED approach decision via AskUserQuestion — the project’s phase-gate convention, exercised for real for the first time since the non-code run. Read implementations firsthand before scoping (Learning #6): traced the full call chain and inspected lacy1989Ped to design exact-set assertions (D→{D,F,G}, focal-D union excludes sibling C + mate E — the discriminating Option-A test). RED was confirmed red before any R/ code. Proved the lint warning was an artifact (re-lint under load_all → 0) rather than dismissing or “fixing” it. Scope discipline: implemented exactly Option A; did NOT pick B; did NOT refactor when the owner declined; did NOT touch the 2 untracked artifacts or the stale CLAUDE.md version. Right-sized SOLO under ultracode (single-helper feature, focused suite = directly verifiable; a Workflow would be ceremony). Stakeholder corrections: 0. The −1 (honest): my RED inventory was not exhaustive — I updated only the obvious integration file and let the GREEN full-suite read surface the second stale test in test_modPedigree.R; a more disciplined RED would have grep-ed the test corpus for trimPedigree/“trims pedigree” and brought every old-contract assertion into the RED set up front (captured as Learning 68(a)). Caught and fixed cleanly, but ideally surfaced in RED. A clean, fully-verified, 0-correction feature shipped with a new exported helper + docs + NEWS + both test layers → honest 9; the −1 keeps me from claiming a flawless RED.

⇒ SUGGESTED NEXT = owner’s pick. #35 is IMPLEMENTED (feature shipped, suite green); nothing mid-flight. (1) Close #35 — the descendants feature is done and verified; close it with a pointer to the S68 commit (would drop 19 → 18 open). I deliberately did NOT close it (no owner confirm yet; closing an issue is outward-facing/irreversible). (2) Consolidate the ID cluster (#38/#32/#26/#31) into one umbrella design issue. (3) Act on #37’s surface — 39 genuinely-unused exports cluster into (i) sim-kinship subsystem, (ii) modORIPReporting* (defined, never mounted), (iii) obfuscation; each a “wire-in-or-retire” issue. (4) Carried enhancements (NOT filed): warn/handle no-parent founders when origin absent (R/orderReport.R:31); regression test for value=="Undetermined"/rank==NA; combined-file genotype tracking. (5) Housekeeping (carried since S56): CLAUDE.md says “Version 1.0.8” but DESCRIPTION is 1.1.0.9000. (6) Push add-methodology to origin (now ahead by 21 after this close-out). Do NOT close #1 (confirmed false-STALE), #5, #9, or any OPEN issue without firsthand evidence.

Key files (this session): NEW R/getDescendantPedigree.R (exported transitive-offspring closure) + man/getDescendantPedigree.Rd + tests/testthat/test_getDescendantPedigree.R (6 unit tests). CHANGED R/modPedigree.R (trim union :292-307; help text :124-126; docstring :166-167), man/modPedigreeServer.Rd (regenerated), NAMESPACE (+export), NEWS.md (NEW-47), tests/testthat/test_modPedigree_processing.R (+2 integration tests), tests/testthat/test_modPedigree.R (:419 trim test updated to new contract). CHANGELOG.md (S68 entry), PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 68 at tail), SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). The 2 pre-existing untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html) left untouched.

Gotchas: (1) #35 is IMPLEMENTED but still OPEN — close it (with a commit pointer) only on owner confirm; the feature is done + suite-green, so this is a clean close candidate, not re-verification work. (2) getDescendantPedigree is the new descendants-only primitive — getProbandPedigree=ancestors(up), getDescendantPedigree=descendants(down), getPedDirectRelatives=both+collaterals, getOffspring/getParents=single-level. (3) The trim is strict-lineal by design — a displayed descendant’s other parent (mate) is intentionally NOT pulled in, so the trimmed table can show an animal whose sire/dam isn’t a row; that is the approved Option-A semantics, not a bug. (4) object_usage_linter will warn on getDescendantPedigree until the package is reinstalled — it’s an install-staleness artifact (0 warnings under load_all); don’t “fix” it. (5) A behavior-change feature can break tests in files you didn’t touch — grep the test corpus for the changed behavior during RED (Learning 68); the clean-regression read is the backstop. (6) CLAUDE.md still says “Version 1.0.8” (DESCRIPTION is 1.1.0.9000) — stale, carried since S56. (7) The 2 untracked files remain — build-ignored / owner’s standing keep-call. Branch model unchanged: this close-out is on add-methodology; master gets it on the next sync/PR (S51 promoted prior work via PR #41).

⟢ Carried — Session 67 (re-scope #35 to descendants, DONE; full detail below): Task (Session 67 — 2026-06-13): Execute S66 SUGGESTED-NEXT #1 — re-scope GitHub issue #35 (“Include ancestors and descendants in pedigree filtering”) to focus on the remaining descendants work, after firsthand-verifying that ancestor-inclusion is implemented and descendant-inclusion is not. (DONE — #35 re-scoped + retitled, kept OPEN.) Started / Completed: 2026-06-13 Status: DONE. Issue-grooming / re-scope session (no production code or tests written; TDD phase = N/A declared every response — same non-code classification S57/S61–S66 used). One close-out commit on add-methodology. No R/, tests/, man/, .lintr, or NEWS.md changes. - The finding (firsthand — confirmed the handoff directionally, added the depth it couldn’t): #35’s compound “ancestors AND descendants” ask is half done. Ancestors DONE — R/modPedigree.R:292-302: when the “Trim pedigree based on focal animals” checkbox is on, it calls trimPedigree(probands, ped, removeUninformative = FALSE, addBackParents = FALSE) → getProbandPedigree() (R/getProbandPedigree.R:24-40), an upward sire/dam closure (ancestors only). The module docstring already says so (R/modPedigree.R:166: “Optionally trimming to ancestors…”). Descendants NOT — neither trimPedigree nor getProbandPedigree walks downward; getOffspring() is single-level only. - Two stale references the inherited framing did NOT flag (caught firsthand): (1) the issue’s cited “Current Code” (lines 246-253, a # TODO: Include ancestors and descendants placeholder) no longer exists — replaced by the live ancestor logic at 292-302; (2) its “Suggested Implementation” called trimPedigree(focalIds(), ped, ancestors = TRUE, descendants = TRUE) — that signature does not exist (trimPedigree(probands, ped, removeUninformative, addBackParents)). Copying the suggested call forward would have handed the implementer a non-compiling spec (→ Learning 67(a)). - The semantic fork surfaced (not silently picked): “include descendants” has two non-equivalent implementations — (A) strict lineal: add a downward closure mirroring getProbandPedigree’s loop (repeated getOffspring() to closure), union with the ancestor set = focal’s ancestors+descendants only; (B) reuse getPedDirectRelatives() (R/getPedDirectRelatives.R:46-59), which already loops getParents() AND getOffspring() to closure but thereby also pulls in collateral relatives (sibs/cousins/mates) = the whole connected pedigree, broader than lineal. The package has the both-directions primitive but no transitive descendants-only helper. - Applied (owner-chosen via AskUserQuestion, 2 questions): FORM = rewrite the body + retitle to “Include descendants in pedigree filtering (ancestors already implemented)” + dated verification comment; SCOPE = document BOTH options as an open design choice (left for the implementation session). Did gh issue edit 35 --title … --body-file + gh issue comment 35 (comment #issuecomment-4699260833). Issue kept OPEN. - Verified the edit landed: title updated + state: open re-read via gh api; 5 grep checks on the saved body all PASS (cites live 292-302; notes the non-existent ancestors=,descendants= API; both Option A + Option B present; references getPedDirectRelatives; notes the stale 246-253 placeholder is gone). Also noted the UI help text already over-promises (“…include only relatives of the focal animals”, R/modPedigree.R:125) while behavior is ancestors-only — flagged in the body as a label-alignment item. - [news-vs-changelog] → CHANGELOG only (S67 entry under [Unreleased]; dev-process/grooming history, not a user-facing release note → NOT NEWS.md; BACKLOG.md has no #35 / descendants reference — verified by grep, nothing to prune). [macos-dupe-scan]: explicit git add of only SESSION_NOTES.md + CHANGELOG.md + PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (excluded the 2 pre-existing untracked: ..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html).

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A and stated — no runtime behavior changed (edited a GitHub issue + build-ignored docs; no R/ code). The runtime facts the re-scope documents ARE verified, though: the ancestors-done / descendants-not partition was read directly off the live call chain in the working-tree source (modPedigree.R → trimPedigree → getProbandPedigree; getOffspring/getParents/getPedDirectRelatives), not from a stale install.

No R/, tests/, man/, .lintr, or NEWS.md changes.

Gotchas: (1) #35 is RE-SCOPED + OPEN (not closed) — title now “Include descendants in pedigree filtering (ancestors already implemented)”. Ancestors are DONE — don’t re-verify/re-implement them; the remaining work is descendants only. (2) The implementation has a documented FORK (A strict-lineal vs B getPedDirectRelatives) — the owner must pick before a code session; they are NOT equivalent (B adds siblings/cousins/mates). There is no transitive descendants-only helper — Option A must add one. (5) The UI help text over-promises (“include only relatives”, R/modPedigree.R:125) vs ancestors-only behavior — align it when implementing. (6) CLAUDE.md still says “Version 1.0.8” (DESCRIPTION is 1.1.0.9000) — stale, carried since S56. (7) The 2 untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html) remain — build-ignored / owner’s standing keep-call; left untouched. (6) Push add-methodology to origin when the owner wants the 19-commit lead published (S51 promoted prior work via PR #41). Do NOT close #1 (confirmed false-STALE), #5, #9, or any OPEN issue without firsthand evidence.

Key files (this session): the merge commit 0f9728e3 (the 4 wording files: CLAUDE.md:171, PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md:3, SESSION_RUNNER.md:167-181,301, docs/methodology/HOW_TO_USE.md:763); CHANGELOG.md (S66 entry under [Unreleased]); PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 66, appended at tail); SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). .Rbuildignore (read-only, to verify build-ignore). No R/, tests/, man/, .lintr, or NEWS.md changes.

Gotchas: (1) The branch chore/methodology-pr2527-wording is merged AND deleted — don’t look for it; its one commit ce7d6779 is now reachable from add-methodology via the merge 0f9728e3. (2) SESSION_RUNNER.md §3C changed this session (now the canonical adopter-vs-canonical routing) — adopter-project learnings go in CLAUDE.md → Project-Specific Methodology Adaptations → Project-specific Learnings (which this repo redirects to PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md); do NOT edit the “Learnings (added by sessions)” table in SESSION_RUNNER.md — it’s synced from canonical and local edits block future syncs. (3) CLAUDE.md/PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md no longer say “40k limit” — the justification is now the documented size budget (“~200 lines / ~25 KB”); same intent (keep CLAUDE.md small), updated wording. (4) merge-tree prints “changed in both” for any file modified on both sides — that is NOT a conflict; grep for ^<<<<<<< to detect real conflicts. (5) CLAUDE.md still says “Version 1.0.8” (DESCRIPTION is 1.1.0.9000) — stale, carried since S56. (6) The 2 untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html) remain — build-ignored / owner’s standing keep-call; left untouched. Branch model unchanged: this close-out is on add-methodology; master gets it on the next sync/PR.

⟢ Carried — Session 65 (update #37, DONE; full detail below):

⟢ Carried — Session 65 (update #37, DONE; full detail below): Task (Session 65 — 2026-06-12): Execute S64 SUGGESTED-NEXT #1 — update GitHub issue #37 (“Exported functions not currently used by app”): firsthand-verify its 70-function inventory against current code, strike the now-resolved rows, keep the still-accurate unused inventory. (DONE — #37 UPDATED, kept open.) Started / Completed: 2026-06-12 Status: DONE. Issue-grooming session (no production code or tests written; TDD phase = N/A declared every response — same classification S57/S61/S62/S63/S64 used). One close-out commit on add-methodology. No R/, tests/, man/, .lintr, or NEWS.md changes. - The finding — far staler than the handoff anticipated: S64 (inheriting the S62 audit) framed #37 as “strike the resolved Shiny-module + genotype rows; keep the still-accurate inventory” (≈7 functions). A from-scratch call-graph reachability recomputation found 45 of the 70 listed functions are now used by the app — the inventory was ~64% stale, not 2 clusters. Verified totals (current code): 155 exports / 116 used / 39 unused (the issue claimed 108 / 38 / 70). - Method (firsthand, reproducible): pkgload::load_all, seed the call graph at {runModularApp, runGeneKeepR, appUI, appServer}, take the transitive closure of codetools::findGlobals(f)$functions ∩ package-functions; exported names outside the closure = unused. Produced concrete call paths for the flipped set (e.g. rankSubjects: appServer → modGeneticValueServer → reportGV → orderReport → rankSubjects; the 4 Shiny modules called directly by appServer; the genotype trio getGenotypes/addGenotype/hasGenotype via modInputServer; filterKinMatrix/Report/Threshold via the breeding-group + GV modules). Confirmed all 22 “still unused” are genuinely unreachable. - Adversarial check of the static method’s blind spots: findGlobals misses dynamic + S3 dispatch, so I checked both explicitly: the ONLY string-dispatched call in R/ is do.call("rbind", …) (base), and none of the 22 still-unused names appear as a string literal anywhere in R/ → no dynamic invocation the closure missed; the 3 S3 methods (print.summary.*, summary.nprcgenekeeprErr) aren’t dispatched on the app path (the app’s summary() calls hit summary.default on numeric vectors). Classification held: 45 flipped / 22 unused / 3 S3 = 70. - Applied (owner-chosen via AskUserQuestion): strikethrough-in-place + dated verification note in the body (gh issue edit 37 --body-file) — struck the 45 now-used rows (verified: exactly 45 ~~…~~ pairs in the saved body; obfuscateId unstruck, rankSubjects struck), corrected the counts + the 5 “Notable findings”, and folded in the 17 newer unused exports (added since 2026-01-25: the sim-kinship subsystem, modORIPReporting*, calcFE/calcFG, makeFounderStatsTable/makeGeneticSummaryTable, getAncestors, create_wkbk, savePlotToFile, logModuleEvent, safeExecute) so the inventory is complete (39 total). Posted a timeline pointer comment (#issuecomment-4696756359) so the silent body edit is visible to watchers. Issue kept OPEN — the 39 genuinely-unused exports remain the actionable surface. - [news-vs-changelog] → CHANGELOG only (S65 entry under [Unreleased]; dev-process/grooming history, not a user-facing release note → NOT NEWS.md; BACKLOG.md has no #37 reference — open work lives in GitHub Issues, nothing to prune). [macos-dupe-scan]: explicit git add of only SESSION_NOTES.md + CHANGELOG.md + PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (excluded the 2 pre-existing untracked: ..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html).

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A and stated — no runtime behavior changed (edited a GitHub issue + build-ignored docs; no R/ code). The runtime fact the issue documents IS verified, though: pkgload::load_all loaded the package cleanly and the reachability closure is computed against the working-tree source, so the “used by the app” set reflects the live call graph, not a stale install.

Session 64 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 65): Score 7/10. S64 pre-named THIS deliverable as SUGGESTED-NEXT #1 with the right method (verify firsthand) and directionally-correct anchors (R/appUI.R:159,177 + R/appServer.R:276,292 — the modules ARE mounted there). Its gotchas held: #8/#14 closed (didn’t re-treat), the 2-untracked-files disposition, the stale CLAUDE.md version, the branch model. The −3: S64’s SCOPE characterization was materially wrong — “strike the resolved Shiny-module + genotype rows; keep the still-accurate unused-export inventory” implied ≈7 functions flipped and the bulk held; firsthand recomputation found 45/70 flipped. A less-careful session would have patched 7 rows and left ~38 stale ones. Not a fabrication (S64’s SUGGESTED-NEXT framing — “Update #37, not close” — was correct, and it said to verify firsthand), but the same per-issue-classifier calibration miss it diagnosed for S62→#14 and S63→#8, here amplified by the 70-row scale. ROI: high — the pre-named task + method made orient→execute frictionless; my job was to supply the recomputation the inherited framing skipped.

Self-assessment (Session 65): 8/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER in full, session-notes top, dashboard 98/100, git, all issues), reported, STOPPED for the owner (“Update #37”). Claimed the session with a Phase 1B stub BEFORE technical work; declared TDD phase (N/A, with reason) every response. Recompute-don’t-patch was the load-bearing move: rather than striking the ≈2 clusters the handoff named, I recomputed the whole inventory by call-graph reachability — which surfaced that 45/70 (not 7) had flipped, and that rankSubjects/filterKinMatrix/calcGU/etc. were stale entries the inherited framing would have left in place. Used the rigorous reproducible method (codetools::findGlobals closure) over grep or agent-judgment, with concrete call paths as evidence, AND adversarially checked its known blind spots (dynamic + S3 dispatch) rather than hand-waving them. Surfaced FORM + SCOPE to the owner via AskUserQuestion with concrete previews before the outward-facing edit; applied exactly what they chose. Scope discipline: updated #37 only; did NOT close it (kept open — 39 unused remain), did NOT file new issues for the sim-kinship/ORIP clusters (noted in the body as candidates), did NOT touch the 2 untracked artifacts or the stale CLAUDE.md version. Stakeholder corrections: 0. Dings (minor, self-caught): (a) my first reachability script had a path-tracer loop bug (cursor became a vector) → one self-corrected round-trip; (b) an early partition undercounted the flip as 42 because filterKinMatrix/Report/Threshold fell through a set-membership gap — caught and reconciled to the definitive 45 before any edit. Clean, firsthand-verified, 0-correction-to-deliverable outward-facing update with an owner-surfaced re-grade → honest 8. Met the S57–S64 discipline bar.

⇒ SUGGESTED NEXT = owner’s pick. #37 is UPDATED (kept open); nothing mid-flight. 19 open issues (unchanged). From S64’s still-standing recommendations + what THIS session surfaced: (1) Re-scope #35 to “include descendants” (ancestors done; R/modPedigree.R:292-302 + R/trimPedigree.R:51). (2) Consolidate the ID cluster (#38/#32/#26/#31) into one umbrella design issue. (3) Act on #37’s now-sharpened surface — the 39 genuinely-unused exports cluster into (i) the simulated-kinship subsystem (createSimKinships, cumulateSimKinships, countKinshipValues, kinshipMatricesToKValues, kinshipMatrixToKValues, summarizeKinshipValues, + getPotentialParents) — overlaps #8’s orphaned “thread B”; (ii) the ORIP reporting module (modORIPReportingServer/UI) — defined but never mounted; (iii) obfuscation (obfuscateId/Date/Ped, mapIdsToObfuscated). Each is a “wire-in-or-retire” decision worth its own issue. (4) Candidate enhancements carried from S64 (NOT filed): (i) warn/handle no-parent founders when origin absent (R/orderReport.R:31); (ii) regression test for value=="Undetermined"/rank==NA; (iii) integrate/retire the sim-kinship subsystem. (5) Carried from S63: file the combined-file genotype-tracking enhancement (split off #14). (6) Housekeeping (carried since S56): CLAUDE.md says “Version 1.0.8” but DESCRIPTION is 1.1.0.9000. Do NOT close #1 (confirmed false-STALE), #5, #9, or any OPEN issue without firsthand evidence.

Key files (this session): issue #37 (updated body + comment #issuecomment-4696756359); R/appServer.R + R/appUI.R (app entry seeds — modules mounted here); reachability evidence touches R/reportGV.R (→ orderReport/calcGU/getGVPopulation/filterReport), R/orderReport.R (→ rankSubjects), R/modInput.R (→ genotype trio; getFocalAnimalPed → LabKey relatives), R/modBreedingGroups.R / groupAddAssign (→ filterKinMatrix, withinIntegerRange, makeGrpNum, getPotentialSires), R/modSummaryStats.R/R/modGeneticValue.R/R/modGvAndBgDesc.R (the now-mounted modules). CHANGELOG.md (S65 entry under [Unreleased]); PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (Learning 65); SESSION_NOTES.md (this handoff). TRANSIENT: /tmp/reach37*.R (reachability scripts), /tmp/issue37_body.md, /tmp/issue37_comment.md. No R/, tests/, man/, .lintr, or NEWS.md changes.

Gotchas: (1) #37 is UPDATED, NOT closed — it’s open on purpose; the 39 genuinely-unused exports are the remaining surface. Don’t re-strike or re-audit the 45 already marked used. (2) The inventory is reproducible — re-run the codetools::findGlobals closure from {runModularApp, runGeneKeepR, appUI, appServer} to refresh it; the method note is in the issue body. (3) Static reachability has 2 blind spots (dynamic dispatch + S3) — both checked clean THIS session, but if someone adds a do.call("<pkgfn>", …) or an S3 method invoked from the app, the closure would mis-classify; re-run the string-literal grep when re-verifying. (4) 3 S3 methods (print.summary.*, summary.nprcgenekeeprErr) are NOT in getNamespaceExports (registered via S3method) — the issue author counted them as exported; they’re kept in the unused list with an S3 caveat. (5) The sim-kinship subsystem spans BOTH the original 22 (getPotentialParents) AND the new 17 (the rest) — it’s ONE orphaned feature (cross-ref #8 / S64 thread B). (6) CLAUDE.md still says “Version 1.0.8” (DESCRIPTION is 1.1.0.9000) — stale, carried since S56. (7) The 2 untracked files (..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html) remain — build-ignored / owner’s standing keep-call; left untouched. Branch model unchanged: this close-out is on add-methodology; master gets it on the next sync/PR (S51 promoted prior work via PR #41).

⟢ Carried — Session 64 (verify + close #8, DONE; full detail below): Task (Session 64 — 2026-06-12): Execute S63 SUGGESTED-NEXT #1 — verify issue #8 (“Improve handling of non-founder animals without either parents assigned”) firsthand against current code, then CLOSE #8 with a caveat note. (DONE — #8 CLOSED, strengthened caveat.) Started / Completed: 2026-06-12 Status: DONE. Verify-and-close session (no production code or tests written; TDD phase = N/A declared every response — same classification S57/S61/S63 used closing #30/#34/#14). One close-out commit on add-methodology. No R/, tests/, man/, .lintr, or NEWS.md changes. - The finding — re-graded from the audit’s footnote-caveat to a STRENGTHENED caveat (with a reproduction): #8’s two proposed solutions are genuinely implemented, BUT firsthand verification + a direct reproduction showed the fix is gated on the optional origin column and silently recreates the original bug in the default (no-origin) case — plus the issue’s 2021 simulated-kinship thread is orphaned (the audit never mentioned it). The owner picked the strengthened-caveat close via AskUserQuestion. - What’s implemented (verified firsthand): Solution 1 (origin/“From Center” segregation) — ONPRC-born founders with no offspring are split into a noParentage bucket: R/orderReport.R:31,44-54 (is.na(rpt$origin) & totalOffspring==0L & id %in% founders), placed last. Solution 2 (don’t rank them) — R/rankSubjects.R:38 sets value="Undetermined", :44 sets rank=NA. Live — reportGV() → orderReport(finalData, ped) (R/reportGV.R:146); finalData carries origin only when the source ped does (R/reportGV.R:119 intersect(getIncludeColumns(), names(ped))). Green — pkgload::load_all runs: test_orderReport.R 8/0/0/0, test_rankSubjects.R 5/0/0/0. - The caveats (firsthand, beyond the audit’s one-liner): (1) origin-gating silently no-ops — REPRODUCED. Whole noParentage block wrapped in if ("origin" %in% names(rpt)) (R/orderReport.R:31); origin is documented optional (R/getPossibleCols.R:29, R/qcStudbook.R:34), absent from most bundled datasets. I built a synthetic ped+rpt (a no-parent founder, 0 offspring, high gu) and ran orderReport(): without origin → value="High Value", rank=1 (the exact #8 bug, no warning); with origin (NA for the ONPRC animal) → "Undetermined"/NA. (2) No regression pin — test_orderReport.R:15-26 runs the origin path but asserts only positional unknown-ID counts; test_rankSubjects.R never exercises noParentage; the only “Undetermined” in tests is a comment in a usually-skipped e2e (test-e2e-genetic-value-tutorial.R:109). (3) Orphaned thread B — the 2021 simulated-kinship subsystem (getPotentialParents, createSimKinships, kinshipMatricesToKValues, summarizeKinshipValues, countKinshipValues, addKinshipValueCount, cumulateSimKinships) is exported but has NO live caller in R/ (only tests + inst/extdata/trulyUnknownParents.R + vignettes/simulatedKValues.Rmd); the issue’s “discuss with Matt … display/use simulated kinship coefficients” design item is unresolved. All three documented in the close comment as candidate future enhancements; none filed (new-issue creation outside the approved “close with caveat” scope — S63 lesson; I did not even attempt it this time). - Adversarial close gate (3-lens refute workflow wf_f37f1b72-b6a, Explore skeptics vs the working tree): SPLIT — 1 hold-open + 2 close-with-caveat — reconciled by REPRODUCTION, not vote. (i) headline-implemented → refuted=true, hold-open (high): the origin-gating makes the fix illusory in the common case; claimed to have reproduced a no-parent founder ranked “High Value”. I reconstructed it myself (the table above) → upheld, which is what upgraded the close from clean→strengthened-caveat. (ii) test-authenticity → refuted=false: no assertion pins the branch → upheld, caveat not blocker. (iii) intent-completeness → refuted=false: simulated-kinship subsystem orphaned (also surfaced the vignette + example file) → upheld, severable. The lone dissenter was the decisive one — verifying it firsthand beat tallying 2-of-3. - Closed: gh issue comment 8 --body-file + gh issue close 8 with the strengthened-caveat resolution comment (both solutions implemented + the reproduced origin-gating failure + the missing test + the orphaned thread B + why-close-anyway). Verified state=closed, closed_at=2026-06-12T22:10:27Z. [news-vs-changelog] → CHANGELOG only (S64 entry under [Unreleased]; dev-process/close history, not a user-facing release note → NOT NEWS.md; BACKLOG.md has no #8 reference — open work lives in GitHub Issues, nothing to prune). [macos-dupe-scan]: explicit git add of only SESSION_NOTES.md + CHANGELOG.md + PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (excluded the 2 pre-existing untracked: ..Rcheck/, PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html).

Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A and stated — no runtime behavior changed (closed a GitHub issue + edited build-ignored docs; no R/ code). The runtime behavior #8 concerns IS verified, though: the firsthand reproduction drove orderReport() end-to-end and showed both branches (High Value rank-1 without origin; Undetermined/NA with it) — a stronger, cheaper gate than a manual app boot.

Session 63 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 64): Score 8/10. S63 pre-named THIS deliverable as SUGGESTED-NEXT #1 with accurate evidence anchors (#8 R/orderReport.R:44-54 + R/rankSubjects.R:38,43, “gated on the optional origin column + no regression test on the Undetermined/rank=NA branch”, “verify firsthand first, same as #14”) — every anchor correct firsthand, and the method (verify firsthand + adversarial refute before an outward-facing close) was exactly right and made orient→execute near-frictionless. Its gotchas held: gh issue view flakiness (I used gh api throughout), the audit RECOMMENDS-not-executes, “#8’s close is CAVEATED”, the 2-untracked-files disposition, the stale CLAUDE.md version. The −2: S63 (inheriting the S62 audit’s framing) presented the origin-gating as a footnote caveat and did not flag (a) that the gating silently recreates the bug in the default no-origin path — a reproduction, not a code-read, makes that vivid — nor (b) the orphaned 2021 simulated-kinship subsystem / the “discuss with Matt” thread at all. Same calibration miss as S62→#14: the per-issue classifier checks the headline + cited anchors and stops; it doesn’t probe the alternate input path or the comment thread’s second arc. Not a fabrication (S63 explicitly said “verify firsthand first” — exactly the discipline that caught it). ROI: very high.

Self-assessment (Session 64): 8/10. Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER in full, session-notes top, dashboard 98/100, git, all issues), reported, STOPPED for the owner (“1”). Claimed the session with a Phase 1B stub BEFORE technical work; declared TDD phase (N/A, with reason) every response. Verify-first + reproduction was the load-bearing move: I built my own evidence base (read orderReport/rankSubjects/reportGV/getIncludeColumns/getFounders, ran the two tests via load_all, traced the pipeline) and — when the adversarial gate split 1 hold-open / 2 close — I reproduced the dissenting verdict’s claim in code rather than going with the 2-of-3 majority; the reproduction (High Value rank-1 without origin) is what upgraded the grade and what I brought to the owner. Surfaced the premise flip (audit footnote → reproduced silent-failure + orphaned subsystem) via AskUserQuestion before the irreversible close — owner chose strengthened caveat. Scope discipline: documented all 3 remaining items in the close comment but filed NO new issues (S63 lesson applied proactively — did not even attempt the create); did NOT touch the 2 untracked artifacts; did NOT fix the stale CLAUDE.md version. Stakeholder corrections: 0. Dings (minor, self-caught): (a) my orientation report + pre-gate framing carried the inherited “close with caveat” framing until the reproduction sharpened it to “strengthened caveat” — though that is exactly what verify-first is for; (b) no novel methodology beyond applying the S63 pattern well (its one genuine extension — reproduce-the-dissenter on a split gate — is captured as Learning 64). Clean, authoritatively-verified (with a reproduction), 0-correction close with a real owner-surfaced re-grade → honest 8. Met the S57–S63 discipline bar.

⇒ SUGGESTED NEXT = owner’s pick. #8 is CLOSED (strengthened caveat); nothing mid-flight. 19 open issues (was 20). From the S62 audit’s still-standing recommendations (map: docs/audits/BACKLOG_STALENESS_AUDIT_2026-06-12.md) plus items THIS session surfaced: (1) Update #37 — strike the resolved Shiny-module + genotype rows; keep the still-accurate unused-export inventory (anchors R/appUI.R:159,177 + R/appServer.R:276,292). (2) Re-scope #35 to “include descendants” (ancestors done; R/modPedigree.R:292-302 + R/trimPedigree.R:51). (3) Consolidate the ID cluster (#38/#32/#26/#31) into one umbrella design issue. (4) New candidate enhancements surfaced by S64 (NOT filed — owner’s call to file): (i) warn/handle no-parent founders when the origin column is absent so they aren’t silently ranked most-valuable (R/orderReport.R:31); (ii) add a regression test asserting value=="Undetermined"/rank==NA for the noParentage branch; (iii) integrate or retire the orphaned simulated-kinship subsystem (the pending Matt discussion). (5) Carried from S63: file the combined-file genotype-tracking enhancement (split off #14). (6) Housekeeping (carried since S56): CLAUDE.md says “Version 1.0.8” but DESCRIPTION is 1.1.0.9000. Do NOT close #1 (confirmed false-STALE), #5, #9, or any of the other OPEN issues without firsthand evidence.

Key files (this session): R/orderReport.R:26-84 (the origin-gated noParentage split at :31,44-54; calls getFounders + rankSubjects); R/rankSubjects.R:35-48 (the noParentage→Undetermined/rank=NA branch at :38,44); R/reportGV.R:119,144-146 (how rpt/finalData gets origin only via intersect(getIncludeColumns(), names(ped))); R/getIncludeColumns.R:14-19 (“origin” in the superset); R/getFounders.R + R/isFounder.R (founder = both parents NA); R/getPossibleCols.R:29 + R/qcStudbook.R:34 (origin documented OPTIONAL); tests/testthat/test_orderReport.R:10-44 (runs origin path, asserts only positional U-counts — does NOT pin Undetermined) + tests/testthat/test_rankSubjects.R:5-12 (never exercises noParentage) + tests/testthat/test-e2e-genetic-value-tutorial.R:99-133 (e2e, asserts only “High Value”, usually skipped); orphaned thread B: R/getPotentialParents.R, R/createSimKinships.R, R/kinshipMatricesToKValues.R, R/summarizeKinshipValues.R, R/countKinshipValues.R, R/addKinshipValueCount.R, R/cumulateSimKinships.R, inst/extdata/trulyUnknownParents.R, vignettes/simulatedKValues.Rmd. Gotchas: (1) #8 is CLOSED (strengthened caveat) — don’t re-add it as a bug to implement. The headline (no-parent founders ranked most-valuable) IS fixed when origin is present. (2) The origin-gating silent-failure is REAL (R/orderReport.R:31): without an origin column, no-parent founders are ranked normally (reproduced: “High Value” rank 1) with no warning. If the owner wants this hardened, it’s candidate enhancement (4)(i) — NOT filed. (3) Thread B (simulated-kinship subsystem) is orphaned — exported, tested, vignette’d, but unintegrated into the live GVA/app pipeline; the “discuss with Matt” design item is open. Candidate enhancement (4)(iii) — NOT filed. (4) Split adversarial gate → reproduce the dissenter, don’t tally votes (Learning 64): the lone hold-open verdict was substantively correct; a code reproduction broke the tie, not the 2-of-3 count. (5) origin reaches the report only via intersect(getIncludeColumns(), names(ped)) (R/reportGV.R:119) — so report-level origin presence == source-pedigree origin presence. (6) CLAUDE.md still says “Version 1.0.8” (DESCRIPTION is 1.1.0.9000) — stale, carried since S56.
